  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this compliance certification expires and requires renewal testing. Drives automated retest scheduling.']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent laboratory test or audit conducted for this material-standard combination. Used to calculate next test due date.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp of the most recent update to this compliance record, tracking audit trail for regulatory purposes.']
  next_test_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next compliance test is required based on test_frequency and last_test_date. Drives quality calendar and lab scheduling.']
  non_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes on any non-compliance findings, failed test parameters, or corrective actions required. Critical for RTV decisions and supplier corrective action requests.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality manager, supplier, or business unit responsible for maintaining this compliance record and scheduling retests.']
  test_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of compliance testing for this specific material-standard pair. May differ from the standard\'s default frequency based on material risk profile or supplier history.']
  test_report_url varchar [note: 'File path or URL to the detailed test report or certification document for this compliance record. Used for audit and supplier communication.']
  testing_lab varchar [note: 'Name or code of the accredited laboratory that performed the compliance testing for this material-standard combination.']
  waiver_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which any granted compliance waiver expires. Null if no waiver granted or waiver is permanent.']
  waiver_granted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a compliance waiver or exception has been granted for this material-standard combination, allowing use despite non-compliance.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance relationship between materials and quality standards in the apparel manufacturing process. It captures the certification status, testing history, and regulatory compliance tracking for each material-standard combination. Each record links one material to one quality standard with attributes that track compliance lifecycle, test results, and certification validity periods.. Existence Justification: In apparel manufacturing, materials must comply with multiple quality standards simultaneously (e.g., a single organic cotton fabric must meet OEKO-TEX, GOTS, REACH, and CPSIA requirements), and each quality standard applies to hundreds or thousands of materials across the product portfolio. The business actively manages compliance as an operational process: quality teams track certification status, schedule lab tests, manage expiration dates, and make material approval decisions based on compliance records. This is not an analytical correlation but a core quality assurance workflow.'
}

Table product.brand {
  brand_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for brand']
  parent_brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent brand if this is a sub-brand or brand extension. Null for standalone brands.']
  brand_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the brand manager for internal communication and coordination.']
  brand_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual responsible for managing the brand strategy, marketing, and performance.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Primary market classification of the brand indicating its positioning and target segment within the apparel and fashion industry.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used internally to identify the brand in systems, SKUs, and reporting. Typically 2-10 uppercase characters.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the brand\'s identity, heritage, values, and market positioning used for internal reference and external marketing.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'The date when the brand was officially discontinued or retired from active marketing and sales. Null for active brands.']
  founding_year varchar [note: 'The year the brand was established or founded, used for heritage marketing and brand storytelling.']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date when the brand was officially launched to the market or introduced to consumers.']
  license_end_date date [note: 'The date when the brand license agreement expires or is set to be renewed. Null for perpetual or owned brands.']
  license_start_date date [note: 'The date when the brand license agreement became effective. Applicable only for licensed brands.']
  license_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the ownership and licensing model for the brand (owned outright, licensed from another entity, licensed to partners, or joint venture).']
  licensor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the entity that owns the brand intellectual property if the brand is licensed in. Null for owned brands.']
  logo_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the official brand logo image file used in digital and print materials.']
  name varchar [note: 'The official registered name of the brand as it appears on products, marketing materials, and legal documents.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the brand was founded or is headquartered.']
  product_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of primary product categories the brand offers (e.g., footwear, apparel, accessories, equipment).']
  social_media_handles varchar [note: 'JSON or comma-separated string containing the brand\'s official social media account handles across platforms (Instagram, Facebook, Twitter, TikTok, etc.).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the brand indicating whether it is actively marketed, discontinued, or in transition.']
  sustainability_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of sustainability and ethical certifications the brand has achieved (e.g., B Corp, Fair Trade Certified, GOTS, OEKO-TEX, bluesign).']
  sustainability_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand holds recognized sustainability certifications (e.g., B Corp, Fair Trade, GOTS, bluesign).']
  tagline varchar [note: 'Official marketing tagline or slogan associated with the brand, used in advertising and promotional materials.']
  target_age_range varchar [note: 'Primary age demographic range the brand targets, expressed as a range or category (e.g., 18-35, youth, mature).']
  target_gender varchar [note: 'Primary gender demographic the brand targets with its product offerings.']
  tier varchar [note: 'Price and quality positioning tier of the brand within the portfolio, used for merchandising and pricing strategy.']
  trademark_registration_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary jurisdiction where the brand trademark is registered.']
  trademark_registration_number varchar [note: 'Official trademark registration number issued by the relevant intellectual property office for the brand name and logo.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the brand record was last modified or updated in the system.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the brand\'s primary digital presence and e-commerce platform.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for brand. Referenced by brand_id.'
}

Table product.fabric_type {
  fabric_type_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for fabric_type']
  parent_fabric_type_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fabric_type (parent_fabric_type_id)']
  abrasion_resistance varchar [note: 'The fabric\'s durability against surface wear from friction and rubbing.']
  anti_microbial boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fabric has been treated with anti-microbial agents to inhibit bacterial growth.']
  breathability_rating varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of the fabric\'s air permeability and moisture vapor transmission.']
  care_instructions varchar [note: 'Recommended washing, drying, ironing, and maintenance instructions for garments made from this fabric.']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the fabric construction method or material family.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used for internal identification and cataloging of the fabric type.']
  colorfastness_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the fabric\'s resistance to color fading or bleeding during washing, light exposure, and wear.']
  cost_per_yard_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'The standard procurement cost of the fabric per linear yard, used for costing and margin analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fabric type record was first created in the system.']
  discontinuation_date date [note: 'The date on which this fabric type was or will be discontinued from the active catalog.']
  dyeing_method varchar [note: 'The method used to apply color to the fabric.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this fabric type became available for use in product development and production.']
  fiber_composition varchar [note: 'Detailed breakdown of fiber content by percentage (e.g., 60% Cotton, 40% Polyester). Required for labeling compliance.']
  finish_treatment varchar [note: 'Chemical or mechanical treatments applied to the fabric (e.g., water-repellent, anti-microbial, wrinkle-resistant, moisture-wicking).']
  flame_retardant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fabric has been treated to resist ignition and slow flame spread.']
  hand_feel varchar [note: 'Subjective assessment of the fabric\'s tactile quality and drape characteristics.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days required from order placement to fabric delivery.']
  minimum_order_quantity_yards decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum quantity of fabric that must be ordered from suppliers, expressed in linear yards.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this fabric type record was last updated.']
  moisture_wicking boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fabric has moisture-wicking properties to draw perspiration away from the body.']
  name varchar [note: 'The common name or designation of the fabric type (e.g., Cotton Jersey, Polyester Twill, Merino Wool).']
  pilling_resistance varchar [note: 'The fabric\'s ability to resist the formation of surface pills or fuzz balls during wear and laundering.']
  primary_fiber varchar [note: 'The dominant fiber in the fabric composition (e.g., Cotton, Polyester, Wool, Nylon, Spandex).']
  print_method varchar [note: 'The technique used to apply patterns or designs to the fabric surface.']
  recycled_content_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of recycled material in the fabric composition, supporting circular economy initiatives.']
  season_suitability varchar [note: 'The seasonal collections or climate conditions for which this fabric is most appropriate.']
  shrinkage_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of dimensional change after washing, critical for pattern grading and fit.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fabric type in the product catalog.']
  stretch_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of stretch the fabric can achieve, typically measured in the direction of greatest elasticity.']
  stretch_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the directional stretch characteristics of the fabric.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the fabric category (e.g., jersey, twill, satin, fleece, mesh).']
  suitable_for_product_category varchar [note: 'Recommended apparel or accessory categories for which this fabric type is best suited (e.g., activewear, outerwear, casualwear, footwear uppers, accessories). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: activewear|outerwear|casualwear|formalwear|footwear|accessories|intimates|swimwear — promote to reference product]']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or ethical certifications held by the fabric (e.g., GOTS, OEKO-TEX, bluesign, Cradle to Cradle, BCI).']
  tear_strength_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the force required to propagate a tear in the fabric.']
  texture varchar [note: 'The tactile surface quality of the fabric (e.g., smooth, brushed, ribbed, textured, slub).']
  uv_protection_rating varchar [note: 'The fabric\'s ability to block ultraviolet radiation, expressed as a UPF value.']
  water_resistance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fabric has been treated or constructed to resist water penetration.']
  weight_gsm decimal(8,2) [note: 'The weight of the fabric measured in grams per square meter, a key specification for fabric density and application suitability.']
  weight_oz_per_sq_yd decimal(8,2) [note: 'The weight of the fabric measured in ounces per square yard, commonly used in North American markets.']
  width_inches decimal(6,2) [note: 'Standard width of the fabric roll or bolt measured in inches.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for fabric_type. Referenced by fabric_type_id.'
}

Table customer.profile {
  profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the consumer profile record. Serves as the primary key and golden identity anchor across all downstream systems including loyalty, preferences, interactions, and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) analytics.']
  circular_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.circular_program. Business justification: Customers enroll in brand take-back, resale, and recycling programs. Tracks which circular program a profile is enrolled in for incentive fulfillment, participation reporting, and customer lifecycle m']
  crm_contact_id varchar [note: 'Native contact identifier from Salesforce CRM (Salesforce Contact ID). Used to cross-reference the golden profile with the operational CRM record for campaign management, service interactions, and sales activity tracking.']
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'FK to customer.loyalty_tier']
  merged_into_profile_id varchar [note: 'When profile_status = \'merged\', this field contains the profile_id of the surviving golden master profile into which this record was consolidated. Enables identity resolution audit trails and supports data subject access requests (DSAR) under GDPR Article 15 by tracing merged identities.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consumer\'s preferred or most frequently visited retail store location. Used for store-level clienteling, localized promotions, in-store event invitations, and store traffic attribution in SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  acquisition_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the consumer profile was first created or acquired. Tracks whether the consumer originated from e-commerce (Salesforce Commerce Cloud), a retail store (SAP CAR POS), wholesale partnership, mobile app, social media campaign, or loyalty program signup. Used for Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) analysis and channel attribution. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ecommerce|retail_store|wholesale|mobile_app|social_media|loyalty_signup|referral|pop_up — promote to reference product]']
  address_city varchar [note: 'City component of the consumer\'s primary residential address. Used for geographic market segmentation, store proximity analysis, and regional campaign targeting.']
  address_country varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the consumer\'s primary residential address. Determines applicable data residency requirements, shipping eligibility, and regulatory jurisdiction for data processing.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the consumer\'s primary residential address (street number and street name). Used for direct mail campaigns, order shipping, loyalty card delivery, and geographic segmentation analytics.']
  address_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the consumer\'s primary residential address. Used for geographic segmentation, trade area analysis, store proximity scoring, and regional assortment planning insights.']
  adobe_ecid varchar [note: 'Adobe Experience Cloud Identity (ECID) assigned by Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) for cross-channel identity stitching, personalization, and real-time audience segmentation. Core identifier in the identity resolution graph.']
  birth_date date [note: 'Consumer\'s date of birth in yyyy-MM-dd format. Used for age verification, birthday loyalty reward triggers, demographic segmentation, and compliance with COPPA (Children\'s Online Privacy Protection Act) for consumers under 13.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the California consumer has exercised their right to opt out of the sale or sharing of personal information under CCPA. True = opted out of data sale/sharing; False = no opt-out exercised. Applicable to California residents. Must be honored within 15 business days per CCPA Section 1798.120.']
  country_of_residence varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the consumer\'s country of residence. Determines applicable privacy regulations (GDPR for EU residents, CCPA for California residents), tax jurisdiction, and eligible product catalog.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consumer profile record was first created in the Silver Layer data lakehouse. Recorded in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for data lineage, audit compliance, and consumer tenure calculations.']
  customer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consumer profile by relationship type. Distinguishes Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) retail shoppers, Business-to-Business (B2B) wholesale account contacts, VIP/high-value consumers, employee accounts, brand influencers, and anonymous guest profiles. Drives differential pricing, service levels, and personalization strategies. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: b2c_consumer|b2b_wholesale|vip|employee|influencer|anonymous — promote to reference product]']
  data_retention_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer has consented to extended data retention beyond the minimum operational period. True = extended retention consented; False = standard minimum retention applies. Supports GDPR Article 5(1)(e) storage limitation principle and data governance policy enforcement.']
  email varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s primary email address used for Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) communications, e-commerce account login, loyalty enrollment, and marketing campaign delivery. Serves as a key deterministic identity resolution signal.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer has provided explicit consent to receive marketing emails. True = opted in; False = opted out or no consent given. Mandatory compliance flag for CAN-SPAM, GDPR Article 6, and CCPA opt-out requirements. Must be honored in all email campaign execution.']
  family_name varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s legal family name (last name or surname) as captured at registration or point of sale. Combined with given_name to form the full display name for communications and identity resolution.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer has provided valid GDPR consent for personal data processing. True = consent granted; False = consent not granted or withdrawn. Applicable to EU/EEA residents. Mandatory for lawful data processing under GDPR Article 6(1)(a). Must be re-evaluated upon consent withdrawal.']
  gender_identity varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s self-reported gender identity. Used for personalized product recommendations, size guidance, targeted marketing, and assortment planning analytics. Collected with explicit consent per GDPR and CCPA requirements.']
  given_name varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s legal given name (first name) as captured at registration or point of sale. Used for personalized communications, loyalty program enrollment, and CRM record matching.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this consumer profile to a household grouping entity. Enables household-level analytics, shared loyalty benefit structures, and family-based personalization strategies. Multiple individual profiles may share the same household_id.']
  identity_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0.0000 to 1.0000) representing the certainty of the identity resolution linking multiple cross-channel identifiers to this golden profile. Higher scores indicate stronger deterministic signal overlap. Used to prioritize profile merge decisions and flag low-confidence profiles for manual review in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  identity_resolution_method varchar [note: 'The method used to resolve and stitch cross-channel identifiers into this golden profile. \'deterministic\' uses exact-match signals (email, loyalty card); \'probabilistic\' uses statistical inference (device fingerprint, behavioral signals); \'hybrid\' combines both; \'manual\' indicates agent-assisted resolution. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform Identity Service.']
  is_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer\'s identity has been verified through email confirmation, phone OTP, or document verification. True = identity verified; False = unverified or pending. Verified profiles receive higher identity confidence scores and are eligible for full loyalty benefits and personalization features.']
  linked_identifier_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cross-channel identifiers (email, phone, cookie ID, device ID, loyalty card, POS customer ID, Salesforce contact ID, Adobe ECID) currently linked to this golden profile in the identity resolution graph. Higher counts indicate a more complete omnichannel view of the consumer.']
  loyalty_card_number varchar [note: 'Physical or digital loyalty program card number issued to the consumer. Used as a cross-channel identifier linking in-store POS transactions, e-commerce orders, and loyalty reward accruals to the golden profile.']
  loyalty_enrollment_date date [note: 'Date on which the consumer enrolled in the brand\'s loyalty program. Used to calculate loyalty tenure, anniversary reward triggers, and cohort-based loyalty program performance analysis.']
  loyalty_points_balance varchar [note: 'Current unredeemed loyalty points balance for the consumer. Represents accrued reward currency from purchases, referrals, and engagement activities. Used for redemption eligibility checks, tier qualification calculations, and loyalty liability reporting.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response from the consumer, ranging from 0 (detractor) to 10 (promoter). Used to classify consumers as Promoters (9-10), Passives (7-8), or Detractors (0-6) for brand health tracking and CRM segmentation in Salesforce.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response was recorded. Used to assess recency of sentiment data and determine eligibility for follow-up NPS surveys based on survey frequency rules.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s primary contact phone number in E.164 international format. Used for SMS marketing opt-ins, order notifications, loyalty communications, and as a probabilistic identity resolution signal.']
  pos_customer_code varchar [note: 'Customer identifier assigned within the SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) POS system for in-store transaction linkage. Enables reconciliation of retail store purchases to the golden consumer profile.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s preferred communication language expressed as an IETF BCP 47 language tag (e.g., en-US, fr-FR, de-DE). Drives localized content delivery, email template selection, and customer service routing in Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  preferred_shoe_size varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s self-reported or purchase-history-derived preferred footwear size including size system notation (e.g., US 10, EU 44, UK 9). Used for footwear product recommendations, inventory allocation, and size curve analytics in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  preferred_size_bottom varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s self-reported or algorithmically inferred preferred size for bottoms/lower body apparel (e.g., waist/inseam measurements or S/M/L sizing). Used for personalized product recommendations, size-based filtering, and returns reduction analytics.']
  preferred_size_top varchar [note: 'Consumer\'s self-reported or algorithmically inferred preferred size for tops/upper body apparel (e.g., XS, S, M, L, XL, XXL, or numeric sizing). Used for personalized product recommendations, size-based filtering, and fit analytics in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consumer profile record. \'active\' indicates a verified, engaged consumer; \'merged\' indicates the record has been consolidated into a master golden profile; \'pending_verification\' indicates email or identity not yet confirmed. Drives CRM suppression lists and data governance workflows.']
  push_notification_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer has enabled push notifications on their registered mobile device(s) for the brand\'s mobile application. True = enabled; False = disabled. Drives mobile engagement campaign eligibility in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  registration_source varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this consumer profile was originally created or first ingested. Identifies whether the profile originated from Salesforce Commerce Cloud (e-commerce), Adobe Experience Platform (digital), SAP Customer Activity Repository (POS/in-store), mobile app, or manual entry. Critical for data lineage and source system reconciliation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: salesforce_commerce_cloud|adobe_aep|sap_car_pos|mobile_app|in_store_tablet|wholesale_portal|manual_entry — promote to reference product]']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the consumer has provided explicit consent to receive SMS/text message marketing communications. True = opted in; False = opted out. Required for TCPA (Telephone Consumer Protection Act) compliance in the US and equivalent regulations in other jurisdictions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to any attribute on this consumer profile record. Recorded in ISO 8601 format. Used for change data capture (CDC) processing, data freshness monitoring, and audit trail compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']

  Note: 'SSOT master record and golden identity for every individual consumer (B2C) across DTC, retail, and e-commerce channels. Captures full identity attributes including name, date of birth, gender, preferred language, contact details, GDPR/CCPA consent flags, communication opt-ins, household membership, and CRM source system identifiers. Embeds the complete identity resolution graph linking multiple cross-channel identifiers (email, phone, cookie ID, device ID, loyalty card number, POS customer ID, Salesforce contact ID, Adobe ECID) with link type, link confidence scores, resolution methods (deterministic, probabilistic), source systems, and active status. Serves as the anchor entity for loyalty, preferences, interactions, CLTV, and all downstream personalization.'
}

Table customer.wholesale_account {
  wholesale_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the wholesale account record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for all downstream joins and lineage tracking.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_agreement. Business justification: Major wholesale accounts negotiate dedicated sourcing agreements for capacity allocation, pricing tiers, and exclusive manufacturing windows. Nordstrom, Macy\'s, and other tier-1 accounts secure produc']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Wholesale accounts in multi-entity apparel businesses must be assigned to the contracting legal entity (company code) for statutory reporting, tax compliance, intercompany revenue recognition, and leg']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal sales representative or account manager responsible for managing this wholesale account relationship. Used for territory management, commission calculation, and CRM activity tracking.']
  parent_account_wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the parent wholesale account record. Supports parent-child hierarchy modeling for franchise networks, buying groups, and corporate group structures. Null for top-level accounts.']
  preferred_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Wholesale accounts (retailers, distributors) often have preferred vendor relationships for private label manufacturing, co-branding, or exclusive production agreements. Critical for vendor allocation,']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.rfq. Business justification: Wholesale accounts (department stores, specialty retailers) participate in RFQ processes for private label and exclusive product development programs. Buyers submit design briefs and cost targets thro']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Large retail buyers require factory-level compliance approval before production. Real business process: factory social compliance audits, buyer-approved factory lists, restricted factory policies. Ess']
  account_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric account number assigned to the wholesale buyer in the ERP system (SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Number). Used as the primary business identifier on purchase orders, invoices, and EDI transactions.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wholesale account. Controls order acceptance, credit extension, and EDI transaction processing. \'On Hold\' blocks new purchase orders pending credit review.']
  account_tier varchar [note: 'Strategic tier classification of the wholesale account reflecting revenue contribution, strategic importance, and service priority. Platinum and Gold accounts receive dedicated sales reps, priority allocation, and preferential credit terms.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the wholesale account by channel and business model. Drives assortment planning, pricing strategy, and service level agreements. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: department_store|specialty_retailer|boutique|online_retailer|franchise|buying_group|distributor|corporate_group — promote to reference product]']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the wholesale account\'s billing location. Used on invoices, credit documents, and financial correspondence.']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City of the wholesale account\'s billing address. Used for invoice generation, tax jurisdiction determination, and geographic sales reporting.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the wholesale account\'s billing address. Drives VAT/GST applicability, currency assignment, and cross-border trade compliance.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the wholesale account\'s billing address. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, freight zone calculation, and geographic analytics.']
  buying_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of purchasing authority held by the primary contact at the wholesale account. \'Full\' = can commit to purchase orders independently; \'Limited\' = requires co-approval; \'None\' = informational contact only. Used to ensure correct approval routing.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Expiry date of the current wholesale trading agreement. Null indicates an open-ended agreement. Used to trigger contract renewal workflows and prevent order acceptance on expired terms.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the current wholesale trading agreement or master service agreement with this account. Defines the period during which agreed pricing, MOQ, and credit terms are binding.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code of the wholesale account\'s primary registered business address. Used for trade compliance, duty calculations, and regulatory reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wholesale account record was first created in the data platform. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR data retention policy enforcement.']
  credit_limit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum outstanding accounts receivable balance approved for this wholesale account in the account\'s transaction currency. Enforced by SAP S/4HANA FI credit management to block orders when exceeded.']
  credit_rating varchar [note: 'Internal or external credit risk rating assigned to the wholesale account. Informs credit limit setting, payment term negotiation, and order hold decisions. Aligned with Dun & Bradstreet or internal credit scoring models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AAA|AA|A|BBB|BB|B|CCC|D — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the wholesale account\'s default transaction currency. Used for purchase order pricing, invoicing, and financial reporting.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit Dun & Bradstreet Data Universal Numbering System (DUNS) identifier uniquely identifying the legal business entity globally. Used for credit risk assessment, EDI partner setup, and supplier onboarding.']
  edi_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wholesale account is set up for Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) transaction processing (purchase orders, invoices, advance ship notices). True = EDI-enabled; False = manual/portal-based ordering.']
  edi_partner_code varchar [note: 'EDI interchange qualifier and sender/receiver ID assigned to the wholesale account for EDI transaction routing. Required for X12 850 (PO), 810 (Invoice), and 856 (ASN) message exchange.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this account within the parent-child hierarchy. Level 1 = top-level corporate group; Level 2 = regional subsidiary; Level 3 = individual franchise or buying office. Used for roll-up reporting and consolidated financial analysis.']
  incoterms_code varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms 2020) code defining the delivery obligations, risk transfer point, and cost responsibility between Apparel Fashion and the wholesale account. FOB and DDP are most common in apparel wholesale. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  industry_segment varchar [note: 'Primary apparel industry segment the wholesale account operates in. Used for targeted collection planning, assortment curation, and marketing alignment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: athletic|lifestyle|luxury|outdoor|workwear|childrenswear|accessories|footwear — promote to reference product]']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the wholesale account as filed with the relevant government authority. Used for contracts, invoicing, and regulatory compliance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA SD Customer Master.']
  moq_units varchar [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) in units agreed with this wholesale account per purchase order or per style. Enforced during order entry to ensure production viability and margin targets.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey result from the wholesale account\'s primary contact, ranging from 0 to 10. Used to measure account satisfaction, predict churn risk, and prioritize retention efforts.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent NPS survey response was recorded for this wholesale account. Used to determine survey recency and schedule follow-up outreach.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date on which the wholesale account was formally onboarded and activated for trading. Used to calculate account tenure, cohort analysis, and anniversary-based relationship milestones.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'SAP payment terms key (e.g., NET30, NET60, 2/10NET30) defining the due date and early payment discount structure for invoices issued to this wholesale account. Directly impacts cash flow forecasting and DSO metrics.']
  preferred_comm_channel varchar [note: 'The wholesale account\'s preferred channel for receiving business communications including order confirmations, collection previews, and account updates. Drives outreach strategy in Salesforce and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  primary_contact_department varchar [note: 'Department of the primary contact person within the wholesale account organization. Used to route communications to the correct functional team (e.g., buying team for assortment decisions, logistics for shipment coordination). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: buying|merchandising|logistics|finance|marketing|operations|executive — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Business email address of the primary contact person at the wholesale account. Used for order confirmations, collection previews, and account communications. Mapped to Salesforce Contact Email field.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Direct business phone number of the primary contact person at the wholesale account. Used for urgent order communications, sales calls, and account management.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the primary contact person at the wholesale account (e.g., Head Buyer, VP Merchandising, Procurement Director). Used to assess buying authority and route communications appropriately.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current health and strategic status of the commercial relationship with the wholesale account. \'At Risk\' triggers proactive account management intervention. Used in CRM pipeline and NPS-driven retention programs.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the wholesale account requires Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tagging on shipped merchandise for inventory tracking and compliance with the account\'s receiving standards.']
  sales_territory_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic or channel-based sales territory to which this wholesale account is assigned. Used for quota allocation, territory performance reporting, and sales rep assignment.']
  salesforce_account_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the corresponding Account object in Salesforce CRM. Enables bi-directional synchronization between the data lakehouse and Salesforce for sales pipeline, opportunity, and contact management.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number (e.g., EIN in the US, VAT registration number in the EU) for the wholesale account. Required for invoicing, customs documentation, and regulatory reporting.']
  trade_name varchar [note: 'Operating or \'doing business as\' (DBA) name of the wholesale account, which may differ from the legal entity name. Used for commercial communications and brand recognition.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the wholesale account record in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'SSOT master record for B2B wholesale buyers, retail partners, and enterprise accounts. Captures legal entity name, DUNS number, tax ID, credit terms, payment terms, assigned sales rep, account tier, EDI capability, MOQ agreements, and parent-child hierarchy relationships for franchise networks, buying groups, and corporate group structures. Includes full contact person management: each contact with first name, last name, job title, department, email, phone, preferred communication channel, buying authority level, relationship status, and Salesforce Contact object mapping. Supports multi-contact management per account with role-based access patterns for buying, logistics, and finance contacts.'
}

Table customer.contact {
  contact_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the contact person record within the Apparel Fashion customer domain. Primary key for the contact data product.']
  buying_authority_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the contact\'s level of purchasing decision-making authority within their organization. Determines the appropriate engagement level for sales and account management teams. Ranges from no authority (none) to full executive sign-off (executive). Critical for B2B sales strategy and PO approval workflows.']
  ccpa_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has exercised their CCPA right to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal information. When True, the contact\'s data must not be sold or shared with third parties per California law.']
  contact_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the contact\'s role in the B2B relationship. Buyer contacts are involved in purchasing decisions; merchandiser contacts manage product assortment; finance contacts handle invoicing and payments; logistics contacts manage shipping and fulfillment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: buyer|merchandiser|finance|logistics|executive|legal|operations|other — promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides the audit trail creation anchor for data governance and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating system or method through which this contact record was created or last updated. Supports data lineage, quality assessment, and reconciliation between Salesforce CRM, Adobe Experience Platform, and the Databricks lakehouse silver layer.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department or functional area within the wholesale account or enterprise partner organization where the contact works (e.g., Buying, Merchandising, Finance, Logistics). Supports targeted B2B communication routing.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary business email address for the contact person. Used for order confirmations, EDI notifications, collection previews, and B2B account communications. Maps to Salesforce Contact Email field.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has opted in to receive marketing emails, including seasonal collection previews, trade show invitations, and promotional communications. Drives email campaign eligibility in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the contact person. Used for personalized communication and salutation in B2B correspondence with wholesale accounts and enterprise partners.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which GDPR consent was most recently granted or withdrawn by this contact. Required for regulatory audit trails demonstrating lawful basis for data processing under GDPR Article 7.']
  gdpr_consent_status varchar [note: 'Current GDPR consent status for processing this contact\'s personal data for marketing and communication purposes. \'granted\' indicates explicit consent; \'withdrawn\' triggers suppression from marketing campaigns; \'not_applicable\' for contacts outside GDPR jurisdiction.']
  is_billing_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive invoices, payment reminders, and financial communications for the associated wholesale account. Supports accounts receivable workflows in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO module.']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has final decision-making authority for purchasing, contract signing, or strategic partnership agreements. Used by sales and account management teams to prioritize engagement and escalation paths.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the designated primary point of contact for the associated wholesale account or enterprise partnership. Only one contact per account should be flagged as primary. Used to route default communications and account management activities.']
  is_shipping_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is designated to receive shipping notifications, delivery confirmations, and logistics communications. Supports OTIF tracking and 3PL coordination for wholesale order fulfillment.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position of the contact person within their organization (e.g., Head Buyer, VP Merchandising, Procurement Manager). Critical for understanding the contact\'s role in the B2B purchasing process.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO 639-1 language code representing the contact\'s preferred language for business communications (e.g., en, fr, de, zh-CN). Drives localization of email templates, product catalogs, and collection presentations sent to wholesale buyers.']
  last_interaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent business interaction with this contact, including calls, emails, meetings, or trade show engagements. Used by account managers to monitor relationship health and identify contacts requiring re-engagement.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name or surname of the contact person. Combined with first_name to form the full display name for B2B relationship management and CRM records.']
  linkedin_url varchar [note: 'LinkedIn professional profile URL for the contact. Used by account managers and sales teams for relationship intelligence, pre-meeting research, and social selling in B2B wholesale and enterprise partnership contexts.']
  mailing_city varchar [note: 'City component of the contact\'s mailing address. Used in conjunction with mailing_street for physical correspondence and geographic segmentation of wholesale accounts.']
  mailing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the contact\'s mailing address country (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Used for international shipping, customs documentation, and regional account management assignment.']
  mailing_street varchar [note: 'Street address line for the contact\'s mailing address. Used for sending physical samples, lookbooks, seasonal collection materials, and formal correspondence to wholesale buyers and enterprise partners.']
  mobile_phone varchar [note: 'Mobile or cell phone number for the contact person in E.164 international format. Supports SMS notifications, WhatsApp business communications, and urgent B2B outreach for wholesale account management.']
  next_followup_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next planned follow-up interaction with this contact. Used by account managers and sales representatives to manage their B2B outreach cadence and ensure timely engagement with wholesale buyers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for account managers to capture qualitative relationship notes, meeting summaries, preferences, and contextual information about the contact. Supports B2B relationship intelligence and account handover documentation. Classified confidential due to potential inclusion of sensitive business relationship details.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) for this contact, ranging from 0 to 10, representing their likelihood to recommend Apparel Fashion to other businesses. Supports B2B relationship health monitoring and partner satisfaction tracking as part of the customer domain NPS strategy.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent NPS survey was administered to this contact. Used to track survey recency and schedule future NPS measurement cycles for B2B partner satisfaction programs.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Primary business phone number for the contact person in E.164 international format. Used for direct B2B account management calls, order follow-ups, and time-sensitive communications.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The contact\'s preferred channel for business communications. Drives outreach strategy for account managers and sales representatives managing wholesale and enterprise partnerships. Supports personalization in CRM workflows.']
  relationship_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the business relationship with this contact. Drives CRM workflows, outreach eligibility, and account management prioritization. \'do_not_contact\' enforces opt-out compliance under GDPR and CCPA.']
  salesforce_contact_code varchar [note: 'External identifier from the Salesforce CRM system corresponding to the Contact object. Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage between the lakehouse silver layer and Salesforce.']
  salutation varchar [note: 'Formal honorific or salutation prefix for the contact person (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.). Used in formal written and email correspondence with wholesale buyers and enterprise partners.']
  secondary_email varchar [note: 'Alternate or secondary business email address for the contact. Used as a fallback communication channel when the primary email is unresponsive or for routing specific communication types such as invoices or shipping notifications.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the contact\'s location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used to schedule calls, meetings, and automated communications at appropriate local times for global wholesale account management.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this contact record was most recently modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks silver layer.']

  Note: 'Individual contact persons associated with a wholesale account or enterprise partnership. Captures first name, last name, job title, department, email, phone, preferred communication channel, buying authority level, and relationship status. Supports B2B relationship management and maps to Salesforce Contact object. Multiple contacts can be linked to a single wholesale account.'
}

Table customer.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each address record in the customer domain. Serves as the primary key for the address data product across B2C and B2B entities.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer (B2C consumer or B2B wholesale account) who owns this address. Links the address to the parent customer profile in the CRM and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.wholesale_account. Business justification: Address table is described as serving \'both B2C profiles and B2B wholesale accounts.\' Currently only has profile_id FK for B2C. Adding wholesale_account_id enables B2B address management. An address b']
  address_source varchar [note: 'The originating channel or system through which this address was captured. Supports data lineage tracking and quality scoring. EDI-sourced addresses originate from wholesale partner Electronic Data Interchange transactions; POS addresses from SAP Customer Activity Repository in-store capture. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ecommerce|pos|crm|edi|manual|import|wholesale_portal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the address indicating its business purpose. Billing addresses are used for invoicing and payment processing; shipping addresses for DTC order fulfillment and wholesale delivery routing; store_pickup for BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) orders; warehouse for distribution center addresses; return for RTV (Return to Vendor) and consumer return processing.']
  carrier_route_code varchar [note: 'USPS carrier route code assigned to the address, identifying the specific mail carrier delivery route. Used for bulk mail rate optimization, direct mail marketing campaigns, and last-mile delivery planning.']
  city varchar [note: 'City, town, or municipality name of the address. Used in order routing, tax jurisdiction determination, and geographic sales analytics for retail store operations and DTC e-commerce.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 3166-1 Alpha-2 country code for the address (e.g., US, GB, DE, JP). Used for international shipping routing, customs documentation, HS Code determination, LDP (Landed Duty Paid) cost calculation, and GSP (Generalized System of Preferences) eligibility.']
  country_name varchar [note: 'Full English name of the country corresponding to the country_code. Stored for display and reporting purposes to avoid repeated lookups against reference data.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the address record was first created in the system. Supports data lineage, GDPR data subject access requests, and audit trail requirements. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform or Salesforce CRM upon initial capture.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite quality score (0.00–100.00) assigned to the address record based on validation status, standardization, geocoding accuracy, and completeness. Used to prioritize address cleansing campaigns and filter high-confidence addresses for marketing and fulfillment operations.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text special instructions for carriers and fulfillment teams regarding delivery at this address (e.g., \'Leave at reception\', \'Dock 3 only\', \'Requires appointment\', \'Signature required\'). Passed to Manhattan Associates WMS and 3PL partners during shipment creation.']
  district varchar [note: 'Administrative district, county, or sub-regional area of the address. Used for tax jurisdiction determination (county-level sales tax), regional sales territory assignment, and wholesale distribution zone mapping.']
  dpv_confirmation_code varchar [note: 'USPS Delivery Point Validation (DPV) confirmation code indicating the deliverability of the address. Y = confirmed deliverable; S = confirmed with secondary number missing; D = confirmed primary but secondary not confirmed; N = not confirmed. Used to assess address quality and reduce undeliverable-as-addressed (UAA) mail.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which this address record is considered valid and active for business use. Supports bi-temporal data modeling for address history tracking, enabling accurate reconstruction of customer address state at any point in time for order dispute resolution and compliance.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which this address record is valid. Null indicates the address is currently active with no planned expiry. Used for managing temporary addresses (e.g., seasonal warehouse locations, pop-up store addresses) and tracking address change history.']
  external_address_code varchar [note: 'Address identifier from the originating source system (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud address ID, SAP S/4HANA partner address number, Adobe Experience Platform profile address key). Enables cross-system reconciliation and deduplication during data integration.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has provided explicit consent for the storage and processing of this address under GDPR Article 6 and CCPA requirements. Required for EU and California-resident customers. False triggers restricted processing workflows.']
  geocoding_accuracy varchar [note: 'Precision level of the geocoordinates assigned to this address. Rooftop indicates exact parcel-level accuracy; range_interpolated is street-level; geometric_center is centroid of a region; approximate is low-precision. Drives confidence scoring for proximity-based analytics and last-mile routing.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address record is currently active and eligible for use in order processing, wholesale delivery routing, and marketing communications. Inactive addresses are retained for historical order reference and audit purposes.']
  is_default_billing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the customer\'s default billing address used for invoice generation and payment processing. Only one address per customer should have this flag set to true at any given time.']
  is_default_shipping boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this address is the customer\'s default shipping address for DTC order fulfillment. Pre-populated at checkout to streamline the purchase experience. Only one address per customer should have this flag set to true.']
  label varchar [note: 'Customer-assigned friendly label for the address (e.g., \'Home\', \'Office\', \'NYC Warehouse\', \'LA Showroom\'). Displayed in the customer\'s address book in the DTC e-commerce storefront and CRM to facilitate address selection at checkout.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude of the address in decimal degrees (WGS 84 datum). Used for store proximity searches, BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store) routing, last-mile delivery optimization, and geographic clustering in customer analytics.']
  line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line including street number, street name, and directional indicators. Required field for all address records used in order fulfillment, wholesale delivery routing, and store operations.']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, apartment, unit, floor, building, or care-of information. Optional field used to capture additional location detail for B2C consumers and B2B wholesale accounts.']
  line3 varchar [note: 'Tertiary address line used for international addresses requiring additional locality, district, or neighborhood detail. Primarily applicable for wholesale accounts in markets with complex addressing conventions.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude of the address in decimal degrees (WGS 84 datum). Used alongside latitude for geospatial analytics, delivery zone mapping, and proximity-based store operations reporting.']
  phone varchar [note: 'Contact phone number associated with this address, formatted per E.164 international standard. Used by carriers for delivery notifications, failed delivery callbacks, and customs clearance contact for international wholesale shipments.']
  po_box_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address is a Post Office (PO) Box. PO Box addresses are ineligible for certain carrier services (e.g., UPS, FedEx ground) and require USPS routing. Used in carrier selection logic during order fulfillment.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the address. Used for carrier rate calculation, delivery zone assignment, tax jurisdiction mapping, and geographic segmentation in marketing analytics.']
  recipient_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual or business entity designated to receive deliveries at this address. May differ from the account holder name for gift shipments, B2B receiving departments, or wholesale distribution contacts.']
  residential_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address is classified as residential (True) or commercial/business (False). Carrier surcharges differ between residential and commercial deliveries; this flag drives accurate shipping cost calculation and carrier rate shopping.']
  standardized_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the address has been standardized to postal authority format (e.g., USPS CASS normalization, Canada Post, Royal Mail PAF). Standardized addresses improve carrier label accuracy and reduce failed delivery rates.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region code of the address using ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes (e.g., CA, NY, ON, NSW). Used for tax calculation, shipping zone determination, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax jurisdiction identifier assigned to this address for sales tax calculation purposes. Used in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and integrated tax engines (e.g., Vertex, Avalara) to determine applicable state, county, and municipal tax rates for DTC and wholesale transactions.']
  timezone varchar [note: 'IANA timezone identifier for the address location (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Used for scheduling delivery windows, store pickup time slots, and localizing customer communications in marketing campaigns.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the address record was last modified. Used to detect stale addresses requiring re-validation, support incremental data loads in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer, and comply with GDPR data accuracy obligations.']
  validation_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party address validation service used to verify this address (e.g., USPS CASS, SmartyStreets, Loqate, Google Maps API). Supports audit trails for address quality management and vendor SLA (Service Level Agreement) tracking.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Current validation state of the address record as determined by address verification services (e.g., USPS CASS, SmartyStreets, Loqate). Validated addresses reduce failed deliveries and carrier surcharges. Invalid addresses are flagged for customer outreach before order fulfillment.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the address was last validated by the address verification service. Used to determine if re-validation is required based on data freshness policies and carrier compliance requirements.']

  Note: 'Standardized address records for both B2C profiles and B2B wholesale accounts. Captures address type (billing, shipping, store, warehouse), street lines, city, state/province, postal code, country ISO code, geocoordinates, address validation status, and default flag. Supports multi-address per customer for DTC shipping, store pickup, and wholesale delivery routing.'
}

Table customer.household {
  household_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the household unit within the Apparel Fashion customer data platform. Serves as the primary key for all household-level analytics, loyalty aggregation, and personalization targeting. Role: MASTER_PARTY.']
  acquisition_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Households are acquired through specific campaigns (family promotions, household bundles). Enables household-level acquisition attribution, family-targeted campaign effectiveness measurement, and hous']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: household table has household_segment_code (STRING) for family-level segmentation and marketing. Normalizing to FK enables referential integrity and supports household-level targeting based on segment']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program under which this household accumulates shared points, benefits, and tier status. Enables household-level loyalty management where multiple members contribute to a single loyalty balance. References the loyalty program entity.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the designated primary member within the household, who holds primary loyalty account ownership, receives primary marketing communications, and is the authoritative contact for household-level decisions. References the customer profile entity.']
  store_associate_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the personal stylist or client advisor assigned to this household for VIP and high-value customer relationship management. Supports personalized styling services and clienteling programs in luxury and premium apparel segments.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'The date on which the household was first acquired as a customer entity, typically aligned with the first qualifying purchase or loyalty enrollment event. Used for cohort analysis, tenure-based segmentation, and CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) reporting.']
  acquisition_source varchar [note: 'The marketing channel or business event through which the household was first acquired (e.g., \'paid_search\', \'retail_store\', \'referral\', \'loyalty_signup\', \'wholesale_partner\'). Used for CAC (Customer Acquisition Cost) analysis and channel attribution reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: paid_search|retail_store|referral|loyalty_signup|wholesale_partner|organic_social|email_campaign|affiliate — promote to reference product]']
  aov decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average transaction value across all household member purchases within the current fiscal year. AOV = Average Order Value. Used for basket-size analysis, upsell strategy development, and household-level revenue forecasting. Expressed in the household\'s operating currency.']
  cltv_segment varchar [note: 'Household-level CLTV segment classification derived from predictive modeling in Adobe Experience Platform. Segments households into value tiers to prioritize marketing investment, retention efforts, and personalization depth. CLTV = Customer Lifetime Value.']
  combined_annual_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross spend across all household members within the current fiscal year, aggregated from DTC, retail store (POS), and e-commerce channels. Used for household-level CLTV analysis, loyalty tier qualification, and personalization strategy. Expressed in the business\'s operating currency (USD).']
  consent_capture_date date [note: 'The date on which the household\'s most recent marketing consent preferences were recorded or last updated. Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails to demonstrate lawful basis for processing household-level personal data.']
  consent_source varchar [note: 'The channel or touchpoint through which the household\'s marketing consent was originally captured. Used for GDPR/CCPA audit compliance and to assess the quality and reliability of consent records across DTC, retail, and digital channels.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when the household record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer data platform. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED marker for data lineage, audit trail compliance, and household tenure calculations.']
  crm_account_code varchar [note: 'The external account identifier assigned to this household in Salesforce CRM. Used for bi-directional synchronization between the Databricks Silver Layer and Salesforce, enabling CRM-driven campaign execution and service case management at the household level.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the household\'s financial metrics (combined annual spend, CLTV score inputs) are denominated. Supports multi-currency household management for international customers.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00–100.00) reflecting the completeness and accuracy of the household profile, calculated by Adobe Experience Platform\'s identity resolution engine. Used to prioritize data enrichment efforts and to qualify households for advanced personalization models.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the household unit was dissolved or deactivated. Null for active households. Used for lifecycle analysis, churn reporting, and regulatory data retention compliance.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has provided explicit consent to receive email marketing communications. True = consented; False = not consented or withdrawn. Managed in compliance with CAN-SPAM Act, GDPR Article 6, and CCPA.']
  formation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the household unit was formally created in the CRM or customer data platform. Used to calculate household tenure, cohort analysis, and loyalty program anniversary milestones.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 or alpha-3 country/region code representing the household\'s primary geographic location. Used for regional assortment personalization, geo-targeted marketing campaigns, and compliance with jurisdiction-specific data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA).']
  household_name varchar [note: 'The display name assigned to the household unit, typically derived from the primary member\'s surname (e.g., \'The Johnson Household\'). Used for personalized communications, loyalty program correspondence, and CRM segmentation in Salesforce and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  household_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the household record. \'active\' indicates an operational household with at least one active member; \'merged\' indicates the household was consolidated into another; \'dissolved\' indicates all members have been disassociated; \'pending\' indicates formation in progress.']
  household_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the household composition for segmentation and personalization strategies. \'family\' denotes multi-generational or parent-child units; \'couple\' denotes two-adult units; \'individual\' denotes a single-person household; \'shared_residence\' denotes non-family cohabitants; \'corporate\' denotes a business account grouped as a household.']
  identity_resolution_code varchar [note: 'The unique identity graph node identifier assigned by Adobe Experience Platform\'s identity resolution service, linking the household to its constituent individual identity fragments across devices, channels, and touchpoints. Used for cross-channel attribution and personalization.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent purchase transaction made by any member of the household across all channels (DTC, retail store, e-commerce). Used for recency scoring in RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) analysis, lapse detection, and win-back campaign targeting.']
  loyalty_points_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current accumulated loyalty points balance at the household level, reflecting the combined earning activity of all associated members. Used for redemption eligibility, tier qualification, and household-level reward management.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current loyalty tier assigned to the household based on combined member spend and engagement. Determines household-level benefits, early access to collections, and exclusive event invitations across DTC, retail, and e-commerce channels.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has consented to receive marketing communications at the household level. True = opted in; False = opted out. Governs household-level campaign eligibility in compliance with GDPR and CCPA consent requirements.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual customer profiles currently associated with this household unit. Used for household-size-based segmentation, family apparel category targeting, and loyalty tier qualification. Reflects active members only.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score recorded at the household level, ranging from 0 to 10. NPS = Net Promoter Score. Captures household-level brand advocacy and satisfaction. Used for retention strategy, service recovery prioritization, and brand health reporting.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'The date on which the most recent NPS survey response was recorded for this household. Used to assess recency of satisfaction data and to schedule follow-up surveys in compliance with survey frequency guidelines.']
  preferred_apparel_category varchar [note: 'The primary apparel category (e.g., athletic, lifestyle, luxury, footwear, accessories) most frequently purchased by household members. Derived from purchase history and used to drive personalized product recommendations, collection previews, and targeted marketing campaigns.']
  preferred_channel varchar [note: 'The primary shopping channel through which the household most frequently transacts, as identified from purchase history analysis. DTC = Direct-to-Consumer. Used for channel-specific personalization, targeted promotions, and omnichannel engagement strategies.']
  purchase_frequency varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct purchase transactions made by all household members within the current fiscal year across all channels. Used as the frequency dimension in RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) analysis and for loyalty tier qualification.']
  return_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Ratio of returned items to total purchased items across all household members within the current fiscal year, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.1500 = 15%). Used for returns risk scoring, fraud detection, and after-sales service prioritization.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has opted into RFID-based in-store personalization services, enabling smart fitting room experiences and automated loyalty point capture at RFID-equipped retail locations. RFID = Radio Frequency Identification.']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has provided explicit consent to receive SMS/text marketing communications. True = consented; False = not consented or withdrawn. Managed in compliance with TCPA, GDPR Article 6, and CCPA.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this household record was originally created or last authoritatively updated. Used for data lineage tracking, conflict resolution during identity stitching, and audit compliance. AEP = Adobe Experience Platform; SFCC = Salesforce Commerce Cloud; SAP CAR = SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The UTC timestamp when the household record was most recently modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental ETL processing, and audit trail compliance.']
  vip_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has been designated as a VIP account, qualifying for exclusive services such as personal styling appointments, early collection access, private sale invitations, and dedicated client advisor support.']

  Note: 'Groups individual B2C profiles into household units for family-level relationship management, shared loyalty benefits, and household-level CLTV analysis. Captures household name, primary member designation, household size, combined annual spend, shared address reference, and household formation date. Enables personalization strategies targeting household purchasing patterns across apparel categories.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_program {
  loyalty_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the loyalty program record. Serves as the primary key for all downstream enrollment, tier, and points tracking references.']
  circular_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.circular_program. Business justification: Loyalty programs integrate circular economy incentives (points for garment returns, trade-in bonuses). Links loyalty mechanics to sustainability initiatives for program design, points calculation rule']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: loyalty_program has eligible_customer_segment (STRING) defining which segment can enroll. Normalizing to FK enables referential integrity for program eligibility rules. Note: This is a simplified 1:N ']
  annual_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'Recurring annual fee charged to maintain active membership in the loyalty program. A value of 0.00 indicates no annual fee. Used for subscription-based program revenue recognition.']
  birthday_bonus_points decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to a member during their birthday month or on their birthday date as a program benefit. A value of 0 indicates no birthday bonus.']
  ccpa_opt_out_supported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this loyalty program supports the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) right to opt out of the sale of personal information, applicable to members in California.']
  cltv_segment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members enrolled in this program are included in Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) segmentation models run through Adobe Experience Platform for personalization and targeted marketing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this loyalty program record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which monetary thresholds, rewards values, and redemption amounts for this program are denominated (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  eligible_country_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes identifying the geographic markets where this loyalty program is available for enrollment (e.g., \'USA,CAN,GBR\'). Null indicates global availability.']
  end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the loyalty program is scheduled to close or was retired. Null for open-ended programs. Used to manage program sunset communications and enrollment cutoffs.']
  enrollment_fee decimal(10,2) [note: 'One-time fee charged to a customer upon joining the loyalty program. A value of 0.00 indicates the program is free to join. Applicable primarily to subscription-type programs.']
  gdpr_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether explicit GDPR consent must be captured from members at enrollment for this program, applicable to programs operating in EU jurisdictions. Drives consent management workflows.']
  is_dtc_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can earn and redeem points through the Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) channel, including brand-owned retail stores and owned digital storefronts.']
  is_ecommerce_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can earn and redeem points through the e-commerce channel, including the brand\'s online store and digital marketplace integrations.']
  is_partner_earn_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can earn loyalty points through purchases or activities with third-party partner brands or co-branded programs affiliated with Apparel Fashion.']
  is_retail_store_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members can earn and redeem points at physical retail store locations via POS transactions tracked through SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  is_wholesale_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether B2B wholesale account holders are eligible to participate in this loyalty program and earn rewards on wholesale purchase volumes.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the loyalty program became publicly available and open for customer enrollment. Used for program age calculations and anniversary promotions.']
  max_points_balance decimal(14,2) [note: 'Maximum cumulative points balance a member may hold at any time. Enforces program liability ceiling and prevents excessive point accumulation. Null indicates no cap.']
  max_points_per_transaction decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cap on the number of loyalty points that can be earned in a single transaction. Prevents abuse of promotional multipliers and controls program liability exposure per event.']
  nps_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Net Promoter Score (NPS) surveys are triggered for members of this loyalty program to measure customer satisfaction and advocacy. Supports CRM and brand management analytics.']
  personalization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Adobe Experience Platform personalization and AI-driven offer targeting is activated for members of this loyalty program, enabling tailored product recommendations and promotions.']
  points_earning_rate decimal(10,4) [note: 'Base number of loyalty points awarded per unit of currency spent (e.g., 1.0000 point per USD spent). Tier-specific multipliers may override this base rate at the enrollment level.']
