  points_expiry_months varchar [note: 'Number of months after which unspent loyalty points expire from a member\'s account if no qualifying activity occurs. A value of 0 or null indicates points do not expire.']
  points_expiry_policy varchar [note: 'Rule governing when points expire: rolling resets the expiry window on each qualifying activity; fixed_anniversary expires points on the member\'s enrollment anniversary; no_expiry means points never expire; calendar_year expires all points at year-end.']
  points_monetary_value decimal(10,6) [note: 'Monetary value assigned to a single loyalty point at redemption (e.g., 0.010000 USD per point). Used to calculate reward discount amounts and program liability on the balance sheet.']
  points_redemption_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum accumulated points balance a member must hold before they are eligible to redeem points for rewards or discounts. Enforces program economics and prevents micro-redemptions.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the loyalty program across all channels and systems (e.g., \'AF_REWARDS_2024\'). Used in EDI, CRM, and POS integrations as the canonical business identifier.']
  program_description varchar [note: 'Full marketing and operational description of the loyalty program, including its value proposition, key benefits, and eligibility criteria as communicated to customers and internal stakeholders.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable marketing name of the loyalty program as presented to customers across DTC, retail, and e-commerce channels (e.g., \'Apparel Fashion Rewards\').']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the loyalty program. Active programs accept new enrollments and transactions; draft programs are in configuration; suspended programs are temporarily halted; retired programs are closed to new activity.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the loyalty program structure. Points-based programs award redeemable points per transaction; tiered programs unlock benefits at spend thresholds; cashback returns a percentage of spend; subscription requires a membership fee; hybrid combines multiple mechanics.']
  redemption_increment decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum unit of points that can be redeemed in a single transaction (e.g., points must be redeemed in multiples of 100). Ensures redemption amounts align with reward value denominations.']
  referral_bonus_points decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of loyalty points awarded to an existing member when they successfully refer a new customer who enrolls in the program. Supports member acquisition through word-of-mouth incentives.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this loyalty program configuration was originated or is mastered (e.g., SFCC for Salesforce Commerce Cloud, AEP for Adobe Experience Platform).']
  terms_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the current version of the loyalty program terms and conditions became legally effective. Members enrolled before this date may require re-consent notification.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the loyalty program\'s terms and conditions document currently in effect (e.g., \'v2.1\'). Used to track member consent to specific T&C versions and manage regulatory compliance.']
  tier_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct membership tiers defined within this loyalty program (e.g., 3 for Silver, Gold, Platinum). Drives tier configuration and progression rule setup.']
  tier_qualification_metric varchar [note: 'The primary metric used to determine a member\'s tier eligibility and progression (e.g., cumulative spend amount, total points earned, number of transactions, or units purchased within the qualification period).']
  tier_qualification_period_months varchar [note: 'Rolling or fixed window in months over which a member\'s qualifying activity is measured for tier assignment or maintenance (e.g., 12 months for annual tier review).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this loyalty program record in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection and incremental processing.']
  welcome_bonus_points decimal(12,2) [note: 'Number of bonus loyalty points awarded to a new member upon successful enrollment in the program. A value of 0 indicates no welcome bonus is offered.']

  Note: 'Master definition of loyalty program tiers, rules, and configurations offered by Apparel Fashion. Captures program name, tier structure (e.g., Silver, Gold, Platinum), points earning rules, redemption thresholds, expiry policies, eligible channel flags (DTC, retail, e-commerce), program launch date, and program status. Serves as the reference template against which individual enrollments are tracked.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_enrollment {
  loyalty_enrollment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each loyalty program enrollment record. One record per customer-program combination.']
  acquisition_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Loyalty enrollments are frequently driven by specific acquisition campaigns (sign-up bonuses, enrollment promotions). Essential for calculating campaign-specific CAC, measuring enrollment campaign eff']
  current_tier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty tier definition the customer currently holds (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum). Drives benefit entitlements and earn rate multipliers.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program definition this enrollment belongs to (e.g., Rewards Club, VIP Program, Wholesale Partner Program).']
  primary_loyalty_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who is enrolled in the loyalty program. Links to the customer master record in the CRM/CDP.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store location where the customer enrolled, if the enrollment channel was in-store. Null for digital enrollments.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has exercised their California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) right to opt out of the sale or sharing of their personal information. Applicable to California-resident members.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this loyalty enrollment record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR accountability requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the monetary values recorded in this enrollment (e.g., tier qualification spend, reward monetary equivalents). Supports multi-currency loyalty programs.']
  data_retention_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this enrollment record is subject to a legal hold or data retention requirement that prevents deletion even after termination. Set by legal or compliance teams.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'The Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) or wholesale channel through which the customer enrolled in the loyalty program. Supports channel attribution and omnichannel analytics.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the customer formally enrolled in the loyalty program. Used for anniversary calculations, tenure segmentation, and cohort analysis.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible, human-readable enrollment reference number used in customer communications, receipts, and CRM lookups. Follows the format LYL-XXXXXXXXXX.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the loyalty enrollment. Active = earning and redeeming; Suspended = temporarily blocked; Terminated = permanently closed; Pending = awaiting verification; Frozen = points locked pending investigation.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the member provided GDPR consent for loyalty data processing. Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance demonstration under GDPR Article 7(1).']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has provided explicit consent for personal data processing under GDPR Article 6 for loyalty program purposes. Mandatory for EU-resident members.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent qualifying loyalty activity (earn, redeem, or bonus event) by this member. Used to identify inactive members, trigger re-engagement campaigns, and enforce inactivity-based points expiry policies.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase transaction that generated a loyalty points earn event. Distinct from last_activity_date which may include non-purchase activities such as profile updates or bonus events.']
  lifetime_points_earned decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cumulative points earned by the customer since enrollment inception, including all earn, bonus, and adjustment credits. Used for Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) analysis and VIP qualification.']
  lifetime_points_expired decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cumulative points that have expired due to inactivity or program policy since enrollment inception. Used for program liability management and customer re-engagement targeting.']
  lifetime_points_redeemed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cumulative points redeemed by the customer since enrollment inception. Used to measure program engagement and redemption rate analytics.']
  member_card_number varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual loyalty card number issued to the member. Used for in-store Point of Sale (POS) identification, RFID scanning, and customer service lookups. Classified as confidential PII identifier.']
  member_card_type varchar [note: 'Format of the loyalty card issued to the member. Physical = plastic card; Virtual = app-based barcode; RFID = contactless chip card; Digital Wallet = Apple/Google Wallet pass.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response from this loyalty member, on a scale of 0-10. Used to segment promoters, passives, and detractors for loyalty program satisfaction analysis.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date on which the most recent NPS survey response was captured for this loyalty member. Used to determine survey recency and schedule follow-up surveys.']
  opt_in_email boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive loyalty-related email communications including points statements, tier upgrade notifications, and exclusive member offers.']
  opt_in_push boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive mobile push notifications for loyalty events such as points earned, tier changes, and reward availability.']
  opt_in_sms boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has opted in to receive loyalty-related SMS/text message communications including points alerts and promotional offers.']
  pending_points decimal(18,2) [note: 'Points earned from recent transactions that are not yet confirmed as redeemable, typically held during a return window or fraud review period before being credited to the redeemable balance.']
  points_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current redeemable points balance available to the customer for redemption against purchases, rewards, or experiences. Reflects all earned, redeemed, adjusted, and expired points.']
  points_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the current redeemable points balance (or the oldest tranche of points) will expire if no qualifying activity occurs. Drives expiry notification campaigns.']
  referral_source_code varchar [note: 'Campaign or referral code used at enrollment to attribute the sign-up to a specific marketing campaign, influencer, or existing member referral. Supports Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC) and campaign ROI analysis.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this enrollment was originated or last updated. AEP = Adobe Experience Platform; SFCC = Salesforce Commerce Cloud; SAP_CAR = SAP Customer Activity Repository; CRM = Salesforce CRM; MANUAL = manual entry.']
  source_system_member_code varchar [note: 'The member or enrollment identifier as recorded in the originating source system (e.g., AEP profile ID, Salesforce Contact ID). Used for cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the loyalty enrollment was terminated or closed, either by customer request, program policy, or fraud investigation outcome. Null for active enrollments.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code explaining why the loyalty enrollment was terminated. Used for compliance reporting, program analytics, and customer win-back strategy.']
  tier_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer\'s current tier status became effective. Used to calculate tier tenure and determine when the next tier review is due.']
  tier_expiry_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer\'s current tier qualification expires and is subject to re-evaluation based on qualifying spend or activity. Null for lifetime tiers.']
  tier_qualification_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative qualifying spend amount (in program currency) accumulated by the customer within the current tier qualification period. Compared against tier thresholds to determine tier status.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this loyalty enrollment record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change detection, and audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Tracks each customer\'s active enrollment in a loyalty program including current tier status, points balance, and full transaction history of points movements. Captures enrollment date, current tier, tier qualification/expiry dates, lifetime points earned, redeemable balance, enrollment channel, and a ledger of all earn/redeem/adjust/expire/bonus events with timestamps, triggering references, and running balances. Provides complete loyalty lifecycle per customer.'
}

Table customer.segment {
  segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for segment']
  parent_segment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to a parent segment when this segment is a sub-segment or refinement of a broader segment hierarchy (e.g., \'High-Value Female Loyalists\' as a child of \'High-Value Loyalists\'). Supports hierarchical segment structures for nested targeting.']
  age_band_max varchar [note: 'Maximum age (in years) of the target demographic for this segment. Used in demographic segmentation to define the upper bound of the age range for personalization and marketing targeting.']
  age_band_min varchar [note: 'Minimum age (in years) of the target demographic for this segment. Used in demographic segmentation to define the lower bound of the age range for personalization and marketing targeting.']
  campaign_budget_usd decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned marketing spend budget allocated to campaigns targeting this segment, expressed in USD. Used for marketing financial planning in Anaplan and ROI measurement. Classified as confidential as it reflects internal budget allocation strategy.']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'The commerce channel(s) this segment is scoped to. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) covers owned e-commerce and retail stores; wholesale covers B2B accounts; omnichannel covers all channels. Drives channel-specific activation in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment record was first created in the Apparel Fashion data platform. Provides audit trail for data lineage and governance compliance.']
  crm_sync_status varchar [note: 'Current synchronization status of this segment to Salesforce CRM for campaign activation. Synced indicates the segment definition and membership are current in CRM; pending indicates a sync is queued; failed indicates a sync error requiring investigation.']
  crm_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent successful synchronization of this segment to Salesforce CRM. Used to monitor data freshness and SLA compliance for campaign activation pipelines.']
  customer_class varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the segment targets B2C (individual consumers), B2B (wholesale accounts), household-level groupings, or enterprise partnerships. Drives different activation logic and CRM workflows in Salesforce and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  data_consent_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether activation of this segment requires verified customer consent under GDPR or CCPA regulations. When true, only profiles with explicit consent records in Adobe Experience Platform may be included in segment activations.']
  data_sensitivity_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level assigned to this segment definition based on the sensitivity of its qualifying criteria and the customer data it processes. Restricted segments may involve sensitive demographic or behavioral data requiring heightened access controls per GDPR and CCPA.']
  definition_criteria varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the business rules and logic used to qualify customers into this segment (e.g., \'Customers with 3+ purchases in last 90 days, AOV > $150, active loyalty member\'). Serves as the business specification for technical implementation in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  definition_expression varchar [note: 'Structured query expression or rule set (e.g., PQL — Profile Query Language from Adobe Experience Platform) that technically defines segment membership criteria. Enables automated re-evaluation and sync to CRM activation systems.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this segment definition expires and is no longer used for active targeting. Nullable for evergreen segments with no planned expiry. Supports time-bound campaign segments tied to seasonal promotions or collection launches.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this segment definition becomes active and eligible for campaign targeting and personalization activation. Supports seasonal and campaign-specific segment scheduling aligned with collection launches and promotional calendars.']
  estimated_size varchar [note: 'Estimated count of customer profiles qualifying for this segment at the last evaluation run. Used for campaign reach planning, budget allocation, and marketing ROI forecasting. Sourced from Adobe Experience Platform segment evaluation output.']
  evaluation_frequency varchar [note: 'How frequently the segment membership is re-evaluated and refreshed. Real-time segments update continuously via streaming; daily/weekly/monthly segments are refreshed on a scheduled batch cadence. Impacts campaign activation timeliness and system resource planning.']
  evaluation_method varchar [note: 'Method by which Adobe Experience Platform evaluates segment membership. Batch evaluation runs on a scheduled cadence; streaming evaluation updates membership in near-real-time as profile events occur; edge evaluation enables on-device personalization at the point of interaction.']
  gender_affinity varchar [note: 'Gender affinity profile of the segment used for product assortment targeting, personalization, and marketing creative selection. Informs merchandising allocation and collection planning for gender-specific product lines.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic market scope for this segment expressed as ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or region identifiers (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'DEU\'). Supports geo-targeted campaign activation and regional merchandising decisions. Multiple values stored as comma-separated codes.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'The customer lifecycle stage this segment targets. Acquisition segments target new prospects; activation targets first-time buyers; retention targets active loyal customers; reactivation targets declining customers; winback targets lapsed customers. Aligns with CRM and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) strategy.']
  loyalty_program_linked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is linked to a loyalty program tier or reward structure. Loyalty-linked segments receive differentiated offers, early access to collections, and exclusive promotions managed through Salesforce CRM.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for business analysts and segment owners to capture contextual notes, assumptions, known limitations, or activation guidance for this segment. Supports knowledge transfer and operational documentation.']
  nps_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is designated as a target population for NPS (Net Promoter Score) survey distribution. NPS-targeted segments are used to measure customer satisfaction and brand loyalty across DTC and wholesale channels.']
  owner_function varchar [note: 'The business function responsible for defining, maintaining, and activating this segment. Marketing-owned segments drive campaign targeting; merchandising-owned segments inform assortment and OTB planning; CRM-owned segments support loyalty and NPS programs.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual business owner or manager accountable for this segment\'s definition, quality, and activation. Supports data stewardship and governance workflows.']
  personalization_use_case varchar [note: 'Primary personalization or activation use case this segment supports (e.g., \'Email re-engagement campaign\', \'Homepage product recommendation\', \'Loyalty tier upgrade offer\', \'Wholesale reorder reminder\'). Guides campaign brief creation and Adobe Experience Platform activation configuration.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking of this segment relative to other segments within the same owner function or campaign cycle. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to resolve conflicts when a customer qualifies for multiple segments and to sequence campaign activation.']
  rfm_tier varchar [note: 'RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) classification tier assigned to this segment when segment_type is \'rfm\'. Champions are highest-value active buyers; loyal are consistent purchasers; at_risk are declining in engagement; lapsed have not purchased recently; prospects are new or low-activity customers.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Apparel season code associated with this segment when the segment is scoped to a specific merchandise season (e.g., \'SS2025\' for Spring/Summer 2025, \'FW2024\' for Fall/Winter 2024). Links segment activation to collection planning and merchandising calendars in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known, human-readable business identifier for the segment (e.g., \'LOYAL_VIP_B2C\', \'WHOLESALE_TIER1\'). Used as the stable reference key across Adobe Experience Platform, Salesforce CRM, and campaign activation systems.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the customer segment (e.g., \'High-Value Loyalists\', \'Lapsed Wholesale Accounts\', \'Gen-Z Streetwear Enthusiasts\'). Used in campaign briefs, merchandising plans, and personalization dashboards.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the segment. Active segments are available for campaign targeting and personalization. Draft segments are under construction. Inactive segments are paused. Archived segments are retained for historical analysis but not used in live activations.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the methodology used to define the segment. Behavioral segments are based on purchase/browse actions; demographic on age/gender/location; psychographic on lifestyle/values; RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) on transactional scoring; channel_based on preferred shopping channel (DTC, wholesale, retail).']
  size_last_evaluated_date date [note: 'Date on which the segment membership count (estimated_size) was last computed by Adobe Experience Platform. Indicates data freshness for campaign planning and audience activation decisions.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record where this segment was originally created and is mastered. Adobe Experience Platform is the primary CDP for B2C segments; Salesforce CRM manages B2B wholesale account segments; Anaplan supports planning-driven segments; manual indicates analyst-defined segments.']
  source_system_segment_code varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this segment in the originating source system (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform Segment GUID, Salesforce CRM Segment Record ID). Enables bidirectional traceability and sync reconciliation between the lakehouse and operational systems.']
  suppression_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is a suppression list used to exclude customers from specific campaigns or communications (e.g., recent purchasers suppressed from acquisition campaigns, opted-out customers). Critical for compliance with GDPR and CCPA opt-out requirements.']
  tags_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of free-form business tags applied to this segment for discoverability, governance, and cross-functional search (e.g., \'loyalty,high-value,email-only,seasonal\'). Supports segment catalog management in Adobe Experience Platform and internal data governance portals.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this segment record, including definition changes, status updates, or ownership reassignments. Supports change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Monotonically incrementing version number tracking revisions to the segment definition criteria. Enables audit trail of definition changes and supports rollback to prior versions when segment performance degrades.']

  Note: 'Defines named market segments used to classify B2C and B2B customers for personalization, merchandising, and marketing targeting. Captures segment name, segment type (behavioral, demographic, psychographic, RFM, channel-based), definition criteria, segment owner (marketing vs. merchandising), creation date, and active status. Segments are managed in Adobe Experience Platform and synced to CRM for campaign activation.'
}

Table customer.preference {
  preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each customer preference or measurement record. Serves as the primary key for the preference data product.']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: preference table has personalization_segment (STRING) for tagging preferences with segment context. Normalizing to FK enables linking preference patterns to segment definitions and supports segment-ba']
  ecolabel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.ecolabel. Business justification: Customers prefer certified products (GOTS, Fair Trade, OEKO-TEX). Enables filtering and personalized merchandising based on certification preferences. Supports targeted marketing campaigns for sustain']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Customer preferences map to specific product categories in the merchandise hierarchy. Powers recommendation engines, personalized emails, and size/style suggestions. Replaces denormalized apparel_cate']
  previous_preference_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the prior version of this preference record when it was superseded or updated. Enables preference history chaining and change tracking without a separate history table. Null for the initial version of a preference.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer whose preference or measurement this record describes. Links to the customer master profile in the CRM and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Customers express material preferences (organic cotton, recycled polyester, Tencel). Drives personalized product recommendations, sustainability-focused marketing segmentation, and preference-based fi']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'The brand to which the preferred_brand_size or brand preference applies. Enables brand-specific size mapping and brand affinity tracking for personalization and assortment planning.']
  channel_of_capture varchar [note: 'The business channel through which this preference was captured or inferred. Supports omnichannel preference reconciliation and channel-specific personalization strategies. Distinct from source_system (technical system) — this represents the customer-facing channel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ecommerce|retail_store|mobile_app|style_quiz|loyalty_portal|wholesale|call_center|social_commerce|pop_up_event — promote to reference product]']
  color_palette varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred color palette or specific color preferences (e.g., \'neutrals\', \'earth tones\', \'navy,white,grey\', \'bold primaries\'). Used to filter product recommendations and personalize marketing communications. May store comma-separated color values.']
  communication_channel varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred channel for receiving marketing communications, personalized recommendations, and promotional offers. Drives omnichannel campaign orchestration in Adobe Experience Platform and Salesforce CRM. Null indicates no stated preference.']
  communication_frequency varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred frequency for receiving marketing and personalization communications. Used to govern outreach cadence in campaign management and suppress over-communication. Supports GDPR and CCPA consent management.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence level (0.00–100.00) indicating the reliability of this preference or measurement. Self-declared and body scan data typically score highest; behavioral inference scores reflect signal strength from purchase/browse history. Used by recommendation engines to weight preference inputs.']
  consent_date date [note: 'Date on which the customer provided consent for this preference data to be collected and used. Required for GDPR and CCPA audit trails. Null for preferences captured prior to consent framework implementation.']
  consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has provided explicit consent for this preference data to be collected, stored, and used for personalization purposes. Mandatory for GDPR and CCPA compliance. False values trigger suppression from personalization pipelines.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this preference record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails, GDPR data retention calculations, and preference lifecycle analytics.']
  data_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite data quality score (0.00–100.00) for this preference record, reflecting completeness, recency, and source reliability. Used by recommendation engines to weight preference inputs and by data stewards to prioritize enrichment efforts.']
  fit_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred garment fit silhouette for the associated apparel category. Drives fit-based filtering in recommendation engines and reduces return rates by surfacing appropriately fitted products. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: slim|regular|relaxed|oversized|athletic|tailored|skinny|straight|wide_leg — promote to reference product]']
  inference_model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the AI/ML model used to infer this preference from behavioral data (e.g., \'style_affinity_v2.3\', \'fit_inference_v1.1\'). Null for self-declared or manually assigned preferences. Supports model governance, A/B testing, and reproducibility audits.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preference record is currently active and should be used by recommendation engines and personalization systems. Inactive records are retained for audit and historical analysis but excluded from real-time personalization.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this preference record, whether from customer self-service, behavioral re-inference, stylist update, or system refresh. Critical for staleness detection and preference freshness scoring in recommendation engines.']
  locale varchar [note: 'IETF BCP 47 locale code representing the language and regional context in which this preference was captured (e.g., \'en-US\', \'fr-FR\', \'de-DE\'). Ensures size system and communication preferences are interpreted in the correct regional context.']
  material_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred fabric or material types (e.g., \'organic cotton\', \'merino wool\', \'recycled polyester\', \'linen\'). Supports sustainability-aligned personalization and allergy/sensitivity filtering. May store comma-separated material values.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes added by a stylist, customer service agent, or automated system providing additional context about this preference (e.g., \'customer mentioned sensitivity to wool\', \'measured during in-store fitting event\'). Supports qualitative enrichment of structured preference data.']
  numeric_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Parsed numeric representation of the preference value for body measurements and quantitative preferences (e.g., chest circumference 92.50, inseam 76.00, shoe size 10.5). Null for non-numeric preferences. Used by recommendation engines for size matching algorithms.']
  occasion_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred occasions or use cases for apparel (e.g., \'workwear\', \'casual\', \'formal\', \'activewear\', \'evening\'). Supports occasion-based product filtering and targeted collection recommendations. May store comma-separated occasion values.']
  override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this preference record was manually overridden by a stylist, customer service agent, or the customer themselves, superseding a previously inferred value. When true, the record takes precedence over behavioral inference for the same preference type.']
  preference_category varchar [note: 'High-level category classifying the type of preference or measurement captured. Drives segmentation and personalization routing in Adobe Experience Platform. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: style|fit|color|size|brand|communication|sustainability|material|fragrance|occasion|price_range — promote to reference product]']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of this preference record. Active records are used in personalization; superseded records have been replaced by a newer version; expired records have passed their validity window; withdrawn records reflect customer opt-out; pending_verification awaits confirmation.']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Specific type of preference or measurement within the category (e.g., \'neckline_style\', \'waist_fit\', \'chest_circumference_cm\', \'preferred_color_palette\', \'inseam_length_cm\'). Provides granular classification for recommendation engine targeting.']
  preference_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual value of the preference or measurement (e.g., \'slim fit\', \'navy blue\', \'92.5\', \'XL\', \'Nike\'). For numeric measurements, this stores the string representation; numeric_value stores the parsed decimal. Supports both categorical and quantitative preference data.']
  preferred_brand_size varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred size label as used by a specific brand for the associated apparel category (e.g., \'M\', \'L\', \'32x30\', \'10.5\'). Captures brand-specific sizing conventions which differ from standardized measurements, enabling brand-aware size recommendations.']
  price_range_preference varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred price tier for apparel purchases, aligned to the brand\'s pricing architecture. Informs personalized product recommendations and promotional targeting. Derived from purchase history behavioral inference or self-declared via style profile.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this preference was captured or inferred. Supports data lineage tracking and conflict resolution when the same preference type is captured from multiple systems. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adobe_experience_platform|salesforce_crm|salesforce_commerce_cloud|sap_car|in_store_scan|virtual_try_on_app|stylist_portal|mobile_app|web_portal — promote to reference product]']
  source_type varchar [note: 'Method by which this preference or measurement was captured. Drives confidence scoring and consent management. Self-declared data from customer profiles, behavioral inference from purchase/browse history, stylist-assigned from in-store consultations, in-store body scan from 3D measurement technology, virtual try-on from digital fitting tools.']
  style_affinity varchar [note: 'Customer\'s preferred aesthetic style or fashion archetype (e.g., \'minimalist\', \'streetwear\', \'classic\', \'bohemian\', \'athleisure\'). Captured via style quiz, behavioral inference from browse/purchase patterns, or stylist assessment. Feeds personalization and collection recommendation engines.']
  sustainability_preference boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has expressed a preference for sustainably sourced, ethically produced, or eco-certified products. When true, recommendation engines prioritize GOTS-certified, BCI cotton, and OEKO-TEX certified products.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit associated with the numeric preference value (e.g., \'cm\' for body measurements, \'in\' for imperial measurements, \'EU\'/\'UK\'/\'US\' for shoe sizing systems). Null for non-numeric or unitless preferences. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cm|in|mm|kg|lbs|EU|UK|US|JP — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  valid_until date [note: 'Date after which this preference record expires and should no longer be applied in personalization. Null indicates an open-ended preference with no expiry. Supports seasonal preference windows and time-limited style profiles.']
  valid_from date [note: 'Date from which this preference record is considered valid and applicable for personalization. Supports temporal preference management, enabling seasonal or time-bounded preferences (e.g., summer color preferences).']

  Note: 'SSOT for all explicit and inferred customer preferences, body measurements, and size profiles across apparel categories. Captures style affinities, fit preferences, color palettes, brand preferences, communication channel preferences, and detailed size/measurement data per category (tops, bottoms, outerwear, footwear, accessories). For each preference or measurement: records type, source (self-declared, behavioral inference, stylist-assigned, in-store body scan, virtual try-on), value with units, preferred brand size per category, confidence level, and last updated timestamp. Consolidates all personalization and fit inputs to feed recommendation engines, enable size/fit personalization, and reduce return rates in Adobe Experience Platform.'
}

Table customer.consent {
  consent_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for consent']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: consent table has customer_segment_code (STRING) for compliance reporting and segmentation. Normalizing to FK enables tracking which segments have specific consent patterns and supports segment-level ']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program associated with this consent record, where consent was captured as part of a loyalty enrollment or re-enrollment process. Links to the loyalty program master in the customer domain.']
  parent_consent_record_consent_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier linking this consent record to a prior version or parent consent record. Enables full consent history lineage tracking when a customer updates or re-grants consent under a new policy version.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer whose consent is being recorded. Links to the master customer profile managed in Adobe Experience Platform (AEP) and Salesforce CRM.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order transaction during which this consent was captured (e.g., checkout opt-in for marketing emails). Links to the order transaction in the order domain. Null if consent was not captured in the context of a purchase.']
  collection_channel varchar [note: 'The touchpoint or channel through which the customer\'s consent was collected. Supports omnichannel compliance tracking across DTC e-commerce (Salesforce Commerce Cloud), POS (SAP CAR), in-store, and wholesale portals. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|pos|email|sms|call_center|in_store_kiosk|wholesale_portal — promote to reference product]']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism by which consent was obtained. Explicit = active affirmative action by the customer; Implicit = inferred from behavior; Pre-ticked = checkbox pre-selected (non-compliant under GDPR); Opt-out = consent assumed unless declined.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the consent record indicating whether the customer has actively granted, withdrawn, or has a pending or expired consent for the specified consent type.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'The category of consent being captured, indicating the specific processing activity or communication channel for which the customer is granting or withdrawing permission. Covers marketing email, SMS, push notification, data sharing, profiling, and cookies.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of the customer at the time of consent. Determines the applicable regulatory jurisdiction and consent requirements (e.g., GDPR for EU countries, CCPA for USA-California).']
  data_subject_age_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer\'s age was verified prior to consent capture to ensure compliance with child data protection requirements (e.g., GDPR Article 8 requires parental consent for children under 16). True = age verified; False = not verified.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The category of device used by the customer when providing consent. Supports channel analytics and helps identify consent collection patterns across DTC digital touchpoints and in-store POS terminals.']
  double_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer completed a double opt-in verification process (e.g., email confirmation link clicked) to confirm their consent. True = double opt-in confirmed; False = single opt-in only. Relevant for marketing email compliance.']
  double_opt_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the customer completed the double opt-in verification step. Null if double opt-in was not required or not completed. Supports email marketing compliance audit trails.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this consent record is considered active and enforceable. May differ from the granted timestamp if consent is scheduled to take effect at a future date (e.g., post-enrollment cooling-off period).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which the consent record expires and must be re-obtained from the customer. Used to enforce time-limited consent policies and trigger re-consent workflows in Adobe Experience Platform.']
  granted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone offset) when the customer actively granted consent. This is the principal business event timestamp for the consent record and is mandatory for GDPR and CCPA audit trails.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the device from which the customer submitted their consent. Captured at the moment of consent for regulatory audit purposes. May be considered PII under GDPR in certain jurisdictions.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent record is the current active version for the customer and consent type combination. True = this is the authoritative current record; False = superseded by a newer consent record. Supports efficient querying of current consent state without full history traversal.']
  legal_basis varchar [note: 'The lawful basis under which customer data is being processed, as required by GDPR Article 6. Consent is one of six possible legal bases. Mandatory for GDPR compliance documentation and DPA audit responses.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'The IETF BCP 47 locale code (e.g., en-US, fr-FR, de-DE) representing the language and region in which the consent notice was presented to the customer. Ensures the customer received consent information in their preferred language.']
  notice_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the privacy notice, cookie policy, or consent form that was presented to the customer at the time of consent capture. Provides an auditable link to the exact disclosure document shown.']
  nps_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has consented to receiving Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey invitations. Derived from the consent record for survey/feedback consent type. Used by the CRM and customer experience teams to build eligible NPS survey audiences.']
  parental_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether parental or guardian consent was obtained on behalf of a minor customer. Required under GDPR Article 8 for data subjects under the age of digital consent in the applicable jurisdiction.']
  personalization_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has consented to data-driven personalization of their shopping experience, including product recommendations and targeted content. Consumed by Adobe Experience Platform personalization workflows.']
  preference_center_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the preference center session or interaction through which the customer managed their consent preferences. Links to the preference management module in Adobe Experience Platform or Salesforce Marketing Cloud.']
  proof_of_consent_reference varchar [note: 'A reference identifier (e.g., document ID, audit log reference, or digital signature hash) pointing to the stored proof of consent artifact. Supports regulatory audit responses and dispute resolution under GDPR Article 7(1) burden-of-proof requirements.']
  re_consent_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consent record requires re-confirmation from the customer due to a policy change, expiry, or regulatory update. True = re-consent workflow must be triggered; False = consent is current and valid.']
  re_consent_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when a re-consent request was dispatched to the customer via the applicable communication channel. Supports SLA tracking for re-consent campaigns and regulatory response timelines.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was first created in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Serves as the audit creation timestamp for data lineage and compliance reporting.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this consent record was last modified in the Silver Layer lakehouse. Tracks updates such as status changes, re-consent confirmations, or suppression flag updates.']
  regulatory_jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The applicable data privacy regulatory framework under which this consent record was captured. Determines the legal basis, retention rules, and customer rights applicable to this record. Supports multi-jurisdiction compliance for global apparel operations.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The native identifier of this consent record in the originating source system (e.g., AEP consent event ID, Salesforce consent object ID). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and upstream operational systems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this consent record originated. Supports data lineage tracking across Adobe Experience Platform (AEP), Salesforce Commerce Cloud (SFCC), SAP Customer Activity Repository (SAP CAR), and Salesforce CRM.']
  suppression_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this customer is suppressed from the specific communication type associated with this consent record. True = customer must not receive communications of this type; False = communications are permitted. Enforced in Salesforce Marketing Cloud and Adobe Experience Platform.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why a customer has been suppressed from communications of this consent type. Supports compliance reporting and operational triage for marketing and CRM teams. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: customer_request|regulatory_requirement|bounce|complaint|deceased|fraud|other — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  text_snapshot varchar [note: 'A verbatim snapshot or hash reference of the consent language presented to the customer at the time of consent. Preserves the exact wording for regulatory audit and dispute resolution purposes.']
  third_party_sharing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has consented to their data being shared with third-party partners (e.g., wholesale partners, affiliate marketers, analytics vendors). Critical for CCPA \'Do Not Sell\' compliance and GDPR data sharing agreements.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The browser or application user agent string captured at the time of consent submission for digital channels. Used for technical audit trails and device fingerprinting in compliance investigations.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the consent notice, privacy policy, or terms and conditions document that was presented to the customer at the time of consent capture. Enables tracking of consent obtained under different policy versions.']
  withdrawn_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time (with timezone offset) when the customer withdrew or revoked a previously granted consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Required for GDPR right-to-withdraw audit compliance.']

  Note: 'Tracks customer consent and privacy preferences in compliance with GDPR and CCPA regulations. Captures consent type (marketing email, SMS, push notification, data sharing, profiling, cookies), consent status (granted, withdrawn, pending), consent timestamp, consent version, collection channel, IP address at time of consent, and regulatory jurisdiction. Mandatory for FTC and GDPR compliance across all customer touchpoints.'
}

Table customer.identity_link {
  identity_link_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the identity link record. Primary key for cross-channel identity resolution.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the household entity that this identity belongs to. Supports household-level identity resolution and marketing for shared addresses and family relationships.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the resolved master customer profile that this identity link belongs to. Links to the unified customer identity in the Customer Data Platform (CDP).']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this identity link is currently active and should be used in identity resolution. True indicates active link, false indicates inactive or deprecated link.']
  channel_source varchar [note: 'The customer interaction channel where this identifier was captured. Indicates the touchpoint origin (web, mobile app, POS, call center, email, social media, wholesale portal, partner system). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: web|mobile_app|pos|call_center|email|social|wholesale|partner — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Customer consent status for using this identifier in identity resolution and marketing. Granted indicates explicit consent, denied indicates opt-out, pending awaits customer response, withdrawn indicates revoked consent, not required for non-regulated identifiers.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when consent status was last updated. Required for GDPR and CCPA compliance to demonstrate when customer consent was obtained or withdrawn.']
  cross_device_cluster_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the cross-device cluster or household that this identity belongs to. Used to link multiple devices and identifiers to the same person or household for unified customer view.']
  data_retention_days varchar [note: 'Number of days this identity link should be retained based on regulatory requirements and business policies. Used to enforce GDPR right to erasure and data minimization principles.']
  deletion_requested_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the customer has requested deletion of this identifier under GDPR right to erasure or CCPA right to deletion. True indicates pending deletion, false indicates normal retention.']
  deletion_scheduled_date date [note: 'Date when this identity link is scheduled for deletion. Null if no deletion is scheduled. Used to manage GDPR and CCPA deletion requests and automated data retention policies.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device associated with this identifier. Relevant for device-based identifiers (cookie ID, device ID, IDFA, GAID). Helps understand customer device preferences and cross-device behavior. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: desktop|mobile|tablet|pos_terminal|kiosk|iot|unknown — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  first_seen_date date [note: 'The date when this identifier was first observed in any system. Helps establish customer relationship timeline and identifier age.']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where this identifier was first captured or is primarily used. Supports regional identity resolution rules and compliance requirements (e.g., GDPR for EU, CCPA for California).']
  identifier_namespace varchar [note: 'The namespace or domain context for the identifier. Used to disambiguate identifiers that may have the same value across different systems (e.g., Email, Phone_SHA256, ECID, IDFA, GAID).']
  identifier_type varchar [note: 'The type of identifier being linked. Indicates the category of identity data (email, phone, cookie ID, device ID, loyalty card number, POS customer ID, Salesforce contact ID, Adobe ECID).']
  identifier_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual value of the identifier (e.g., email address, phone number, cookie string, device UUID, loyalty card number). This is the raw identity data point being resolved.']
  is_pii_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this identifier contains personally identifiable information (PII) subject to GDPR, CCPA, or other privacy regulations. True for email, phone, name; false for anonymous cookies or device IDs.']
  is_primary_identifier boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the primary identifier of its type for the customer. True indicates this is the preferred identifier for communication and identification, false indicates secondary or alternate identifier.']
  last_seen_date date [note: 'The most recent date when this identifier was observed in any system activity. Used to identify stale or inactive identifiers.']
  link_confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Confidence score (0.0000 to 1.0000) indicating the certainty of the identity link. Deterministic links typically score 1.0, while probabilistic links have lower scores based on matching algorithm confidence.']
  link_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this identity link was first established in the identity graph. Represents when the resolution process connected this identifier to the customer profile.']
  link_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this identity link expires or should be re-verified. Null for permanent links. Used for time-bound identifiers like session cookies or temporary device IDs.']
  link_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking of this identity link when multiple identifiers of the same type exist for a customer. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used to determine the primary identifier for each type.']
  link_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the identity link. Active links are used in identity resolution, inactive links are historical, pending links await verification, disputed links require review, merged links have been consolidated, split links have been separated.']
  link_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this identity link record. Tracks when link attributes (status, confidence, priority) were last modified.']
  link_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this identity link was last verified or validated. Used for links that require periodic confirmation (e.g., email verification, phone validation).']
  match_attributes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of attributes used to establish this identity link (e.g., email+last_name, phone+postal_code). Documents the matching logic for audit and troubleshooting.']
  match_weight decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to this identity link based on the strength of matching attributes. Higher weights indicate stronger matches. Used in probabilistic identity resolution scoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this identity link. Used to document manual overrides, dispute resolutions, data quality issues, or special handling instructions.']
  resolution_algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the identity resolution algorithm or model used to create this link. Enables tracking of resolution logic changes over time and supports reprocessing when algorithms are updated.']
  resolution_method varchar [note: 'The method used to establish this identity link. Deterministic uses exact matches (e.g., same email across systems), probabilistic uses statistical matching, manual indicates human review, third-party uses external identity resolution services, machine learning uses AI/ML models.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the system of record that provided this identity link (e.g., SAP_CAR for POS, SFCC for e-commerce, AEP for CDP, SFDC for CRM, LOYALTY for loyalty platform).']
  third_party_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned by the third-party identity resolution provider. Used for reconciliation and audit with external identity graphs.']
  third_party_source varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party identity resolution provider or data enrichment service that contributed to this identity link (e.g., LiveRamp, Neustar, Experian). Null for first-party links.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the authenticity of this identity link. Email click indicates email verification, SMS code indicates phone verification, login indicates authenticated session, purchase indicates transaction confirmation, customer service indicates agent verification.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification status of this identity link. Verified links have been confirmed, unverified links are assumed but not confirmed, failed links did not pass verification, expired links need re-verification, pending links await verification.']

  Note: 'Manages cross-channel identity resolution by linking multiple identifiers (email, phone, cookie ID, device ID, loyalty card number, POS customer ID, Salesforce contact ID, Adobe ECID) to a single resolved profile. Captures identifier type, identifier value, source system, link confidence score, resolution method (deterministic, probabilistic), link creation date, and active flag. Core to the CDP identity graph managed in Adobe Experience Platform.'
}

Table customer.cltv_record {
  cltv_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the CLTV record. Primary key.']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: cltv_record has segment_code (STRING) which is a business key reference to segment table. Normalizing to FK enables referential integrity and allows JOINing to segment definition, criteria, and metada']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: CLTV models segment customers by preferred product category to predict future value. Category affinity (footwear vs apparel vs accessories) significantly impacts lifetime value calculations and retent']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer for whom this CLTV record is calculated. Links to the customer master data.']
  aov decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per order for this customer, calculated as historical revenue divided by historical order count. Key metric for customer spending behavior.']
  avg_inter_purchase_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days between consecutive purchases. Used to predict next purchase timing and identify at-risk customers.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'The date on which this CLTV calculation was performed. Used to track historical CLTV snapshots and model refresh cycles.']
  calculation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to calculate CLTV for this record. Historical = past revenue only; Predictive = forecasted value; Hybrid = historical + predictive; RFM = recency-frequency-monetary segmentation.']
  churn_probability decimal(5,4) [note: 'Predicted probability (0.0000 to 1.0000) that the customer will churn within the next defined period. Generated by churn prediction model; used for proactive retention campaigns.']
  cltv_tier varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the customer based on their CLTV score. Used for segmentation, prioritization, and targeted marketing strategies.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Statistical confidence level (0.0000 to 1.0000) in the predicted future value. Higher scores indicate more reliable predictions based on data quality and model fit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CLTV record was first created in the lakehouse. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent currency context for multi-currency operations.']
  customer_tenure_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since the customer\'s first purchase or account creation date. Used in CLTV model to normalize metrics and segment by lifecycle stage.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Source system from which the customer transaction and behavioral data was extracted for CLTV calculation. Typically Adobe Experience Platform for DTC or Salesforce for B2B wholesale accounts.']
  discount_sensitivity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0.00 to 100.00) indicating the customer\'s responsiveness to promotional pricing and markdowns. High scores indicate price-driven behavior; low scores indicate brand loyalty.']
  first_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the customer\'s first completed order. Anchor date for tenure calculation and cohort analysis.']
  forecast_horizon_months varchar [note: 'Number of months into the future for which the predicted future value is calculated (e.g., 12, 24, 36). Defines the time window for CLTV projection.']
  historical_orders varchar [note: 'Total number of completed orders placed by the customer from their first purchase to the calculation date.']
  historical_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross revenue generated by the customer from their first purchase to the calculation date. Excludes returns and discounts unless specified in model logic.']
  last_purchase_date date [note: 'Date of the customer\'s most recent completed order as of the calculation date. Used to calculate recency and identify dormant customers.']
  loyalty_member_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) showing whether the customer is enrolled in the brand\'s loyalty program. Loyalty members typically have higher CLTV.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level in the loyalty program (if enrolled). Higher tiers correlate with higher CLTV and receive differentiated benefits.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the CLTV calculation model or algorithm used (e.g., v2.3, RFM-2024-Q1). Enables tracking of model evolution and comparison across versions.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent NPS score provided by the customer (scale -100 to 100 or 0 to 10 depending on survey design). Measures customer satisfaction and likelihood to recommend the brand.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent NPS score was collected. Used to assess recency of sentiment data.']
  predicted_future_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted revenue expected from this customer over a defined future period (typically 12-36 months). Generated by predictive CLTV model using historical behavior and cohort analysis.']
  primary_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the customer has generated the majority of their revenue. Used for channel-specific CLTV analysis and personalization strategies.']
  purchase_frequency decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average number of purchases per time period (typically per year). Calculated as total orders divided by customer tenure in years.']
  recency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days since the customer\'s last purchase as of the calculation date. Key component of RFM (Recency, Frequency, Monetary) segmentation.']
  retention_risk_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) marking customers at high risk of churn based on churn probability threshold or behavioral signals. Triggers retention workflow in CRM.']
  return_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of orders returned by the customer (returned orders divided by total orders). High return rates may indicate fit issues, quality concerns, or wardrobing behavior.']
  total_cltv decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of historical revenue and predicted future value. Represents the total expected value of the customer relationship. Primary metric for retention prioritization and marketing investment decisions.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CLTV record was last updated or recalculated. Reflects the most recent model refresh cycle.']

  Note: 'Stores calculated Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) metrics per customer, updated on a scheduled basis. Captures historical gross revenue, total orders, AOV (Average Order Value), purchase frequency, average inter-purchase interval, predicted future value, CLTV model version, calculation date, and CLTV tier classification (high, mid, low). Used for retention prioritization, personalization investment decisions, and CRM segmentation in Salesforce.'
}

Table customer.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer interaction event.']
  associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store associate or agent who facilitated the interaction.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this interaction, if applicable.']
  circular_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.circular_program. Business justification: Customer interactions (inquiries, in-store participation events, digital engagement) related to specific circular programs. Tracks engagement for program effectiveness measurement, customer education ']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the digital property (website, app, portal) where the interaction occurred.']
  household_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the household associated with this interaction, if applicable.']
  interaction_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who initiated or was the subject of this interaction.']
  interaction_referred_customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who was referred, applicable for referral-type interactions.']
  interaction_referrer_customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who made the referral, applicable for referral-type interactions.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Browsing and engagement interactions are tracked at category level for analytics (time spent in activewear vs luxury). Drives category affinity scoring, personalization algorithms, and merchandising i']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotion or offer that triggered or was presented during the interaction.']
  quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.quote. Business justification: Sales teams track customer interactions during wholesale quote negotiation process. Enables quote-related interaction analysis, sales cycle tracking, and touchpoint measurement for B2B quote conversio']
  referral_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the referral program under which this referral interaction occurred.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store where the interaction occurred, if applicable.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order placed or discussed during this interaction, if applicable.']
  session_id varchar [note: 'Unique session identifier for web or app interactions, used for journey tracking.']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Point of Sale (POS) transaction associated with this interaction, if applicable.']
  ccpa_opt_out_requested boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the customer requested CCPA opt-out during this interaction.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel or medium through which the interaction took place. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: in_store|web|mobile_app|email|phone|chat|social_media|sms|direct_mail — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this interaction record was first created in the data platform in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  csat_score varchar [note: 'Customer Satisfaction score captured for this interaction, typically on a scale of 1 to 5.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the customer during the interaction.']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The length of the interaction in seconds, applicable for sessions, calls, and visits.']
  gdpr_consent_captured boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether GDPR consent was captured or confirmed during this interaction.']
  geolocation_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The latitude coordinate of the customer location during the interaction, if captured.']
  geolocation_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'The longitude coordinate of the customer location during the interaction, if captured.']
  interaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the interaction event.']
  interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the interaction occurred in format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'The category of customer touchpoint or engagement event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store_visit|website_session|app_open|email_open|email_click|chat_session|call_center_contact|stylist_consultation|referral|social_media_engagement|sms_interaction — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address of the customer device during the interaction, used for fraud detection and analytics.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments captured during or after the interaction by the associate or system.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score captured during or immediately after this interaction, ranging from 0 to 10.']
  outcome varchar [note: 'The business result or resolution of the interaction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase|inquiry|complaint|support_resolved|support_escalated|no_action|referral_sent|appointment_booked|abandoned — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  page_url varchar [note: 'The URL of the web page where the interaction occurred, applicable for digital interactions.']
  product_viewed_sku varchar [note: 'The SKU of the product viewed or discussed during the interaction, if applicable.']
  referral_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which the referral was made, applicable for referral-type interactions.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'The unique referral code used or generated during this interaction, applicable for referral-type interactions.']
  referral_conversion_date date [note: 'The date when the referred customer completed the conversion action in format yyyy-MM-dd.']
  referral_conversion_status varchar [note: 'The status of the referral conversion, indicating whether the referred customer completed the desired action.']
  referral_reward_issued boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a reward was issued to the referrer for this referral.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric sentiment score derived from interaction content, ranging from -1.00 (negative) to 1.00 (positive).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that captured this interaction event. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: adobe_aep|salesforce_commerce|sap_car|centric_plm|call_center_crm|store_pos|mobile_app — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  source_system_interaction_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this interaction in the source operational system.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief subject or title describing the purpose or topic of the interaction.']
  user_agent varchar [note: 'The user agent string identifying the browser or application used during the interaction.']

  Note: 'Records every customer touchpoint and engagement event across channels including in-store visits, website sessions, app opens, email opens/clicks, chat sessions, call center contacts, stylist consultations, and referral events. Captures interaction type, channel, timestamp, duration, outcome, associated campaign or promotion reference, store or digital property identifier, and agent or associate ID. For referral-type interactions: captures referral code, referrer profile reference, referred profile reference, referral channel, conversion status, conversion date, reward issuance, and program reference. Supports CAC reduction tracking, word-of-mouth growth measurement, and feeds behavioral data into Adobe Experience Platform for journey analytics.'
}

Table customer.service_request {
  service_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer service request case. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Service requests often relate to campaign-driven purchases (defective items, sizing issues from campaign promotions). Enables campaign quality analysis, post-purchase support cost attribution, and cam']
  circular_enrollment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.circular_enrollment. Business justification: Service cases related to circular program enrollments (item condition disputes, incentive redemption issues, collection logistics problems). Links support tickets to specific enrollments for resolutio']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Service requests (returns, warranty claims, complaints) incur customer service costs that must be allocated to cost centers for departmental cost accounting, chargeback analysis to stores/channels, an']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer service agent or representative assigned to handle this case. Links to workforce or employee entity.']
  handling_associate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.associate. Business justification: Service requests handled by store associates (in-store returns, complaints, styling consultations) require associate-level tracking for performance reviews, training needs identification, customer sat']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Service requests and quality issues tracked by product category enable trend analysis (footwear sizing issues, outerwear zipper defects). Merchandising teams use this for vendor quality scorecards and']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who submitted the service request. Links to the customer who initiated the case.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store where the service request was initiated, if submitted in-store. Links to store operations entity.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.rma. Business justification: Customer service tracks service cases related to returns. Links service requests to RMAs for return case management, resolution tracking, and customer satisfaction analysis on return-related issues.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with this service request, if applicable. Used for order-related inquiries, returns, and exchanges.']
  sample_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sample_request. Business justification: Customer service requests for fit issues, quality defects, or product inquiries trigger sourcing sample requests to evaluate construction, measurements, or material specifications. Quality team reques']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Customer service requests for damaged, lost, or delayed items require direct reference to the specific shipment for carrier claims, insurance filing, and root cause analysis. Service teams need shipme']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Factory-level traceability for quality issues enables targeted corrective actions, factory-specific audits, and compliance remediation. Essential for apparel industry quality management systems, regul']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Quality complaints and product defects require tracing to the manufacturing vendor for root cause analysis, corrective action requests, and vendor scorecarding. Critical for vendor performance managem']
  assigned_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the customer service team responsible for handling this case type. Used for workload distribution and routing.']
  case_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible case number provided to the customer for tracking and reference purposes. Human-readable identifier.']
  case_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the service request. Tracks progression from initial submission through resolution and closure.']
  case_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the service request type. Categorizes the nature of the customer inquiry or issue.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was formally closed. May differ from resolved timestamp if additional administrative steps were required.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address used by the customer for this specific service request. May differ from primary customer email if submitted by alternate contact.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number used by the customer for this specific service request. Used for follow-up communication and case resolution.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the service request originated. Used for regional routing and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was initially created in the system. Represents the moment the customer issue was logged.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for any monetary amounts associated with this service request, such as refunds or credits.']
