  customer_satisfaction_score varchar [note: 'Customer satisfaction rating provided by the customer after case resolution. Typically on a scale of 1-5 or 1-10.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether this service request was escalated to a higher support tier or management. True if escalation occurred.']
  escalation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of why the service request was escalated. Captures business justification for escalation to higher support tiers.']
  first_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the first response was provided to the customer by a service agent. Used for Service Level Agreement (SLA) tracking.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes and comments visible only to service agents and management. Contains sensitive operational details not shared with customers.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the service request was submitted and should be handled.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request record was last updated. Tracks most recent activity or status change.']
  loyalty_points_adjustment varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points added or deducted as part of the service request resolution. Positive for credits, negative for deductions.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Net Promoter Score provided by the customer indicating likelihood to recommend the brand. Scale of 0-10 where 9-10 are promoters, 7-8 passive, 0-6 detractors.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the service request based on urgency and business impact. Determines Service Level Agreement (SLA) targets.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier of the product related to this service request, if applicable. Used for product-specific complaints or inquiries.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount refunded to the customer as part of the service request resolution, if applicable. Excludes currency code.']
  resolution_category varchar [note: 'Classification of how the service request was resolved. Used for analytics on resolution patterns and customer satisfaction drivers.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes documenting the actions taken and resolution provided to the customer. Captures final outcome and customer satisfaction details.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the service request was marked as resolved. Indicates when the issue was addressed to customer satisfaction.']
  service_request_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the customer issue, inquiry, or complaint as initially reported. Captures full context of the service request.']
  sla_breach_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator of whether the Service Level Agreement (SLA) target was breached for this case. True if response or resolution exceeded target timeframes.']
  sla_target_resolution_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target number of hours within which the case should be resolved based on case priority and Service Level Agreement (SLA) rules.']
  sla_target_response_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target number of hours within which the first response should be provided based on case priority and Service Level Agreement (SLA) rules.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this service request in the source system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation with operational systems.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the system from which this service request record originated. Typically Salesforce Service Cloud or other Customer Relationship Management (CRM) platform.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Brief summary or title of the service request. Provides quick overview of the customer issue or inquiry.']
  submission_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the customer submitted the service request. Tracks customer contact preferences and channel effectiveness. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store|email|chat|phone|web|mobile_app|social_media — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  survey_response_date date [note: 'Date when the customer completed the satisfaction or Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey related to this service request.']

  Note: 'Tracks post-purchase customer service cases including returns inquiries, exchange requests, product complaints, order issues, loyalty disputes, and general support tickets. Captures case type, priority, status (open, in-progress, resolved, closed), channel of submission (store, email, chat, phone), assigned agent, resolution notes, resolution date, SLA breach flag, and linked order or return reference. Managed via Salesforce Service Cloud.'
}

Table customer.payment_method {
  payment_method_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the payment method record. Primary key.']
  billing_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing address associated with this payment method. Links to customer address record.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who owns this payment method. Links to the customer master record.']
  added_channel varchar [note: 'The channel or interface through which the customer added this payment method (web storefront, mobile app, physical store, call center, API integration).']
  added_date date [note: 'The date when the customer added this payment method to their account.']
  auto_update_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this payment method is enrolled in automatic card updater services that refresh expiry dates and card numbers when cards are reissued.']
  bnpl_provider varchar [note: 'The buy-now-pay-later service provider when payment_type is buy_now_pay_later (Klarna, Afterpay, Affirm, Sezzle, Zip, QuadPay). Null for non-BNPL payment types.']
  card_brand varchar [note: 'The card network or brand (Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, JCB, Diners Club). Null for non-card payment types.']
  card_funding_type varchar [note: 'The funding mechanism of the card (credit, debit, prepaid, charge card, or deferred debit). Null for non-card payment types.']
  cardholder_name varchar [note: 'The name of the cardholder as it appears on the payment instrument. Used for verification and display purposes.']
  ccpa_opt_out_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the customer has opted out of the sale or sharing of their payment method data under California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment method record was first created in the data platform.']
  data_retention_date date [note: 'The date when this payment method record is scheduled for deletion or archival according to data retention policies and regulatory requirements.']
  digital_wallet_type varchar [note: 'The specific digital wallet provider when payment_type is digital_wallet (Apple Pay, Google Pay, PayPal, Samsung Pay, Venmo, Alipay, WeChat Pay). Null for non-wallet payment types. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: apple_pay|google_pay|paypal|samsung_pay|venmo|alipay|wechat_pay — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expiry_month varchar [note: 'The expiration month of the payment card (1-12). Null for non-expiring payment types.']
  expiry_year varchar [note: 'The expiration year of the payment card (four-digit year). Null for non-expiring payment types.']
  failed_transaction_count varchar [note: 'The total number of failed or declined transaction attempts using this payment method. High failure counts may trigger automatic suspension.']
  fraud_check_date date [note: 'The date when the most recent fraud risk assessment was performed on this payment method.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A risk score (0.00 to 100.00) assigned by fraud detection systems indicating the likelihood that this payment method is associated with fraudulent activity. Higher scores indicate higher risk.']
  gdpr_consent_date date [note: 'The date when GDPR consent was obtained for storing and processing this payment method data.']
  gdpr_consent_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the customer has provided explicit consent for storage and processing of this payment method data under GDPR requirements.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the customers default payment method for one-click checkout and recurring billing. Only one payment method per customer should be marked as default.']
  issuing_bank varchar [note: 'The name of the financial institution that issued the card or payment instrument. Derived from Bank Identification Number (BIN) lookup.']
  issuing_country_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code of the country where the payment instrument was issued. Used for fraud detection and regional compliance.']
  last_four_digits varchar [note: 'The last four digits of the card number or account number, used for customer identification and display purposes. PCI-DSS compliant truncated value.']
  last_used_date date [note: 'The date when this payment method was last successfully used for a transaction.']
  nickname varchar [note: 'Optional customer-defined nickname or label for this payment method to help identify it in their account (e.g., \'Work Visa\', \'Personal Amex\').']
  payment_method_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment method. Active methods are available for transactions; expired methods have passed their expiry date; suspended methods are temporarily blocked; pending or failed verification indicates incomplete validation.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'The category of payment instrument (credit card, debit card, digital wallet such as Apple Pay or Google Pay, gift card, buy-now-pay-later services such as Klarna or Afterpay, or store credit).']
  pci_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Flag confirming that this payment method record complies with PCI-DSS requirements (no raw card data stored, only tokenized references).']
  recurring_billing_enabled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this payment method is authorized for recurring billing or subscription charges without additional customer authentication.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this payment method in the source system, used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this payment method record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, Adobe Experience Platform, internal payment gateway).']
  three_d_secure_enrolled boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this payment method is enrolled in 3D Secure authentication (Verified by Visa, Mastercard SecureCode, etc.) for enhanced transaction security.']
  token varchar [note: 'The tokenized reference to the actual payment instrument, provided by the payment gateway or tokenization service. No raw card data is stored. PCI-DSS compliant token.']
  tokenization_provider varchar [note: 'The name of the payment gateway or tokenization service that issued the token (e.g., Stripe, Adyen, Braintree, Salesforce Commerce Cloud Payment Gateway).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment method record was last modified in the data platform.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'The total number of successful transactions processed using this payment method since it was added.']
  verification_date date [note: 'The date when the payment method was last verified or validation was attempted.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the payment method has been successfully verified through Address Verification Service (AVS), Card Verification Value (CVV) check, or other validation mechanisms.']

  Note: 'Stores tokenized payment instruments on file for a customer, enabling one-click checkout and recurring billing. Captures payment type (credit card, debit card, digital wallet, gift card, buy-now-pay-later), card brand, last four digits, expiry date, billing address reference, tokenization provider, default flag, and active status. PCI-DSS compliant — no raw card data stored; only tokenized references from Salesforce Commerce Cloud.'
}

Table customer.size_profile {
  size_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the customer size profile record. Primary key.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Size profiles are category-specific (tops vs bottoms vs footwear have different sizing systems). Essential for size recommendations, virtual try-on, and reducing returns. Replaces denormalized apparel']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who owns this size profile. Links to the customer master record.']
  body_shape_type varchar [note: 'Optional classification of the customer\'s body shape (e.g., \'athletic\', \'pear\', \'rectangle\', \'hourglass\', \'inverted_triangle\'). Used for advanced fit personalization and style recommendations.']
  bra_band_size varchar [note: 'Bra band size measurement (e.g., \'32\', \'34\', \'36\'). Specific to women\'s intimate apparel and activewear.']
  bra_cup_size varchar [note: 'Bra cup size measurement (e.g., \'A\', \'B\', \'C\', \'D\', \'DD\'). Specific to women\'s intimate apparel and activewear.']
  chest_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Chest circumference measurement. Critical for tops, outerwear, and activewear sizing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this size profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'The source system or platform from which this size profile data originated (e.g., \'Adobe Experience Platform\', \'Salesforce Commerce Cloud\', \'In-Store Kiosk\', \'Mobile App\').']
  fit_preference varchar [note: 'The customer\'s preferred fit style for this apparel category. Influences product recommendations and helps reduce returns driven by fit dissatisfaction.']
  foot_length_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Foot length measurement from heel to toe. Critical for footwear sizing.']
  foot_width_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Foot width measurement at the widest point. Used for footwear width sizing (narrow, medium, wide).']
  height decimal(10,2) [note: 'The customer\'s height measurement. Critical for inseam, sleeve length, and overall garment length recommendations.']
  height_unit varchar [note: 'The unit in which height is expressed (inches, centimeters, or feet).']
  hip_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Hip circumference measurement. Critical for bottoms and activewear sizing, particularly for women\'s apparel.']
  inseam_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Inseam length measurement from crotch to ankle. Critical for pants and jeans sizing.']
  is_primary_profile boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the customer\'s primary size profile used for default fit recommendations and personalization. Only one profile per customer should be marked as primary.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'The date when the customer last verified or confirmed their size profile measurements. Used to prompt customers to update outdated profiles.']
  measurement_date date [note: 'The date when this measurement was captured or last updated. Body measurements can change over time; recent measurements are prioritized for fit recommendations.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'The method by which the measurement was captured. Self-reported measurements may be less accurate than professional scans or tailor measurements. Used to weight fit recommendations.']
  measurement_type varchar [note: 'The specific body measurement captured (e.g., chest, waist, hip, inseam, shoulder_width, foot_length, neck, sleeve_length, rise, thigh). Varies by apparel category.']
  measurement_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The numeric value of the body measurement. Precision supports fractional measurements for accurate fit personalization.']
  neck_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Neck circumference measurement. Critical for dress shirts, formal wear, and outerwear sizing.']
  preferred_brand_size varchar [note: 'The customer\'s preferred size designation within the brand\'s sizing system for this apparel category (e.g., \'M\', \'L\', \'32x34\', \'10\', \'42EU\'). Complements body measurements for fit personalization.']
  profile_completeness_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage (0-100) indicating how complete this size profile is based on the number of measurements captured relative to the ideal set for the apparel category. Used to prompt customers to complete their profiles.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the size profile. Active profiles are used for fit recommendations; inactive profiles are retained but not applied; archived profiles are historical records.']
  return_rate_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this size profile has been effective in reducing the customer\'s return rate for sizing-related issues. Used to measure the business impact of fit personalization.']
  shoulder_width_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Shoulder width measurement from shoulder point to shoulder point. Critical for tops, outerwear, and formal wear sizing.']
  size_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'A calculated confidence score (0-100) indicating the reliability of this size profile based on measurement source, recency, and consistency with purchase history. Higher scores indicate more reliable fit recommendations.']
  sleeve_length_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Sleeve length measurement from shoulder to wrist. Critical for tops, outerwear, and formal wear sizing.']
  stylist_notes varchar [note: 'Optional free-text notes from personal stylists or store associates regarding the customer\'s fit preferences, body shape considerations, or special sizing needs. Treated as confidential business data.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the measurement value is expressed. Supports both metric (centimeters) and imperial (inches) systems, as well as regional sizing standards (US, UK, EU) for footwear and ready-to-wear sizes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this size profile record was last modified. Used for audit trail and data synchronization.']
  virtual_try_on_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this size profile is enabled for virtual try-on experiences in the e-commerce platform. Requires sufficient measurement data and customer consent.']
  waist_measurement decimal(10,2) [note: 'Waist circumference measurement. Critical for bottoms, activewear, and outerwear sizing.']
  weight decimal(10,2) [note: 'The customer\'s weight measurement. Optional field used for advanced fit modeling and size recommendations. Treated as confidential business data.']
  weight_unit varchar [note: 'The unit in which weight is expressed (pounds or kilograms).']

  Note: 'Stores a customer\'s body measurements and size preferences across apparel categories (tops, bottoms, outerwear, footwear, accessories). Captures measurement type (chest, waist, hip, inseam, shoulder width, foot length), measurement value, unit of measure, measurement source (self-reported, in-store scan, virtual try-on), measurement date, and preferred brand size per category. Enables fit personalization and reduces return rates driven by sizing issues.'
}

Table customer.referral {
  referral_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the referral event. Primary key for the referral data product.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Referrals are often campaign-driven (refer-a-friend campaigns, seasonal referral bonuses). Tracks campaign-specific referral performance, referral program ROI, and enables campaign-level referral rewa']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the referral program under which this referral was made. Links to the specific campaign or program offering referral incentives.']
  referral_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the existing customer who initiated the referral. Links to the customer profile who is advocating for the brand.']
  referral_referred_customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the new customer who was referred. Links to the customer profile who received the referral and may convert.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: In-store referral programs require store attribution for associate performance metrics, store-level referral campaign ROI tracking, and commission calculations. Store associates actively drive referra']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order that triggered the referral conversion. Links to the specific transaction that qualified the referral for reward issuance.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'The date when the referred customer accepted the referral by clicking the link or entering the code. Null if not yet accepted.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The channel or medium through which the referral was shared by the referrer. Indicates how the referral code or link was distributed to the referred customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: email|sms|social_media|in_store|mobile_app|website|whatsapp|messenger|other — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  conversion_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the conversion order placed by the referred customer. Used to calculate reward eligibility and track referral program ROI.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'The date when the referred customer completed their first qualifying purchase or conversion event. Null if conversion has not yet occurred.']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the referred customer completed a qualifying conversion event, typically their first purchase. True indicates successful conversion; False indicates no conversion yet.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country from which the referral was initiated. Used for geographic segmentation and compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this referral record was first created in the lakehouse. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the conversion amount. Indicates the currency in which the conversion transaction was conducted.']
  days_to_conversion varchar [note: 'The number of days between the referral date and the conversion date. Measures the time lag from referral to purchase. Null if not yet converted.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the referrer when initiating the referral. Helps understand channel preferences and optimize referral program UX.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date when this referral expires and can no longer be used by the referred customer. After this date, the referral code becomes invalid.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this referral has been flagged as potentially fraudulent. True indicates suspected fraud; False indicates legitimate referral.']
  fraud_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason why this referral was flagged as fraudulent. Null if fraud_flag is False. Used for fraud pattern analysis and prevention.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the referral was initiated. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis. May be anonymized per privacy regulations.']
  link_url varchar [note: 'The unique URL generated for the referrer to share with potential customers. Contains tracking parameters to attribute conversions back to this referral.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used by the referrer to share with potential customers. This code tracks the referral attribution and is entered by the referred customer during signup or purchase.']
  referral_date date [note: 'The date when the referral event was initiated by the referrer. Represents the business event timestamp when the referral code was generated or shared.']
  referral_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the referral. Tracks whether the referred customer has accepted the referral, made a qualifying purchase, or if the referral has expired or been declined.']
  referral_tier varchar [note: 'The tier or level of the referral program under which this referral was made. Different tiers may offer different reward structures or benefits.']
  referral_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise timestamp when the referral event was recorded in the system. Captures the exact moment the referral was logged, including time zone information.']
  referred_customer_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the referred customer at the time of referral. Used for tracking and communication purposes. May be null if referral was made without email capture.']
  referred_customer_phone varchar [note: 'The phone number of the referred customer at the time of referral. Used for tracking and communication purposes. May be null if referral was made without phone capture.']
  reward_expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the issued reward expires and can no longer be redeemed by the referrer. Null for rewards with no expiration.']
  reward_issued_date date [note: 'The date when the reward was issued to the referrer. Null if reward has not yet been issued.']
  reward_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the referrer has been issued their reward for this successful referral. True indicates reward has been granted; False indicates pending or not eligible.']
  reward_type varchar [note: 'The type of reward issued to the referrer for the successful referral. Indicates the form of incentive provided as part of the referral program.']
  reward_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'The monetary or points value of the reward issued to the referrer. Quantifies the incentive amount granted for the successful referral.']
  source_campaign varchar [note: 'The marketing campaign or initiative that prompted the referrer to make this referral. Used for campaign attribution and effectiveness analysis.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this referral record in the source operational system. Used for data lineage and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system that originated this referral record. Indicates whether the referral came from Adobe Experience Platform, Salesforce Commerce Cloud, mobile app, or other channels.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this referral record was last updated in the lakehouse. Tracks the most recent modification for change data capture and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Tracks customer referral events where an existing customer refers a new customer through a referral program. Captures referrer profile reference, referred profile reference, referral code used, referral channel (email, social share, in-store), referral date, conversion status (referred customer made first purchase), conversion date, reward issued to referrer, and program reference. Supports CAC reduction strategies and word-of-mouth growth programs.'
}

Table customer.attendance {
  attendance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this event attendance record. Primary key.']
  event_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing event for which attendance is being tracked']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the consumer profile who was invited to or attended the event']
  attendance_status varchar [note: 'Actual attendance outcome for this consumer at this event. Captured during or after the event through check-in systems or manual confirmation. Distinguishes confirmed attendees from no-shows.']
  check_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the consumer checked in at the event venue or virtual platform. Null if consumer did not attend. Used for attendance verification and engagement timing analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this attendance record was first created in the system (typically when invitation was generated or RSVP was received).']
  guest_count varchar [note: 'Number of additional guests (plus-ones) brought by this consumer to the event. Defaults to 0 if consumer attended alone. Used for capacity planning and actual attendance reconciliation.']
  invitation_channel varchar [note: 'Communication channel through which the invitation was delivered to this consumer (email, SMS, direct mail, in-app notification, sales associate outreach, VIP concierge). Used for channel effectiveness analysis.']
  invitation_sent_date date [note: 'Date when the event invitation was sent to this consumer profile. Tracks the start of the engagement funnel for this profile-event combination.']
  post_event_survey_score decimal(3,1) [note: 'Consumer satisfaction or Net Promoter Score captured via post-event survey for this specific event experience. Scale typically 0.0 to 10.0. Null if survey not completed. Used for event effectiveness measurement.']
  rsvp_date date [note: 'Date when the consumer submitted their RSVP response for this event. Null if no response received. Used to calculate response time and urgency of follow-up.']
  rsvp_status varchar [note: 'Current RSVP response status from the consumer for this specific event. Tracks whether the consumer confirmed attendance, declined, or has not responded.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this attendance record was last modified (RSVP change, attendance confirmation, survey submission).']
  vip_access_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this consumer was granted VIP or exclusive access privileges for this specific event (backstage access, meet-and-greet, priority seating, exclusive preview). VIP status is event-specific, not profile-level.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Event participation relationship between consumer profiles and marketing events. It captures invitation management, RSVP tracking, attendance confirmation, and post-event engagement for each profile-event combination. Each record links one consumer profile to one marketing event with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific event participation instance.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion marketing operations, consumer profiles are invited to and attend multiple marketing events over time (runway shows, pop-ups, VIP experiences, seasonal launches), and each marketing event hosts multiple consumer attendees. The business actively manages event attendance as an operational process with invitation management, RSVP tracking, check-in operations, and post-event engagement measurement. This is a recognized business entity called \'event attendance\' or \'event registration\' that marketing teams create, update, and analyze.'
}

Table customer.account_contact_role {
  account_contact_role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this account-contact role assignment record']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the contact person in this account role relationship']
  wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the wholesale account in this contact role relationship']
  account_role_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contact assumed their current role or position within the associated wholesale account or enterprise partner organization. Supports relationship tenure tracking and account history analysis. [Moved from contact: This date represents when the contact assumed their role for a specific account. When a contact manages multiple accounts, there must be a separate start date for each account relationship. This attribute belongs in the association as relationship_start_date.]']
  buying_authority_level varchar [note: 'Level of purchasing decision-making authority this contact has for this specific wholesale account. Authority may vary by account even for the same contact person.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account contact role record was created in the system']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this account-contact role assignment is currently active. Derived from relationship_end_date but denormalized for query performance.']
  is_billing_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact receives invoices and payment reminders for this wholesale account. Multiple billing contacts may exist per account.']
  is_decision_maker boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact has final purchasing decision authority for this wholesale account. Used to prioritize outreach and negotiation efforts.']
  is_primary_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact is the primary point of contact for this wholesale account in this role. Multiple contacts may exist but only one is primary per account.']
  is_shipping_contact boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this contact receives shipping notifications and logistics updates for this wholesale account.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when this contact ended their role with this wholesale account. NULL indicates an active relationship. Used for historical analysis and contact turnover tracking.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when this contact began their role with this wholesale account. Used for relationship tenure analysis and historical tracking.']
  role varchar [note: 'Functional role this contact performs for this specific wholesale account (e.g., Buyer, Logistics Coordinator, Finance Contact). A contact may have different roles for different accounts.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this account contact role record was last modified']

  Note: 'This association product represents the Role-based relationship between wholesale_account and contact. It captures the functional assignment of individual contacts to wholesale accounts with specific responsibilities and authority levels. Each record links one wholesale_account to one contact with attributes that define the nature, scope, and temporal validity of that business relationship.. Existence Justification: In B2B wholesale apparel operations, a single wholesale account (e.g., a retail chain with 50 store locations) has multiple contacts serving different functional roles (regional buyers, logistics coordinators, finance contacts, store managers). Simultaneously, a regional buyer contact may manage purchasing relationships for multiple wholesale accounts (e.g., 10 different store entities within a franchise network or buying group). The business actively manages these account-contact role assignments with specific authority levels, functional responsibilities, and temporal validity.'
}

Table customer.targeting {
  targeting_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for targeting']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign that is targeting this segment']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the customer segment being targeted in this campaign']
  actual_conversions varchar [note: 'Actual number of conversions (purchases, sign-ups, or other defined campaign actions) achieved from this segment within this campaign. Measured and updated as the campaign executes. Used to calculate segment-specific conversion rates and compare against target_conversion_count.']
  actual_reach varchar [note: 'Actual number of unique customers from this segment who were exposed to this campaign (impressions, emails delivered, ads served). Measured and updated as the campaign executes. Used to calculate segment-specific reach rates and campaign penetration.']
  budget_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the total campaign budget allocated to this segment. Calculated as (target_spend_amount / campaign.budget_allocated_amount) * 100. Sum of budget_allocation_percent across all segments for a campaign should equal 100%.']
  campaign_segment_targeting_reference varchar [note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this campaign-segment targeting record. Primary key.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment targeting record was created in the system. Audit field for tracking when segments were added to campaigns.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this segment targeting concludes within the campaign. May differ from the campaign\'s overall end_date if segments are deactivated early due to budget exhaustion or performance issues. Enables dynamic segment optimization during campaign execution.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the individual who last modified this segment targeting record. Audit field for accountability in campaign optimization.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this segment targeting record was last updated. Audit field for tracking changes to targeting parameters, budget allocation, or status.']
  offer_code varchar [note: 'Promotional offer or discount code specifically assigned to this segment within this campaign. Enables segment-specific offers (e.g., higher-value offers for high-value segments). Used for attribution and redemption tracking.']
  personalization_variant_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the creative or messaging variant used for this segment within this campaign. Enables segment-specific personalization where different segments receive tailored creative, offers, or messaging within the same campaign. References creative asset management system.']
  segment_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Ordinal ranking of this segment\'s priority within this campaign (1 = highest priority). Used when campaigns target multiple segments with different strategic importance. Influences budget allocation decisions and creative personalization priority.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when this segment targeting becomes active within the campaign. May differ from the campaign\'s overall start_date if segments are activated in phases or waves. Enables phased rollout strategies and A/B testing by segment.']
  target_conversion_count varchar [note: 'Planned number of conversions (purchases, sign-ups, or other defined campaign actions) expected from this segment within this campaign. Used to calculate segment-specific CAC targets and measure campaign effectiveness by segment.']
  target_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned marketing investment allocated to this specific segment within this campaign. Represents the portion of the campaign budget designated for reaching and converting this segment. Sum of target_spend_amount across all segments for a campaign should align with the campaign\'s budget_allocated_amount.']
  targeting_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this segment targeting within the campaign. Planned = scheduled but not yet active; Active = currently executing; Paused = temporarily suspended; Completed = finished execution; Cancelled = terminated before completion. Allows independent management of segment targeting within a campaign.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the individual who created this segment targeting record. Audit field for accountability in campaign planning.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the targeting relationship between customer segments and marketing campaigns. It captures the operational assignment of specific customer segments to campaigns with segment-specific budget allocation, performance targets, and actual results. Each record links one segment to one campaign with attributes that exist only in the context of this targeting relationship, enabling multi-segment campaigns and segment participation across concurrent campaigns.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion marketing operations, campaigns routinely target multiple customer segments simultaneously (e.g., a seasonal launch campaign targets both high-value loyalists and aspirational shoppers with different creative and budget allocations), and segments participate in multiple concurrent campaigns (a VIP segment may be targeted by seasonal, promotional, and retention campaigns running in parallel). The business actively manages segment-specific budget allocation, performance targets, personalization variants, and actual results for each campaign-segment pairing. This is operationally managed as \'campaign segment targeting\' with distinct planning, activation, and measurement workflows.'
}

Table customer.loyalty_tier {
  loyalty_tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for loyalty_tier']
  upgraded_from_loyalty_tier_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on loyalty_tier (upgraded_from_loyalty_tier_id)']
  annual_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual membership fee charged to maintain this tier status, if applicable. Zero for free tiers, positive amount for premium paid tiers. Expressed in the program\'s base currency.']
  birthday_reward_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of this tier receive special birthday rewards, discounts, or bonus points. True if birthday benefits are included, false otherwise.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this loyalty tier record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and tier lifecycle tracking.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard discount percentage offered to members of this tier on eligible purchases (e.g., 5%, 10%, 15%). Applied at checkout for tier-based pricing benefits.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Sequence number controlling the display order of tiers in user interfaces and marketing materials. Lower numbers appear first. Used for consistent tier presentation across channels.']
  downgrade_protection_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members are protected from immediate tier downgrade if they fail to meet qualification thresholds. True if grace period or soft landing is provided, false for immediate downgrade.']
  early_access_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of this tier receive early access to new product launches, seasonal collections, and exclusive sales events. True if early access is granted, false otherwise.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this loyalty tier is retired or no longer available for new assignments. Null for ongoing tiers. Existing members may retain tier status beyond this date based on program rules.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this loyalty tier becomes active and available for customer assignment. Supports phased rollout of new tier structures or seasonal programs.']
  exclusive_events_access boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of this tier receive invitations to exclusive brand events, fashion shows, VIP shopping experiences, or member-only gatherings. True if event access is granted, false otherwise.']
  free_shipping_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of this tier receive complimentary standard shipping on all orders. True if free shipping is a tier benefit, false otherwise.']
  invitation_only_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier is available only by invitation rather than automatic qualification. True for exclusive VIP tiers requiring brand approval, false for standard qualification-based tiers.']
  max_members_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of customers allowed in this tier at any given time. Used for exclusive invitation-only tiers. Null for unlimited tiers.']
  partner_benefits_description varchar [note: 'Description of benefits provided through partner brands, co-branded programs, or affiliate networks (e.g., hotel upgrades, airline lounge access, dining rewards). Enhances tier value proposition through ecosystem partnerships.']
  points_earning_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base points earning rate for members in this tier (e.g., 1.0 for base tier, 1.5 for silver, 2.0 for gold). Rewards higher-tier members with accelerated points accumulation.']
  priority_support_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members of this tier receive priority customer service through dedicated support channels, faster response times, or concierge services. True if priority support is available, false otherwise.']
  qualification_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months over which the qualification threshold must be met (e.g., 12 months for annual qualification, 3 months for quarterly). Defines the rolling window for tier evaluation.']
  qualification_threshold_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum spend amount required to qualify for or maintain this loyalty tier within the qualification period. Expressed in the program\'s base currency.']
  qualification_threshold_points varchar [note: 'Minimum loyalty points required to qualify for or maintain this tier within the qualification period. Alternative or complementary to spend-based qualification.']
  retention_period_months varchar [note: 'Number of months a member retains this tier status after qualification, regardless of subsequent activity. Provides stability and prevents frequent tier fluctuations.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Extended return window in days offered to members of this tier (e.g., 30 days for base tier, 60 days for premium tiers). Provides flexibility for product returns beyond standard policy.']
  terms_and_conditions_url varchar [note: 'URL to the legal terms and conditions governing this tier\'s benefits, qualification rules, and member obligations. Required for regulatory compliance and customer transparency.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the loyalty tier (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, VIP). Used as a business identifier in external systems and customer communications.']
  tier_color_hex varchar [note: 'Brand color associated with this tier in hexadecimal format (e.g., #CD7F32 for bronze, #C0C0C0 for silver, #FFD700 for gold). Used for visual representation in mobile apps, websites, and member cards.']
  tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the loyalty tier benefits, privileges, and value proposition. Used for customer education and marketing communications.']
  tier_icon_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the tier badge or icon image used in digital channels. Provides visual identification of tier status in customer profiles and communications.']
  tier_level varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking of the tier within the loyalty program hierarchy (e.g., 1=Bronze, 2=Silver, 3=Gold, 4=Platinum). Used for tier comparison and upgrade/downgrade logic.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Bronze Member, Silver Elite, Gold Premier, Platinum VIP). Used in customer-facing applications and marketing materials.']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loyalty tier. Active tiers are available for customer assignment; inactive tiers are no longer offered but may have existing members; deprecated tiers are being phased out; planned tiers are under development.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this loyalty tier record was last modified. Tracks changes to tier benefits, thresholds, or configuration over time.']
  welcome_bonus_points varchar [note: 'One-time bonus loyalty points awarded to customers upon first achieving this tier status. Used as an incentive for tier advancement.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for loyalty_tier. Referenced by current_tier_id.'
}

Table order.sales_order {
  sales_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sales order record. Primary key for the sales order entity.']
  billing_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the billing address for this order. Links to customer address master. Used for invoicing and payment processing.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Orders frequently originate from marketing campaigns. Direct link enables ROAS calculation, CAC analysis, campaign performance measurement, and marketing attribution reporting—core analytics for appar']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: Wholesale/B2B pre-orders during market weeks reference design concepts before full production. Buyers place line buys against concept presentations. Critical for pre-season order tracking and concept-']
  contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: B2B orders are placed by specific contacts at wholesale accounts. Captures ordering contact for account management, sales commission tracking, and contact-level purchasing behavior analysis in wholesa']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales representative or associate who assisted with the order. Applicable for retail POS (Point of Sale), call center, and wholesale orders. Null for self-service e-commerce orders.']
  esg_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_report. Business justification: Sales orders drive scope 3 category 9 (downstream transportation) emissions reporting. ESG reports aggregate order-level carbon data for CDP, GRI, and TCFD disclosures. Apparel brands report sold-prod']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Orders may be involved in compliance incidents (restricted party screening hits, export control violations, sanctioned country shipments). Audit trail for regulatory investigations and OFAC/BIS compli']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the payment method used for this order. Links to customer payment method master for credit card, bank transfer, or other payment instruments.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who placed this sales order. Links to the customer master record for DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) orders.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Export orders require regulatory filings for customs clearance, export licenses, and product safety certifications. Critical for international trade compliance and shipment authorization in apparel-fa']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Sales orders are executed within seasonal merchandise cycles. Essential for seasonal sales performance reporting, assortment planning validation, and aligning order fulfillment with seasonal delivery ']
  shipping_address_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipping address for order delivery. Links to customer address master. Used for fulfillment and logistics planning.']
  trade_compliance_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_record. Business justification: Export orders require trade compliance screening for ITAR/EAR controls, OFAC sanctioned parties, and customs classification. Regulatory mandate for international shipments preventing illegal exports a']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or distribution center assigned to fulfill this order. Links to warehouse location master. Determined by inventory availability and shipping address proximity.']
  wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the wholesale account for B2B orders. Null for DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) orders. Links to wholesale account master for wholesale EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) orders.']
  carrier_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the logistics carrier assigned to deliver this order. Examples include UPS, FedEx, DHL, USPS, or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider codes.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'System-confirmed delivery date based on ATP (Available to Promise) and logistics capacity. May differ from requested date due to inventory or capacity constraints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales order record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the order transaction. All monetary amounts in this order are denominated in this currency.']
  customer_po_number varchar [note: 'Customer-provided purchase order number for wholesale and B2B orders. Required for EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) orders and accounts payable matching. Null for DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) orders.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the order, including promotional discounts, markdown (MD) adjustments, and customer-specific pricing. Reduces the subtotal amount.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel classification for the order. Retail includes owned stores, wholesale includes B2B accounts, e-commerce is DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) online, outlet is off-price stores, franchise is partner-operated stores.']
  division varchar [note: 'Product division or category for the order. Used for business unit reporting and merchandise planning. Represents the primary product line of the order. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: mens|womens|kids|accessories|footwear|athletic|lifestyle — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fulfillment_status varchar [note: 'Status of order fulfillment operations. Tracks progress through warehouse picking, packing, and shipping. Used for operational monitoring and OTIF (On Time In Full) reporting.']
  gift_message varchar [note: 'Customer-provided gift message for gift orders. Printed on packing slip or gift card. Null for non-gift orders.']
  is_gift boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this order is a gift. True for gift orders requiring special packaging and gift message inclusion. False for regular orders.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales order record was last modified. Updated whenever order details, status, or amounts change. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the order was placed. E-commerce includes DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) web orders, retail POS (Point of Sale) captures in-store transactions, wholesale EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) handles B2B orders, call center represents phone orders, mobile app captures mobile commerce, and marketplace includes third-party platforms.']
  order_date date [note: 'The date when the sales order was placed by the customer. Represents the business event timestamp for order creation. Used for sales reporting, trend analysis, and OTIF (On Time In Full) tracking.']
  order_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or special instructions for order fulfillment. May include customer requests, delivery instructions, or internal processing notes.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the sales order. Used for customer communication, tracking, and cross-system reference. Unique across all sales channels.']
  order_source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record where the order was originally created. SAP S/4HANA SD handles wholesale and B2B orders, Salesforce Commerce Cloud manages DTC e-commerce, SAP CAR (Customer Activity Repository) captures retail POS transactions, Oracle Retail handles store operations.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sales order. Draft orders are incomplete, submitted orders await confirmation, confirmed orders are accepted, allocated orders have inventory reserved, picked orders are in warehouse fulfillment, packed orders are ready to ship, shipped orders are in transit, delivered orders are complete, cancelled orders are voided, returned orders are processing RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|confirmed|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|delivered|cancelled|returned — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  order_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the order was placed, including time zone. Used for order sequencing, fulfillment prioritization, and customer service.']
  order_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales order by business purpose. Standard orders are regular sales, rush orders require expedited fulfillment, pre-orders are for future collection releases, backorders are for out-of-stock items, sample orders are for sales representatives, and return orders process RMA (Return Merchandise Authorization).']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of payment processing for the order. Pending indicates awaiting payment, authorized means funds are reserved, captured means payment is collected, partially paid applies to installment orders, fully paid indicates complete payment, refunded applies to returns, failed indicates payment rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|captured|partially_paid|fully_paid|refunded|failed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms for the order. Immediate applies to DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) orders, net 30/60/90 are standard B2B terms, COD (Cash on Delivery) for specific channels, prepaid for advance payment, LC (Letter of Credit) for international wholesale. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: immediate|net_30|net_60|net_90|cod|prepaid|lc — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  priority_tier varchar [note: 'Fulfillment priority classification. VIP orders are for high-value customers, rush orders require expedited processing, high priority orders are time-sensitive, standard orders follow normal processing, backorder priority applies to replenishment orders.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Promotional code or coupon code applied to the order. Used for marketing campaign tracking and discount attribution.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Customer-requested delivery date. Used for fulfillment planning and OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement.']
  sales_organization varchar [note: 'Sales organization code responsible for this order. Represents the legal entity or business unit that owns the customer relationship and revenue. Used for financial reporting and commission calculation.']
  shipping_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Shipping and handling charges for order delivery. May be zero for free shipping promotions or wholesale FOB (Free on Board) orders.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Delivery method selected for the order. Standard is regular ground shipping, express is expedited delivery, overnight is next-day, two-day is second-day, ground is economy shipping, freight is for large wholesale orders, pickup is for BOPIS (Buy Online Pickup In Store). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|express|overnight|two_day|ground|freight|pickup — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  subtotal_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all line item amounts before taxes, shipping, and discounts. Represents the gross merchandise value of the order.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on the order, including sales tax, VAT (Value Added Tax), GST (Goods and Services Tax), or other applicable taxes based on ship-to jurisdiction.']
  total_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final order total including subtotal, taxes, shipping, minus discounts. Represents the total amount charged to the customer or invoiced to the wholesale account.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking number for shipment visibility. Used for customer self-service tracking and delivery confirmation.']

  Note: 'Core master record for all outbound sales orders across DTC e-commerce, retail POS, and wholesale EDI channels. Captures order header data including channel type, order date, customer/account reference, billing and shipping addresses, currency, total amounts, payment terms, order source system (SAP S/4HANA SD or Salesforce Commerce Cloud), priority tier, and overall lifecycle status. Tracks order-level amendments (quantity changes, address updates, cancellations) as audit-trail change records. Serves as the SSOT for all outbound sales order transactions in the apparel-fashion business.'
}

Table order.sales_order_line {
  sales_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sales_order_line']
  environmental_impact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.environmental_impact. Business justification: Line-level product LCA data enables customer-facing carbon labels, scope 3 category 11 (use of sold products) calculations, and product environmental passport compliance (EU Digital Product Passport).']
  ftc_label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ftc_label. Business justification: Order lines must reference correct FTC-compliant labeling for shipped SKUs. Federal Trade Commission Textile and Wool Acts require accurate fiber content, care instructions, and country of origin on c']
  product_safety_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_safety_test. Business justification: Order lines must verify SKU safety test compliance before fulfillment. CPSC and consumer product safety regulations require documented testing for children\'s apparel, flammability standards, and chemi']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific promotion or marketing campaign that generated the discount on this line. Enables promotional ROI analysis.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sales order header. Links this line item to its containing order.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment or delivery document that contains this line item. Links order line to physical shipment execution.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU representing the style, colorway, and size combination ordered on this line.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or distribution center from which this line item will be fulfilled. Drives allocation and fulfillment routing.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'The actual date on which this line item was shipped to the customer. Used for OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement.']
  allocation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory allocation record that reserved stock for this line item. Links order line to allocation planning.']
  atp_confirmed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item has been confirmed against available inventory through ATP check. True if inventory is reserved, false if pending or backordered.']
  cancel_date date [note: 'The date on which this line item was cancelled. Populated only for cancelled lines. Used for order cancellation analysis.']
  colorway_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific color variant of the product ordered. Used for inventory and fulfillment tracking.']
  confirmed_ship_date date [note: 'The date on which the business has confirmed it can ship this line item. May differ from requested date based on ATP availability.']
  cost_of_goods_sold decimal(18,4) [note: 'The standard or actual cost of the goods sold on this line item. Used for margin calculation and profitability analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this order line record was first created in the system. Represents the initial order capture event.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this line. Enables multi-currency order processing.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total monetary discount applied to this line item. Calculated from discount percentage or promotional pricing rules.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied to the unit price for this line item. Used for promotional pricing and markdown tracking.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The category or reason for the discount applied. Used for promotional effectiveness analysis and margin tracking. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promotional|seasonal|volume|employee|wholesale|markdown|clearance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  imu_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The initial markup percentage for this line item, calculated as (unit_price - COGS) / unit_price. Key metric for pricing strategy and profitability.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the sales order. Determines the ordering and display sequence of line items within the parent order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current fulfillment status of this order line. Tracks the line through its lifecycle from order entry to delivery or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: open|confirmed|allocated|picked|packed|shipped|delivered|cancelled|backordered — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  line_subtotal decimal(18,4) [note: 'The extended price for this line item before tax. Calculated as (unit_price * quantity_ordered) - discount_amount.']
  line_total decimal(18,4) [note: 'The final total amount for this line item including all discounts and taxes. Represents the customer\'s payment obligation for this line.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this order line record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to any field on this line.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'The manufacturer\'s suggested retail price for this item. Used for markdown calculation and pricing analytics.']
  order_source varchar [note: 'The channel or system through which this order line was captured. Enables channel-specific analytics and fulfillment routing.']
  product_code varchar [note: 'Business-readable product identifier or style number for the item ordered. Used for display and reporting purposes.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the product ordered on this line. Includes style name and key attributes for business user recognition.']
  quantity_backordered decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units on backorder due to insufficient inventory at time of order processing. Awaiting future stock availability.']
  quantity_cancelled decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units cancelled from this line item, either by customer request or due to inability to fulfill.']
  quantity_confirmed decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units confirmed for fulfillment after ATP (Available to Promise) check. May differ from quantity ordered due to inventory constraints.']
  quantity_ordered decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units requested by the customer for this line item. Represents the original order request before any adjustments.']
  quantity_shipped decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of units shipped to the customer for this line item. Updated as shipments are processed.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the customer has requested delivery of this line item. Critical for customer satisfaction and logistics planning.']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'The date by which the customer has requested this line item to be shipped. Used for fulfillment prioritization and OTIF tracking.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for return if this line item was returned by the customer. Used for return merchandise authorization (RMA) processing and quality analysis.']
  size_code varchar [note: 'Size designation for the product ordered (e.g., S, M, L, XL, or numeric sizes). Critical for fulfillment and inventory allocation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total tax amount applied to this line item. Calculated based on applicable tax rates and jurisdiction rules.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the quantity is measured. Common values: EA (Each), PR (Pair), DZ (Dozen), CS (Case), PK (Pack).']
  unit_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The selling price per unit for this line item before any discounts or adjustments. Represents the base transaction price.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit barcode identifier for the specific SKU. Used for point-of-sale scanning and inventory tracking.']

  Note: 'Individual line-item detail within a sales order, representing a specific SKU (style/colorway/size) and quantity requested. Captures product reference, quantity ordered, quantity confirmed, unit price, MSRP, applied discount (amount, type, source), IMU percentage, line status, requested ship date, cancel date, and ATP confirmation flag. Supports both single-unit DTC orders and multi-line wholesale EDI orders with size-curve breakdowns. Each line drives downstream allocation, fulfillment, and backorder processing.'
}

Table order.order_purchase_order {
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the purchase order record in the lakehouse.']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: PO-level upstream transportation emissions (scope 3 category 4: upstream transportation and distribution) tracked for supplier carbon accounting, OTIF carbon intensity metrics, and modal shift decisio']
  destination_location_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the receiving warehouse, distribution center, or store where goods will be delivered. Used for inventory allocation and receiving planning.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity or business unit placing the purchase order. May represent corporate headquarters, regional office, or individual store depending on procurement model.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Purchase orders are planned and tracked by merchandise hierarchy (division/department/class). Essential for category-level procurement reporting, compliance with category budgets, and merchandise plan']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: Purchase orders consume open-to-buy budget. Fundamental to OTB management, budget tracking, and preventing over-commitment. Merchandisers must track PO value against available OTB by category and peri']
  primary_order_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor facility or origin location from which goods will ship. Used for logistics planning and landed cost calculation.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Import purchase orders require regulatory filings for customs entry, import permits, and textile quota documentation. Mandatory for international sourcing compliance and customs clearance in apparel i']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandising season or collection for which goods are being purchased. Used for assortment planning and markdown management. Nullable for non-seasonal or replenishment orders.']
  trade_compliance_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_record. Business justification: Import POs require trade compliance validation for HS code classification, duty rates, country of origin determination, and trade agreement eligibility. Customs clearance and landed cost calculation d']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor fulfilling this purchase order. Links to supplier master data.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Actual date goods were received at destination. Used to calculate OTIF performance. Nullable until goods receipt posted.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status. Pending indicates awaiting authorization, approved indicates all required approvals obtained, rejected indicates approval denied. Approval may be required based on PO value thresholds or buyer authority limits.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who provided final approval for this purchase order. Nullable if approval not required or still pending.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when final approval was granted. Nullable if approval not required or still pending.']
  confirmed_delivery_date date [note: 'Vendor-confirmed delivery date. May differ from requested date based on vendor capacity and lead time. Nullable until vendor acknowledgment received.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts on this purchase order.']
  edi_acknowledgment_received_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vendor has sent EDI 855 Purchase Order Acknowledgment confirming receipt and acceptance of the PO. True if acknowledgment received, False if pending or not applicable.']
  edi_transmission_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order was transmitted to vendor via EDI (True) or through manual/email channel (False). Used for process automation tracking.']
  fob_terms varchar [note: 'Specific FOB terms detailing the point at which ownership and risk transfer from vendor to buyer. May include location details beyond standard Incoterms.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual freight and logistics cost for this shipment. May be buyer or vendor responsibility depending on Incoterms. Expressed in currency_code.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Standard codes per ICC Incoterms 2020. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lc_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number for letter of credit securing payment for this purchase order. Used for international trade finance. Nullable for domestic or non-LC transactions.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this purchase order record. Audit trail for change accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this purchase order record was last modified. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  moq_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order meets the vendor\'s minimum order quantity requirements. True if compliant, False if exception was granted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional instructions, special handling requirements, or internal comments related to this purchase order. May include packaging instructions, quality requirements, or delivery constraints.']
  otif_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this purchase order met OTIF performance targets. True if delivered on or before confirmed_delivery_date with full quantity, False otherwise. Nullable until delivery complete.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Code defining payment conditions including due date calculation, cash discount periods, and discount percentages. Examples: Net 30, 2/10 Net 30.']
  po_date date [note: 'Date the purchase order was issued to the vendor. Principal business event timestamp for the transaction.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique purchase order number issued to vendor. Business identifier used in EDI transactions and vendor communications.']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order. Tracks progression from draft through vendor acknowledgment, production, shipment, receipt, and closure. Cancelled indicates order was terminated before completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|acknowledged|in_production|shipped|received|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of purchase order by business purpose: replenishment (NOS restocking, VMI, min-max), wholesale (bulk buying for resale), drop-ship (direct-to-customer fulfillment), demand-driven (responsive to actual demand signal), consignment (vendor-owned inventory), or sample (pre-production or marketing samples).']
  priority_code varchar [note: 'Urgency classification for this purchase order. Rush and critical orders may incur premium freight or vendor surcharges. Used for vendor capacity allocation and logistics planning.']
  purchasing_group varchar [note: 'Code identifying the buyer or buying team responsible for this PO. Used for workload distribution and performance tracking.']
  purchasing_organization varchar [note: 'Code identifying the organizational unit responsible for procurement. May represent centralized or decentralized buying structure.']
  replenishment_trigger varchar [note: 'Business rule or event that triggered creation of this replenishment purchase order. Reorder_point indicates stock fell below threshold, wos_target indicates weeks-of-supply planning, vmi_signal indicates vendor-managed inventory auto-replenishment, manual indicates buyer-initiated, forecast_driven indicates demand planning system, promotion indicates event-based buying.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the buyer requests delivery of goods. Used for OTIF tracking and supply chain planning.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applicable to this purchase order. May include VAT, GST, customs duties depending on jurisdiction and Incoterms. Expressed in currency_code.']
  total_landed_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost to land goods at destination including PO value, taxes, freight, duties, and other charges. Used for inventory valuation and profitability analysis. Expressed in currency_code.']
  total_po_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the purchase order including all line items, before taxes and freight. Expressed in currency_code.']
  vendor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary vendor contact for this purchase order. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary vendor contact person for this purchase order. Used for order coordination and issue resolution.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this purchase order record. Audit trail for accountability.']

  Note: 'Purchase orders issued for replenishment (NOS restocking, VMI, min-max), wholesale buying, and drop-ship fulfillment. Captures PO number, buyer entity, vendor/supplier reference, PO type (replenishment, wholesale, drop-ship, demand-driven), FOB terms, LC reference, MOQ compliance flag, total PO value, currency, incoterms, expected delivery date, replenishment trigger (reorder point, WOS target), source/destination locations, and PO status. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM and supports OTIF tracking for inbound goods flow. Consolidates all inbound demand order types into a single entity differentiated by PO type.'
}

Table order.order_purchase_order_line {
  order_purchase_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for purchase_order_line']
  buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Line-level buyer assignment enables split PO responsibilities across categories/vendors; critical for workload distribution, commission calculation, and vendor relationship management. Buyer_name is d']
  ftc_label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ftc_label. Business justification: PO lines must specify required FTC labeling for sourced products. Procurement specification ensuring vendors produce goods with compliant fiber content, care, and origin labeling per FTC regulations.']
  material_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.material_certification. Business justification: PO lines require certification validation (GOTS, GRS, OEKO-TEX, BCI) for regulatory compliance, brand standards, and customer claims. Apparel sourcing teams verify certifications at PO line level befo']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent purchase order header. Links this line to the overall PO document.']
