  Note: 'Pre-order quotation or proforma record for wholesale seasonal booking, capturing buyer interest during market week and trade shows before conversion to confirmed sales orders. Tracks quote number, wholesale account reference, season/delivery window, quoted styles and quantities, preliminary pricing (FOB/wholesale cost), quote revision history, validity period, buyer approval status, and conversion-to-order flag. Supports the 4-6 month pre-season booking cycle critical to apparel wholesale where buyers commit to seasonal assortments that later convert to firm EDI 850 purchase orders. Enables pipeline visibility, production planning inputs, and seasonal revenue forecasting.'
}

Table order.allocation_rule {
  allocation_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for allocation_rule']
  fallback_allocation_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on allocation_rule (fallback_allocation_rule_id)']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'The foundational metric or data source used to drive allocation decisions (inventory levels, demand signals, capacity constraints, historical sales, forecast, manual override).']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available inventory or capacity to allocate when using proportional allocation logic (0.00 to 100.00).']
  allocation_scope varchar [note: 'The organizational or geographic scope to which this allocation rule applies (global, regional, country-level, channel-specific, store-level, warehouse, customer segment).']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allocations generated by this rule require manual approval before execution (true = approval required, false = auto-execute).']
  backorder_handling varchar [note: 'Defines how this allocation rule handles backorder scenarios (allow backorders, prevent backorders, allow partial backorders).']
  channel_filter varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) to which this allocation rule applies (Direct-to-Consumer, wholesale, retail stores, e-commerce, marketplace, or all channels).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation rule record was first created in the system.']
  customer_tier_filter varchar [note: 'Customer tier or segment classification to which this allocation rule applies. Null indicates rule applies to all tiers.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this allocation rule expires and is no longer applied. Null indicates rule is open-ended.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this allocation rule becomes active and begins to be applied in allocation processing.']
  execution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which this allocation rule is scheduled to execute (real-time, hourly, daily, weekly, on-demand).']
  external_rule_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for this allocation rule in the source system of record, used for integration and reconciliation.']
  geographic_region_filter varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) to which this allocation rule applies. Null indicates rule applies globally.']
  last_execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation rule was last executed in an allocation run.']
  maximum_allocation_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity cap that can be allocated under this rule. Used to prevent over-concentration of inventory.']
  minimum_allocation_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum quantity threshold that must be allocated when this rule is applied. Prevents fractional or uneconomical allocations.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last modified this allocation rule record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation rule record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this allocation rule.']
  override_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether users are permitted to manually override allocations generated by this rule (true = overrides allowed, false = system-enforced).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for rule execution when multiple rules apply (lower number = higher priority).']
  product_category_filter varchar [note: 'Product category or categories to which this allocation rule applies. Null indicates rule applies to all categories.']
  rounding_method varchar [note: 'Method for rounding fractional allocation quantities (round up, round down, round to nearest, or no rounding).']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the allocation rule, used for external reference and integration.']
  rule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the allocation rule logic, business purpose, and application context.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the allocation rule describing its purpose.']
  rule_owner varchar [note: 'Business owner or team responsible for maintaining and governing this allocation rule.']
  rule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation rule (active and in use, inactive, draft/pending approval, temporarily suspended, archived).']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation rule methodology (proportional distribution, priority-based, round-robin, geographic, channel-based, customer tier, product category).']
  rule_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this allocation rule, incremented with each modification to support change tracking and audit.']
  safety_stock_reserve_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of inventory to reserve as safety stock and exclude from allocation calculations (0.00 to 100.00).']
  system_of_record varchar [note: 'Source system where this allocation rule is defined and maintained (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, OMS, WMS).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that created this allocation rule record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for allocation_rule. Referenced by allocation_rule_id.'
}

Table order.trading_partner {
  trading_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for trading_partner']
  parent_trading_partner_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on trading_partner (parent_trading_partner_id)']
  account_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal account manager responsible for this trading partner relationship.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the trading partner\'s primary business address including street number and name.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the trading partner\'s address for suite, building, or additional location details.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the trading partner\'s primary business location.']
  compliance_certification_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trading partner has provided required compliance certifications (e.g., social compliance, environmental standards).']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the trading partner\'s primary business location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trading partner record was first created in the system.']
  credit_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum credit amount extended to the trading partner for outstanding orders and invoices.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for transactions with this trading partner.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for business entity identification.']
  edi_capability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the trading partner has EDI integration capability for automated order processing.']
  edi_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique EDI identifier or interchange ID used to route electronic transactions to the trading partner.']
  edi_standard varchar [note: 'The EDI messaging standard used by the trading partner for electronic transactions.']
  gln varchar [note: 'GS1 standard 13-digit identifier for physical locations and legal entities used in EDI and supply chain transactions.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Standard Incoterms code defining delivery responsibilities and risk transfer for shipments.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the trading partner record was last updated in the system.']
  last_order_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent order placed with or by this trading partner.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from order placement to expected delivery for this trading partner.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum order quantity required for orders placed with or by this trading partner.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special instructions, or comments about the trading partner.']
  otif_target_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage for on-time and in-full delivery performance agreed with the trading partner.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the trading partner used in EDI transactions and system integrations.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the trading partner organization.']
  partner_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the trading partner relationship.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trading partner based on their business relationship and role in the supply chain.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the trading partner (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 2/10 Net 30).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the trading partner\'s primary business address.']
  preferred_carrier varchar [note: 'Name of the preferred shipping carrier for deliveries to this trading partner.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact for order management and operational communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact person at the trading partner organization.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the trading partner business contact.']
  relationship_end_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with the trading partner was terminated or is scheduled to end.']
  relationship_start_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with the trading partner was established.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Business risk assessment rating for the trading partner based on credit, compliance, and operational factors.']
  ship_to_location_count varchar [note: 'Number of active ship-to locations associated with this trading partner.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the trading partner\'s primary business location.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identification number for the trading partner (e.g., EIN, VAT number).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for trading_partner. Referenced by trading_partner_id.'
}

Table order.installment_plan {
  installment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for installment_plan']
  order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order associated with this installment plan.']
  payment_method_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the default payment method used for automatic installment payments.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who owns this installment plan.']
  refinanced_installment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on installment_plan (refinanced_installment_plan_id)']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the installment plan was activated and became effective.']
  amount_outstanding decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining balance owed on the installment plan, including principal, interest, and fees.']
  amount_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount paid by the customer to date across all completed installments.']
  auto_pay_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automatic payment processing is enabled for installments. True if auto-pay is active, false if manual payments required.']
  cancellation_date date [note: 'Date when the installment plan was cancelled before completion.']
  completed_installments varchar [note: 'Number of installment payments that have been successfully completed and received.']
  completion_date date [note: 'Date when all installment payments were completed and the plan was closed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the installment plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in the installment plan (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  down_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial down payment amount paid at plan origination, if applicable. Zero if no down payment required.']
  down_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the down payment was received, if applicable.']
  early_payoff_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer is permitted to pay off the remaining balance early without penalty. True if early payoff is allowed, false if restricted.']
  early_payoff_penalty decimal(15,2) [note: 'Penalty fee charged if the customer pays off the installment plan before the scheduled final payment date. Zero if no penalty applies.']
  final_payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the final installment payment, representing plan maturity.']
  financing_partner varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party financing partner or BNPL provider facilitating the installment plan (e.g., Affirm, Klarna, Afterpay). Null if financed internally.']
  first_payment_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the first installment payment.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after the due date before a late fee is assessed or the payment is considered delinquent.']
  installment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard amount due for each regular installment payment. Final payment may differ.']
  interest_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to the installment plan, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0599 for 5.99% APR). Zero for interest-free plans.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard late fee charged when an installment payment is not received within the grace period.']
  missed_payments_count varchar [note: 'Total number of installment payments that were missed or not received by the due date.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the installment plan record was last modified or updated.']
  next_payment_date date [note: 'Date when the next installment payment is due.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the installment plan, including special arrangements, customer requests, or internal annotations.']
  partner_plan_code varchar [note: 'External identifier assigned by the financing partner for this installment plan, used for reconciliation and partner API integration.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which installment payments are scheduled: weekly, biweekly (every two weeks), monthly, quarterly, or custom interval.']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the installment plan, used in customer communications and billing statements.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the installment plan structure: equal installments (fixed periodic payments), deferred payment (single future payment), layaway (pay before delivery), buy now pay later (BNPL partner), or custom arrangement.']
  principal_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Original principal amount financed through the installment plan, excluding interest and fees.']
  remaining_installments varchar [note: 'Number of installment payments still outstanding in the plan.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the installment plan: draft (not yet activated), active (payments in progress), suspended (temporarily paused), completed (all payments received), defaulted (missed payments), cancelled (terminated before completion).']
  terms_accepted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer accepted the installment plan terms and conditions.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terms and conditions document accepted by the customer at plan origination.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount to be paid over the life of the installment plan, including principal, interest, and fees.']
  total_fees decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total administrative, processing, or late fees associated with the installment plan.']
  total_installments varchar [note: 'Total number of scheduled installment payments in the plan.']
  total_interest decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total interest charges to be paid over the life of the installment plan.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for installment_plan. Referenced by installment_plan_id.'
}

Table inventory.stock_position {
  stock_position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock position record. Primary key for the stock position entity.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.digital_storefront. Business justification: Stock positions are allocated to specific digital storefronts for online selling. Business process: real-time ATP calculation per storefront, regional inventory pooling, and channel-specific stock vis']
  inventory_pool_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_pool. Business justification: Stock positions should be assigned to inventory pools for allocation management and omnichannel fulfillment. Inventory pools represent logical groupings of stock (e.g., retail pool, wholesale pool, ec']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: Stock positions are planned and monitored against OTB budgets by merchandise category/season. Essential for OTB compliance monitoring, variance reporting, and inventory financial planning in apparel m']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Store-level stock positions are fundamental to retail inventory management - tracks on-hand, ATS, ATP quantities by store for BOPIS fulfillment, ship-from-store allocation, store replenishment plannin']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Inventory is inherently seasonal in apparel fashion. Stock positions must track seasonal merchandise for seasonal inventory planning, end-of-season clearance decisions, and sell-through analysis by se']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Stock valuation, aging analysis, and markdown planning require product lifecycle_stage, cost_of_goods_sold, category, and sustainability attributes from product master. Current string \'sku\' field insu']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Stock positions must track material sustainability attributes (organic, recycled content, certifications) for ESG reporting, customer transparency labels, and sustainable inventory KPIs. Standard prac']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the physical location where inventory is held. References warehouse, distribution center (DC), retail store, or third-party logistics (3PL) facility.']
  aging_bucket varchar [note: 'Classification of inventory based on time in stock and seasonal relevance. Current season represents fresh merchandise, prior season is last season carryover, aged is multi-season old inventory, and clearance is marked for liquidation. Drives markdown strategies and inventory valuation.']
  allocation_group varchar [note: 'Classification code used to group inventory for allocation planning. Allocation groups define how inventory is distributed across channels and locations based on demand forecasts, store grades, and strategic priorities.']
  atp_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity available for future customer commitments, including current ATS plus expected inbound receipts minus planned outbound allocations. Used for forward order promising and pre-order management.']
  ats_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity available for new customer orders. Calculated as on-hand quantity minus reserved quantity minus damaged/held quantity. This is the inventory pool visible to e-commerce and point-of-sale systems for order promising.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating where the product was manufactured. Required for customs compliance, trade agreement qualification, and consumer labeling per FTC textile labeling requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this stock position record was first created in the data warehouse. Used for data lineage tracking and audit compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost. Supports multi-currency inventory valuation for global operations.']
  cycle_count_variance decimal(15,3) [note: 'Difference between system on-hand quantity and physical count quantity from the last cycle count. Positive values indicate physical surplus, negative values indicate shortage. Used to measure inventory accuracy and identify shrinkage.']
  damaged_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory identified as damaged, defective, or unsellable. This inventory is physically present but excluded from available-to-sell calculations pending disposition decisions (repair, return to vendor, or disposal).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the inventory should not be sold or used. Applicable to items with shelf life such as cosmetics, fragrances, or performance fabrics with time-sensitive treatments. Null for non-perishable apparel.']
  held_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity placed on administrative hold for quality inspection, regulatory compliance review, or investigation. Inventory is physically present but temporarily unavailable for sale or allocation.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'International tariff classification code for the product. Used for customs clearance, duty calculation, and trade compliance. Essential for cross-border inventory movements and landed cost calculation.']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity currently being transported to this location from suppliers, manufacturing facilities, or other distribution points. Includes goods in-transit via ocean freight, air freight, and ground transportation.']
  last_adjustment_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inventory adjustment transaction at this location. Adjustments include cycle count corrections, damage write-offs, and system corrections. Used for audit trail and inventory accuracy monitoring.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical cycle count performed for this SKU at this location. Supports inventory accuracy monitoring and audit compliance.']
  last_receipt_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inventory receipt at this location. Used to track inventory freshness and identify slow-moving stock.']
  last_sale_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent sale or allocation of this SKU from this location. Used to identify dormant inventory and trigger markdown or transfer decisions.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch identifier for traceability. Enables tracking of inventory by production run for quality control, recall management, and compliance with textile safety standards.']
  nos_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is designated as a never-out-of-stock item requiring continuous availability. NOS items receive priority replenishment and higher safety stock levels to prevent stockouts.']
  on_hand_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total physical inventory quantity currently present at the location. Represents the actual count of units physically available in the facility, verified through cycle counts and RFID tracking.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the legal ownership of the inventory. Owned is company-owned stock, consignment is supplier-owned until sold, vendor-owned is VMI stock, and customer-owned is inventory held on behalf of wholesale customers.']
  reorder_point decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory level that triggers automatic replenishment. When on-hand quantity falls below the reorder point, a purchase order or transfer order is generated. Calculated as lead time demand plus safety stock.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Strategy used to replenish inventory at this location. Automatic uses system-driven reorder points, manual requires planner intervention, VMI is supplier-managed, cross-dock bypasses storage, and drop-ship ships directly from supplier to customer.']
  reserved_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory allocated to specific orders or commitments but not yet shipped. Includes customer orders, store allocations, and wholesale commitments that are pending fulfillment.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU at this location is tracked using RFID technology. RFID enables real-time inventory visibility, automated cycle counting, and improved inventory accuracy.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum inventory buffer maintained to protect against demand variability and supply chain disruptions. Safety stock levels are calculated based on demand volatility, lead time variability, and desired service level.']
  snapshot_date date [note: 'Business date for which this stock position record represents the inventory state. Enables point-in-time inventory analysis and historical trend reporting. Updated daily through batch processing.']
  stock_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the inventory at this location. Available indicates sellable stock, reserved is allocated to orders, quarantine is under quality review, damaged is unsellable, in-transit is being received, and blocked is administratively restricted.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of inventory at this location. Represents the landed cost including product cost, freight, duties, and other acquisition costs. Used for inventory valuation and COGS calculation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this stock position record was last modified. Tracks the most recent change to any attribute in the record for change data capture and audit purposes.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Financial accounting classification for inventory valuation. Determines the general ledger accounts used for inventory asset and cost of goods sold postings. Supports multi-GAAP reporting requirements.']
  vmi_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory is managed under a vendor-managed inventory program where the supplier monitors stock levels and triggers replenishment. VMI programs shift inventory management responsibility to suppliers.']
  wos decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of weeks the current on-hand inventory will last based on forecasted demand. Calculated as on-hand quantity divided by average weekly sales rate. Key metric for inventory health and replenishment planning.']

  Note: 'Core master record of current stock levels for each SKU across all locations (warehouses, DCs, retail stores, 3PL facilities). Tracks on-hand quantity, reserved quantity, ATS (Available to Sell), ATP (Available to Promise), in-transit quantity, damaged/held quantity, WOS (Weeks of Supply), and aging bucket (current season, prior season, aged). Serves as the real-time inventory position SSOT feeding omnichannel fulfillment, allocation engines, markdown triggers, and financial reporting.'
}

Business justification: Production-triggered inventory movements (finished goods receipts, component issues, production returns) must reference originating work order for cost accounting, variance analysis, and production-to']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the movement required managerial approval and the current approval state. Critical for adjustments, write-offs, and high-value movements.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the movement was approved. Part of the audit trail for compliance and internal controls.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved the movement transaction. Required for audit trail on adjustments and write-offs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this stock movement record was first created in the database. Part of the audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the cost amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Enables multi-currency inventory valuation for global operations.']
  destination_location_code varchar [note: 'Business code for the destination location (e.g., warehouse code, store number, DC identifier). Human-readable identifier for reporting.']
  destination_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination location to enable location-type-specific analytics and reporting.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the source document that triggered the movement (e.g., PO date, transfer order date, physical count date). May differ from posting date due to processing delays.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this stock movement record was last updated. Tracks changes for audit and data quality monitoring.']
  movement_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the movement transaction type for reporting and analytics. Groups detailed movement types into business-meaningful categories.']
  movement_document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known business document number for this inventory movement (e.g., goods receipt number, transfer order number, adjustment document number). Used for audit trail and cross-system reconciliation.']
  movement_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of inventory moved in this transaction. Positive for receipts and negative for issues. Expressed in the base unit of measure.']
  movement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the stock movement transaction. Indicates whether the movement has been finalized in inventory records or is still in process.']
  movement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the physical inventory movement occurred or was recorded in the warehouse management system. Used for real-time inventory tracking and RFID event correlation.']
  movement_type_code varchar [note: 'Three-digit code classifying the nature of the inventory movement (e.g., 101=Goods Receipt from PO, 311=Transfer Posting, 551=Scrapping, 601=Goods Issue for Delivery). Aligns with SAP movement type taxonomy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about the movement. Used by warehouse staff and inventory analysts.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The business date on which the inventory movement was posted to the ledger and inventory balances were updated. This is the official date of record for inventory valuation and financial reporting.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for the movement, especially for adjustments, returns, and write-offs (e.g., damaged, obsolete, cycle count correction, customer return, quality hold release).']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context for the movement reason. Used when reason code alone is insufficient to explain the transaction.']
  reference_document_line_number varchar [note: 'The line item number within the reference document that this movement relates to. Enables line-level traceability.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The business document number of the source transaction (e.g., PO number, sales order number, STO number, RTV number, ASN number). Used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  reference_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of source document that triggered or authorized this inventory movement. Enables traceability back to originating business transaction. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: purchase_order|sales_order|transfer_order|return_authorization|adjustment_request|delivery_note|asn — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this movement is a reversal of a previous transaction. True if this is a correcting entry that negates a prior movement.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The movement document number of the original transaction being reversed. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true. Enables audit trail of corrections.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized inventory items (e.g., high-value accessories, luxury goods). Enables item-level tracking and authentication.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The unique product identifier for the item being moved. Represents the specific product variant at the most granular level (style-color-size).']
  source_location_code varchar [note: 'Business code for the source location (e.g., warehouse code, store number, DC identifier). Human-readable identifier for reporting.']
  source_location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the source location to enable location-type-specific analytics and reporting.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'Classification of inventory status indicating availability and ownership. Unrestricted stock is available for sale; other types have restrictions or special handling requirements.']
  total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total cost of the movement (movement_quantity × unit_cost). Posted to inventory accounts and used for financial reporting and COGS calculation.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'The cost per unit of the inventory item at the time of movement. Used for inventory valuation and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation. Expressed in the company\'s functional currency.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the movement quantity is expressed (EA=Each, CS=Case, PK=Pack, DZ=Dozen, PR=Pair, ST=Set). Aligns with product packaging hierarchy.']
  upc varchar [note: 'The barcode identifier for the product. Used for scanning and point-of-sale transactions. May be GTIN-12, GTIN-13, or GTIN-14 format.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'The inventory valuation method applied to this movement for financial accounting purposes. Determines how cost is calculated and posted to the general ledger.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every inventory movement event including goods receipts, goods issues, inter-location transfers, stock adjustments, returns, write-offs, and corrections. Captures movement type, quantity, unit of measure, source and destination locations, reference document (PO, STO, RTV, delivery note, ASN), posting date, movement reason code, and adjustment approval reference. Serves as the single audit trail for all inventory quantity changes across all channels and locations.'
}

Table inventory.warehouse_location {
  warehouse_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the warehouse location record. Primary key for the warehouse location entity.']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent facility (distribution center, warehouse, retail store, 3PL site, bonded warehouse, or cross-dock) where this location resides.']
  energy_consumption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.energy_consumption. Business justification: Warehouse locations roll up to facilities for scope 1/2 emissions reporting. Location-level energy tracking enables zone-specific efficiency programs, renewable energy allocation, and granular carbon ']
  facility_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Warehouse facilities require designated facility managers responsible for operations, safety compliance, labor scheduling, and performance. Essential for operational accountability, incident reporting']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: 3PL-operated warehouse locations. Linking enables 3PL space utilization analysis, billing validation (storage charges), capacity planning, and location master data management—standard for apparel bran']
  waste_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.waste_record. Business justification: Waste generation tracked by warehouse location for zero-waste-to-landfill (ZWTL) programs, circular economy initiatives, and facility-level diversion rate reporting. Location-level data enables target']
  water_usage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.water_usage. Business justification: Facility water consumption tracking requires location-level granularity for operational efficiency, water stewardship programs, and ESG disclosure. Warehouse locations aggregate to facility-level wate']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.zone. Business justification: Warehouse locations currently have zone_code (STRING) but should FK to the zone master table for referential integrity. The zone_code string should be removed and replaced with zone_id FK, allowing pr']
  aisle_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the physical aisle where the location resides. Used for travel optimization and picker routing.']
  bin_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the individual bin or position within the shelf. Represents the most granular storage unit.']
  bonded_warehouse_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is within a customs bonded area where imported goods can be stored without paying duties until they are released for domestic consumption.']
  capacity_cubic_meters decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum cubic volume (in cubic meters) that can be stored in this location. Used for dimensional slotting and space utilization analysis.']
  capacity_units decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum number of inventory units (eaches, cases, pallets) that can be stored in this location. Used for slotting optimization and capacity planning.']
  capacity_weight_kg decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity (in kilograms) for this location. Used to ensure safe storage and compliance with structural load limits.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warehouse location record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  cycle_count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled cycle counts for this location. High-value or high-velocity locations typically have shorter frequencies.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this warehouse location was deactivated or retired from operations. Null for currently active locations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this warehouse location became active and available for inventory operations. Supports historical tracking of location lifecycle.']
  hazmat_approved_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is certified and approved for storing hazardous materials (e.g., certain dyes, chemicals, aerosols) in compliance with safety regulations.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent cycle count was performed for this location. Used to schedule next cycle count and track inventory accuracy.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this warehouse location record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier for the warehouse location within the facility. Used for directed putaway, picking, and cycle count operations. Typically follows facility-specific naming conventions (e.g., A-01-02-03 for Aisle-Rack-Shelf-Bin).']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the warehouse location for operational reference and reporting.']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the warehouse location. Determines whether the location is available for putaway, picking, or other warehouse operations.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the warehouse location by its operational purpose. Determines handling rules, slotting strategies, and fulfillment routing logic. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: bin|shelf|rack|aisle|zone|staging|dock|receiving|shipping|quarantine|returns|bulk|reserve|forward_pick|cross_dock|bonded|stockroom — 17 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  mixed_lot_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether inventory from different lots or batches of the same SKU can be stored together in this location. Important for lot traceability and FIFO compliance.']
  mixed_sku_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether multiple different SKUs can be stored in this location simultaneously. Forward pick locations typically allow mixed SKUs while reserve locations may be single-SKU.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for operational notes, special handling instructions, or location-specific comments. Used by warehouse staff for operational context.']
  pick_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric sequence used to optimize picker travel paths during order fulfillment. Lower numbers are visited first in the pick route.']
  putaway_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for directed putaway operations. Higher priority locations are filled first during receiving and replenishment.']
  rack_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the rack or bay within the aisle. Part of the hierarchical location structure.']
  replenishment_trigger_level decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory quantity threshold that triggers automatic replenishment from reserve to forward pick locations. Supports Never Out of Stock (NOS) strategies.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is equipped with RFID readers for automated inventory tracking and real-time visibility.']
  shelf_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the shelf or level within the rack. Part of the hierarchical location structure.']
  temperature_zone varchar [note: 'Temperature control classification for the location. Critical for storing temperature-sensitive apparel materials, adhesives, or specialty items.']
  vendor_managed_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated for Vendor Managed Inventory programs where suppliers own and manage inventory until point of sale or consumption.']
  x_coordinate decimal(10,3) [note: 'Horizontal coordinate (X-axis) of the location within the facility floor plan. Used for travel distance calculations and warehouse mapping.']
  y_coordinate decimal(10,3) [note: 'Vertical coordinate (Y-axis) of the location within the facility floor plan. Used for travel distance calculations and warehouse mapping.']
  z_coordinate decimal(10,3) [note: 'Height coordinate (Z-axis) of the location representing vertical position or level. Used for multi-level warehouse operations and automated storage systems.']

  Note: 'Hierarchical master record of all inventory storage locations from facility level down to individual bin positions. Encompasses facilities (owned DCs, 3PL sites, retail stockrooms, bonded warehouses, cross-docks), zones, aisles, racks, shelves, and bins. Captures location type, capacity (units and cubic), temperature zone, RFID capability, customs bonded status, zone assignment, and operational status. Supports directed putaway, slotting optimization, and omnichannel fulfillment routing.'
}

Table inventory.sku_location {
  sku_location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the SKU-location association record. Primary key for the sku_location entity.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.digital_storefront. Business justification: SKU-location assignments designate which warehouse locations fulfill which digital storefronts (e.g., US DC serves US site). Business process: fulfillment routing, ship-from logic, and regional invent']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse, distribution center, retail store, or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) facility where the SKU is located.']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: Location-level inventory planning is tied to OTB budgets by category. Essential for store-level OTB allocation, inventory financial planning, and ensuring location inventory aligns with merchandise bu']
  packaging_sustainability_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.packaging_sustainability. Business justification: Location-specific packaging requirements vary by market (EU recyclability mandates vs. US standards). SKU-location assignments must link to applicable packaging sustainability profiles for compliance ']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Store-level SKU slotting and location assignments (sales floor zones, stockroom bins) require linking to specific retail stores for planogram compliance audits, visual merchandising execution, and sto']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: SKU-location assignments are seasonal in apparel retail; floor sets change by season. Critical for seasonal floor set planning, seasonal assortment execution, and seasonal inventory allocation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU assigned to this location. Links to the product master data.']
  vmi_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor responsible for managing inventory replenishment under the VMI program for this SKU-location.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: sku_location associates SKUs with storage locations but currently uses facility_id and location_code strings. Adding warehouse_location_id FK provides a structured link to the hierarchical warehouse_l']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.zone. Business justification: SKU location assignments currently have zone_code (STRING) but should FK to the zone master table for referential integrity. The zone_code string should be removed and replaced with zone_id FK, enabli']
  allocated_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory at this location that has been allocated to specific customer orders but not yet picked or shipped.']
  assignment_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the SKU-location assignment became or will become effective. Used for planning seasonal slotting changes and location transitions.']
  assignment_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the SKU-location assignment expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null for permanent assignments.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the SKU-location assignment indicating whether the assignment is active and operational or in another state.']
  atp_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Available to Promise quantity at this location. Includes current ATS plus expected receipts minus committed orders, used for order promising logic.']
  ats_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Available to Sell quantity at this location. Represents inventory available for customer orders after deducting allocated, reserved, and quality hold quantities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SKU-location assignment record was first created in the system.']
  current_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Current on-hand inventory quantity of the SKU at this specific location. Real-time stock level updated by warehouse transactions.']
  cycle_count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Frequency in days for conducting cycle counts of this SKU at this location. High-value or high-velocity items typically have shorter cycle count intervals.']
  damaged_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory at this location that has been identified as damaged and is pending disposition (return to vendor, disposal, or salvage).']
  in_transit_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory in transit to this location from suppliers, other warehouses, or distribution centers. Expected to arrive and increase available stock.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent cycle count was performed for this SKU at this location.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the SKU-location assignment record was most recently modified.']
  location_capacity_units decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum capacity of the storage location expressed in inventory units (e.g., number of units, cases, or pallets that can be stored).']
  location_capacity_volume_cubic_meters decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum volumetric capacity of the storage location expressed in cubic meters. Used for space utilization and slotting optimization.']
  location_capacity_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Maximum weight capacity of the storage location expressed in kilograms. Ensures safe loading and compliance with facility structural limits.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the storage location type indicating its operational purpose within the warehouse or facility.']
  max_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum inventory quantity that should be stored at this location. Defines the upper limit for replenishment to prevent overstocking.']
  min_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum inventory quantity that should be maintained at this location to trigger replenishment. Part of min-max inventory control strategy.']
  next_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next cycle count of this SKU at this location based on the cycle count frequency.']
  nos_classification_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier indicating the priority level for NOS (Never Out of Stock) strategy. Tier 1 represents highest priority items that must always be in stock.']
  nos_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific NOS policy applied to this SKU-location combination, defining replenishment rules, safety stock levels, and escalation procedures.']
  overflow_location_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the overflow or secondary storage location for this SKU when primary location capacity is exceeded.']
  pick_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequence number defining the order in which this location should be picked during wave picking or batch picking operations to optimize picker travel path.']
  primary_pick_location_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this location is designated as the primary pick location for the SKU. True if this is the first location pickers should retrieve inventory from.']
  quarantine_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory at this location that is held in quarantine pending quality inspection, compliance verification, or issue resolution.']
  reorder_point_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Inventory level at which replenishment should be triggered for this SKU at this location. When ATS (Available to Sell) falls below this threshold, a replenishment order is generated.']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to replenish inventory at this location from the time a replenishment order is triggered until goods are received and available.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to trigger and calculate replenishment for this SKU at this location (e.g., min-max, reorder point, periodic review, demand-driven, VMI).']
  reserved_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of inventory at this location that has been reserved for specific purposes (e.g., promotional events, wholesale orders, store transfers) and is not available for general sale.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tracking is enabled for this SKU at this location for real-time inventory visibility and automated cycle counting.']
  safety_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Buffer inventory quantity maintained at this location to protect against demand variability and supply chain disruptions. Part of NOS (Never Out of Stock) strategy.']
  slotting_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numerical rank indicating the priority of this SKU for optimal warehouse slotting. Lower numbers indicate higher priority for placement in prime pick locations.']
  vmi_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU-location is managed under a VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) program where the supplier is responsible for monitoring and replenishing inventory levels.']
  wos_current decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current WOS (Weeks of Supply) calculated as current stock quantity divided by average weekly demand. Indicates how many weeks the current inventory will last.']
  wos_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target WOS (Weeks of Supply) for this SKU at this location. Represents the desired number of weeks of inventory to maintain based on forecasted demand.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking SKUs to their assigned storage locations within a facility, capturing slotting rules, primary pick location, overflow location, min/max replenishment levels, reorder point, safety stock quantity, NOS (Never Out of Stock) classification tier and policy attributes, and VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) indicator. Drives replenishment triggers, NOS compliance monitoring, and omnichannel fulfillment positioning.'
}

Table inventory.inventory_cycle_count {
  inventory_cycle_count_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory cycle count event. Primary key for the cycle count record.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Cycle counts often need to track lot/batch-specific inventory (especially for dye-lot consistency in apparel). inventory_cycle_count has lot_number string but no FK to lot_batch master. Adding lot_bat']
  primary_inventory_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse associate or employee assigned to perform the physical count.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Store cycle counts must be attributed to specific retail stores for inventory accuracy KPIs, shrinkage reconciliation, and store operational performance tracking - critical for retail loss prevention ']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Cycle counts are often scheduled by season for seasonal merchandise, especially pre-season and end-of-season. Essential for seasonal inventory accuracy audits and seasonal inventory reconciliation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU being counted in this cycle count line. Links to product master for item details.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse, distribution center, retail store, or 3PL facility where the cycle count is being performed.']
  zone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific zone, aisle, or storage area within the location being counted. Enables zone-level cycle counting strategies.']
  abc_classification varchar [note: 'ABC analysis classification of the SKU determining cycle count frequency: A (high value, frequent counts), B (medium value, moderate counts), C (low value, infrequent counts).']
  adjustment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial value of the inventory adjustment calculated as variance quantity multiplied by unit cost. Impacts COGS and inventory valuation.']
  adjustment_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the inventory adjustment document posted to SAP or ERP system as a result of the approved variance.']
  adjustment_posted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that the variance has been posted to inventory records and financial ledgers. True when adjustment posted, False when pending.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cycle count was approved by the supervisor and cleared for adjustment posting.']
  approved_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Final quantity approved by supervisor or system after count validation and any required recounts. This quantity is posted to inventory records.']
  count_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the physical counting activity was completed and submitted for review.']
  count_method varchar [note: 'Technology or process used to perform the physical count: manual (visual count and paper), handheld_scanner (barcode scanning device), rfid_reader (RFID tag reading), automated_system (automated storage and retrieval system count).']
  count_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or observations recorded by the counter during the cycle count, documenting issues, discrepancies, or special conditions.']
  count_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the cycle count event, typically formatted as CC-YYYYMMDD-NNNNNN for external reference and tracking.']
  count_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason triggering the cycle count: scheduled (routine calendar-based), variance_triggered (system detected discrepancy), stock_adjustment (manual correction needed), audit (compliance or quality check), neveroutofstock (NOS item verification), receiving_discrepancy (inbound variance), returns_verification (RTV or customer return validation). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: scheduled|variance_triggered|stock_adjustment|audit|neveroutofstock|receiving_discrepancy|returns_verification — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  count_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the physical counting activity began, captured from WMS or handheld device.']
  count_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cycle count: planned (scheduled but not started), assigned (counter designated), in_progress (counting underway), completed (count finished pending review), approved (variance accepted and posted), rejected (count invalidated), recount_required (variance exceeds threshold). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|assigned|in_progress|completed|approved|rejected|recount_required — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  count_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cycle count methodology: full (complete location inventory), partial (sample-based), abc_class (frequency based on item value), rfid_assisted (RFID technology enabled), blind (no system quantity shown), targeted (specific SKU focus).']
  counted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual physical quantity counted by the assigned counter during the cycle count event.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cycle count record was first created in the system, typically during count planning or scheduling.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the adjustment amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  expected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'System-recorded quantity on hand for the SKU at the location prior to the physical count, sourced from perpetual inventory records.']
  inventory_status varchar [note: 'Quality and availability status of the counted inventory: available (ATS), reserved (allocated to orders), quarantine (quality hold), damaged (not sellable), expired (past shelf life), return_to_vendor (RTV candidate).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the cycle count record was last updated, tracking changes through the count lifecycle.']
  recount_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that the variance exceeds tolerance and a recount is required before posting adjustment. True when recount needed, False otherwise.']
  recount_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating how many times this item has been recounted. Zero for initial count, increments with each recount attempt.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the cycle count event to be executed, based on ABC classification frequency or calendar-driven schedule.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for serialized inventory items, enabling unit-level tracking for high-value or regulated products.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which quantities are expressed: EA (each), CS (case), PK (pack), DZ (dozen), PR (pair), ST (set). Must align with SKU base UOM.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variance quantity expressed as a percentage of expected quantity, used to determine if recount threshold is exceeded.']
  variance_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Calculated difference between expected quantity and counted quantity (counted minus expected). Positive indicates overage, negative indicates shortage.']
  variance_threshold_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Configured tolerance percentage for acceptable variance. Variances exceeding this threshold trigger recount or management approval requirements.']

  Note: 'Operational record of scheduled and ad-hoc physical inventory cycle count events at a location or zone level, including line-level SKU count details. Header captures count plan date, count type (full, partial, ABC-class, RFID-assisted), assigned counter, count status, and variance threshold. Line-level detail records expected quantity, counted quantity, variance, recount flag, and final approved quantity. Drives stock adjustment postings when variance exceeds tolerance.'
}

Table inventory.transfer_order {
  transfer_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the transfer order. Primary key for the transfer order entity.']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Inter-facility transfers generate scope 3 category 4 emissions (upstream transportation). Transfer orders must link to emission records for carbon accounting, SBTi reporting, and logistics optimizatio']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics carrier or third-party logistics provider responsible for transporting the inventory.']
  destination_location_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the receiving location to which inventory is being transferred (warehouse, distribution center, retail store, or third-party logistics facility).']
  freight_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_invoice. Business justification: Inter-facility transfers generate freight invoices. Linking enables transfer cost allocation, budget variance analysis, carrier rate validation, and network cost optimization—standard apparel distribu']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: Inter-location transfers may be planned against OTB budgets, especially for allocation transfers. Essential for allocation transfer planning and ensuring transfers align with merchandise financial pla']
  primary_transfer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or system process that initiated the transfer order.']
