  quality_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Inter-facility transfers may require quality verification inspections before shipment to ensure only conforming inventory moves between locations. Enforces quality gates in multi-echelon distribution ']
  routing_guide_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.routing_guide. Business justification: Transfers follow routing guides. Linking enables routing compliance measurement, freight budget accuracy, network optimization validation, and carrier selection audit—standard apparel distribution ope']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Transfers of seasonal merchandise must be tracked by season for seasonal allocation transfers, seasonal inventory balancing, and end-of-season clearance logistics.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Inter-facility transfers generate outbound shipments. Linking enables freight cost allocation to transfers, transit tracking, replenishment lead time analysis, and network optimization—core apparel di']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific product SKU being transferred. Links to the product master for detailed item information.']
  source_retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Inter-store transfers originate from source retail stores for stock balancing, allocation optimization, and ship-from-store fulfillment - requires tracking source store for inventory deduction, transf']
  tertiary_transfer_cancelled_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who cancelled the transfer order, if applicable.']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: 3PL-managed transfers. Linking enables 3PL fulfillment performance measurement, cost allocation, SLA compliance tracking, and service level validation—standard for apparel brands using 3PL distributio']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date the inventory was received at the destination location.']
  actual_freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The actual transportation cost incurred for the transfer order in the base currency.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'The actual date the inventory was shipped from the source location.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the transfer order was approved for execution.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the transfer order was cancelled, if applicable.']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the transfer order was cancelled, if applicable.']
  completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the transfer order was fully received and closed in the system.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of units confirmed available and allocated for transfer at the source location.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the transfer order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this transfer order.']
  estimated_freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The estimated transportation cost for the transfer order in the base currency.']
  freight_terms varchar [note: 'Terms defining who bears the cost and risk of transportation: prepaid, collect, third party, Free on Board (FOB) origin, or FOB destination.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transferred inventory contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance documentation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the transfer order record was most recently updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional instructions, special handling requirements, or contextual information about the transfer.']
  otif_status varchar [note: 'Performance indicator showing whether the transfer was delivered on time and in full quantity as requested.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the transfer order determining processing sequence and resource allocation.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Business reason code explaining the purpose or trigger for the transfer order (e.g., low stock alert, seasonal demand, product recall, store opening).']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of units received and accepted at the destination location.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The date by which the inventory is requested to arrive at the destination location.']
  requested_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units originally requested to be transferred from source to destination location.']
  requested_ship_date date [note: 'The date by which the inventory is requested to be shipped from the source location.']
  rfid_tracked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transferred inventory is tracked using RFID technology for real-time visibility.']
  shipment_method varchar [note: 'The transportation mode used for the transfer: ground, air, ocean, rail, intermodal, or courier service.']
  shipped_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of units physically shipped from the source location.']
  source_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the upstream business document that triggered this transfer order (e.g., allocation plan ID, replenishment rule ID).']
  source_document_type varchar [note: 'The type of upstream business document or process that triggered the creation of this transfer order.']
  temperature_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the transfer requires temperature-controlled transportation and storage conditions.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking reference number for monitoring shipment status and location during transit.']
  transfer_order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reference number for the transfer order, used for tracking and communication across systems and stakeholders.']
  transfer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the transfer order indicating its progression through the fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|in_transit|received|cancelled|partially_received|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transfer order purpose: replenishment (store restocking), rebalancing (inventory optimization), recall (product withdrawal), return (reverse logistics), consolidation (inventory centralization), or emergency (urgent fulfillment).']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the transferred quantity: EA (each), CS (case), PK (pack), BX (box), PL (pallet), DZ (dozen).']

  Note: 'Operational record of internal stock transfer requests and executions between locations (warehouse-to-store, DC-to-DC, store-to-store, 3PL-to-DC). Captures transfer reference, source location, destination location, requested quantity, confirmed quantity, transfer type (replenishment, rebalancing, recall), priority, and OTIF status.'
}

Table inventory.rfid_tag {
  rfid_tag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RFID tag record. Primary key for item-level tracking.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Batch or lot identifier for the group of RFID tags encoded together. Used for quality control and batch-level issue tracking.']
  pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Transaction identifier from the POS system when the item was sold. Links RFID data to sales transaction for reconciliation and customer analytics.']
  product_safety_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_safety_test. Business justification: RFID tags enable item-level traceability to safety test results for recall management and consumer transparency (QR code scanning for test certificates). Required for CPSC Fast Track recall programs a']
  return_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Transaction identifier for the return event. Links to return transaction record for return reason and disposition analysis.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: RFID tags encoded at production encode work order reference for end-to-end supply chain traceability, authentication, and anti-counterfeiting. Standard practice in premium apparel for serialized item ']
  attachment_location varchar [note: 'Facility where the tag was attached to the product. Typically a CMT (Cut Make Trim) factory, OEM facility, or distribution center.']
  attachment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RFID tag was physically attached to the garment, footwear, or accessory. Marks the start of item-level tracking.']
  authentication_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RFID tag was authenticated. Used for supply chain security and brand protection.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this RFID tag record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data governance.']
  decommission_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the RFID tag was decommissioned. Used for shrinkage analysis, loss prevention, and tag quality management. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sold|damaged|lost|stolen|expired|tag_failure|product_disposal — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  decommission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RFID tag was decommissioned and removed from active tracking. Occurs after final sale, disposal, or tag failure.']
  encoding_location varchar [note: 'Facility or location where the RFID tag was encoded. May be a manufacturing plant, distribution center, or third-party logistics provider.']
  encoding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the RFID tag was encoded with EPC data. Typically occurs at the manufacturing facility or distribution center.']
  epc varchar [note: 'Globally unique Electronic Product Code encoded on the RFID tag, typically in SGTIN-96 or SGTIN-198 format. Enables item-level identification across the supply chain.']
  first_read_location varchar [note: 'Location where the tag was first read in the system. Typically a receiving dock at a distribution center or warehouse.']
  first_read_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the first successful read of this RFID tag in the system. Marks entry into active inventory tracking.']
  is_authenticated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFID tag has been authenticated as genuine. Critical for luxury goods and anti-counterfeiting programs.']
  is_reusable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the RFID tag is designed for reuse across multiple products or is single-use. Reusable tags are common for returnable assets and rental programs.']
  last_read_context varchar [note: 'Business context or event type of the last RFID read. Indicates the operational process during which the tag was scanned. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: receiving|cycle_count|inventory_audit|pos_sale|fitting_room|exit_gate|shipment|return — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_read_device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the RFID reader device that performed the last successful read. Links to reader device master for troubleshooting and analytics.']
  last_read_location varchar [note: 'Physical location where the tag was last read. May be a warehouse zone, store department, fitting room, or point-of-sale terminal.']
  last_read_signal_strength decimal(5,2) [note: 'Signal strength in decibels-milliwatts (dBm) of the last successful read. Used for read quality assessment and reader placement optimization.']
  last_read_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent date and time when the RFID tag was successfully read by an RFID reader. Used for real-time inventory visibility and movement tracking.']
  lock_status varchar [note: 'Write-protection status of the RFID tag memory. Locked tags cannot be rewritten, permalocked tags are permanently locked. Used for security and data integrity.']
  return_location varchar [note: 'Store or facility where the item was returned. May differ from original sale location for omnichannel returns.']
  return_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the item was returned by the customer. Enables return rate analysis and reverse logistics tracking.']
  sale_location varchar [note: 'Store or channel where the item was sold. Links to retail store master or e-commerce fulfillment center.']
  sale_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the item with this RFID tag was sold at point of sale. Marks transition from inventory to sold status.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for the individual item. Enables item-level traceability and authentication, particularly for luxury and high-value products.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The product SKU that this RFID tag is attached to. Links the tag to the specific style-color-size combination in the product master.']
  tag_encoding_standard varchar [note: 'EPC encoding scheme used on the tag. SGTIN (Serialized Global Trade Item Number) is most common for apparel SKUs, SSCC for logistics units, GRAI for returnable assets, GIAI for fixed assets.']
  tag_lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the RFID tag in its operational lifecycle. Tracks progression from encoding through sale or decommissioning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: encoded|attached|in_transit|received|in_store|sold|returned|decommissioned — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  tag_manufacturer varchar [note: 'Name of the company that manufactured the RFID tag. Used for quality tracking and vendor performance analysis.']
  tag_memory_size varchar [note: 'Memory capacity of the RFID tag in bits. Determines how much data can be encoded on the tag beyond the basic EPC.']
  tag_model varchar [note: 'Specific model or part number of the RFID tag. Different models may have varying read ranges, durability, and form factors.']
  tag_type varchar [note: 'Radio frequency type of the RFID tag. UHF (Ultra High Frequency) for long-range warehouse and store reads, HF (High Frequency) or NFC (Near Field Communication) for close-proximity interactions, LF (Low Frequency) for specialized applications.']
  total_read_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of times this RFID tag has been successfully read since encoding. Used for tag performance analysis and lifecycle management.']
  upc varchar [note: '12-digit UPC barcode associated with the SKU. Used for point-of-sale scanning and cross-reference with legacy barcode systems.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this RFID tag record was last updated. Tracks data freshness and change frequency.']

  Note: 'Master record of individual RFID tags assigned to garments, footwear, or accessories for item-level inventory visibility, including read event history. Captures EPC (Electronic Product Code), tag type (UHF, HF), encoding status, associated SKU and UPC, tag lifecycle state (encoded, attached, in-store, sold, returned, decommissioned), last read timestamp, last read location, and read event log (reader device, signal strength, read context). Enables real-time inventory accuracy, loss prevention, shrinkage detection, and fitting room analytics.'
}

Table inventory.replenishment_order {
  replenishment_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the replenishment order record.']
  allocation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchandise allocation plan that generated this replenishment order, if the order is part of a planned allocation strategy.']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Replenishment logistics create transportation emissions tracked under scope 3 category 4. Orders must link to emission events for carbon footprint reporting, CDP disclosure, and supply chain decarboni']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the logistics carrier or 3PL (Third-Party Logistics) provider responsible for transporting the replenishment shipment.']
  destination_location_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the retail store, distribution center, or warehouse that will receive the replenished inventory.']
  destination_retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Store replenishment orders from DC to retail stores must track destination store for fulfillment planning, store WOS management, replenishment lead time tracking, and store-level inventory flow optimi']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.digital_storefront. Business justification: Replenishment orders are often triggered by demand from specific digital channels. Tracking the requesting storefront enables channel-specific inventory planning and demand attribution. Business proce']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Replenishment planning is organized by merchandise hierarchy (department/class/subclass). Essential for category-level replenishment planning, replenishment strategy by category, and merchandise hiera']
  nos_policy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.nos_policy. Business justification: Replenishment orders triggered by Never Out of Stock policies should reference the NOS policy that generated them. This allows tracking NOS policy effectiveness and prioritizing NOS-driven replenishme']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: Replenishment orders consume OTB budget. Essential for OTB budget tracking for replenishments, ensuring replenishment spending aligns with merchandise financial plans and budget compliance.']
  primary_replenishment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the inventory planner or merchandise planner responsible for reviewing and approving this replenishment order.']
  primary_replenishment_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, distribution center, or 3PL facility from which inventory will be shipped to fulfill this replenishment order.']
  routing_guide_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.routing_guide. Business justification: Replenishment follows routing rules. Linking enables allocation plan freight assumptions validation, routing compliance, cost-to-serve accuracy, and replenishment cost optimization—critical for appare']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchandise season or collection to which the replenished SKU belongs, used for seasonal inventory planning.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Replenishment fulfillment creates shipments. Linking enables landed cost per unit for allocation plans, supply chain velocity metrics, and allocation plan performance measurement—critical for apparel ']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being replenished, representing a unique product variant by style, color, size, and other attributes.']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_standard. Business justification: Replenishment orders specify quality standards that must be met for inventory to be eligible for allocation and sale. Ensures only compliant inventory enters replenishment workflows, supporting brand ']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent product or style to which the SKU belongs, used for aggregated reporting and planning.']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: 3PL-managed replenishment. Linking enables 3PL service level tracking, cost allocation, performance benchmarking, and SLA compliance measurement—standard for apparel brands using 3PL fulfillment.']
  vmi_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.vmi_program. Business justification: Replenishment orders generated automatically by VMI programs should reference the VMI program that triggered them. This enables tracking of VMI program performance and distinguishes vendor-managed rep']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'The actual date when the replenishment order was received and confirmed at the destination location.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'The actual date when the replenishment order was shipped from the source location.']
  approved_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units approved for shipment after review by inventory planner or system validation, may differ from requested quantity due to availability or budget constraints.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the replenishment order was approved for execution.']
  ats_quantity_at_request decimal(15,3) [note: 'The ATS (Available to Sell) inventory quantity at the destination location when the replenishment order was created, representing on-hand minus allocated/reserved inventory.']
  cancelled_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation of why the replenishment order was cancelled, if applicable (e.g., inventory no longer needed, source location stockout, budget constraints).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the replenishment order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for freight cost and other monetary values (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  expected_ship_date date [note: 'The planned date when the source location will ship the replenishment order, calculated based on lead time and requested delivery date.']
  freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The transportation cost incurred for shipping this replenishment order, used for landed cost calculation and logistics analytics.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the replenishment order record was last updated, used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The expected number of days from order release to delivery at the destination location, based on historical performance and carrier SLA.']
  max_stock_level decimal(15,3) [note: 'The target maximum inventory level at the destination location, used in min/max replenishment logic to determine order quantity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments added by planners or system users regarding special handling, exceptions, or business context for this replenishment order.']
  on_hand_quantity_at_request decimal(15,3) [note: 'The physical inventory quantity available at the destination location at the time the replenishment order was created, used to calculate replenishment need.']
  order_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique order number for the replenishment request, typically system-generated with REP prefix.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the replenishment order: critical (NOS breach or stockout risk), high (below safety stock), normal (routine replenishment), low (opportunistic or consolidation).']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of units received and confirmed at the destination location, may differ from shipped quantity due to transit loss or damage.']
  reorder_point decimal(15,3) [note: 'The inventory level threshold at the destination location that triggered this replenishment order, calculated based on lead time demand and safety stock.']
  replenishment_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the replenishment order: proposed (system-generated, awaiting review), approved (planner-approved), released (sent to WMS for execution), in_transit (shipment en route), received (goods received at destination), fulfilled (order complete), cancelled (order voided), rejected (approval denied). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: proposed|approved|released|in_transit|received|fulfilled|cancelled|rejected — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  replenishment_trigger varchar [note: 'The business rule or condition that triggered this replenishment order: below reorder point, NOS (Never Out of Stock) breach, OTB (Open-to-Buy) driven, demand forecast, safety stock breach, or allocation plan execution.']
  replenishment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of how the replenishment order was initiated: automatic (system-triggered by min/max rules or MRP), manual (planner-initiated), VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory), emergency (expedited NOS breach), seasonal (collection-driven), or promotional (event-driven).']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'The target date by which the destination location requires the replenished inventory to arrive, based on demand forecast or min/max rules.']
  requested_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units requested by the replenishment algorithm or planner to bring inventory to target levels.']
  safety_stock_level decimal(15,3) [note: 'The minimum inventory buffer maintained at the destination location to protect against demand variability and supply disruptions.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The delivery speed commitment for the replenishment shipment: standard (5-7 days), expedited (2-3 days), next_day, two_day, economy (7-10 days).']
  shipment_method varchar [note: 'The transportation mode used for the replenishment shipment: ground (truck), air (air freight), ocean (container ship), rail (train), intermodal (multi-mode), courier (express delivery).']
  shipped_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of units shipped from the source location, may differ from approved quantity due to picking variances or stock adjustments.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The carrier-provided tracking number for monitoring the shipment in transit.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which quantities are expressed: EA (each/piece), CS (case), PK (pack), BX (box), PL (pallet).']

  Note: 'Operational record of store or DC replenishment requests generated by min/max rules, MRP, or demand-driven algorithms. Captures replenishment type (automatic, manual, VMI), source location, destination location, SKU, requested quantity, approved quantity, replenishment trigger (below reorder point, NOS breach, OTB-driven), status (proposed, approved, released, fulfilled), and expected delivery date.'
}

Table inventory.vmi_program {
  vmi_program_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the VMI program agreement. Primary key.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: VMI programs are defined by merchandise category (department/class). Essential for category-level VMI management, vendor collaboration by category, and merchandise hierarchy-based vendor performance t']
  program_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: VMI programs require dedicated program managers to coordinate vendor relationships, monitor SLA compliance, resolve discrepancies, and conduct performance reviews. Essential for accountability in vend']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: VMI programs are often seasonal or have seasonal parameters in apparel. Essential for seasonal VMI planning, seasonal vendor collaboration, and aligning VMI with seasonal merchandise strategies.']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_standard. Business justification: VMI agreements specify quality standards vendors must meet for inventory they manage on behalf of the retailer. Defines inspection protocols, AQL levels, and compliance requirements in vendor contract']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: VMI programs often operate at specific factory level for capacity planning, direct shipments, and factory-specific inventory management agreements. Enables factory-level VMI performance monitoring and']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: VMI programs may use 3PL services. Linking enables VMI fulfillment cost analysis, 3PL performance in VMI context, service model optimization, and cost allocation—relevant for apparel VMI programs.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier responsible for managing inventory replenishment under this VMI agreement.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: VMI programs operate at specific facilities/DCs with location-specific rules, vendor access control, and performance tracking. Link enables facility-level VMI configuration, vendor-managed stock posit']
  wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.wholesale_account. Business justification: VMI (Vendor-Managed Inventory) programs in apparel-fashion are contractual agreements with specific wholesale accounts (department stores, specialty retailers) where the vendor manages stock levels at']
  auto_renewal_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the VMI program agreement automatically renews at the end of its term unless terminated by either party.']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the legal contract document governing this VMI program agreement.']
  cost_sharing_model varchar [note: 'Model defining how inventory carrying costs, obsolescence risk, and markdowns are shared between supplier and retailer.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VMI program record was first created in the system.']
  data_sharing_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which inventory position, sales, and forecast data are shared with the supplier through EDI or other integration methods.']
  edi_integration_method varchar [note: 'Method used for electronic exchange of inventory data with supplier. EDI 846 (Inventory Inquiry/Advice), EDI 852 (Product Activity Data), EDI 867 (Product Transfer and Resale Report), API (Application Programming Interface), portal (web-based), or manual file exchange.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the VMI program agreement expires or is scheduled to terminate. Null for open-ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the VMI program agreement becomes active and supplier replenishment responsibility begins.']
  facility_coverage varchar [note: 'List of warehouse, distribution center, or retail store locations covered by this VMI program. May reference facility codes or location identifiers.']
  financial_settlement_terms varchar [note: 'Description of payment terms, pricing agreements, and financial settlement conditions between retailer and supplier for VMI inventory.']
  forecast_sharing_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether demand forecasts are shared with the supplier to enable proactive replenishment planning.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this VMI program record was last updated.']
  last_performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review conducted for this VMI program.']
  markdown_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for absorbing markdown costs when VMI inventory must be sold at reduced prices.']
  next_performance_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next formal performance review of this VMI program.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special conditions, or operational details about the VMI program.']
  ownership_transfer_point varchar [note: 'Point in the supply chain where inventory ownership transfers from supplier to retailer. Critical for consignment vs owned inventory models. FOB (Free on Board) terms define shipping responsibility.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allowed for payment after invoice date or ownership transfer, depending on program terms.']
  performance_review_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency for formal review of supplier performance against VMI program SLAs and KPIs.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact for VMI program communications and issue resolution.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary supplier contact responsible for managing this VMI program.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary supplier contact for urgent VMI program matters.']
  program_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the VMI program, used in operational systems and supplier communications.']
  program_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the VMI program for business reference and reporting.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the VMI program agreement.']
  program_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the VMI program model. Consignment means supplier owns inventory until sale; owned inventory means retailer owns upon receipt; hybrid combines both; NOS (Never Out of Stock) program focuses on continuous replenishment of basics; seasonal basics covers seasonal core items.']
  replenishment_frequency varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the supplier reviews inventory levels and initiates replenishment orders.']
  replenishment_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days from replenishment order placement to inventory receipt at destination facility.']
  return_to_vendor_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the retailer can return unsold or slow-moving VMI inventory back to the supplier.']
  rfid_tracking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID technology is used for real-time inventory visibility and tracking within this VMI program.']
  rtv_terms varchar [note: 'Conditions and restrictions governing returns of VMI inventory to the supplier, including timeframes, restocking fees, and eligible reasons.']
  scope_category varchar [note: 'Product category or merchandise hierarchy level covered by this VMI program (e.g., Basic Tees, Denim Bottoms, Trims and Notions).']
  scope_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of specific SKUs included in the VMI program scope. Used when program covers specific items rather than entire categories.']
  sla_fill_rate_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target fill rate percentage that the supplier must achieve, representing the percentage of demand that must be fulfilled from available inventory.']
  sla_lead_time_target_days varchar [note: 'Maximum lead time in days that the supplier commits to for replenishment orders under this VMI program.']
  sla_otif_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target OTIF percentage that the supplier must achieve, representing orders delivered on time and complete.']
  target_max_stock_level decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum inventory level (in units) that should not be exceeded to avoid overstock and carrying cost issues.']
  target_min_stock_level decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum inventory level (in units) that the supplier must maintain to avoid stockouts. Triggers replenishment when inventory falls below this threshold.']
  target_wos decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target inventory coverage expressed in weeks of supply based on forecasted demand. Used to calculate optimal stock levels.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required by either party to terminate the VMI program agreement.']

  Note: 'Master record of Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) agreements with suppliers for designated SKUs or categories. Captures agreement terms, supplier replenishment responsibility scope, target min/max stock levels, replenishment frequency, EDI integration method, performance SLAs (fill rate, lead time, OTIF), program status, and financial terms (consignment vs owned). Common in basics/NOS programs with key fabric and trim suppliers.'
}

Table inventory.nos_policy {
  nos_policy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Never Out of Stock policy record. Primary key.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product category covered by this NOS policy when policy applies at category level rather than individual SKU.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or role responsible for maintaining and reviewing this NOS policy.']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: NOS (Never Out of Stock) inventory is planned within OTB budgets. Essential for NOS budget allocation, ensuring NOS spending aligns with merchandise financial plans and OTB compliance.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific retail store where this NOS policy is enforced. Null indicates policy applies across all stores.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise season during which this NOS policy is applicable, enabling seasonal NOS strategies.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU designated under this NOS policy. Links to product master.']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_standard. Business justification: Never-out-of-stock policies reference quality standards to ensure only conforming inventory qualifies for priority replenishment and allocation. Prevents quality issues from compromising NOS service l']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the preferred supplier for replenishment under this NOS policy, enabling Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) programs.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific warehouse or distribution center where this NOS policy is enforced. Null indicates policy applies across all facilities.']
  allocation_priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking used to prioritize inventory allocation when multiple demands compete for limited stock. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  auto_replenishment_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether automated replenishment orders are generated when inventory falls below threshold without manual intervention.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(15,2) [note: 'Standard unit cost used for inventory valuation and replenishment cost calculations under this NOS policy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NOS policy record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost and financial calculations associated with this NOS policy.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this NOS policy expires or is scheduled to be deactivated. Null indicates open-ended policy.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this NOS policy becomes active and begins driving replenishment and allocation decisions.']
  eligible_channel_dtc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NOS policy applies to Direct-to-Consumer e-commerce channel.']
  eligible_channel_marketplace boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NOS policy applies to third-party marketplace channel.']
  eligible_channel_retail boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NOS policy applies to physical retail store channel.']
  eligible_channel_wholesale boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NOS policy applies to wholesale distribution channel.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the geographic coverage of this NOS policy, indicating whether it applies globally or to specific regions, countries, or locations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NOS policy record was last updated or modified.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this NOS policy was last reviewed and validated by the policy owner.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of days from replenishment order placement to inventory receipt, used to calculate reorder points.']
  max_order_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be ordered in a single replenishment transaction under this NOS policy.']
  minimum_floor_stock_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The absolute minimum inventory quantity that must be maintained at all times to prevent stockouts. Triggers immediate replenishment when breached.']
  moq decimal(15,3) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by supplier for replenishment orders under this NOS policy.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Date when this NOS policy is scheduled for next review and validation.']
  nos_tier varchar [note: 'Classification tier indicating priority level of the NOS policy. Platinum represents highest priority items requiring maximum availability.']
  order_multiple decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity increment in which replenishment orders must be placed (e.g., case packs, pallet quantities).']
  policy_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the NOS policy, used for external reference and reporting.']
  policy_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the NOS policy purpose, scope, and business rationale.']
  policy_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the NOS policy for business user identification.']
  policy_notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, special instructions, or business context related to this NOS policy.']
  policy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NOS policy indicating whether it is actively enforced in replenishment and allocation systems.']
  priority_replenishment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NOS policy triggers priority replenishment processing, bypassing standard allocation queues.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled reviews of this NOS policy to assess continued relevance and parameter accuracy.']
  rfid_tracking_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether items under this NOS policy require RFID tracking for real-time inventory visibility and accuracy.']
  safety_stock_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base safety stock calculation to determine buffer inventory levels for NOS items. Higher values provide greater protection against demand variability.']
  target_wos decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target inventory coverage expressed in weeks of supply. Represents the desired inventory level to maintain continuous availability.']
  vmi_program_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this NOS policy is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory program where the supplier monitors and replenishes stock.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining Never Out of Stock (NOS) policies for designated SKUs or product categories. Captures NOS classification tier, minimum floor stock quantity, safety stock multiplier, priority replenishment flag, eligible channels (DTC, wholesale, retail), review frequency, and policy owner. Drives automated replenishment triggers and allocation priority rules.'
}

Table inventory.reservation {
  reservation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the inventory reservation record. Primary key for the inventory reservation entity.']
  allocation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the allocation record if this reservation has been converted to a physical allocation. Null while reservation is still pending or active.']
  cart_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.cart. Business justification: When customers add high-demand apparel items to cart, inventory is soft-reserved to prevent overselling. Business process: cart-to-inventory reservation, ATS calculation, and abandoned cart inventory ']
  checkout_session_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.checkout_session. Business justification: Hard reservations are created during checkout to hold inventory while payment is processed. Business process: checkout inventory hold, payment-to-fulfillment handoff, and reservation expiry management']
  digital_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.digital_order. Business justification: Final reservations are converted to committed allocations when digital order is placed. Business process: order-to-fulfillment inventory commitment, reservation-to-shipment tracking, and inventory acc']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer or wholesale account for whom inventory is reserved. Null for non-customer reservations such as photo shoots or events.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: BOPIS and ship-from-store inventory reservations must link to fulfilling retail store for omnichannel allocation, store fulfillment capacity planning, and store-level ATP calculation - critical for un']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order, purchase order, or wholesale order that triggered this reservation. Null for non-order reservations such as event holds or sample pulls.']
  sales_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.sales_order_line. Business justification: Reservations must link to specific order line (not just header) for line-level ATP confirmation, multi-line order partial fulfillment, SKU-level allocation accuracy, and line-item cancellation handlin']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: ATP allocation rules require product.sku attributes: lifecycle_stage (prioritize core vs. seasonal), ats_flag, nos_flag, and product_category for channel-specific reservation priority logic. String sk']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, distribution center, retail store, or Third-Party Logistics (3PL) facility where the inventory is reserved.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code for reservation cancellation: CUSTOMER_CANCEL (customer-initiated), CART_ABANDON (ecommerce cart timeout), INVENTORY_UNAVAILABLE (stock discrepancy), ORDER_MODIFY (order change), SYSTEM_ERROR (technical issue), BUSINESS_RULE (policy-driven cancellation).']
  cancelled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation was cancelled and inventory released. Null if reservation was not cancelled.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this reservation record was first inserted into the data platform. Supports data lineage and audit requirements.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation will automatically expire and release inventory back to Available to Sell (ATS) pool if not fulfilled or extended.']
  extended_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the reservation expiry has been extended beyond the original expiry timestamp. Used to track reservation aging and potential inventory lock issues.']
  fulfilled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation was converted to a physical allocation and inventory was committed for shipment or pickup. Null if not yet fulfilled.']
  fulfillment_channel varchar [note: 'Sales and fulfillment channel for which inventory is reserved: ecommerce (online direct-to-consumer), retail_store (brick-and-mortar), wholesale (B2B partner), marketplace (third-party platform), bopis (buy online pick up in store), ship_from_store (store fulfillment for online orders).']
  hard_reservation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a hard reservation (true - guaranteed allocation, cannot be overridden) or soft reservation (false - tentative hold, can be reallocated based on priority).']
  last_extended_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent reservation expiry extension. Null if reservation has never been extended.']
  last_updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that last modified the reservation record. Supports change tracking and audit trail.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this reservation record was last modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or business justification for the reservation.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority rank determining allocation sequence when multiple reservations compete for limited inventory. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the business reason for the reservation: CUSTOMER_ORDER (committed customer purchase), CART_HOLD (ecommerce shopping cart), WHOLESALE_COMMIT (B2B partner commitment), EVENT_RESERVE (marketing or promotional event), PHOTO_SHOOT (content creation), SAMPLE_REQUEST (product sampling).']
  released_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reservation was released and inventory returned to the Available to Sell (ATS) pool. Null if reservation is still active.']
  reservation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique reservation number used for tracking and reference across systems and channels.']
  reservation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the reservation: active (currently holding inventory), expired (reservation period elapsed), released (inventory returned to available pool), fulfilled (reservation converted to allocation), cancelled (reservation voided), pending (awaiting confirmation).']
  reservation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inventory reservation was created in the system. Represents the business event time when stock was earmarked.']
  reservation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reservation purpose: sales order (committed to customer order), ecommerce cart (held in online shopping cart), wholesale commitment (allocated to wholesale partner), event_hold (reserved for marketing or promotional event), photo_shoot_hold (reserved for photography or content creation), sample_pull (reserved for product sampling or showroom).']
  reserved_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Number of units earmarked under this reservation. Reduces Available to Sell (ATS) but not Available to Promise (ATP) until allocation occurs.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Unique product identifier at the style-color-size level representing the specific item variant being reserved.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the reservation: SAP_SD (SAP Sales & Distribution), SFCC (Salesforce Commerce Cloud), OMS (Order Management System), WMS (Warehouse Management System), POS (Point of Sale), WHOLESALE_PORTAL (B2B ordering portal).']
  source_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier from the source system that initiated this reservation, enabling end-to-end traceability.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit in which the reserved quantity is expressed: EA (each/piece), PR (pair for footwear), DZ (dozen), CS (case), PK (pack).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier or system account that created the reservation record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of inventory reservations that earmark stock for specific orders, channels, or events before physical allocation. Captures reservation type (sales order, e-commerce cart, wholesale commitment, event hold, photo shoot hold, sample pull, trunk show), reserved quantity, reservation expiry timestamp, priority rank, fulfillment channel, and soft vs hard reservation indicator. Feeds ATS and ATP calculations across omnichannel fulfillment.'
}

Table inventory.stock_valuation {
  stock_valuation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the stock valuation record.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Compliance incidents (product recalls, safety violations, regulatory penalties) directly impact inventory valuation through write-downs, obsolescence reserves, and net realizable value adjustments. Re']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Valuation reporting is organized by merchandise hierarchy (department/class/subclass). Essential for category-level inventory valuation reporting, financial analysis by category, and merchandise hiera']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: Non-conformances drive inventory write-downs, markdown reserves, and aged inventory provisions. Links quality issues to financial impact for accurate inventory valuation, COGS allocation, and financia']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: Inventory valuation is monitored against OTB budgets for financial planning. Essential for inventory financial planning, OTB variance analysis, and ensuring inventory value aligns with merchandise bud']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Store-level inventory valuation is required for store P&L reporting, shrinkage reserve calculation, markdown impact analysis, and financial statement preparation by retail location - fundamental to re']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the fashion season or collection to which this inventory belongs (e.g., Spring/Summer 2024, Fall/Winter 2024). Critical for seasonal lifecycle valuation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the SKU being valued. Links to the product master.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Landed cost valuation for materials requires sourcing PO reference to allocate freight, duty, and agent commissions. Critical for accurate COGS calculation and margin analysis in apparel costing.']
  valuation_area_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the valuation area (plant, warehouse, distribution center, or store) where inventory is valued. Links to location master.']
  aged_inventory_provision decimal(18,2) [note: 'Provision amount for obsolete, slow-moving, or aged inventory that may not be sold at full value. Reflects inventory obsolescence risk.']
  cogs_allocation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Portion of inventory value allocated to COGS for goods sold during the period. Used for profit and loss calculation.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for managing this inventory (e.g., warehouse operations, retail store). Used for management accounting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this valuation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this valuation record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  current_unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The effective unit cost currently applied for valuation, based on the valuation method in use (standard cost or moving average price).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year (1-12) to which this valuation record belongs.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this valuation record belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  gl_account_number varchar [note: 'General Ledger account number to which this inventory valuation is posted for balance sheet reporting.']
  inventory_turnover_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days inventory remains in stock before being sold. Calculated as (average inventory value / COGS) × 365. Key metric for inventory efficiency.']
  inventory_write_down_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount written down from book value to net realizable value when NRV is lower than cost. Impacts Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) and profitability.']
  last_cost_update_date date [note: 'Date when the unit cost (standard cost or moving average price) was last updated or recalculated.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this valuation record was last updated or adjusted.']
  last_physical_count_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory count or cycle count for this SKU at this valuation area. Used to assess valuation accuracy.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage in the product lifecycle: pre-season (not yet selling), in-season (active selling), end-of-season (markdown phase), clearance (deep discount), obsolete (no longer saleable).']
  markdown_reserve_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Reserve amount set aside to account for anticipated future markdowns on seasonal or slow-moving inventory. Reduces net realizable value.']
  markdown_reserve_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total stock value reserved for anticipated markdowns, typically based on historical markdown rates and seasonal lifecycle stage.']
  moving_average_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Weighted average unit cost recalculated with each goods receipt when moving average valuation method is used.']
  net_realizable_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated selling price in the ordinary course of business less estimated costs of completion and sale. Inventory must be valued at lower of cost or NRV.']
  price_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between standard cost and actual purchase price for goods received. Tracked separately in standard costing environments.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center to which this inventory valuation is attributed for profitability analysis (e.g., product line, brand, region).']
  seasonal_write_down_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage write-down applied to inventory value for end-of-season or aged seasonal merchandise to reflect lower net realizable value.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Predetermined unit cost used for valuation when standard costing method is applied. Includes material, labor, and overhead components.']
  total_stock_quantity decimal(18,3) [note: 'Total quantity of inventory on hand at the valuation area for this SKU at the valuation date.']
  total_stock_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total book value of inventory calculated as total stock quantity multiplied by current unit cost. Represents balance sheet inventory value.']
  valuation_adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for any manual adjustment made to the valuation (e.g., physical count variance, damage, theft, pricing error).']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'Classification code used for grouping materials for accounting purposes (e.g., finished goods, raw materials, trading goods). Maps to General Ledger (GL) accounts.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The date on which this valuation snapshot was taken. Typically end-of-month (EOM) or end-of-season for financial reporting.']
  valuation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the valuation record: active (current), frozen (locked for period close), adjusted (manual adjustment applied), written_off (inventory disposed), under_review (pending audit).']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Method used to value inventory: standard cost (predetermined cost), moving average (weighted average of purchases), FIFO (First In First Out), LIFO (Last In First Out), or retail method (cost-to-retail ratio).']
  weeks_of_supply decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of weeks the current inventory level will last based on forecasted or historical sales rate. Critical for replenishment planning.']

  Note: 'Master record of inventory valuation parameters and current book value for each SKU at each valuation area. Captures valuation method (standard cost, moving average, FIFO), standard cost, moving average price, total stock value, last valuation date, currency, valuation class, markdown reserve, seasonal write-down percentage, and aged inventory provision. Supports COGS calculation, balance sheet inventory reporting, end-of-season markdown accounting, and inventory obsolescence provisioning critical for fashion\'s seasonal lifecycle.'
}

Business process: return quality control, disposition decision (restock/markdown/RTV), and cust']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Quarantine holds triggered by compliance violations (failed safety tests, restricted substance detection, labeling errors) must link to formal compliance incidents for regulatory reporting, investigat']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Quarantine holds are often applied to entire production lots/batches (e.g., dye-lot quality issues). quarantine_hold has lot_number string but no FK to lot_batch master. Adding lot_batch_id FK enables']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: Quarantine holds are initiated by non-conformance reports documenting root cause, severity, and corrective action requirements. Direct link to the specific test that triggered the hold is essential for disposition decisions, retest planning, and vendor acc']

Business justification: Cross-references inventory quarantine records with quality hold records for synchronized disposition management. Quality holds may exist independently in QMS; this link ensures WMS and QMS remain alig']
  quality_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the quality inspection record that identified the issue leading to quarantine hold.']

Business justification: Quarantine disposition decisions (release, rework, scrap) require quality manager approval for compliance and financial control. Critical for defect tracking, vendor chargebacks, and regulatory compli']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Quality holds at store level (damaged goods, customer returns awaiting disposition, quality defects) must track which retail store holds the inventory for disposition workflow, RTV processing, and sto']
  rfid_tag_id varchar [note: 'RFID tag identifier associated with the quarantined inventory for tracking purposes.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Quality holds may originate from shipment inspection. Linking enables supplier quality trending by shipment, customs hold analysis, landed quality metrics, and root cause investigation—standard appare']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Quality disposition decisions require product.sku material_composition, safety_certification, sustainability_certification, and care_instructions to determine regulatory compliance, rework feasibility']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Quality holds on inbound materials require linkage to sourcing PO for vendor chargeback, RTV processing, and supplier quality rating. Essential for apparel quality management and vendor performance tr']
  stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the inventory record that is under quarantine or quality hold.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier who provided the inventory under quarantine hold.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse, distribution center, retail store, or 3PL facility where the quarantined inventory is physically located.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Quality holds on production output must link to originating work order for factory accountability, root cause analysis, claim processing, and corrective action tracking. Essential for apparel quality ']
  actual_release_date date [note: 'The actual date when the quarantine hold was resolved and disposition decision was executed.']
  actual_release_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the quarantine hold was resolved, including time of day.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the quarantine hold is related to regulatory compliance or safety standards violation.']
  corrective_action_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the corrective action plan associated with this quarantine hold.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether corrective action is required to prevent recurrence of the issue.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quarantine hold record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated financial impact.']
  customer_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the quarantine hold affects customer orders or shipments.']
  disposition_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the final disposition decision.']
  disposition_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision on how to handle the quarantined inventory (return to stock, RTV=Return to Vendor, destroy, rework, donate, transfer to another location).']
  disposition_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding the disposition decision and execution.']
  estimated_financial_impact decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact or cost associated with the quarantine hold, including potential loss of inventory value.']
  expected_release_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the quarantine hold will be resolved and inventory may be released or disposed.']
  hold_approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the quality manager or authorized person who approved the quarantine hold.']
  hold_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person, system, or department that initiated the quarantine hold.']
  hold_quantity decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity of inventory units placed under quarantine hold.']
  hold_quantity_uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the hold quantity (EA=Each, CS=Case, PL=Pallet, CTN=Carton, PR=Pair).']
  hold_reason_code varchar [note: 'Categorical code indicating the primary reason for placing inventory under quarantine hold.']
  hold_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of why the inventory was placed under quarantine or quality hold.']
  hold_start_date date [note: 'The date when the quarantine hold was initiated and inventory was blocked from sale or shipment.']
  hold_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the quarantine hold was initiated, including time of day.']
  hold_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the quarantine hold record.']
  initiating_event_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the event that triggered the quarantine hold (e.g., quality inspection ID, recall notice number, complaint ticket number).']
  initiating_event_type varchar [note: 'Type of event that triggered the quarantine hold (quality inspection failure, supplier defect notification, CPSC recall, customer complaint, internal audit finding, customs flag).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the quarantine hold record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General notes or comments field for additional information about the quarantine hold.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number associated with the quarantined inventory.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the quarantine hold for resolution urgency (critical, high, medium, low).']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether the quarantine hold is part of a product recall event.']
  regulatory_authority varchar [note: 'Name of the regulatory body involved in the hold (e.g., CPSC, FTC, Customs) if the hold is regulatory in nature.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number or case number from the regulatory authority related to the hold.']
  root_cause_analysis_completed boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator whether root cause analysis has been completed for the issue that triggered the quarantine hold.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number of the quarantined inventory item, if applicable.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The SKU code of the product under quarantine hold.']

  Note: 'Operational record of inventory placed under quarantine or quality hold status, preventing it from being sold or shipped. Captures hold reason (quality failure, regulatory hold, customs inspection, recall, damage), hold quantity, hold location, initiating event reference (quality inspection, supplier defect, CPSC recall), hold start date, expected release date, and disposition decision (return to stock, RTV, destroy).'
}

Table inventory.asn {
  asn_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the advance shipment notice record.']
  asn_ship_to_location_warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the destination location (distribution center, warehouse, retail store, or 3PL) where goods will be received.']

Linking enables receiving document validation, freight claim filing, carrier liability determination, and shipment traceability—fundamental apparel receiving oper']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Inbound shipments (ASNs) are primary scope 3 emission sources. ASN-to-emission linkage enables carbon accounting per shipment, carrier performance benchmarking, and modal shift analysis for decarboniz']
  customs_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.customs_entry. Business justification: Imported ASNs tied to customs filings. Linking enables pre-clearance planning, ISF compliance validation, customs hold impact analysis, and entry-level traceability—critical for apparel trade complian']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order that this ASN fulfills.']
  packing_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.packing_list. Business justification: ASN contains packing list details. Linking enables receiving validation (expected vs. actual), carton-level traceability, receiving productivity measurement (scan vs. manual), and vendor compliance sc']
  pre_shipment_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: ASNs reference pre-shipment inspections conducted at supplier/factory before goods ship. Enables receiving teams to verify inspection completion, expedite receipt processing, and flag discrepancies be']
  receiving_supervisor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: ASN receiving operations require supervisor assignment for dock door coordination, labor allocation, and exception handling. Critical for receiving performance metrics, labor productivity tracking, an']
  ship_to_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: ASN destination is the receiving DC. Role-prefixed to distinguish from origin. Enables dock appointment scheduling, labor planning, inbound volume forecasting, and DC capacity management—critical for ']
  ship_to_retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Store-bound advance ship notices must identify destination retail store for receiving appointment scheduling, dock door assignment, labor planning, and inbound logistics coordination at store receivin']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: ASN is advance notice of inbound shipment. Linking enables receiving variance analysis (expected vs. actual), dock scheduling optimization, carrier performance tracking, and OTIF measurement—standard ']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: ASNs for inbound materials/fabrics from suppliers reference the originating sourcing PO. Required for receiving planning, dock scheduling, and 3-way match validation in apparel material procurement.']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: ASNs originate from specific factories for shipment tracking, dock scheduling, factory performance monitoring, and logistics planning. Role-prefixed as origin_factory_id to distinguish from ship_from_']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: 3PL-managed inbound shipments. Linking enables 3PL receiving performance measurement, SLA compliance tracking, cost allocation, and service level validation—standard for apparel brands using 3PL recei']
  trade_compliance_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_record. Business justification: Every inbound international shipment requires trade compliance validation (HS code classification, duty rates, country of origin determination) for customs clearance. ASN links to trade compliance rec']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier, factory, or 3PL sending the shipment.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Advanced ship notices for finished goods from factories reference originating work order for pre-receipt validation, dock scheduling, and customs documentation. Enables matching ASN to production orde']
  acknowledged_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ASN was acknowledged by the receiving system or warehouse management system.']
  actual_arrival_date date [note: 'Actual date when the shipment physically arrived at the destination location.']
  appointment_date timestamp [note: 'Scheduled date and time for the shipment to be received at the destination facility.']
  asn_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the advance shipment notice, typically provided by supplier or 3PL.']
  asn_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ASN in the receiving workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|transmitted|acknowledged|in_transit|arrived|receiving|received|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  asn_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ASN based on shipment purpose and origin.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the shipping carrier or logistics provider transporting the goods.']
  carrier_service_level varchar [note: 'Service level or shipping method used by the carrier (e.g., ground, express, air freight, ocean freight).']
  commercial_invoice_number varchar [note: 'Commercial invoice number associated with this shipment for customs and payment reconciliation.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'Shipping container identification number for ocean or intermodal freight.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the goods were manufactured or produced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ASN record was first created in the system.']
  customs_value_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Declared customs value of the shipment for duty and tax calculation purposes.']
  customs_value_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the customs value amount.']
  dock_door_assignment varchar [note: 'Assigned dock door or bay number at the receiving facility for unloading this shipment.']
  expected_arrival_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the shipment will arrive at the destination location for receiving.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System code for customs classification and duty calculation of the goods.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International commercial term defining the responsibilities and costs between buyer and seller for this shipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_hazmat boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment contains hazardous materials requiring special handling and compliance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this ASN record was last updated or modified.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for receiving and processing this ASN shipment.']
  receiving_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical receiving of the shipment was completed and goods were put away.']
  receiving_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when physical receiving of the shipment began at the destination facility.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment contains RFID-tagged items for automated receiving and tracking.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'Security seal number applied to the container or trailer for tamper detection.']
  shipment_date date [note: 'Date when the goods were shipped from the origin location.']
  special_handling_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text instructions for special handling requirements such as fragile, temperature-controlled, or hazardous materials.']
  supplier_reference_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s internal reference or packing list number for this shipment.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the shipment requires temperature-controlled handling and storage.']
  total_carton_count varchar [note: 'Total number of cartons or packages included in this ASN shipment.']
  total_pallet_count varchar [note: 'Total number of pallets included in this ASN shipment.']
  total_unit_quantity varchar [note: 'Total number of individual units (SKUs) across all line items in this ASN.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total volume of the shipment in cubic meters.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Total gross weight of the shipment in kilograms.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Carrier-provided tracking or waybill number for shipment visibility and tracing.']
  transmitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ASN was electronically transmitted to the receiving system via EDI or API.']

  Note: 'Record of Advance Shipment Notices received from suppliers, factories, or 3PLs prior to physical goods arrival, including line-level SKU detail. Captures ASN number, supplier reference, expected arrival date, ship-from/ship-to locations, carrier, tracking number, carton count, total units, line-level SKU/UPC/quantity breakdown, lot numbers, country of origin, and HS codes. Enables pre-receiving planning, dock scheduling, carton-level receiving reconciliation, and landed cost allocation upon receipt.'
}

  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_certification. Certification tracking at lot level enables market-spe']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Lot/batch quality inspections are mandatory at goods receipt and production completion per AQL protocols. Links lot traceability to inspection outcomes for compliance, disposition decisions, and suppl']
  material_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.material_certification. Business justification: Production batches must link to material certifications (GOTS, OEKO-TEX, BCI) for compliance verification, customer claims substantiation, and recall traceability. Standard practice in certified organ']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.purchase_order. Business justification: Lot/batch traceability to originating PO enables supplier quality tracking, product recall management, cost reconciliation for batch-costed inventory, and regulatory compliance (textile traceability).']
  product_safety_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_safety_test. Business justification: Production batches undergo mandatory safety testing (flammability CPSC 16 CFR 1610, lead content CPSIA, phthalates) before release to market. Direct link enables batch release decisions and recall tra']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store.
