Business justification: Store-level lot/batch tracking for apparel (dye lots, production runs, fabric batches) enables quality traceability, recall management, and colorway consistency verification at retail locations - crit']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Lot/batch traceability through supply chain. Linking enables recall management (which shipments contained affected lots), quality issue root cause analysis, and supplier performance by lot—critical fo']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the product master record. Links the lot to the specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) or product that was manufactured in this batch.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Material lot/batch traceability to sourcing PO is mandatory for quality audits, compliance (OEKO-TEX, GOTS), and vendor accountability. Critical for fabric/trim defect root cause analysis and supplier']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Lot/batch traceability requires factory-level origin tracking for quality control, compliance audits, recall management, and regulatory reporting. Critical for apparel industry supply chain transparen']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier or vendor master record. Identifies the Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM), Original Design Manufacturer (ODM), or material supplier for this lot.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: lot_batch records where production lots are stored via storage_location_code string but has no FK to warehouse_location. Adding warehouse_location_id FK provides structured link to the location hierar']
  batch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the batch purpose: production (regular manufacturing), sample (pre-production samples), prototype (design validation), rework (quality correction), return (customer returns).']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the certification for this lot expires. Null if certification does not expire or is perpetual.']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique certificate or license number issued by the certifying body (e.g., OEKO-TEX certificate number, GOTS transaction certificate). Used for audit verification and customer transparency claims.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Type of industry certification held by this lot. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OEKO-TEX Standard 100|GOTS|BCI Cotton|Fair Trade|Bluesign|Cradle to Cradle|ISO 14001|WRAP|FLA|Organic Content Standard|Recycled Claim Standard — promote to reference product]']
  colorway_consistency_grade varchar [note: 'Quality grade for color consistency within the lot: A (excellent match), B (acceptable variation), C (minor deviation), D (noticeable deviation), F (failed, not usable). Used for quality control and customer claims resolution.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Landed Duty Paid (LDP) cost per unit for this lot, including Free on Board (FOB) cost, freight, duties, and tariffs. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation and margin analysis.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the lot was manufactured. Required for customs compliance, Harmonized System (HS) Code classification, and Generalized System of Preferences (GSP) eligibility.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lot batch record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost_per_unit (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  defect_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of defective units identified during quality inspection. Used for supplier scorecarding and Acceptable Quality Level (AQL) compliance tracking.']
  dye_lot_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the dye batch used in fabric coloring. Critical for color consistency matching in apparel production. Ensures garments from same order use same dye lot to avoid shade variation.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the lot reaches end of usable life. Applicable for materials with shelf-life constraints (adhesives, elastics, certain finishes). Null for non-perishable items.']
  factory_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing facility or Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) / Original Design Manufacturer (ODM) that produced this lot. Used for supplier performance tracking and recall traceability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lot batch record was last updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  lot_batch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the lot: active (available for use), quarantined (quality hold), consumed (fully allocated), expired (past shelf life), recalled (CPSC recall), disposed (destroyed).']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the production lot. Externally-known unique code used for traceability across supply chain partners, factories, and regulatory reporting.']
  material_composition varchar [note: 'Fiber content and composition percentages for the lot (e.g., \'60% Cotton, 40% Polyester\'). Required for Federal Trade Commission (FTC) textile labeling compliance and customer product information.']
  mill_origin_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the textile mill or raw material supplier that produced the fabric or material in this lot. Critical for material traceability and certification verification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special handling instructions, or quality observations related to this lot. Used for operational communication and customer claims documentation.']
  production_date date [note: 'Date when the lot was manufactured or produced. Critical for shelf-life calculation, warranty tracking, and regulatory compliance.']
  quantity_available decimal(15,3) [note: 'Current available quantity remaining in the lot after consumption and allocation. Used for Available to Sell (ATS) and Available to Promise (ATP) calculations.']
  quantity_produced decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity manufactured in this lot. Unit of measure varies by product category (units for garments, yards/meters for fabric, kilograms for raw materials).']
  quarantine_date date [note: 'Date when the lot was placed into quarantine status. Null if lot has never been quarantined.']
  quarantine_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for placing the lot in quarantine status (e.g., \'Failed color fastness test\', \'Pending certification verification\', \'Customer complaint investigation\'). Null if lot has never been quarantined.']
  recall_date date [note: 'Date when the recall was initiated for this lot. Null if lot has never been recalled.']
  recall_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is subject to a Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) recall or voluntary recall. True if recalled, False otherwise.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Official recall identification number issued by Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) or internal recall tracking number. Null if lot has never been recalled.']
  release_date date [note: 'Date when the lot was released from quarantine and made available for use. Null if lot has never been quarantined or is currently quarantined.']
  rfid_tag_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) tags have been applied to units in this lot for automated tracking and inventory accuracy. True if RFID-enabled, False otherwise.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantity fields: EA (each/units), YD (yards), M (meters), KG (kilograms), LB (pounds), SQM (square meters), SQY (square yards). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|YD|M|KG|LB|SQM|SQY — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vendor_managed_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lot is part of a Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) program where the supplier manages replenishment. True if VMI, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Master record of production lots or batches for inventory traceability, critical for dye-lot consistency in apparel, certified material tracking (OEKO-TEX, GOTS, BCI cotton), and regulatory recall compliance. Captures lot number, batch origin (factory, mill), production date, expiry date, certification references, dye lot code, colorway consistency grade, and lot status (active, quarantined, consumed, expired). Supports CPSC recall traceability and customer claims resolution.'
}

Table inventory.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the goods receipt transaction. Primary key for this entity.']
  asn_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the advanced shipping notice that preceded this goods receipt, if applicable.']
  bill_of_lading_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.bill_of_lading. Business justification: Physical receipt tied to BOL. Linking enables receiving discrepancy resolution, freight claim support documentation, document audit trail, and carrier accountability—standard apparel receiving operati']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Goods receipt events trigger carbon footprint allocation for inbound logistics. Link enables scope 3 category 4 emissions tracking, product-level carbon labeling, and supplier sustainability scorecard']
  correction_goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on goods_receipt (correction_goods_receipt_id)']
  customs_entry_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.customs_entry. Business justification: Physical receipt of imported goods tied to customs clearance. Enables 4-way match (PO/ASN/GR/Customs), duty accrual posting, compliance reporting, and customs audit support—standard apparel import acc']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse, distribution center, or store location where goods were received.']
  freight_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.freight_invoice. Business justification: Receipt events tied to freight invoices. Linking enables landed cost posting to inventory, freight variance analysis, payment approval workflow, and freight accrual reconciliation—fundamental apparel ']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Incoming goods receipts trigger quality inspections per receiving protocols. Inspection results determine acceptance, quarantine, or RTV disposition. Essential for WMS integration, supplier performanc']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: goods_receipt captures lot/batch information for received goods (critical for dye-lot traceability in apparel) but currently has only lot_number string. Adding lot_batch_id FK to lot_batch master enab']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order against which goods are being received.']
  packing_list_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.packing_list. Business justification: Physical receipt validated against packing list. Linking enables receiving accuracy measurement, discrepancy root cause analysis, vendor compliance scoring, and receiving audit trail—fundamental appar']
  primary_goods_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse employee who physically received and processed the goods.']
  quality_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_hold. Business justification: Quality holds can block goods receipt posting to available inventory, requiring WMS to reference quality hold status before stock becomes allocatable. Enforces quality gates at receiving and prevents ']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.retail_store. Business justification: Store goods receipts (from DC or direct vendor shipments) must be attributed to receiving retail store for inventory accuracy, receiving productivity tracking, ASN-to-receipt matching, and store opera']
  return_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.return_shipment. Business justification: Return receipts. Linking enables return processing time measurement, restocking accuracy validation, refund trigger workflow, and reverse logistics performance—standard apparel returns operations.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Physical receipt event tied to inbound shipment. Required for 3-way match (PO/ASN/GR), freight accrual posting, customs reconciliation, and landed cost accuracy—fundamental apparel import operations.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Receiving quality validation requires product master material_composition, sustainability_certification, care_instructions, and safety_certification to verify compliance. Cost variance analysis needs ']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Goods receipts for materials/fabrics must reference the sourcing PO for 3-way match (PO-ASN-GR), cost verification, and quality traceability. Core apparel procurement process linking inbound materials']
  third_party_logistics_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.third_party_logistics. Business justification: 3PL receiving operations. Linking enables 3PL productivity measurement, billing validation, service level tracking, and performance benchmarking—standard for apparel brands using 3PL warehousing.']
  trade_compliance_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_record. Business justification: Goods receipts finalize customs entry and duty payment for imported goods. Direct link to trade compliance record provides audit trail for duty calculations, preferential trade agreement claims, and c']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or supplier from whom the goods were received.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Finished goods receipts from factories must reference originating work order for production order closure, cost settlement, quantity reconciliation, and variance reporting. Core ERP integration point ']
  accepted_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of goods that passed quality inspection and were accepted into available inventory.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'The name of the shipping carrier or logistics provider that delivered the goods.']
  carton_count varchar [note: 'The total number of cartons or boxes received in this goods receipt transaction.']
  container_number varchar [note: 'The ISO standard container number for ocean freight shipments, if applicable.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The country where the goods were manufactured or produced, using ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this goods receipt record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO currency code for the cost values recorded in this goods receipt.']
  discrepancy_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the type of discrepancy found during goods receipt, if any.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date printed on the goods receipt document, which may differ from the posting date.']
  hazmat_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods are classified as hazardous materials requiring special handling.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The international customs classification code for the received goods, used for trade compliance and duty calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this goods receipt record was last updated or modified.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of goods that was originally ordered on the purchase order.']
  pallet_count varchar [note: 'The total number of pallets received in this goods receipt transaction.']
  po_line_number varchar [note: 'Line item number on the purchase order corresponding to this goods receipt.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the goods receipt was posted to inventory and financial ledgers. This is the official accounting date.']
  product_description varchar [note: 'Human-readable description of the product being received, including style, color, and size details.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the goods receipt document. Used for tracking and reference across systems.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the goods receipt transaction in the warehouse management workflow.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the physical receipt of goods occurred at the receiving location.']
  receipt_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the goods receipt transaction based on business purpose and origin.']
  received_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of goods physically received and accepted at the facility.']
  receiving_location_code varchar [note: 'Specific dock door, staging area, or receiving bay code where goods were physically received.']
  receiving_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes recorded by the receiving personnel regarding condition, discrepancies, or special observations.']
  rejected_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of goods that failed quality inspection and were rejected or placed on hold.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this goods receipt has been reversed or cancelled after initial posting.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The goods receipt document number that reversed this transaction, if applicable.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods are tracked using RFID technology for enhanced visibility.']
  seal_number varchar [note: 'The security seal number applied to the container or trailer to ensure tamper-proof delivery.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Unique serial number for individually tracked items, if applicable.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The unique product identifier for the item being received. Core business identifier for inventory tracking.']
  stock_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory stock status after receipt, determining availability for use or sale.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the received goods require temperature-controlled storage and handling.']
  total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total cost of the goods receipt, calculated as received quantity multiplied by unit cost.']
  total_volume_cbm decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total volume of the received goods in cubic meters, used for warehouse space planning.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'The total gross weight of the received goods in kilograms, including packaging.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'The shipment tracking number provided by the carrier for tracing the delivery.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'The cost per unit of the received goods as recorded in the purchase order or invoice.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit in which the received quantity is measured (e.g., each, case, pallet, carton). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|CS|PL|CTN|KG|LB|YD|MT — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  upc varchar [note: 'The standard barcode identifier for the product, used for scanning and point-of-sale transactions.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'The inventory valuation method applied to the received goods for financial accounting purposes.']

  Note: 'Records the physical receipt of goods at a warehouse, DC, or port of entry against a purchase order including receipt date, received quantities, and quality status'
}

Table inventory.storefront_fulfillment_network {
  storefront_fulfillment_network_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this storefront-warehouse fulfillment network configuration record. Primary key.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital storefront that can be served by this warehouse location']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the warehouse location that can fulfill orders for this storefront']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for this warehouse when fulfilling orders from this storefront. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by order routing logic to determine which warehouse should fulfill an order when multiple warehouses can serve the storefront.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment network configuration record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this fulfillment network configuration became active and operational. Used for temporal tracking of network changes and historical analysis of fulfillment strategy evolution.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this fulfillment network configuration was or will be deactivated. Null if still active. Used to manage network reconfigurations, warehouse closures, or storefront market exits.']
  fulfillment_sla_hours varchar [note: 'Service level agreement in hours for order fulfillment from this warehouse to customers of this storefront. Represents the committed time from order placement to shipment departure. Used for delivery promise calculations and warehouse performance measurement.']
  is_primary_fulfillment_location boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this warehouse is the primary/default fulfillment location for this storefront. Typically one primary location per storefront with others serving as backup or overflow capacity.']
  shipping_cost_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of shipping cost economics for this warehouse-storefront pair. Reflects distance, carrier rates, and zone-based pricing. Used to optimize fulfillment routing based on shipping cost considerations.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment network configuration record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the fulfillment network configuration between warehouse locations and digital storefronts. It captures the operational routing rules that determine which warehouses can fulfill orders for which storefronts, including allocation priority, service level agreements, and shipping cost tiers. Each record links one warehouse location to one digital storefront with attributes that govern order routing, inventory allocation, and fulfillment strategy in multi-region omnichannel operations.. Existence Justification: In multi-region apparel ecommerce operations, digital storefronts are served by multiple warehouse locations to optimize shipping costs, delivery speed, and inventory availability (e.g., US site ships from East Coast, West Coast, and Midwest DCs based on customer location and stock levels). Conversely, a single warehouse location serves multiple storefronts (e.g., EU distribution center fulfills orders for UK, Germany, France, and Spain storefronts). The business actively manages this fulfillment network configuration with specific allocation priorities, SLAs, and cost tiers for each storefront-warehouse pair.'
}

Table inventory.inventory_pool {
  inventory_pool_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for inventory_pool']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location or facility where this inventory pool is located.']
  organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business organization or entity that owns or manages this inventory pool.']
  parent_inventory_pool_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on inventory_pool (parent_inventory_pool_id)']
  allocation_strategy varchar [note: 'The inventory allocation method used for fulfillment from this pool (First In First Out, Last In First Out, First Expired First Out, etc.).']
  atp_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to calculate Available to Promise inventory for this pool considering future supply and demand.']
  ats_calculation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to calculate Available to Sell inventory for this pool (real-time, batch processing, scheduled updates).']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the capacity metric (units, pallets, cubic meters, square feet).']
  capacity_units decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity of the inventory pool measured in standard units.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code associated with this inventory pool for accounting and reporting purposes.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory pool record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for financial transactions and valuations associated with this inventory pool.']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which cycle counts are performed for inventory accuracy verification in this pool.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this inventory pool ceased or will cease operations. Null for active pools with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this inventory pool became or will become operational and available for inventory allocation.']
  external_pool_code varchar [note: 'External system identifier for this inventory pool in source WMS or ERP systems.']
  fulfillment_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for order fulfillment allocation (lower number = higher priority).']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard replenishment lead time in days from order to receipt for this inventory pool.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this inventory pool record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the inventory pool for operational reference and documentation.']
  omnichannel_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this inventory pool is available for omnichannel fulfillment (ship-from-store, buy-online-pickup-in-store, etc.).']
  pool_category varchar [note: 'Business category indicating ownership and management model of the inventory pool.']
  pool_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the inventory pool used across systems and business communications.']
  pool_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the inventory pool for business identification and reporting purposes.']
  pool_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the inventory pool based on facility type and operational model.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'The method used to replenish inventory in this pool (automatic, manual, Vendor Managed Inventory, Just-In-Time, scheduled).']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID tracking technology is enabled for inventory in this pool.']
  safety_stock_days varchar [note: 'Number of days of safety stock maintained in this pool to buffer against demand variability and supply disruptions.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the inventory pool in its lifecycle.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the inventory pool location used for timestamp conversions and operational scheduling.']
  utilization_threshold_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target utilization percentage threshold that triggers replenishment or rebalancing actions.']
  wms_system_code varchar [note: 'The warehouse management system that manages this inventory pool (Manhattan Associates, SAP EWM, Blue Yonder, etc.).']
  wos_target_weeks decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target weeks of supply to maintain in this inventory pool based on demand forecasts.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for inventory_pool. Referenced by inventory_pool_id.'
}

Table inventory.zone {
  zone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for zone']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or distribution center to which this zone belongs.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent facility (DC, 3PL, retail store) containing this zone.']
  parent_zone_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on zone (parent_zone_id)']
  zone_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for managing operations within this zone.']
  active_from_date date [note: 'Date when the zone became operational and available for inventory operations.']
  active_to_date date [note: 'Date when the zone was deactivated or is scheduled for deactivation. Null for currently active zones.']
  aisle_range_end varchar [note: 'Ending aisle identifier for the zone\'s physical location range within the warehouse.']
  aisle_range_start varchar [note: 'Starting aisle identifier for the zone\'s physical location range within the warehouse.']
  available_capacity_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current available capacity in the zone, representing unused storage space.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the zone capacity (e.g., pallets, bins, cubic meters).']
  capacity_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum storage capacity of the zone measured in units (pallets, bins, cubic meters, or square meters depending on zone type).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was first created in the system.']
  cycle_count_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Frequency in days for conducting cycle counts within this zone to ensure inventory accuracy.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the zone including its purpose, special characteristics, and operational notes.']
  floor_level varchar [note: 'Physical floor or level number within the facility where the zone is located.']
  hazmat_certified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the zone is certified and equipped to handle hazardous materials according to regulatory requirements.']
  last_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent cycle count was performed in this zone.']
  next_cycle_count_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next cycle count in this zone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional operational notes, special instructions, or comments related to the zone.']
  picking_method varchar [note: 'Method used for order picking operations within the zone (e.g., manual, voice-directed, pick-to-light, automated).']
  replenishment_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for inventory replenishment operations, with lower numbers indicating higher priority.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the zone is equipped with RFID tracking technology for real-time inventory visibility.']
  security_level varchar [note: 'Security classification of the zone indicating access control requirements and monitoring level.']
  storage_type varchar [note: 'Physical storage method or infrastructure used within the zone.']
  temperature_controlled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the zone requires temperature control (refrigerated or frozen storage).']
  temperature_max_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum allowable temperature for the zone in degrees Celsius, applicable for temperature-controlled zones.']
  temperature_min_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Minimum allowable temperature for the zone in degrees Celsius, applicable for temperature-controlled zones.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the zone record was last modified in the system.']
  utilization_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Current utilization rate of the zone expressed as a percentage of total capacity.']
  zone_category varchar [note: 'Storage environment or handling category for the zone, indicating special requirements or conditions.']
  zone_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the zone, used for operational reference and labeling (e.g., A1, RCV-01, PKG-ZONE).']
  zone_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the zone (e.g., Receiving Zone, Picking Area A, Packing Station 1).']
  zone_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the zone indicating availability for inventory operations.']
  zone_type varchar [note: 'Functional classification of the zone indicating its primary operational purpose within the warehouse or distribution center.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for zone. Referenced by zone_id.'
}

Table inventory.valuation_area {
  valuation_area_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for valuation_area']
  parent_valuation_area_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on valuation_area (parent_valuation_area_id)']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The company code to which this valuation area is assigned for financial accounting and reporting purposes.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center code responsible for managing inventory costs within this valuation area.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166 country code where the valuation area is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this valuation area record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which inventory is valued within this valuation area (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  cycle_count_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which cycle counts are performed within this valuation area to ensure inventory accuracy.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the valuation area\'s purpose, scope, and business context.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The primary distribution channel served by this valuation area (retail stores, wholesale partners, e-commerce, outlet stores, franchise, or direct-to-consumer).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this valuation area ceased to be active, if applicable. Null for currently active valuation areas.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this valuation area became active and operational for inventory transactions.']
  general_ledger_account varchar [note: 'The general ledger account number to which inventory value in this valuation area is posted for financial reporting.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region or market served by this valuation area (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC, LATAM).']
  inventory_account_determination_group varchar [note: 'The account determination group used to automatically assign GL accounts for inventory transactions within this valuation area.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who last modified this valuation area record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this valuation area record was last updated or modified.']
  last_physical_inventory_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent physical inventory count conducted within this valuation area.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the valuation area (e.g., \'North America Distribution Center\', \'EMEA Retail Operations\').']
  never_out_of_stock_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this valuation area follows a never-out-of-stock strategy for critical SKUs to ensure continuous availability.']
  next_scheduled_inventory_date date [note: 'The date scheduled for the next physical inventory count or cycle count within this valuation area.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this valuation area.']
  plant_code varchar [note: 'The plant or facility code associated with this valuation area for manufacturing and logistics operations.']
  price_control_method varchar [note: 'The method used to control and update inventory prices within this valuation area (standard price, moving average price, or manual).']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The profit center code to which inventory in this valuation area is assigned for internal profitability analysis.']
  rfid_enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID tracking technology is enabled for inventory management within this valuation area.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the valuation area indicating whether it is operational and available for inventory transactions.']
  storage_location_code varchar [note: 'The storage location code within the plant where inventory is physically stored and valued.']
  third_party_logistics_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party logistics provider managing inventory within this valuation area, if applicable.']
  valuation_area_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the valuation area used in operational systems and reporting.']
  valuation_category varchar [note: 'Additional classification for grouping valuation areas by business segment, product category, or operational model.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'The inventory valuation method applied within this valuation area (standard cost, moving average, FIFO, LIFO, weighted average, or retail method).']
  valuation_control_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether inventory valuation is controlled centrally at company code level, decentralized at plant level, or hybrid.']
  valuation_date date [note: 'The date on which the current inventory valuation parameters became effective for this valuation area.']
  valuation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the valuation area indicating the organizational level at which inventory is valued (plant-level, company-code-level, storage-location-level, distribution center, warehouse, or retail store).']
  vendor_managed_inventory_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this valuation area participates in vendor-managed inventory programs where suppliers manage stock levels.']
  warehouse_management_system varchar [note: 'The warehouse management system used to manage inventory operations within this valuation area (e.g., Manhattan Associates, SAP EWM).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who created this valuation area record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for valuation_area. Referenced by valuation_area_id.'
}

Table merchandising.assortment_plan {
  assortment_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the assortment plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key generated by the lakehouse ingestion pipeline from Oracle Retail Merchandising System assortment planning module.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_agreement. Business justification: Assortment plans reference master sourcing agreements that govern vendor relationships, pricing tiers, and capacity allocation for planned collections. Essential for validating plan feasibility agains']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Assortment plans are sized to support seasonal marketing campaigns. Merchandisers need campaign timing, expected lift, and channel mix to plan OTB budgets and style counts. This link enables campaign-']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent collection or line plan in Centric PLM or Oracle Retail. Links the assortment plan to the upstream collection planning and product design lifecycle managed in Centric PLM collection management module.']
  collection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.collection_plan. Business justification: Assortment plans operationalize collection plans by defining the specific SKU-level assortments, store clusters, and channel strategies for a collection. The collection_plan defines the thematic visio']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Assortment plans are budgeted and tracked against cost centers for departmental P&L accountability. Merchandising teams must report plan performance (sales, margin, markdown) by cost center for financ']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Assortment plans target specific customer segments (Gen Z urban, affluent suburban, value-conscious families). Segment demographics and preferences drive category mix, price points, and style selectio']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Assortment plans have department_id, class_id, subclass_id but no FK to merchandise_hierarchy master. Adding merchandise_hierarchy_id FK normalizes merchandise classification and removes redundant hie']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: Assortment plans reference OTB budget amounts but have no FK to otb_budget master. Adding otb_budget_id FK normalizes budget reference and removes redundant otb_budget_amount. FK will reference mercha']
  primary_assortment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise planner (employee) responsible for creating and managing this assortment plan. The planner owns OTB budget reconciliation, STR tracking, and markdown decisions. Sourced from Workday HCM employee master. Serves as the PARTY_REFERENCE for this entity.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory. Business justification: Strategic assortment plans designate preferred factories based on capability, capacity, and cost structure. Drives sourcing strategy and factory capacity reservation for seasonal collections.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Assortment plans drive revenue and margin by channel/brand, tracked via profit centers. Financial reporting requires linking planned vs actual performance (sales, GMROI, STR) to profit centers for seg']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Assortment plans are inherently seasonal; season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant season_code. FK will reference merchandis']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_standard. Business justification: Assortment plans specify quality standards by product category, market, and channel. Standards define AQL levels, certifications, and testing requirements for planned assortments. Essential for qualit']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store cluster grouping used for assortment localization. Store clusters segment stores by volume tier, climate zone, or customer demographics to enable differentiated assortment depth and size curve optimization within Oracle Retail allocation module.']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Assortment plans specify sustainable material requirements for collections. The sustainability_certified flag exists but needs FK to define which certified materials are planned. Merchandisers select ']
  tertiary_assortment_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (typically a Director of Merchandising or VP of Planning) who approved the assortment plan and committed the OTB budget. Required for audit trail and financial governance under IFRS/GAAP internal controls.']
  allocation_strategy varchar [note: 'Inventory allocation methodology applied to this assortment plan. top_down = corporate-driven allocation to stores; bottom_up = store-requested replenishment; hybrid = combination approach; vmi = Vendor Managed Inventory (VMI) where the supplier manages replenishment. Drives Oracle Retail allocation module configuration.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the assortment plan was formally approved and the OTB budget was committed. Marks the transition from \'submitted\' to \'approved\' status and triggers downstream PO generation and T&A calendar activation in SAP S/4HANA and Infor PLM.']
  aur_target decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned Average Unit Retail (AUR) price for the assortment, representing the expected average selling price per unit across all styles and colorways in the plan. A key KPI in Anaplan merchandise financial planning used to project net sales and validate GMROI targets.']
  carry_forward_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of styles in this assortment plan that are carried forward from the prior season versus new introductions. A key collection planning metric used by merchants to balance newness investment against proven performers. Expressed as a percentage of total style count.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Distribution channel for which this assortment plan is defined. Drives channel-specific assortment depth, pricing strategy, and OTB budget allocation. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) includes both retail stores and e-commerce. Values: retail (brick-and-mortar stores), ecommerce (Salesforce Commerce Cloud digital storefront), wholesale (partner accounts), dtc (combined direct channels), outlet (off-price stores).']
  colorway_count_planned varchar [note: 'Number of distinct colorways planned across all styles in the assortment. Colorway depth is a key assortment decision in apparel fashion, balancing color story cohesion against inventory fragmentation risk.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the assortment plan record was first created in the Oracle Retail Merchandising System and ingested into the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED for this entity. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this assortment plan (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency wholesale and DTC channels in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and Anaplan.']
  imu_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Initial Markup (IMU) percentage for the assortment plan, expressed as a percentage of retail price. IMU = (Retail Price − Cost) / Retail Price × 100. Drives cost negotiation targets with suppliers and is a primary input to GMROI optimization in Anaplan merchandise financial planning.']
  is_final_version boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record represents the final approved version of the assortment plan (True) or a superseded draft/revision (False). Used to filter active plan versions in reporting and analytics without requiring version_number logic.']
  labor_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether suppliers fulfilling this assortment plan must hold active FLA (Fair Labor Association) or WRAP (Worldwide Responsible Accredited Production) certification. Enforced during supplier onboarding in Infor PLM sourcing and SAP S/4HANA MM vendor master.']
  markdown_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total markdown (MD) budget allocated to this assortment plan for planned price reductions during the selling season. Markdown budget is a key input to MMU calculation and is managed in Anaplan merchandise financial planning and Oracle Retail Price Management.']
  mmu_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Maintained Markup (MMU) percentage after accounting for planned markdowns (MD), shrinkage, and employee discounts. MMU reflects the realized margin and is the primary profitability metric tracked in Anaplan merchandise financial planning alongside GMROI.']
  msrp_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Highest Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) point within the assortment plan\'s price architecture. Defines the ceiling price tier and is used alongside msrp_min to establish the price range strategy and IMU targets for the planned assortment.']
  msrp_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lowest Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) point within the assortment plan\'s price architecture. Defines the entry price tier for the planned assortment and is used in IMU (Initial Markup) and AUR (Average Unit Retail) calculations in Oracle Retail Price Management.']
  otb_units_planned varchar [note: 'Total number of units planned for procurement under this assortment plan\'s OTB budget. Used to calculate average unit cost, validate MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) compliance with suppliers, and drive purchase order (PO) generation in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the assortment plan, assigned by the Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Used by merchants and planners to reference the plan across systems (e.g., ORM-AP-2025-FALL-WMN-001). Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this entity.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the assortment plan expires or the season closes. Marks the end of the active selling window and triggers end-of-season markdown (MD) and clearance processes. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_UNTIL for this entity. Nullable for open-ended NOS assortments.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the assortment plan (e.g., \'Fall 2025 Women\'s Outerwear – North America DTC\'). Used by merchants and planners to identify the plan in reporting dashboards and Anaplan merchandise financial planning views. Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this entity.']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'Date on which the assortment plan becomes effective for in-season execution — i.e., the first floor-set or go-live date for the planned assortment. Used to align T&A (Time and Action) calendar milestones and pre-production (PP) sample review schedules. Serves as the EFFECTIVE_FROM for this MASTER_AGREEMENT-role entity.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the assortment plan within the Oracle Retail Merchandising System workflow. Drives downstream OTB budget release, allocation activation, and collection planning milestones. Valid states: draft (initial creation), submitted (pending merchant approval), approved (OTB committed), active (in-season execution), closed (season ended), cancelled (plan voided). Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this entity.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the assortment plan by merchandise strategy. seasonal = time-bound seasonal collection; core = evergreen carry-forward styles; nos = Never Out of Stock (NOS) replenishment assortment; capsule = limited-edition capsule collection; collab = brand collaboration or licensed assortment. Drives OTB budget methodology and replenishment logic.']
  replenishment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether styles within this assortment plan are eligible for automated replenishment (True) or are one-time buys with no replenishment (False). NOS (Never Out of Stock) assortments are always True. Drives replenishment trigger logic in Oracle Retail allocation and Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  season_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit calendar year associated with the merchandise season (e.g., 2025). Used for year-over-year sell-through rate (STR) comparisons and GMROI trend analysis in Anaplan financial planning.']
  size_range varchar [note: 'Planned size range for the assortment (e.g., \'XS-3XL\', \'00-24\', \'5-13\'). Defines the inclusive size offering for the plan and is used to validate size curve alignment with store cluster demographics and FTC textile labeling requirements.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this assortment plan record originated. ORM = Oracle Retail Merchandising System; ANAPLAN = Anaplan Merchandise Planning; CENTRIC = Centric PLM; MANUAL = manually entered override. Used for data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  sourcing_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code of the primary manufacturing/sourcing country for this assortment plan (e.g., BGD=Bangladesh, VNM=Vietnam, CHN=China). Used for landed duty paid (LDP) cost modeling, HS Code tariff classification, and GSP eligibility assessment in SAP S/4HANA MM and Infor PLM sourcing.']
  str_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Sell-Through Rate (STR) for the assortment plan, expressed as a percentage of units sold versus units received. STR = (Units Sold / Units Received) × 100. Used to set end-of-season inventory clearance expectations and validate OTB buy depth in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.']
  style_count_planned varchar [note: 'Number of distinct styles (parent product silhouettes) planned in the assortment, independent of colorway or size. Style count is a key assortment breadth metric used by merchants to balance newness versus carry-forward depth in collection planning.']
  sustainability_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the assortment plan requires sustainability certification compliance (True) such as GOTS (Global Organic Textile Standard), OEKO-TEX Standard 100, or BCI (Better Cotton Initiative). Drives supplier qualification requirements in Infor PLM sourcing and compliance reporting.']
  total_sku_count_planned varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) planned within this assortment, representing the breadth of the assortment. Includes all style-color-size combinations. Used to assess assortment productivity and compare planned vs. actual SKU rationalization outcomes.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the assortment plan record in the source system or Silver Layer. Tracks plan revisions, OTB reforecasts, and status transitions. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED for this entity. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version number of the assortment plan, starting at 1. Incremented each time the plan is revised (e.g., OTB reforecast, style additions/deletions). Enables version history tracking and comparison of plan iterations in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.']
  wos_target decimal(5,1) [note: 'Target Weeks of Supply (WOS) for the assortment plan, representing the planned number of weeks of inventory coverage at the expected rate of sale. WOS = On-Hand Units / Average Weekly Sales. Used to calibrate buy depth and replenishment triggers in Oracle Retail allocation module.']

  Note: 'Master assortment planning entity capturing the seasonal merchandise assortment strategy by department, class, and subclass. Defines which styles, colorways, and sizes will be carried per channel and store cluster, aligned with OTB budgets and collection planning cycles. Sourced from Oracle Retail Merchandising System assortment planning module.'
}

Table merchandising.collection_plan {
  collection_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the seasonal collection plan record. Primary key for the collection_plan data product.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_agreement. Business justification: Collection plans built on foundation of sourcing agreements that define capacity, pricing structures, and terms with key vendors. Critical for validating collection feasibility against contracted vend']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand under which this collection is planned and marketed (e.g., athletic, lifestyle, luxury sub-brand). Supports multi-brand portfolio planning and brand-level GMROI reporting.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Collection plans are coordinated with marketing campaigns for go-to-market execution. Merchandisers need to align first delivery dates, sell windows, and channel allocation with campaign flights. This']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_certification. Business justification: Collections track sustainability_rating and sustainable_material_pct; must link to certifications (GOTS, OEKO-TEX, Fair Trade) proving marketing claims and preventing greenwashing violations.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: Merchandising collection plans are built from approved design concepts that define creative direction, theme, and product mix. Line review and range planning processes require tracing collection finan']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Collection development costs (design, sampling, line review, travel) are charged to merchandising cost centers. Finance requires this link to allocate collection planning expenses and track budget vs ']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Collections are designed for target customer segments (athleisure for active millennials, workwear for professionals). Segment personas inform design briefs, price architecture, and channel allocation']
  division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise division (e.g., Womens, Mens, Kids, Accessories) to which this collection plan belongs. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System merchandise hierarchy.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Collection plans have department_id but no FK to merchandise_hierarchy master. Adding merchandise_hierarchy_id FK normalizes merchandise classification and removes redundant department_id. FK will ref']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.otb_budget. Business justification: Collection plans reference OTB budget amounts but have no FK to otb_budget master. Adding otb_budget_id FK normalizes budget reference and removes redundant otb_budget_amount. FK will reference mercha']
  primary_collection_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the creative director or lead designer responsible for the thematic direction of this collection plan. Links to workforce records in Workday HCM.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory. Business justification: Collection plans specify factory partners based on technical capability, quality standards, and capacity. Critical for collection development, costing, and production timeline planning.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Collections generate revenue by brand/channel, tracked via profit centers for margin analysis. Financial reporting requires linking planned collection revenue and gross margin to profit centers for br']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Collection plans are seasonal by nature; season_code and season_year exist but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant attributes. FK will referen']
  seasonal_launch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.seasonal_launch. Business justification: Collections are introduced through seasonal launch events. This is the primary coordination mechanism between merchandising (product readiness, delivery dates) and marketing (launch date, embargo, PR ']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_standard. Business justification: Collections define quality tier (luxury vs. value) and sustainability standards. Quality standards cascade to all styles in collection. Critical for brand positioning, regulatory compliance, and quali']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Collections track sustainable_material_pct and sustainability_rating against corporate sustainability targets. Merchandisers must align collection plans with specific company-wide sustainability goals']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the business stakeholder (e.g., VP Merchandising, Creative Director) who formally approved this collection plan. Supports audit trail and governance requirements.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the collection plan received formal business approval. Marks the transition from planning to execution phase and triggers downstream T&A calendar activities.']
  channel_allocation_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned channel distribution strategy for this collection. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) First = prioritize own retail and e-commerce; Wholesale First = prioritize wholesale partners; Balanced = equal distribution. Drives allocation decisions in Oracle Retail and Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  collection_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the collection plan across systems (e.g., PLM, ERP, Anaplan). Used as the business identifier in cross-system references and EDI communications.']
  collection_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the merchandise collection (e.g., \'Spring/Summer 2025 Active\', \'Holiday Resort 2024\'). Used in creative briefs, line sheets, and wholesale presentations.']
  collection_theme varchar [note: 'Descriptive thematic concept or creative narrative for the collection (e.g., \'Urban Nomad\', \'Coastal Luxe\', \'Performance Elevated\'). Guides design direction, marketing storytelling, and visual merchandising.']
  collection_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the collection by commercial strategy. Mainline = full seasonal assortment; Capsule = focused limited assortment; Collaboration = co-branded with external partner; Limited_Edition = restricted volume; Carry_Over = NOS (Never Out of Stock) continuity styles; Core = foundational basics.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this collection plan record was first created in the system. Provides audit trail for record origination and supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary planning amounts in this collection plan (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations.']
  design_brief_url varchar [note: 'URL or document path to the creative design brief for this collection plan stored in Centric PLM or a connected document management system. Provides access to mood boards, color palettes, and thematic direction.']
  dtc_allocation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned percentage of total collection inventory allocated to Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) channels (own retail stores and e-commerce). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6000 = 60%). Used in channel allocation planning.']
  first_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date of the first merchandise delivery to distribution centers or stores for this collection. Anchors the T&A (Time and Action) calendar and drives pre-production planning milestones.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'Primary gender demographic target for this collection plan. Drives assortment architecture, sizing standards, and marketing segmentation. Aligns with Oracle Retail merchandise hierarchy gender classification.']
  imu_target_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Target Initial Markup (IMU) percentage for the collection, representing the planned markup from cost to MSRP at the time of first receipt. A foundational pricing metric in merchandise financial planning.']
  last_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date of the final merchandise delivery for this collection. Defines the end of the inbound supply window and informs markdown timing and end-of-season clearance planning.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this collection plan record. Supports change tracking, incremental data loading, and audit compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  line_architecture_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the line architecture document associated with this collection plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Tracks iterative changes to style count, category mix, and price tier targets during the planning process in Centric PLM.']
  otb_lock_date date [note: 'Date on which the OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget for this collection plan is locked and committed. After this date, budget changes require formal approval. Managed in Anaplan Merchandise Planning.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the collection plan record. Drives workflow routing in Centric PLM and Anaplan. Draft = initial creation; In_Review = pending creative/commercial sign-off; Approved = locked for production; Active = in-season execution; Closed = season ended; Cancelled = plan abandoned.']
  planned_aur_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned Average Unit Retail (AUR) price for the collection in the planning currency. Represents the expected average selling price per unit across all styles and channels, used in revenue and unit planning.']
  planned_cogs_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for this collection plan in the planning currency. Used to calculate planned gross margin and GMROI targets in Anaplan financial planning.']
  planned_gross_margin_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned gross margin percentage for the collection, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.6200 = 62.00%). Derived from planned revenue and COGS targets; stored as a planning input in Anaplan to support IMU (Initial Markup) and MMU (Maintained Markup) targets.']
  planned_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned net sales revenue target for this collection plan in the planning currency. Used as the top-line financial KPI for merchandise financial planning and GMROI optimization in Anaplan.']
  planned_str_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Target Sell-Through Rate (STR) for the collection, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.8500 = 85%). Represents the planned percentage of received inventory expected to sell at full price or planned markdown cadence before end of season.']
  price_tier_distribution varchar [note: 'Planned percentage distribution of styles across price tiers (e.g., \'Entry:20%|Mid:50%|Premium:30%\'). Guides assortment architecture to balance accessibility and margin. Stored as a structured string; detailed tier breakdowns managed in Anaplan.']
  product_category_mix varchar [note: 'Planned distribution of styles across product categories within the collection (e.g., \'Tops:35%|Bottoms:25%|Outerwear:20%|Footwear:15%|Accessories:5%\'). Stored as a structured string; detailed breakdowns managed in Anaplan and Centric PLM.']
  range_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the formal range review meeting where the collection plan is presented to commercial and creative stakeholders for approval. A key milestone in the T&A (Time and Action) calendar.']
  season_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the retail season type for this collection plan. Drives line architecture, delivery cadence, and OTB budget allocation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Spring/Summer|Fall/Winter|Resort|Pre-Fall|Holiday|Cruise — promote to reference product if additional seasons are added]']
  sell_end_date date [note: 'Planned date when the collection is retired from active selling. Triggers end-of-season markdown strategy and clearance allocation. Effective-until date for the collection\'s commercial lifecycle.']
  sell_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when the collection becomes available for sale to consumers across channels. Aligns with marketing campaign launch and store floor set dates. Effective-from date for the collection\'s commercial lifecycle.']
  sustainability_rating varchar [note: 'Planned sustainability certification tier for the collection based on material sourcing and production standards. Reflects compliance with GOTS, BCI, OEKO-TEX, and FLA standards. Drives supplier selection and marketing claims.']
  sustainable_material_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned percentage of styles in the collection using certified sustainable materials (organic cotton, recycled polyester, BCI cotton, etc.). Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4000 = 40%). Supports ESG reporting and FTC textile labeling compliance.']
  target_market_region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic market region(s) for which this collection is planned (e.g., \'NAM\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', \'GLOBAL\'). Influences sizing standards, labeling requirements, and channel allocation strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: NAM|EMEA|APAC|LATAM|GLOBAL|DOMESTIC — promote to reference product]']
  target_sku_count varchar [note: 'Planned total number of SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) across all styles, colors, and sizes in this collection. Drives warehouse capacity planning, OTB budget, and assortment depth decisions.']
  target_style_count varchar [note: 'Planned number of distinct styles (SKU families) to be included in this collection. A key line architecture metric used to manage assortment breadth and design resource allocation in Centric PLM.']
  wholesale_allocation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned percentage of total collection inventory allocated to wholesale and distribution partners. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.4000 = 40%). Complements DTC allocation to sum to 100% of planned inventory.']

  Note: 'Seasonal collection planning master record defining the thematic merchandise collection (e.g., Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter, Resort, Pre-Fall) including line architecture, style count targets, price tier distribution, and channel allocation strategy. Managed in Centric PLM and Anaplan, bridging creative direction with commercial planning.'
}

Table merchandising.otb_budget {
  otb_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying each Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for the otb_budget data product.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: OTB budgets are financial budgets controlling merchandise spend. Finance requires direct linkage for budget vs actual reporting, variance analysis, and consolidation of merchandise budgets into corpor']
  merchandise_financial_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_financial_plan. Business justification: OTB budgets are the tactical monthly execution of the strategic merchandise financial plan. The MFP sets top-down financial targets (planned sales, COGS, GM%, IMU%, markdown budget) for a season/depar']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: OTB budgets have department_id, class_id but no FK to merchandise_hierarchy master. Adding merchandise_hierarchy_id FK normalizes merchandise classification and removes redundant hierarchy attributes.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the channel or location (e.g., retail store, e-commerce, wholesale) for which this OTB budget is defined. Enables channel-level OTB tracking across DTC and wholesale.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise season (e.g., Spring/Summer 2025, Fall/Winter 2025) to which this OTB budget belongs. Drives seasonal buy plan alignment in Anaplan.']
  actual_receipts_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual merchandise receipt value at cost consumed by confirmed purchase orders (POs) for the OTB budget month to date. Used for open-to-buy cost-basis tracking and cash flow management. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  actual_receipts_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual merchandise receipt value at retail price consumed by confirmed buy plans and purchase orders (POs) for the OTB budget month to date. Compared against planned receipts to compute remaining OTB. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  alert_threshold_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'The variance threshold percentage (e.g., 0.10 = 10%) at which a merchant alert is triggered for this OTB budget record. When actual receipts or sales deviate from plan by more than this threshold, an alert is raised for merchant review and OTB recalculation.']
  alert_triggered boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a merchant alert has been triggered for this OTB budget record due to variance exceeding the defined alert threshold. True = alert active and pending merchant review; False = within acceptable variance range.']
  anaplan_model_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the Anaplan model and workspace from which this OTB budget record was sourced. Enables traceability back to the originating Anaplan planning model for reconciliation and audit.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the merchant or planning manager who approved this OTB budget record. Required for financial control governance and audit trail.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which this OTB budget record was formally approved by the merchant or planning manager. Marks the transition from submitted to approved status and authorizes buying activity.']
  bom_inventory_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of on-hand inventory at cost (landed duty paid) at the beginning of the OTB budget month. Used for COGS tracking and gross margin analysis. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  bom_inventory_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The value of on-hand inventory at retail (selling) price at the beginning of the OTB budget month. Used as the opening inventory position in the OTB formula: OTB = Planned EOM Inventory + Planned Sales + Planned Markdowns - BOM Inventory. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  budget_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month (1–12) to which this OTB budget record applies. OTB is recalculated monthly; this field identifies the specific planning month within the budget year.']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the OTB budget record. Draft: under construction by planner. Submitted: pending merchant approval. Approved: authorized for buying. Locked: frozen for the period. Closed: period ended. Revised: re-opened for adjustment.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the OTB budget record, incremented each time the budget is revised or re-submitted. Supports version control and audit trail for merchandise financial planning changes in Anaplan.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal or calendar year (e.g., 2025) to which this OTB budget record applies. Used for annual merchandise financial planning and year-over-year variance analysis.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The distribution channel for which this OTB budget is defined. Retail: physical stores. E-Commerce: digital storefront. Wholesale: B2B partners. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer): brand-owned channels. Outlet: off-price stores.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this OTB budget record was first created in the Databricks Silver Layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and compliance with IFRS/GAAP record-keeping requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this OTB budget record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent currency denomination across retail and cost values.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The last date through which this OTB budget record is effective. Typically the last day of the budget month. After this date, the budget is closed and actuals are reconciled.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The first date from which this OTB budget record is effective and binding for merchandise purchasing decisions. Typically the first day of the budget month.']
  eom_inventory_target_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The planned ending inventory value at retail price at the end of the OTB budget month. This is the target stock position that feeds into the next month\'s BOM inventory and is a key driver of the OTB formula. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  imu_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'The Initial Markup (IMU) percentage applied to convert retail values to cost values for this OTB budget period. IMU = (Retail - Cost) / Retail. Used to derive OTB at cost from OTB at retail and to assess margin health.']
  last_recalculation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the OTB budget values were last recalculated in Anaplan. Supports audit trail and ensures merchants are working with the most current OTB position.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes entered by the merchant or planner to document assumptions, exceptions, or rationale for OTB budget decisions (e.g., promotional event impact, supply chain disruption, vendor delay). Supports audit trail and planning transparency.']
  otb_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining open-to-buy dollars at cost available for the OTB budget month. Derived from OTB at retail adjusted by the Initial Markup (IMU) percentage. Used for purchase order (PO) commitment and cash flow management. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  otb_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining open-to-buy dollars at retail price available for the OTB budget month. Calculated as: Planned EOM Inventory + Planned Sales + Planned Markdowns - BOM Inventory - Actual Receipts at Retail. A positive value indicates buying capacity; negative indicates over-bought. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  planned_gmroi decimal(10,4) [note: 'The planned Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) for the OTB budget period. GMROI = Gross Margin / Average Inventory at Cost. Measures the return generated per dollar of inventory investment. Used to prioritize department and class-level OTB allocations.']
  planned_markdown_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The planned markdown (MD) dollar amount at retail for the OTB budget month. Markdowns reduce the retail inventory value and are a key input to the OTB formula and Maintained Markup (MMU) calculation. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  planned_receipts_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total planned merchandise receipt value at cost (landed duty paid / LDP) for the OTB budget month. Used for cash flow planning, purchase order (PO) commitment tracking, and COGS forecasting. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  planned_receipts_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total planned merchandise receipt value at retail price for the OTB budget month. Represents the maximum retail value of goods the merchant plans to receive. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  planned_sales_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The planned net sales revenue at retail price for the OTB budget month, as defined in the merchandise financial plan. Drives the OTB calculation and sell-through rate (STR) targets. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  planned_sales_units varchar [note: 'The planned number of units to be sold during the OTB budget month. Used alongside planned sales at retail to derive the Average Unit Retail (AUR) and validate unit-level buy quantities.']
  planned_sell_through_rate decimal(7,4) [note: 'The planned Sell-Through Rate (STR) for the OTB budget month, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.75 = 75%). STR = Units Sold / (Beginning Inventory Units + Received Units). Used to validate receipt plans and assess inventory productivity.']
  planned_weeks_of_supply decimal(7,2) [note: 'The planned Weeks of Supply (WOS) at the end of the OTB budget month. WOS = EOM Inventory Units / Average Weekly Sales Units. Used to assess inventory coverage and prevent stockouts or overstock situations.']
  recalculation_trigger_date date [note: 'The date on which the weekly OTB recalculation was last triggered for this budget record. OTB is recalculated weekly in Anaplan based on updated actual receipts, sales actuals, and revised forecasts.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this OTB budget record originated. Anaplan is the primary planning system; Oracle Retail provides actuals integration; SAP S/4HANA provides cost and PO data; Manual indicates planner-entered overrides.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this OTB budget record was last modified in the Databricks Silver Layer. Tracks the most recent update for change management, audit trail, and downstream pipeline refresh detection.']
