  variance_receipts_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The variance between planned receipts at retail and actual receipts at retail for the OTB budget month. Positive = under-received vs plan; Negative = over-received vs plan. Triggers merchant alerts when variance exceeds the defined threshold. Expressed in the operating currency.']
  variance_sales_retail decimal(18,2) [note: 'The variance between planned sales at retail and actual sales at retail for the OTB budget month. Used to assess sales performance against plan and trigger OTB recalculation when variance exceeds the alert threshold. Expressed in the operating currency.']

  Note: 'Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget master record tracking the planned versus actual merchandise purchasing budget by department, class, season, and month. Captures beginning-of-month (BOM) inventory at retail and cost, planned sales, planned markdowns, planned receipts, actual receipts consumed by buy plans, ending inventory targets, and remaining open-to-buy dollars. Includes weekly OTB recalculation triggers and variance thresholds for merchant alerts. Core financial control mechanism preventing over-buying and ensuring inventory investment aligns with sales plans. Managed in Anaplan with Oracle Retail integration.'
}

Table merchandising.buy_plan {
  buy_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated surrogate identifier for the seasonal merchandise buy plan record. Primary key for the buy_plan data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  assortment_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.assortment_plan. Business justification: Buy plans execute the assortment plan by committing to specific purchase quantities, vendors, costs, and receipt timing. The assortment_plan defines WHAT to buy (styles, SKU counts, target units); the']
  awarded_vendor_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.vendor_quote. Business justification: Buy plans are awarded based on accepted vendor quotes. Tracks which specific quote was selected for procurement, enabling cost reconciliation and vendor performance tracking. Essential for procurement']
  buyer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Buy plans are owned by merchandise buyers who negotiate vendor terms, manage procurement strategy, and approve purchase commitments. Essential for accountability, approval workflows, and buyer perform']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Buy plans allocate inventory to support marketing campaigns. Buyers need campaign timing, expected conversion lift, and channel mix to size buys and set receipt dates. This link enables campaign-drive']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_certification. Business justification: Buy plans have sustainability_certification text field; replacing with FK to compliance_certification enables validation that vendor/factory holds required certifications before PO issuance.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Buy plan costs (product development, sourcing overhead, sample costs) are allocated to merchandising cost centers. Finance requires this link to track departmental expenses and allocate sourcing costs']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Buy plans drive pre-production and inline inspections at vendor factories. Inspection results gate PO confirmation and receipt acceptance. Critical for vendor performance tracking and quality gate enf']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Buy plans have department_id, class_id, subclass_id but no FK to merchandise_hierarchy master. Adding merchandise_hierarchy_id FK normalizes merchandise classification and removes redundant hierarchy ']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Open-to-Buy budget authorization record that funds this buy plan. Links financial OTB authorization in Anaplan to actual purchase order execution in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory. Business justification: Buy plans specify manufacturing factory for sourcing decisions, capacity booking, and lead time planning. Essential for procurement and production planning processes in apparel sourcing.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Buy plans drive inventory investment and future margin by channel/brand, tracked via profit centers. Financial planning requires linking buy plan commitments to profit centers for forward-looking marg']
  quality_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_audit. Business justification: Factory audit scores gate new buy plan approvals and sourcing decisions. Audit results determine vendor eligibility for new orders. Essential for responsible sourcing, compliance risk management, and ']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Buy plans with HS code and country_of_origin require import/export regulatory filings (FDA, CPSC, EPA); links plan to actual filing for customs clearance tracking.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination location (store, distribution center, or warehouse) for which this buy plan is created. Used for allocation planning and receipt flow scheduling in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.rfq. Business justification: Buy plans originate from RFQ processes when sourcing new styles. Tracks which RFQ led to the buy decision, enabling cost variance analysis between quoted and planned costs. Essential for procurement a']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Buy plans are seasonal procurement plans; season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant season_code. FK will reference merchandis']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Buy plans convert to sourcing POs upon approval. Tracks execution of planned buys and enables plan-vs-actual reconciliation for cost, timing, and quantity variances. Critical for procurement performan']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product style being planned in this buy plan. Links to the PLM-managed style master in Infor PLM / Centric PLM. A buy plan may cover one or multiple styles depending on planning granularity.']
  supplier_esg_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.supplier_esg_assessment. Business justification: Buy plans require vendor ESG compliance verification before approval. The sustainability_certification flag exists but needs link to actual assessment records. Buyers must verify supplier ESG scores m']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary supplier/vendor committed to fulfilling this buy plan. Links to the vendor master in SAP S/4HANA MM. Drives purchase order creation and supplier commitment tracking.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Links merchandising buy plan to production work order for order fulfillment tracking, production status visibility, and delivery date management. Core integration point between merchandising and produ']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the merchandise planning manager or director who approved this buy plan. Required for audit trail and financial authorization compliance.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date on which the buy plan was formally approved by the authorized merchandise planning manager. Triggers OTB budget consumption and purchase order release authorization in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  buy_plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the buy plan, used in cross-system communication with Oracle Retail Merchandising System, Anaplan, and SAP S/4HANA MM. Follows the format BP-{YEAR}-{ALPHANUMERIC_CODE}.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Merchandise distribution channel for which this buy plan is created. Drives channel-specific assortment and allocation strategies. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) includes both retail stores and e-commerce. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail|ecommerce|wholesale|dtc|outlet|marketplace — promote to reference product]']
  colorway_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct colorways (color/material combinations) included in this buy plan. Used for assortment breadth analysis and collection planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the merchandise is manufactured. Required for customs duty calculation, HS Code classification, GSP eligibility, and FTC textile labeling compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this buy plan record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and compliance reporting requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this buy plan (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency sourcing operations across global vendor base.']
  ex_factory_date date [note: 'Planned date on which finished goods are expected to leave the manufacturing facility (ex-factory). Key milestone in the T&A (Time and Action) calendar. Used to calculate transit lead times and planned receipt dates.']
  floor_set_date date [note: 'Planned date for merchandise to be on the retail floor or live on the e-commerce site. Aligns with the seasonal collection launch calendar and marketing campaign timing.']
  fob_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Free on Board unit cost agreed with the vendor, representing the product cost at the point of origin before freight, duties, and insurance. Used as the base cost in COGS and IMU calculations. Expressed in the plan currency.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System tariff classification code for the merchandise. Used for customs duty rate determination, import compliance, and LDP cost calculation. Required for cross-border trade documentation.']
  imu_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Initial Markup percentage calculated as (MSRP - LDP Cost) / MSRP * 100. Represents the planned gross margin at the time of the buy decision. Key financial KPI in merchandise financial planning reviewed in Anaplan.']
  in_dc_date date [note: 'Planned date for merchandise to arrive at the distribution center. Drives inbound scheduling in Manhattan Associates WMS and store allocation timing. Critical for floor-set and launch date compliance.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) governing the transfer of risk and cost responsibility between buyer and vendor for this buy plan shipment. Determines the point at which ownership and risk transfer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|EXW|DDP|FCA|CFR|DAP — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the merchandise in this buy plan is an exclusive product (e.g., retailer-exclusive colorway, channel-exclusive style). Exclusives may have different MOQ, pricing, and allocation rules.']
  is_nos boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this buy plan is for a Never Out of Stock (NOS) / core replenishment item that is carried across multiple seasons, as opposed to a seasonal fashion buy. NOS items follow continuous replenishment planning rules.']
  ldp_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Landed Duty Paid unit cost inclusive of FOB cost, ocean/air freight, customs duties, insurance, and port handling charges. Represents the true total cost of goods at the destination DC. Used for COGS, GMROI, and IMU calculations.']
  moq varchar [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity committed to the vendor for this buy plan. Sourced from vendor negotiation terms in SAP S/4HANA MM. Buy plan units must meet or exceed MOQ to trigger purchase order creation.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price for the planned style. Used as the reference retail price for IMU (Initial Markup) and AUR (Average Unit Retail) calculations in merchandise financial planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for merchandiser annotations, buyer comments, or special instructions related to this buy plan (e.g., vendor negotiation notes, allocation constraints, marketing tie-ins).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Vendor payment terms agreed for this buy plan (e.g., LC 90 days, Net 60, FOB 30). Includes Letter of Credit (LC) terms where applicable. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM vendor payment terms configuration.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the buy plan within the merchandise planning workflow. Drives approval routing in Anaplan and purchase order release authorization in SAP S/4HANA MM. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|locked|cancelled|closed — promote to reference product if additional states are required]']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the buy plan iteration. \'initial\' = first-pass seasonal buy, \'revised\' = updated after market feedback, \'final\' = locked for PO execution, \'carryover\' = NOS (Never Out of Stock) replenishment plan.']
  planned_receipt_end_date date [note: 'Latest planned date for all merchandise receipts under this buy plan to be completed. Defines the receipt window for seasonal floor-set readiness and OTB period closure.']
  planned_receipt_start_date date [note: 'Earliest planned date for merchandise receipts to arrive at the destination DC or store. Drives the receipt flow schedule and warehouse inbound planning in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  planned_str_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned Sell-Through Rate (STR) percentage for this buy plan, representing the expected proportion of units sold at full price by end of season. Used as a key input for OTB budget sizing and markdown risk assessment in Anaplan.']
  planned_total_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned cost commitment for this buy plan, calculated as planned_units × ldp_cost. Represents the OTB budget consumption amount in the merchandise financial plan. Expressed in the plan currency.']
  planned_total_retail decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total planned retail value of the buy plan, calculated as planned_units × MSRP. Used for OTB retail budget tracking and GMROI planning in Anaplan merchandise financial planning.']
  planned_units varchar [note: 'Total number of units planned for purchase in this buy plan across all sizes and colorways. Represents the gross buy quantity before MOQ adjustments. Used as the primary quantity driver for OTB consumption calculation.']
  po_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA MM Purchase Order number generated from this buy plan upon approval. Links the financial buy plan to the operational procurement execution. A buy plan may generate one or multiple POs.']
  size_range varchar [note: 'Size range covered by this buy plan (e.g., XS-XXL, 6-12, 28-38). Represents the full size spectrum planned for purchase. Detailed size-level quantity breakdowns are managed in the buy plan line items.']
  sourcing_type varchar [note: 'Manufacturing sourcing model for this buy plan. CMT (Cut Make Trim) = vendor provides labor only; OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) = vendor manufactures to spec; ODM (Original Design Manufacturer) = vendor designs and manufactures; direct = owned factory.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this buy plan record. Used for change tracking, incremental data pipeline processing, and audit compliance. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  wos_target decimal(7,2) [note: 'Target Weeks of Supply (WOS) for this buy plan, representing the planned inventory coverage in weeks at the expected rate of sale. Used to calibrate receipt flow timing and prevent over/under-buying.']

  Note: 'Seasonal merchandise buy plan capturing planned purchase quantities, costs, receipt timing, and supplier commitments by style, colorway, size, and vendor. Includes MOQ commitments, FOB and LDP cost calculations, planned receipt flow by week, and OTB budget consumption tracking. Links financial OTB authorization to actual purchase order execution. Drives procurement workflows in SAP S/4HANA MM.'
}

Table merchandising.buy_plan_line {

  trade_compliance_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_record.   vendor_cost_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.vendor_cost_quote. Business justification: Line-level cost traceability from vendor quote to buy plan.   vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier (vendor/manufacturer) allocated to produce and deliver the units on this buy plan line. Supports supplier allocation analysis and sourcing strategy reporting.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Line-level work order link enables granular production tracking, variance analysis between planned and produced quantities, and SKU-level delivery status. Essential for inventory accuracy and fulfillm']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this buy plan line was formally approved by the merchandising team, authorizing the purchase commitment and OTB charge. Distinct from creation and update timestamps as it marks a specific business lifecycle event.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'The distribution channel for which this buy plan line is intended: DTC (Direct-to-Consumer retail stores), WHOLESALE (wholesale partners), ECOMM (e-commerce), OUTLET, or FRANCHISE. Drives channel-specific allocation and OTB management.']
  colorway_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the specific colorway (color variant) of the style being purchased on this line. Colorways are defined in the PLM system and drive assortment breadth decisions in collection planning.']
  confirmed_units varchar [note: 'The number of units formally confirmed with the supplier via Purchase Order (PO). May differ from planned or revised units due to Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) constraints or supplier capacity limitations.']
  cost_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the cost basis for the unit cost on this line: CMT (Cut Make Trim), FOB (Free on Board), LDP (Landed Duty Paid), DDP (Delivered Duty Paid), or EXW (Ex Works). Determines what logistics and duty costs are included in the unit cost figure.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating where the goods on this buy plan line are manufactured. Required for customs declarations, HS Code classification, duty calculation, and FTC country-of-origin labeling compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this buy plan line record was first created in the system, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail requirements and data lineage tracking.']
  fabric_content varchar [note: 'The fiber/material composition of the product on this buy plan line (e.g., 100% Cotton, 60% Polyester 40% Nylon). Required for FTC textile labeling compliance, OEKO-TEX certification tracking, and sustainability reporting under GOTS/BCI standards.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System (HS) Code classifying the product for international trade, customs duty assessment, and import/export compliance. Required for cross-border shipments and duty calculation under the World Customs Organization framework.']
  initial_markup_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Initial Markup (IMU) percentage for this buy plan line, calculated as (MSRP - Unit Cost) / MSRP. Represents the planned gross margin at full retail price before any markdowns. Key metric in merchandise financial planning.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this buy plan line represents an exclusive product (e.g., retailer-exclusive colorway or style) that is not available through other distribution channels. Affects pricing strategy and wholesale allocation decisions.']
  is_never_out_of_stock boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this buy plan line is designated as a Never Out of Stock (NOS) item, meaning it is a core replenishment SKU that must be continuously available. NOS items receive priority in allocation and replenishment planning.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the parent buy plan, used to order and reference individual SKU-level purchase commitments within a single seasonal buy plan.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'The current workflow status of this buy plan line, tracking its lifecycle from initial planning through receipt. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|approved|po_issued|in_production|shipped|received|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  markdown_allowance_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The planned markdown (MD) allowance percentage for this buy plan line, representing the anticipated price reduction from MSRP to clear end-of-season inventory. Used in Maintained Markup (MMU) calculations and merchandise financial planning.']
  moq varchar [note: 'The Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required by the supplier for this buy plan line. Constrains the minimum units that can be ordered and may cause confirmed units to differ from planned units. Critical for buy plan feasibility analysis.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the SKU on this buy plan line. Used to calculate Initial Markup (IMU) and supports wholesale pricing, retail pricing strategy, and GMROI analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments associated with this buy plan line, capturing merchandiser annotations, supplier negotiation notes, or special handling instructions not captured in structured fields.']
  original_cancel_date date [note: 'The original date by which the purchase order for this buy plan line must be cancelled if the supplier cannot fulfill. Established at the time of initial buy plan creation as part of the Time and Action (T&A) calendar.']
  planned_receipt_date date [note: 'The planned date on which the units for this buy plan line are expected to be received at the distribution center or warehouse. Drives inventory flow planning, OTB timing, and Weeks of Supply (WOS) calculations.']
  planned_sell_through_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'The planned Sell-Through Rate (STR) percentage for this buy plan line, representing the expected proportion of units sold at full price or within the season. Used as a benchmark for post-season performance analysis and future buy plan calibration.']
  planned_units varchar [note: 'The total number of units planned for purchase on this buy plan line for the season. Represents the initial buy commitment before any revisions. Core quantity field for OTB tracking and inventory planning.']
  pre_production_sample_status varchar [note: 'The approval status of the Pre-Production (PP) sample for this buy plan line. PP sample approval is a prerequisite for production release in the T&A calendar. Tracked in the PLM system.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'The high-level product category for this buy plan line (e.g., Tops, Bottoms, Outerwear, Footwear, Accessories). Used for cross-category OTB analysis and assortment balance reporting.']
  purchase_currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the unit cost is denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Required for multi-currency buy plan consolidation and financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.']
  revised_cancel_date date [note: 'The most recently revised cancel date for this buy plan line, updated during the T&A (Time and Action) calendar review process. Tracks changes to delivery commitments and supplier negotiation outcomes.']
  revised_units varchar [note: 'The most recently revised unit quantity for this buy plan line, reflecting any adjustments made after the initial plan was set. Enables tracking of buy plan changes against original commitments and OTB impact analysis.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The merchandising season code to which this buy plan line belongs (e.g., SS25 for Spring/Summer 2025, FW25 for Fall/Winter 2025). Drives collection planning, OTB budgeting, and sell-through rate (STR) analysis by season.']
  size_code varchar [note: 'The size designation for this buy plan line (e.g., XS, S, M, L, XL, 28x30, 7.5). Represents the specific size within the size run being committed to in this purchase. Drives size curve analysis and allocation planning.']
  size_run_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this buy plan line represents a full size run, partial size run, core size run (best-selling sizes only), or extended size run (including plus/petite). Drives assortment breadth decisions and size curve planning.']
  style_number varchar [note: 'The alphanumeric style identifier assigned during product design and development in the PLM system. Represents the base product design before colorway and size differentiation. Used for cross-SKU reporting and collection planning.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The negotiated cost per unit for this buy plan line, expressed in the purchase currency. May represent CMT (Cut Make Trim) cost or FOB (Free on Board) cost depending on the sourcing arrangement. Core input for COGS and GMROI calculations.']
  upc varchar [note: 'The Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the specific SKU on this buy plan line. Used for downstream POS scanning, warehouse receiving, and EDI transactions with suppliers.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this buy plan line record was last modified, in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Tracks the most recent change for audit and data freshness monitoring.']

  Note: 'Line-level detail of the seasonal buy plan representing individual SKU-level purchase commitments within a buy plan. Captures style number, colorway, size run, planned units, unit cost (CMT/FOB), planned receipt date, and supplier allocation. Enables granular tracking of buy plan execution against OTB.'
}

Table merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy {
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each node in the merchandise hierarchy. Primary key for the merchandise_hierarchy data product in the Oracle Retail Merchandising System (ORM) silver layer.']
  labeling_requirement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.labeling_requirement. Business justification: Each merchandise class/subclass has specific FTC/CPSC labeling requirements (children\'s sleepwear, wool products, fur); hierarchy determines applicable label rules for all products in category.']
  parent_hierarchy_merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing identifier pointing to the immediate parent node in the merchandise hierarchy. A Division node has a null parent; a Department references its Division; a Class references its Department; a Subclass references its Class. Enables hierarchical roll-up for OTB, GMROI, and financial planning.']
  channel_applicability varchar [note: 'Indicates which sales channels this merchandise hierarchy node applies to: all channels, retail stores, e-commerce (DTC digital), wholesale, or direct-to-consumer (DTC). Governs channel-specific assortment planning, OTB allocation, and pricing strategies in Oracle Retail and Salesforce Commerce Cloud.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the Class level node within a Department (e.g., \'JKT\' for Jackets, \'PNT\' for Pants). Null for Division and Department-level records. Used in Oracle Retail for class-level assortment planning, price management, and STR analysis.']
  class_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the Class (e.g., \'Jackets\', \'Pants\', \'T-Shirts\', \'Sneakers\'). Null for Division and Department-level records. Displayed in Oracle Retail assortment planning, allocation, and price management modules.']
  cpsc_regulated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this merchandise hierarchy node are subject to Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) safety standards (e.g., childrens apparel flammability, drawstring regulations). Triggers mandatory safety testing and compliance documentation requirements in Infor PLM and quality management workflows.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this merchandise hierarchy record was first created in the Oracle Retail Merchandising System and ingested into the Databricks silver layer. Supports data lineage, audit trail, and change management tracking.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the Department level node (e.g., \'ACT\' for Activewear, \'OTW\' for Outerwear). Null for Division-level records. Used in Oracle Retail for department-level OTB budgeting, allocation strategies, and merchandise financial planning.']
  department_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the Department (e.g., \'Activewear\', \'Outerwear\', \'Denim\', \'Footwear\'). Null for Division-level records. Denormalized for flat reporting in assortment planning, OTB dashboards, and Anaplan merchandise financial plans.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the top-level Division to which this hierarchy node belongs (e.g., \'MNS\' for Mens, \'WMN\' for Womens, \'KDS\' for Kids). Populated at all levels for denormalized reporting and OTB budget allocation in Anaplan.']
  division_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the top-level Division (e.g., \'Mens\', \'Womens\', \'Kids\', \'Accessories\'). Denormalized at all hierarchy levels to support flat reporting in Oracle Retail, Anaplan, and BI dashboards without requiring joins.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which this merchandise hierarchy node is no longer active for new planning, OTB, or SKU assignment. Null indicates an open-ended, currently active node. Supports hierarchy versioning and historical reporting in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this merchandise hierarchy node becomes effective and available for use in assortment planning, OTB budgeting, and SKU assignment in Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Supports temporal validity management for hierarchy restructuring.']
  erp_category_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding material group or product category code in SAP S/4HANA (MM module) that maps to this merchandise hierarchy node. Ensures alignment between Oracle Retail merchandise taxonomy and SAP procurement, inventory valuation, and COGS reporting.']
  fiber_content_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this merchandise hierarchy node require fiber content disclosure on product labels per FTC Textile and Apparel Labeling regulations. Drives compliance requirements in Infor PLM tech pack specifications and product labeling workflows.']
  gender_affinity varchar [note: 'Gender classification associated with this merchandise hierarchy node, used to segment assortments, OTB budgets, and marketing campaigns. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System division and department structures for gender-based merchandise financial planning.']
  hierarchy_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code that uniquely identifies this hierarchy node within the Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Used as the business key in OTB budgets, assortment plans, and financial reporting across Oracle Retail and Anaplan.']
  hierarchy_depth varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of this node in the hierarchy tree: 1=Division, 2=Department, 3=Class, 4=Subclass. Supports hierarchical traversal, roll-up aggregation, and level-specific filtering in OTB and GMROI reporting.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the level of this node within the four-tier merchandise classification structure: Division > Department > Class > Subclass. Governs how OTB budgets, assortment plans, and GMROI reporting are structured and rolled up in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  hierarchy_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the merchandise hierarchy node (e.g., \'Mens Activewear\', \'Outerwear\', \'Footwear\'). Displayed in assortment planning, OTB reporting, and financial planning dashboards in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.']
  hierarchy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this merchandise hierarchy node. Active nodes are available for assortment planning, OTB budgeting, and SKU assignment. Inactive or archived nodes are retained for historical reporting but excluded from new planning cycles in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.']
  hs_code_prefix varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System (HS) Code prefix (4-6 digit chapter/heading) associated with this merchandise hierarchy node, used for customs classification, import duty calculation, and trade compliance reporting. Supports LDP cost calculation and GSP eligibility assessment in SAP S/4HANA and supply chain operations.']
  is_nos_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether products in this merchandise hierarchy node are designated as Never Out of Stock (NOS) items requiring continuous replenishment. NOS-eligible nodes receive dedicated OTB budget allocation and automated replenishment triggers in Oracle Retail and Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  is_private_label boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this merchandise hierarchy node is designated for private label (own-brand) products as opposed to national or licensed brands. Affects sourcing strategy (CMT, OEM, ODM), IMU targets, and margin planning in Infor PLM and Anaplan.']
  labor_compliance_standard varchar [note: 'Labor compliance certification standard required for suppliers producing goods in this merchandise hierarchy node (e.g., FLA, WRAP, SA8000, SEDEX). Governs supplier qualification, factory audit requirements, and sourcing decisions in SAP S/4HANA MM and Infor PLM sourcing modules.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this merchandise hierarchy record was last updated in the Oracle Retail Merchandising System or the Databricks silver layer. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change detection, and audit compliance.']
  lifestyle_category varchar [note: 'Lifestyle or end-use category classification for this hierarchy node, distinguishing athletic performance, lifestyle, luxury, workwear, casual, and outdoor segments. Supports brand positioning, collection planning, and targeted marketing strategies in Adobe Experience Platform and Anaplan.']
  markdown_strategy varchar [note: 'Markdown (MD) strategy designation for this merchandise hierarchy node, indicating the planned approach to price reductions during the selling season. Governs Oracle Retail Price Management markdown rules, Anaplan financial planning assumptions, and MMU maintenance targets.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_description varchar [note: 'Free-text business description of this merchandise hierarchy node, providing additional context about the product category, its scope, and any special planning or sourcing considerations. Maintained in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and used in buyer-facing assortment planning documentation.']
  merchandise_type varchar [note: 'Broad merchandise type classification indicating the nature of products within this hierarchy node. Drives category-specific planning rules, margin targets, and allocation strategies in Oracle Retail and Anaplan merchandise financial planning.']
  otb_budget_method varchar [note: 'Indicates the Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget planning methodology applied to this merchandise hierarchy node: top-down (executive-driven), bottom-up (buyer-driven), or hybrid. Determines how Anaplan merchandise financial plans are structured and reconciled with Oracle Retail OTB budgets.']
  plm_category_code varchar [note: 'Corresponding category code in the Infor PLM or Centric PLM system that maps to this merchandise hierarchy node. Enables cross-system alignment between the Oracle Retail merchandise taxonomy and the PLM product design and BOM management classification for collection planning and tech pack organization.']
  season_applicability varchar [note: 'Seasonal classification indicating which selling seasons this hierarchy node is active for. Drives seasonal buy plan creation, OTB budget phasing, and collection planning cycles in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan.']
  sort_sequence varchar [note: 'Numeric ordering sequence for displaying this hierarchy node among its siblings within the same parent. Controls the display order in Oracle Retail Merchandising System screens, assortment planning tools, and reporting dashboards.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this merchandise hierarchy record originated (e.g., ORM for Oracle Retail Merchandising System, SAP for SAP S/4HANA). Supports data lineage, master data management reconciliation, and silver layer provenance tracking in the Databricks lakehouse.']
  subclass_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric code identifying the Subclass level node within a Class (e.g., \'PUFF\' for Puffer Jackets, \'WNDBRKR\' for Windbreakers). Null for Division, Department, and Class-level records. The most granular level for SKU assignment, OTB allocation, and STR tracking.']
  subclass_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the Subclass (e.g., \'Puffer Jackets\', \'Windbreakers\', \'Slim Fit Jeans\'). Null for Division, Department, and Class-level records. Used in Oracle Retail for the most granular assortment planning, allocation, and markdown optimization.']
  target_gmroi decimal(7,4) [note: 'Target Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) for this merchandise hierarchy node, calculated as gross margin dollars divided by average inventory cost. A primary KPI for merchandise financial planning and OTB budget justification in Anaplan and Oracle Retail.']
  target_imu_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Initial Markup (IMU) percentage for products within this merchandise hierarchy node, representing the planned markup from cost to MSRP at the time of first receipt. A key financial planning metric in Anaplan OTB and Oracle Retail Price Management for setting pricing strategy and GMROI targets.']
  target_str_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Sell-Through Rate (STR) percentage for this merchandise hierarchy node, representing the planned proportion of received inventory sold within a season. Used in Oracle Retail and Anaplan to evaluate assortment performance, trigger markdown decisions, and set OTB buy quantities.']
  target_wos decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Weeks of Supply (WOS) for this merchandise hierarchy node, representing the planned number of weeks of inventory on hand relative to forecasted sales rate. Drives replenishment triggers, OTB phasing, and allocation strategies in Oracle Retail and Manhattan Associates WMS.']

  Note: 'Master reference for the merchandise classification hierarchy defining the organizational structure of product categories: Division > Department > Class > Subclass. Governs how assortments, OTB budgets, and financial plans are structured and reported. Managed in Oracle Retail Merchandising System as the foundational taxonomy.'
}

Table merchandising.price_master {
  price_master_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each price master record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Serves as the primary key for the price_master data product.']
  channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales channel for which this price record is applicable (e.g., DTC retail store, e-commerce, wholesale). Supports channel-specific pricing strategies.']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Differential pricing strategies by customer segment (VIP early access pricing, employee discounts, wholesale vs retail). Segment-based price optimization and dynamic pricing models require linking pri']
  ftc_label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ftc_label. Business justification: Pricing records require FTC-compliant labeling (fiber content, care, RN/WPL, origin); price_master must link to approved label ensuring legal compliance before product can be sold.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price changes trigger revenue recognition and markdown accounting entries to specific GL accounts. Finance requires this link to automate GL posting of price changes and ensure proper revenue/markdown']
  price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the price list or price zone under which this record is governed. Price lists segment pricing by channel, region, or customer tier in Oracle Retail Price Management.']
  quality_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_certification. Business justification: Certified products (organic, fair trade, GOTS) command premium pricing. Certifications justify MSRP and support brand claims. Essential for pricing strategy, regulatory compliance, and marketing claim']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic region or market for which this price record applies. Supports regional pricing strategies across domestic and international markets.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise season (e.g., Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter) associated with this price record. Supports seasonal buy plan and OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget alignment.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) for which this price record applies. A SKU represents the most granular sellable unit (style + color + size). Links to the product/SKU master.']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent style record for which this pricing applies. A style may encompass multiple SKUs (colors and sizes). Enables style-level pricing analysis and rollup reporting.']
  vendor_cost_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.vendor_cost_quote. Business justification: Retail pricing decisions reference vendor cost quotes to calculate IMU and validate margin targets. Essential for pricing strategy, ensuring retail prices achieve target margins based on actual source']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Wholesale and FOB cost pricing are negotiated per vendor; merchandising requires vendor-specific cost tracking for margin analysis, vendor performance evaluation, and buy plan costing accuracy in appa']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval status of the price record within the Oracle Retail Price Management approval process. Prices must be approved before becoming active. Supports governance and audit trail for pricing decisions.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or employee identifier of the merchandising manager or pricing analyst who approved this price record in Oracle Retail Price Management. Supports audit trail and pricing governance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price record was formally approved in the Oracle Retail Price Management workflow. Provides a precise audit trail for pricing governance and compliance reporting.']
  aur decimal(18,4) [note: 'Average Unit Retail (AUR) — the average price at which a unit is actually sold after accounting for promotions, markdowns (MD), and clearance events. A key KPI in merchandise financial planning and STR (Sell-Through Rate) analysis. Managed in Anaplan merchandise planning.']
  competitor_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The observed or benchmarked competitor price for a comparable product at the time of pricing decision. Used to support competitive pricing analysis and market positioning strategy. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code.']
  cost_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The landed cost price of the product including COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) components such as CMT (Cut Make Trim), materials, LDP (Landed Duty Paid) duties, and freight. Used as the denominator for IMU (Initial Markup) and MMU (Maintained Markup) calculations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 country code identifying the specific country market for which this price record applies. Supports country-level pricing compliance, VAT/tax treatment, and international trade reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price master record was first created in the system. Provides the audit trail creation anchor for the price record lifecycle.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary price fields (MSRP, wholesale price, cost price, AUR, markdown price) are expressed. Supports multi-currency pricing for international markets and regional price management.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this price record expires and is no longer applicable for transactions. Nullable for open-ended pricing. Used to manage price validity windows, promotional end dates, and seasonal clearance cutoffs.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this price record becomes active and applicable for transactions. Used to manage price change calendars, promotional windows, and seasonal pricing transitions in Oracle Retail Price Management.']
  fob_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'FOB (Free on Board) cost representing the factory gate price paid to the supplier before freight and duty charges. Used in sourcing negotiations, BOM (Bill of Materials) costing, and supplier performance benchmarking.']
  imu_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Initial Markup Percentage (IMU%) — the percentage markup applied at the time of first pricing, calculated as (MSRP - Cost Price) / MSRP × 100. A foundational metric in merchandise financial planning and OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget setting.']
  landed_duty_paid_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total LDP (Landed Duty Paid) cost including FOB (Free on Board) price, import duties, freight, insurance, and customs clearance charges. Used as the true cost basis for margin calculations and sourcing decisions.']
  markdown_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage reduction from MSRP applied during a markdown (MD) event, calculated as (MSRP - Markdown Price) / MSRP × 100. Used to track markdown depth, promotional cadence, and margin erosion in merchandise financial planning.']
  markdown_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The reduced selling price applied during a markdown (MD) event, clearance, or end-of-season promotion. Tracked separately from MSRP to enable markdown depth analysis and STR (Sell-Through Rate) optimization.']
  min_advertised_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The minimum price at which wholesale partners and retailers are contractually permitted to advertise this product. Enforced to protect brand equity and prevent price erosion in wholesale and marketplace channels.']
  mmu_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maintained Markup Percentage (MMU%) — the actual markup percentage realized after all markdowns (MD), promotions, and shrinkage adjustments. Reflects true profitability versus the initial pricing plan. Used in GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) — the recommended consumer-facing selling price set by the brand. Used as the baseline for IMU (Initial Markup) calculation, wholesale negotiations, and consumer price communication. Expressed in the currency defined by the currency_code field.']
  price_change_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason code for the price change event (e.g., seasonal markdown, competitive response, cost increase, promotional event, clearance). Supports pricing audit trail and merchandise financial planning analysis.']
  price_elasticity_index decimal(8,4) [note: 'A numeric index representing the price sensitivity of demand for this SKU/style, derived from historical sales and pricing data in Anaplan. Used to optimize markdown timing, promotional depth, and AUR (Average Unit Retail) planning.']
  price_master_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the price master record as assigned in Oracle Retail Price Management. Used for cross-system reference and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) integration with wholesale partners and ERP systems.']
  price_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price master record. Active records are currently in use; pending records are approved but not yet effective; expired records have passed their effective end date; superseded records have been replaced by a newer price version.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the product\'s price positioning within the brand\'s pricing architecture. Drives assortment planning, channel allocation, and marketing strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: luxury|premium|mid_market|value|outlet|off_price — promote to reference product if tiers expand beyond 6]']
  price_type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the nature of the price record — regular everyday price, promotional price, clearance/end-of-season price, wholesale price, cost price, or internal transfer price. Governs which price is applied in each transaction context.']
  price_version varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the price record, incremented each time the price is updated or superseded. Enables price history tracking and point-in-time pricing reconstruction for audit and analytics purposes.']
  price_zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic or channel-based price zone to which this price record belongs. Price zones allow differentiated pricing across markets (e.g., US_EAST, EU_WEST, APAC) within Oracle Retail Price Management.']
  sales_channel_type varchar [note: 'The type of sales channel for which this price is applicable. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) retail, DTC e-commerce, wholesale, outlet, marketplace, or franchise. Enables channel-specific pricing strategy and margin analysis.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this price record originated. ORM = Oracle Retail Merchandising, SAP_SD = SAP Sales & Distribution, SFCC = Salesforce Commerce Cloud, ANAPLAN = Anaplan Planning, MANUAL = manually entered. Supports data lineage and reconciliation.']
  tax_inclusive_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the MSRP and selling prices include applicable taxes (VAT, GST, sales tax). True = tax-inclusive pricing (common in EU/UK markets); False = tax-exclusive pricing (common in US markets). Governs POS and e-commerce price display.']
  transfer_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The internal price used for inter-company or inter-division product transfers (e.g., from distribution center to retail store, or between legal entities). Used in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO for intercompany accounting and IFRS transfer pricing compliance.']
  upc_code varchar [note: 'Universal Product Code (UPC) barcode identifier for the SKU. Used for POS (Point of Sale) scanning, RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tagging, and retail shelf management. Conforms to GS1 UPC-A or EAN-13 standards.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price master record was most recently modified. Used for incremental data pipeline processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  vat_rate_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The applicable VAT (Value Added Tax) or GST rate percentage for this product in the specified country/region. Used for tax-inclusive price calculation, consumer receipt display, and regulatory tax reporting.']
  wholesale_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The price at which the product is sold to wholesale partners, department stores, and distribution accounts. Used in wholesale margin analysis, partner negotiations, and GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) reporting.']

  Note: 'Master pricing record for each style/SKU capturing MSRP, AUR (Average Unit Retail), IMU (Initial Markup), wholesale price, and cost price across channels and regions. Tracks price tier classification (luxury, premium, value), currency, and effective date ranges. Managed in Oracle Retail Price Management module.'
}

Table merchandising.promotion {
  promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the promotion record. Primary key for the promotion data product in the merchandising domain.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Promotions consume markdown and promotional budget; must link to budget for spend tracking and variance analysis. Finance requires this link to monitor promotional spend against budget and forecast re']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Merchandising promotions (markdowns, discounts) are executed as part of marketing campaigns. This link tracks which campaign drives each pricing action, enabling integrated promotional planning and RO']
  circular_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.circular_program. Business justification: Promotions incentivize circular economy participation (e.g., 20% off when trading in old garments). Retailers run promotional campaigns tied to specific circular programs for customer engagement and s']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Promotion costs (advertising, discounts, loyalty rewards) are charged to marketing/merchandising cost centers. Finance requires this link to allocate promotional expenses and track cost center budget ']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Promotions target customer segments beyond loyalty programs (student discounts, military offers, first-time buyer incentives). Segment-specific promotions drive acquisition and retention strategies wi']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program associated with loyalty-linked promotions. Null for non-loyalty promotion types. Links to CRM (Customer Relationship Management) loyalty program master data in Adobe Experience Platform or Salesforce.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Promotions are created and managed by marketing or merchandising employees who define discount strategy, channel scope, and performance targets. Required for approval workflows, promotion effectivenes']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Promotions have department_id, class_id but no FK to merchandise_hierarchy master. Adding merchandise_hierarchy_id FK normalizes merchandise classification and removes redundant hierarchy attributes. ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Promotions impact margin by channel/brand, tracked via profit centers for ROI analysis. Financial reporting requires linking promotional lift and cost to profit centers to measure promotional effectiv']
  promo_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.promo_code. Business justification: Marketing promo codes trigger merchandising promotions at checkout. This link connects the code distribution mechanism (marketing) to the discount rules and budget impact (merchandising). Essential fo']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Promotions are seasonal events (Spring Sale, Holiday Clearance); season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant season_code. FK wi']
  source_promotion_id varchar [note: 'Original promotion identifier as recorded in the source operational system (e.g., Oracle Retail promotion number, SAP SD condition record number). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the Databricks Silver Layer and source systems of record.']
  actual_lift_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Realized percentage increase in unit sales or revenue achieved by this promotion compared to baseline, captured post-promotion from SAP CAR POS data and Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Compared against planned_lift_pct for STR (Sell-Through Rate) analysis and future promotion planning.']
  actual_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Realized gross revenue generated during the promotion period, sourced from SAP CAR POS transactions and Salesforce Commerce Cloud order data. Compared against planned_revenue for post-promotion performance analysis and GMROI optimization.']
  actual_units varchar [note: 'Realized number of units sold during the promotion period, sourced from SAP CAR POS and Salesforce Commerce Cloud. Used in STR (Sell-Through Rate) analysis and post-promotion inventory reconciliation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Workflow approval state of the promotion record within the merchandising governance process. Pending: awaiting review; Approved: authorized by merchandising director or finance; Rejected: not approved; Escalated: referred to senior leadership. Distinct from promotion_status which tracks operational lifecycle.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the merchandising manager or director who authorized this promotion. Supports audit trail requirements for pricing governance and FTC compliance on promotional advertising claims.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion was formally approved by the authorized merchandising stakeholder. Supports audit trail and compliance with FTC promotional advertising disclosure requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  channel_applicability varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) where the promotion is valid. Retail_store: physical POS via SAP CAR; ecommerce: digital storefront via Salesforce Commerce Cloud; wholesale: B2B distribution partners; dtc: Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) channels; all: applicable across all channels. Drives channel-specific promotional lift analysis.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric coupon or voucher code that customers enter at POS or e-commerce checkout to activate the promotion when coupon_required is True (e.g., SAVE20, LOYALTY10). Null when coupon_required is False.']
  coupon_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer must present or enter a coupon code to redeem the promotion. True = coupon code required at POS or e-commerce checkout; False = automatic/stackable promotion applied without customer action.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion record was first created in the source system (Oracle Retail or SAP SD). Supports data lineage, audit trail, and GDPR/CCPA data governance requirements. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code applicable to all monetary fields on this promotion record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across international retail and wholesale channels.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Markdown (MD) percentage applied to the original MSRP or AUR (Average Unit Retail) for percentage-off promotion types. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 = 25% off). Null for non-percentage promotion types such as fixed_price or bogo. Used in IMU/MMU (Initial Markup/Maintained Markup) impact analysis.']
  end_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the promotion expires and is no longer applicable. Nullable for open-ended evergreen promotions. Used to calculate WOS (Weeks of Supply) impact and OTB (Open-to-Buy) planning in Anaplan.']
  fixed_price_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Absolute promotional selling price applied to eligible items for fixed_price promotion types (e.g., $29.99). Null for percentage-off or BOGO mechanics. Represents the promotional AUR (Average Unit Retail) override in Oracle Retail Price Management.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Pipe-delimited list of ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes where the promotion is valid (e.g., USA|CAN|GBR). Null = global applicability. Supports multi-market promotional planning and compliance with regional consumer protection regulations.']
  gmroi_impact decimal(10,4) [note: 'Estimated or realized GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) impact of the promotion, calculated as gross margin dollars generated per dollar of inventory investment during the promotional period. A core KPI in apparel merchandising financial planning. Classified confidential as it reveals margin strategy.']
  is_loyalty_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion is exclusively available to loyalty program members. True = restricted to loyalty members only (tracked via Adobe Experience Platform or CRM); False = available to all customers. Supports CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) and loyalty program ROI analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion record was most recently modified in the source system. Tracks changes to promotion terms, dates, or discount mechanics for audit and data lineage purposes. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  markdown_budget decimal(18,4) [note: 'Allocated markdown (MD) budget for this promotion, representing the maximum planned reduction in gross margin approved by the merchandising team. Expressed in currency_code. Managed in Anaplan OTB (Open-to-Buy) financial planning. Classified confidential as it reflects internal pricing strategy.']
  merchandise_scope varchar [note: 'Level of the merchandise hierarchy to which the promotion applies. Determines eligible SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) in Oracle Retail: all_merchandise (storewide), department (e.g., Footwear), class (e.g., Running Shoes), subclass (e.g., Men\'s Running), sku (specific SKU), style (all colorways of a style). Drives assortment planning and allocation strategies.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Minimum transaction value (in local currency) a customer must reach to qualify for the promotion. Used for threshold-based promotions (e.g., spend $100 get 20% off). Supports AOV (Average Order Value) uplift strategies in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and SAP CAR.']
  minimum_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of units a customer must purchase to qualify for the promotion. Relevant for BOGO, bundle, and quantity-threshold promotions. Aligns with MOQ (Minimum Order Quantity) concepts in wholesale channel promotions.']
  planned_lift_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Forecasted percentage increase in unit sales or revenue expected from this promotion compared to baseline, as planned in Anaplan merchandise financial planning. Used to set OTB (Open-to-Buy) budgets and evaluate GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) impact pre-promotion.']
  planned_revenue decimal(18,4) [note: 'Forecasted gross revenue expected to be generated during the promotion period, as modeled in Anaplan merchandise financial planning. Expressed in the currency defined by currency_code. Used for OTB budget setting and GMROI planning.']
  planned_units varchar [note: 'Forecasted number of units expected to be sold during the promotion period, as planned in Anaplan demand planning. Used to set inventory allocation targets and ATS (Available to Sell) thresholds in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple non-stackable promotions apply to the same transaction or item. Lower number = higher priority. Managed in Oracle Retail Price Management and SAP SD condition access sequences.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code used to identify and reference the promotion across Oracle Retail, SAP SD, and customer-facing channels (e.g., SUMMER25, BOGO-DENIM). Used in EDI transactions, POS systems, and e-commerce storefronts.']
  promotion_description varchar [note: 'Free-text business description of the promotion\'s terms, conditions, and marketing narrative as communicated to customers and store associates (e.g., \'Get 25% off all women\'s outerwear this weekend only\'). Used in POS displays, e-commerce banners, and marketing collateral.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable business name of the promotion as displayed in merchandising systems and marketing materials (e.g., \'Summer Clearance 2025\', \'Buy One Get One Denim\'). Used in Oracle Retail and Anaplan merchandise financial planning.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion record within Oracle Retail and SAP SD workflow. Draft: under construction; Approved: authorized by merchandising; Active: currently running; Paused: temporarily suspended; Expired: past end date; Cancelled: terminated before activation.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Categorical classification of the promotion\'s discount mechanic. Drives how the discount is calculated at POS and in e-commerce. Values: percentage_off (e.g., 20% off), fixed_price (e.g., $29.99), bogo (Buy One Get One), bundle (multi-item offer), loyalty (loyalty program linked reduction), clearance (end-of-season markdown). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage_off|fixed_price|bogo|bundle|loyalty|clearance|gift_with_purchase|threshold_discount — promote to reference product]']
  sell_through_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of available promotional inventory sold during the promotion period (units sold / units available × 100). A key KPI in apparel merchandising for evaluating promotion effectiveness and informing future markdown (MD) and clearance strategies. Sourced post-promotion from Oracle Retail and SAP CAR.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Operational system of record from which this promotion record originated. Oracle_retail: Oracle Retail Merchandising System; sap_sd: SAP S/4HANA Sales and Distribution; salesforce_cc: Salesforce Commerce Cloud; anaplan: Anaplan planning platform; manual: manually entered. Supports data lineage in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion can be combined with other active promotions on the same transaction. True = can stack with other promotions; False = exclusive promotion, cannot be combined. Critical for margin protection and MMU (Maintained Markup) management.']
  start_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the promotion becomes effective and eligible for application at POS, e-commerce, and wholesale channels. Aligns with the T&A (Time and Action) calendar in Anaplan merchandise planning.']
  usage_limit_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer can redeem this promotion. Null = unlimited per customer. Enforced at POS via SAP CAR and at e-commerce checkout via Salesforce Commerce Cloud to prevent abuse.']
  usage_limit_total varchar [note: 'Maximum total number of times this promotion can be redeemed across all customers and transactions. Null = unlimited redemptions. Used for limited-time flash sales and exclusive loyalty offers to control promotional liability.']

  Note: 'Master record for merchandise promotions including seasonal sales events, clearance promotions, bundle offers, and loyalty-linked price reductions. Captures promotion type, discount mechanics (percentage off, BOGO, fixed price), eligible merchandise scope, channel applicability, and planned versus actual promotional lift. Managed in Oracle Retail and SAP SD.'
}

Table merchandising.allocation_plan {
  allocation_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the merchandise allocation plan record within the Oracle Retail Allocation module.']
  allocator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Allocation plans are created and owned by allocation analysts who determine store-level distribution strategy, size curve application, and replenishment rules. Required for approval workflows, audit t']
  buy_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.buy_plan. Business justification: Allocation plans distribute inventory that was committed via buy plans. The buy_plan commits to purchasing X units of a style from a vendor; the allocation_plan determines how to distribute those unit']
  class_merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise class within the Oracle Retail hierarchy (below department, above subclass), used to scope the allocation plan to a specific product grouping such as \'Outerwear\' or \'Athletic Footwear\'.']
  colorway_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific style/colorway combination being allocated. A colorway represents a distinct color variant of a style, one level above the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) in the product hierarchy.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_certification. Business justification: Allocation plans cannot distribute product without valid certifications (safety, sustainability); allocation approval gates depend on compliance status to prevent shipping non-compliant inventory.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Allocation planning costs (labor, systems, logistics overhead) are charged to merchandising operations cost centers. Finance requires this link to allocate allocation planning expenses for departmenta']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Store allocations consider local customer segment demographics (allocate more activewear to fitness-focused markets, more workwear to business districts). Segment mix by geography drives size curves a']
  destination_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: Store allocations often route through regional DCs before final delivery. Destination DC link enables multi-echelon allocation planning, inter-DC transfer planning, and regional replenishment strategi']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source warehouse or distribution center from which allocated inventory will be shipped. Managed in Manhattan Associates WMS and SAP S/4HANA WM module.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise department (e.g., Men\'s Apparel, Women\'s Footwear) within the Oracle Retail merchandise hierarchy under which this allocation plan is organized.']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the source purchase order (PO) from which inventory is being allocated. Links the allocation plan to the procurement event in SAP S/4HANA MM module.']
  otb_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget record that governs the financial envelope for this allocation plan. Links to the Anaplan merchandise financial planning budget to ensure allocation activity stays within approved spend.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory. Business justification: Allocation plans track factory of origin for inventory traceability, quality control, and compliance documentation. Required for country-of-origin labeling and audit trails in retail operations.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Allocation plans distribute seasonal merchandise; season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant season_code. FK will reference me']
  size_curve_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the size curve profile that defines the percentage distribution of units across sizes (e.g., XS/S/M/L/XL) for this allocation plan. Derived from historical sell-through rate (STR) data by store cluster and product category.']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the store cluster grouping used to drive allocation distribution logic. Store clusters segment retail locations by performance tier, geography, climate, or customer demographics to enable targeted allocation strategies.']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific product style being allocated. A style represents a distinct design within the product hierarchy, above colorway and SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) level.']
  subclass_merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandise subclass within the Oracle Retail hierarchy (most granular classification level), scoping the allocation plan to a specific product type such as \'Puffer Jackets\' or \'Running Shoes\'.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Connects allocation plans to production batches for inventory lot control, quality traceability, and production date tracking. Required for FIFO/LIFO inventory management and quality issue resolution.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation strategy applied in this plan. \'Initial\' distributes new receipts to stores; \'replenishment\' refills depleted stock; \'push\' proactively sends inventory based on forecasts; \'transfer\' moves stock between locations; \'pre_pack\' allocates pre-assembled size sets.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the merchandise planner or manager who approved the allocation plan for execution. Supports governance and audit trail requirements.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the allocation plan was formally approved for execution by the authorized merchandise planner or manager.']
  available_to_sell_units varchar [note: 'The number of units confirmed as ATS (Available to Sell) at the time of allocation plan creation. Represents the inventory pool from which allocations are drawn, net of committed and reserved quantities.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel to which inventory is being allocated. Retail store covers brick-and-mortar locations; ecommerce covers fulfillment centers serving digital orders; wholesale covers partner accounts; DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) covers owned digital and physical channels.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(18,4) [note: 'The unit cost (COGS - Cost of Goods Sold basis) of the item being allocated, expressed in the operating currency. Used for GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis and financial planning reconciliation in Anaplan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this allocation plan record was first captured in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and GDPR/CCPA data governance requirements.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values in this allocation plan record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency wholesale and international retail operations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the allocation plan expires or is no longer valid for execution. Nullable for open-ended replenishment plans. Aligns with season end dates and OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget periods.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the allocation plan becomes active and inventory distribution should commence. Used to align allocation execution with the merchandise floor set calendar and T&A (Time and Action) calendar.']
  in_store_date date [note: 'The planned date on which allocated inventory is expected to arrive and be available on the retail floor or in the fulfillment center. Critical for floor set planning, marketing campaign alignment, and NOS (Never Out of Stock) compliance.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any allocation quantities in this plan have been manually overridden by a planner, deviating from the system-calculated distribution. Supports audit trail requirements and exception reporting.']
  is_nos_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the items in this allocation plan are designated as NOS (Never Out of Stock) items, requiring continuous replenishment allocation to maintain minimum stock levels across all qualifying locations.']
  is_size_curve_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the default size curve profile has been manually overridden by the merchandise planner for this allocation plan. When True, the size_curve_id reflects a custom profile rather than the system-recommended curve.']
  max_store_units varchar [note: 'The maximum number of units that can be allocated to any single store location in this plan. Prevents over-stocking and ensures equitable distribution across the store network.']
  min_store_units varchar [note: 'The minimum number of units that must be allocated to each qualifying store location in this plan. Ensures NOS (Never Out of Stock) compliance and prevents under-stocking of key locations.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'The MSRP (Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price) for the allocated item at the time of plan creation. Used as the baseline for IMU (Initial Markup) calculations and markdown (MD) planning.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for merchandise planners to capture contextual notes, exceptions, or business rationale associated with the allocation plan (e.g., \'Adjusted for regional weather event\', \'Held pending PO confirmation\').']
  plan_date date [note: 'The business date on which the allocation plan was created or initiated by the merchandise planner. Represents the principal real-world event time for this allocation planning transaction.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the allocation plan, typically referencing the season, collection, or merchandise category (e.g., \'Fall 2025 Outerwear Initial Push\').']
  plan_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the allocation plan, used in cross-system references, buyer communications, and Oracle Retail Allocation module tracking. Follows the format ALLOC-YYYY-NNNNNN.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the allocation plan within the Oracle Retail Allocation workflow. Controls whether the plan is editable, executable, or archived. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|in_progress|completed|cancelled|on_hold — promote to reference product]']
  priority_rule varchar [note: 'The business rule governing how inventory is prioritized across locations when supply is constrained. Options include sales volume ranking, store grade, cluster rank, manual override, OTB (Open-to-Buy) balance, or WOS (Weeks of Supply) target. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_volume|store_grade|cluster_rank|manual|otb_balance|wos_target — promote to reference product]']
  ship_date date [note: 'The planned date on which inventory is scheduled to ship from the distribution center to allocated store or fulfillment locations. Drives warehouse pick/pack scheduling in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the originating operational system that created or last updated this allocation plan record. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer lakehouse.']
  str_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target STR (Sell-Through Rate) percentage for this allocation plan, expressed as a decimal percentage (e.g., 85.00 = 85%). Used to evaluate allocation effectiveness and inform markdown (MD) timing decisions.']
  total_actual_units varchar [note: 'The total number of units actually allocated and confirmed for distribution across all locations. May differ from planned units due to inventory availability, ATS (Available to Sell) constraints, or manual overrides.']
