  total_planned_units varchar [note: 'The total number of units planned for distribution across all locations in this allocation plan. Represents the gross planned allocation quantity before any adjustments or overrides.']
  unallocated_units varchar [note: 'The number of units from the available inventory pool that remain unallocated after the plan is executed. Represents residual inventory requiring further allocation decisions or return to the warehouse.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this allocation plan record was most recently modified in the data platform. Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  wos_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target WOS (Weeks of Supply) used to calculate allocation quantities per location. Drives replenishment allocation logic by ensuring each store receives enough inventory to cover the defined number of selling weeks based on forecasted sales velocity.']

  Note: 'Merchandise allocation plan defining how available inventory is distributed across retail stores, e-commerce fulfillment centers, and wholesale accounts. Captures allocation strategy (initial allocation, replenishment, push, transfer), store cluster assignments, size curve profiles, priority rules, and planned versus actual allocated units by style/colorway. Managed in Oracle Retail Allocation module.'
}

Table merchandising.allocation_detail {
  allocation_detail_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each allocation detail line record within the allocation plan. Primary key for this entity in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  allocation_analyst_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Detailed allocation decisions at SKU-location level are made by specific analysts for manual overrides, exception handling, and store-specific adjustments. Essential for accountability, quality contro']
  allocation_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent allocation plan header under which this detail line was created. Links the line-level allocation to its governing plan in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Detail-level inspection results determine if specific allocated quantities can ship. Failed inspections trigger allocation adjustments and replenishment delays. Essential for store-level quality contr']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Purchase Order (PO) from which inventory is being allocated. Links the allocation detail to the sourcing event in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  product_safety_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_safety_test. Business justification: Individual SKU allocations require passed safety tests; cannot ship product failing CPSC, ASTM, or EN standards. Links allocation to test result for shipment release approval.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory. Business justification: Detail-level factory tracking enables shipment-specific origin documentation, customs compliance, and quality issue root cause analysis. Essential for supply chain traceability and regulatory complian']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the destination location (retail store, distribution center, or fulfillment channel) receiving the allocated units. Drives WMS fulfillment and store receiving workflows in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the outbound shipment record in Manhattan Associates WMS that fulfilled this allocation detail line. Links the allocation to the physical shipment for OTIF (On Time In Full) tracking and logistics reconciliation.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) being allocated. Identifies the exact product variant (style, color, size) targeted by this allocation line.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order_line. Business justification: Detail-level allocation tied to specific PO lines. Enables precise inventory flow tracking from factory to location, reconciliation of received vs. allocated quantities, and SKU-level supply chain vis']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Detail-level production batch tracking enables lot-specific quality control, recall management, and production date verification. Critical for compliance, traceability, and quality assurance processes']
  actual_receipt_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the destination location confirmed receipt of the allocated units. Used for OTIF compliance measurement and inventory availability updates.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'The actual date on which allocated units were physically shipped from the distribution center. Used for OTIF variance analysis and supply chain performance reporting.']
  allocation_date date [note: 'The business date on which the allocation was created and authorized in Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Used for OTB (Open-to-Buy) period alignment and merchandise financial planning.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to determine the allocation quantity for this line: sales_history (based on historical sales velocity), store_grade (based on store tier/grade), capacity (based on store capacity), manual (planner override), or model_stock (based on ideal stock model). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_history|store_grade|capacity|manual|model_stock — promote to reference product]']
  allocation_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible business identifier for the allocation detail line, used for cross-system reference between Oracle Retail Merchandising System, Manhattan Associates WMS, and SAP S/4HANA. Follows the format ALLOC-YYYYMMDD-NNNN.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the allocation detail line. Tracks progression from planning through fulfillment: draft (in planning), approved (authorized), released (sent to WMS), shipped (dispatched from DC), received (confirmed at destination), cancelled (voided). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|approved|released|shipped|received|cancelled — promote to reference product]']
  approved_qty varchar [note: 'The number of units formally approved for allocation after review and authorization. May differ from planned quantity due to inventory availability constraints or OTB budget adjustments.']
  asr_number varchar [note: 'The Advance Shipment Receipt number generated by Manhattan Associates WMS when the allocation is released for fulfillment. Used by destination locations to pre-receive and reconcile inbound inventory against the allocation.']
  ats_qty varchar [note: 'The Available to Sell (ATS) quantity for this SKU at the time the allocation was created, representing total on-hand and on-order inventory minus existing commitments. Determines the maximum allocatable quantity and is sourced from Oracle Retail Merchandising System inventory positions.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text or coded reason for cancellation when allocation_status is \'cancelled\'. Common reasons include inventory shortage, OTB budget cut, assortment change, or supplier failure. Required for cancelled lines to support merchandise planning post-mortems.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The commerce channel to which units are being allocated: retail (physical store), ecommerce (digital storefront via Salesforce Commerce Cloud), wholesale (B2B distribution), or dtc (Direct-to-Consumer). Supports channel-level sell-through rate (STR) and ATS (Available to Sell) analysis.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise class code within the department hierarchy for the allocated SKU (e.g., Tops, Bottoms, Outerwear). Supports class-level allocation analysis and assortment planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this allocation detail record was first created in the source system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and SLA compliance reporting.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary values on this allocation detail line (unit_cost, msrp). Supports multi-currency operations across international wholesale and DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) channels.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise department classification code for the allocated SKU (e.g., Men\'s Apparel, Women\'s Footwear, Accessories). Used for department-level allocation reporting and OTB budget tracking in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  is_pre_pack boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the allocated units are configured as pre-packs (assorted size/color combinations packed together) rather than individual units. Pre-pack allocations require different WMS handling and store receiving procedures in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  is_replenishment boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation detail line is a replenishment allocation (true) triggered by store inventory depletion, as opposed to an initial buy allocation (false) for a new season or collection. Supports NOS (Never Out of Stock) and VMI (Vendor Managed Inventory) program tracking.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number of this detail record within the parent allocation plan, used for ordering and referencing individual allocation lines during fulfillment and receiving.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the destination location receiving the allocation: store (retail store), dc (distribution center), warehouse (storage facility), or channel (e-commerce or wholesale channel). Determines the fulfillment workflow in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for the SKU at the time of allocation. Used for IMU (Initial Markup) calculation, markdown planning, and sell-through rate (STR) analysis.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for merchandiser or planner annotations on this allocation detail line. May include special handling instructions, buyer comments, or cross-reference notes to T&A (Time and Action) calendar events.']
  otif_status varchar [note: 'OTIF (On Time In Full) compliance status for this allocation detail line, assessed after actual shipment and receipt. Classifies delivery performance against planned ship and receipt dates and quantities. Critical KPI for supply chain SLA (Service Level Agreement) reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: on_time_in_full|on_time_short|late_in_full|late_short|not_shipped — promote to reference product]']
  pack_config_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the pre-pack or inner-pack configuration for this allocation line (e.g., size ratio pack, color assortment pack). Applicable when is_pre_pack is true. Drives carton-level fulfillment logic in Manhattan Associates WMS.']
  planned_qty varchar [note: 'The number of units originally planned for allocation to the destination location for this SKU within the allocation plan. Represents the merchandiser\'s intended distribution quantity before any adjustments.']
  planned_receipt_date date [note: 'The expected date on which the destination location (store or DC) is scheduled to receive the allocated units. Drives store receiving workflows and floor-set planning.']
  planned_ship_date date [note: 'The scheduled date on which allocated units are planned to ship from the distribution center or warehouse to the destination location. Used for OTIF (On Time In Full) tracking and T&A (Time and Action) calendar management.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking assigned to this allocation detail line when inventory is constrained and cannot fulfill all planned allocations. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used by Oracle Retail Merchandising System to sequence fulfillment when ATS (Available to Sell) is insufficient.']
  qty_variance varchar [note: 'The difference between approved allocation quantity and actual shipped quantity (approved_qty minus shipped_qty). Negative values indicate under-shipment; positive values indicate over-shipment. Used for allocation accuracy KPI reporting.']
  received_qty varchar [note: 'The actual number of units confirmed as received at the destination location. May differ from shipped quantity due to transit damage, short shipments, or receiving discrepancies.']
  rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tagging is required for units in this allocation line. When true, Manhattan Associates WMS triggers RFID encoding workflows during pick-and-pack operations.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The merchandise season code associated with this allocation (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024). Aligns allocation activity with collection planning cycles and OTB (Open-to-Buy) budgets in Anaplan.']
  shipped_qty varchar [note: 'The actual number of units physically shipped from the distribution center to the destination location. Captured from Manhattan Associates WMS shipment confirmation and used for OTIF and variance analysis.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this allocation detail record: ORM (Oracle Retail Merchandising System), SAP (SAP S/4HANA), ANAPLAN (Anaplan Merchandise Planning), or MANUAL (planner-entered). Supports data lineage and audit traceability in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  subclass_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise subclass code providing the most granular product classification level within the department-class hierarchy (e.g., T-Shirts, Denim Jeans, Running Shoes). Enables granular allocation and STR (Sell-Through Rate) analysis.']
  unit_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) per unit for this SKU at the time of allocation, expressed in the operating currency. Used for inventory valuation, GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) analysis, and financial reporting under IFRS/GAAP.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this allocation detail record was last modified in the source system. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change detection, incremental ETL processing, and audit trail in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  wos_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target Weeks of Supply (WOS) for the destination location at the time of allocation, representing the number of weeks the allocated inventory is expected to cover based on forecasted sales rate. Used for replenishment planning and NOS (Never Out of Stock) management.']

  Note: 'Line-level allocation transaction capturing the specific units allocated per SKU per location (store, DC, or channel) within an allocation plan. Records planned allocation quantity, actual shipped quantity, allocation date, and variance. Feeds WMS fulfillment and store receiving workflows in Manhattan Associates WMS.'
}

Table merchandising.merchandise_financial_plan {
  merchandise_financial_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the merchandise financial plan record in the Databricks Silver Layer. Primary key for this entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_agreement. Business justification: Financial plans reference sourcing agreements to model committed costs, capacity constraints, and pricing tiers. Essential for financial planning accuracy, ensuring planned COGS and receipts align wit']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: MFP is the merchandise budget itself; must link to finance budget master for consolidation. Corporate financial planning requires integrating merchandise financial plans into overall budget framework ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: MFP planned costs (COGS, markdowns, shrink) are allocated to cost centers for departmental accountability. Finance requires this link to track planned vs actual expenses by cost center for P&L varianc']
  customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Financial plans forecast revenue by target customer segment. Segment penetration, wallet share, and growth projections drive OTB budgets and investment allocation across categories. Essential for segm']
  financial_planner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Merchandise financial plans are owned by financial planning analysts who forecast sales, margin, and inventory targets. Critical for approval workflows, forecast accuracy tracking, and planner perform']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: MFP planned revenue and margin are tracked via profit centers by channel/brand. Financial reporting requires linking merchandise financial plans to profit centers for segment P&L planning and performa']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Merchandise financial plans are seasonal planning entities; season_code and season_year exist but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant attribut']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or employee identifier of the merchandising leader or finance officer who formally approved this merchandise financial plan.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date on which the merchandise financial plan was formally approved by the merchandising leadership and finance team. Required for audit trail and OTB budget release.']
  channel_inventory_allocation_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Percentage of total planned inventory allocated to this specific distribution channel (DTC retail, DTC e-commerce, wholesale, outlet). Drives channel-level receipt planning and allocation strategies in Oracle Retail.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise class code within the department hierarchy (e.g., Denim, Knitwear, Running Shoes). Represents the second level of the Oracle Retail merchandise hierarchy below department.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this merchandise financial plan record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all monetary plan values are expressed (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency planning for international wholesale and DTC channels.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise department code to which this financial plan applies (e.g., Womens Apparel, Mens Footwear, Accessories). Aligns with the Oracle Retail Merchandising System department hierarchy.']
  distribution_channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel for which this financial plan is scoped: DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) retail stores, DTC e-commerce, wholesale partnerships, or outlet stores. Drives channel-specific pricing strategy and inventory allocation.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter or period identifier within the fiscal year to which this plan applies (e.g., FY2025-Q1). Supports quarterly OTB budget reviews and financial reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The company\'s fiscal year to which this merchandise financial plan belongs. May differ from calendar year depending on the company\'s fiscal calendar (e.g., fiscal year ending January 31).']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this merchandise financial plan has been locked and frozen for execution. When True, no further edits are permitted and the plan serves as the official baseline for OTB budget management and performance tracking.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying this merchandise financial plan within Anaplan and Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Used for cross-system reconciliation and business reference.']
  plan_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the planning period covered by this merchandise financial plan. Marks the end of the season\'s selling window and the EOM (End of Month) inventory target date.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name of the merchandise financial plan, typically including season, year, department, and channel (e.g., \'FW2025 Womens Apparel DTC Retail Plan\').']
  plan_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the planning period covered by this merchandise financial plan. Marks the beginning of the season\'s selling window for OTB and inventory planning purposes.']
  plan_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the merchandise financial plan. Draft indicates work-in-progress; submitted indicates pending approval; approved indicates finance/merchant sign-off; locked indicates frozen for execution; archived indicates superseded.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the plan was generated using a top-down (executive-driven targets cascaded down), bottom-up (buyer/planner-driven builds rolled up), or reconciled (merged top-down and bottom-up) planning approach.']
  plan_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the merchandise financial plan (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Supports iterative planning cycles where plans are revised across pre-season, in-season, and post-season phases.']
  planned_aur decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned AUR (Average Unit Retail) representing the expected average selling price per unit across the department/class/channel combination. Drives unit sales plan derivation from the net sales dollar plan.']
  planned_bop_inventory_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned inventory value at retail at the beginning of the planning period. Used as the opening stock position for OTB calculations and inventory flow modeling.']
  planned_cogs_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned COGS (Cost of Goods Sold) for the season, representing the total cost of merchandise expected to be sold. Used to derive planned gross margin and GMROI targets.']
  planned_eop_inventory_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned inventory value at retail at the end of the planning period (EOM). Represents the target closing stock position and feeds into the next season\'s BOP. Critical for OTB and WOS (Weeks of Supply) planning.']
  planned_gmroi decimal(10,4) [note: 'Planned GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) representing the gross margin dollars generated per dollar of average inventory investment. Core KPI for evaluating merchandise productivity and capital efficiency.']
  planned_gross_margin_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned gross margin in absolute dollar terms (planned net sales minus planned COGS). Represents the financial contribution of the merchandise category before operating expenses.']
  planned_gross_margin_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned gross margin expressed as a percentage of planned net sales (planned gross margin / planned net sales). Key KPI for merchandising performance and pricing strategy evaluation.']
  planned_imu_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned IMU (Initial Markup) percentage representing the difference between the cost of merchandise and its original retail price, expressed as a percentage of retail. Establishes the pricing floor before markdowns.']
  planned_inventory_turns decimal(10,4) [note: 'Planned number of times inventory is expected to sell through and be replenished during the season (COGS / average inventory at cost). Drives OTB budget and receipt flow planning.']
  planned_markdown_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned markdown (MD) dollars for the season representing the anticipated reduction from original retail price to drive sell-through. Directly impacts MMU and gross margin performance.']
  planned_markdown_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned markdown as a percentage of planned net sales. Used to benchmark markdown aggressiveness across departments and channels and to set MMU expectations.']
  planned_mmu_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned MMU (Maintained Markup) percentage representing the actual markup achieved after markdowns and promotions, expressed as a percentage of net sales. Reflects the realized pricing performance versus IMU.']
  planned_msrp_avg decimal(10,2) [note: 'Planned average MSRP (Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price) across the SKUs in this department/class/channel plan. Establishes the retail price anchor for IMU calculation and consumer-facing pricing.']
  planned_net_sales_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned net sales revenue (gross sales minus returns and allowances) for the season, department, class, and channel combination. Core financial target in the merchandise financial plan. Expressed in the plan\'s currency.']
  planned_otb_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Planned OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget in dollars representing the amount of merchandise the buyer is authorized to purchase for the season. Calculated as planned EOP inventory + planned sales + planned markdowns - planned BOP inventory.']
  planned_receipts_amt decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned merchandise receipts (inbound inventory) at retail value for the season. Represents the planned flow of new inventory into the channel to support the sales plan.']
  planned_str_pct decimal(7,4) [note: 'Planned STR (Sell-Through Rate) expressed as a percentage of units sold versus units received. Target sell-through rate used to calibrate buy quantities, markdown timing, and end-of-season inventory positions.']
  planned_units_sold varchar [note: 'Total planned number of units expected to be sold during the season for the department/class/channel combination. Derived from planned net sales divided by planned AUR. Feeds into allocation and replenishment planning.']
  planned_wos decimal(7,2) [note: 'Planned WOS (Weeks of Supply) representing the number of weeks the current inventory position is expected to last at the planned rate of sale. Used to identify over/under-stock risk and trigger replenishment actions.']
  planning_horizon varchar [note: 'Indicates the planning phase this plan represents: pre-season (initial buy plan before season starts), in-season (revised plan during active selling), or post-season (actuals reconciliation and hindsight).']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Planned pricing strategy for this channel and merchandise category: full_price (MSRP-driven), promotional (planned promotional events), value (everyday low price positioning), or outlet (clearance/off-price). Informs IMU and MMU targets.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this merchandise financial plan record was last modified. Tracks iterative plan revisions across pre-season, in-season, and post-season planning cycles.']

  Note: 'Merchandise financial plan (MFP) capturing top-down and channel-level financial targets for a season by department, class, and distribution channel (DTC retail, DTC e-commerce, wholesale, outlet). Includes planned net sales, planned COGS, planned gross margin, planned GMROI, planned inventory turns, planned markdown dollars, channel-specific pricing strategy, and channel inventory allocation percentages. Serves as the financial planning backbone and GMROI target-setting mechanism for the merchandising organization. Managed in Anaplan.'
}

Table merchandising.size_curve {
  size_curve_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the size curve profile record in the merchandising system. Primary key for the size_curve data product. Entity role: MASTER_RESOURCE — this is a planning reference resource defining expected unit sales distribution across size runs.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Size curves are often season-specific (seasonal adjustment factors, basis periods); season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant']
  size_scale_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.size_scale. Business justification: Size curves define percentage distributions across size ranges and must reference the actual size scale (XS-XXL, numeric, etc.) to be operationally meaningful. Allocation planning requires mapping cur']
  basis_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the historical sell-through data period used to derive this size curve\'s distribution percentages. Together with basis_period_start_date, defines the historical window for statistical calibration of size ratios.']
  basis_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the historical sell-through data period used to derive this size curve\'s distribution percentages. Defines the lookback window for statistical calibration. Typically covers 1-3 prior seasons of comparable selling history.']
  channel varchar [note: 'The distribution channel for which this size curve is calibrated. Size distributions differ materially by channel — e-commerce tends to over-index on extreme sizes (XS, XL) versus retail stores. DTC (Direct-to-Consumer) curves may differ from wholesale curves. Drives channel-specific buy plan size ratios.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise class code within the department hierarchy from Oracle Retail (e.g., \'WOVEN_TOPS\', \'SLIM_FIT_JEANS\'). A size curve may be defined at class level for more granular distribution accuracy. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System class hierarchy.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Statistical confidence level of the size curve based on the volume and quality of historical sell-through data used in derivation. High confidence curves are used directly in buy plans; low confidence curves require planner override review. Drives curve applicability flags in Anaplan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this size curve record was first created in the system. Serves as the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED field for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) per platform conventions.']
  curve_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the size curve profile, used in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan for cross-system reference. Serves as the BUSINESS_IDENTIFIER for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  curve_derivation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to derive the size distribution percentages for this curve. Historical STR uses actual sell-through data; planner_override reflects manual planner judgment; vendor_recommended uses supplier-provided size ratios; statistical_model uses algorithmic forecasting; blended combines multiple methods. Affects confidence level assignment.']
  curve_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable descriptive name for the size curve profile (e.g., \'Women\'s Fitted Tops Standard\', \'Men\'s Denim 28-42 Bell Curve\'). Serves as the IDENTITY_LABEL for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. Used in Oracle Retail and Anaplan planning interfaces.']
  curve_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the size curve profile indicating the fit and silhouette category that drives the distribution shape. Standard curves follow bell-curve distributions; fitted curves skew toward smaller sizes; oversized curves skew toward larger sizes. Serves as the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: standard|fitted|oversized|petite|plus|tall|slim|relaxed — promote to reference product]']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise department code from Oracle Retail hierarchy (e.g., \'WOMENS_TOPS\', \'MENS_DENIM\', \'FOOTWEAR\'). Defines the merchandise department scope for which this size curve is applicable. Used in assortment planning and OTB budget allocation.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which this size curve profile is no longer active for new planning runs. Null indicates an open-ended curve with no planned expiry. Used to manage curve versioning and seasonal transitions in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which this size curve profile becomes effective for use in buy planning, allocation, and replenishment. Curves are versioned by effective date to support seasonal transitions and curve updates without disrupting in-flight planning cycles.']
  is_default_for_class boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this size curve is the default curve applied to new styles within the associated merchandise class when no style-specific curve exists. True = default fallback curve for the class. Used in Oracle Retail automated allocation and buy plan generation.']
  is_replenishment_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this size curve is approved for use in automated replenishment size pack calculations in Manhattan Associates WMS. True = curve can drive replenishment size breaks. False = curve is restricted to initial buy plan and allocation use only.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date on which this size curve was last reviewed and validated by a merchandise planner. Curves not reviewed within a defined period (e.g., 2 seasons) are flagged for re-validation to ensure continued accuracy. Supports governance of curve quality.']
  markdown_risk_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite risk score (0.00-100.00) indicating the likelihood of size-related markdown exposure if this curve is applied to a buy plan. Derived from historical size fragmentation patterns and STR variance across sizes. Higher scores indicate greater markdown risk from size imbalance. Critical for GMROI optimization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for merchandise planner annotations regarding this size curve, including rationale for manual overrides, known anomalies in the historical basis data, or special instructions for application. Supports planning governance and knowledge transfer.']
  numeric_size_distribution varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the size distribution percentages for numeric size runs (waist sizes 28-42 or equivalent), stored as a delimited string of size:percentage pairs (e.g., \'28:5.0|30:12.0|32:22.0|34:25.0|36:18.0|38:12.0|40:4.0|42:2.0\'). Null for alpha or shoe size scale types. Used when the number of numeric sizes exceeds the available discrete percentage columns.']
  pct_l decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size L in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 25.00 = 25%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and replenishment size pack optimization.']
  pct_m decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size M in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 28.00 = 28%). Typically the modal size in standard bell-curve distributions. Null for numeric or shoe size scale types.']
  pct_s decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size S in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 18.00 = 18%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and allocation size breaks.']
  pct_xl decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XL in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 14.00 = 14%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Used in buy plan size ratio calculations and initial distribution planning.']
  pct_xs decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XS in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 8.50 = 8.5%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. All size distribution percentages must sum to 100.00 for a valid curve. Serves as the principal MEASUREMENT_OR_VALUE for this MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  pct_xxl decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of total unit sales allocated to size XXL in the alpha size run. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 6.50 = 6.5%). Null for numeric or shoe size scale types. Monitoring XXL distribution is critical for plus-size assortment planning and markdown minimization.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region for which this size curve applies (e.g., \'NA\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\', \'LATAM\'). Size distributions vary significantly by region due to demographic differences. Used to apply region-specific size ratios in allocation and buy planning.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name or user identifier of the merchandise planner who last reviewed and validated this size curve. Provides accountability for curve quality and supports audit trail requirements for merchandise planning governance.']
  sample_unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of units sold in the historical basis period used to derive this size curve. Higher sample counts yield higher statistical confidence. Used to validate confidence_level assignment — curves based on fewer than a threshold number of units are flagged as low confidence.']
  seasonal_adjustment_factor decimal(5,4) [note: 'Multiplicative adjustment factor applied to the base size distribution percentages to account for seasonal demand shifts (e.g., 1.0500 = 5% upward adjustment). Captures known seasonal size preference changes such as outerwear seasons skewing larger. Applied in Anaplan seasonal buy plan calculations.']
  shoe_size_distribution varchar [note: 'Serialized representation of the size distribution percentages for shoe size runs (US sizes 5-14 or equivalent), stored as a delimited string of size:percentage pairs (e.g., \'7:8.0|7.5:10.0|8:15.0|8.5:18.0|9:17.0|9.5:14.0|10:12.0|10.5:6.0\'). Null for alpha or numeric apparel size scale types.']
  size_count varchar [note: 'Total number of discrete sizes in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., 6 for XS-XXL alpha, 8 for 28-42 even numeric). Drives the number of size break columns populated in allocation and replenishment size pack calculations.']
  size_curve_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the size curve profile. Active curves are used in live buy plans and allocation runs. Draft curves are under development. Archived curves are retained for historical analysis but excluded from new planning cycles. Serves as the LIFECYCLE_STATUS for this MASTER_RESOURCE entity.']
  size_range_max varchar [note: 'The largest size in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., \'XXL\', \'42\', \'14\'). Combined with size_range_min to define the full size run span. Used to validate that buy plan size breaks fall within the defined range.']
  size_range_min varchar [note: 'The smallest size in the size run covered by this curve (e.g., \'XS\', \'28\', \'5\'). Combined with size_range_max to define the full size run span. Used to validate that buy plan size breaks fall within the defined range.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this size curve record originated. Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer. Oracle Retail is the primary system of record; Anaplan is used for planning-layer curves; Centric PLM may provide initial vendor-recommended curves.']
  store_cluster_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the store cluster or store tier to which this size curve applies (e.g., \'FLAGSHIP\', \'MALL_A\', \'OUTLET_B\'). Store clusters group locations with similar size-selling patterns. Enables cluster-level size allocation optimization to reduce size-related markdowns.']
  str_basis_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum Sell-Through Rate (STR) achieved in the historical basis period for the data to be considered statistically valid for curve derivation. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 75.00 means 75% STR). Curves derived from low-STR periods may be flagged as low-confidence.']
  subclass_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise subclass code within the class hierarchy from Oracle Retail. Enables the most granular level of size curve definition when size distributions differ within a class (e.g., \'STRETCH_WOVEN\' vs \'NON_STRETCH_WOVEN\' within WOVEN_TOPS).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this size curve record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to any attribute of the size curve profile. Used for incremental data pipeline processing in the Databricks Silver Layer and for audit trail purposes.']

  Note: 'Size curve profile master defining the expected unit sales distribution across size runs (alpha: XS/S/M/L/XL/XXL, numeric: 28-42, shoe: 5-14) for a style, merchandise class, or product category by channel, region, and store cluster. Captures curve type (standard, fitted, oversized), historical sell-through basis period, confidence level, and seasonal adjustment factors. Used to optimize buy plan size ratios, allocation size breaks, replenishment size packs, and initial distribution. Critical for minimizing size-related markdowns and stockouts in apparel where size fragmentation drives 15-20% of excess inventory. Managed in Oracle Retail and Anaplan.'
}

Table merchandising.season {
  season_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for the merchandise season record. Primary key for the season master reference table in the merchandising domain.']
  carbon_target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_target. Business justification: Seasonal collections align with annual carbon reduction targets. Merchandisers plan seasons to contribute to company-wide carbon goals, tracking emissions from materials, production, and logistics aga']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_standard. Business justification: Seasonal quality standards vary by delivery window, market regulations, and brand strategy. Standards are versioned by season for compliance tracking. Essential for seasonal quality planning and regul']
  target_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_target. Business justification: Seasons track broader sustainability goals beyond carbon (water, waste, circularity). Merchandisers plan seasonal targets for sustainable material percentage, water reduction, and circular design prin']
  channel_scope varchar [note: 'The distribution channel scope for which this season is planned. \'All\' covers all channels; \'DTC\' covers direct-to-consumer retail and e-commerce; \'Wholesale\' covers wholesale and distribution partners. Drives channel-specific OTB allocation and assortment planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  clearance_end_date date [note: 'The date by which all clearance selling for this season must be completed. After this date, remaining inventory is subject to return-to-vendor (RTV), off-price channel transfer, or write-off. Defines the final boundary of the season\'s sell-through window.']
  collection_theme varchar [note: 'The creative or commercial theme name assigned to this season\'s collection (e.g., \'Urban Explorer\', \'Coastal Luxe\'). Defined during collection planning in Centric PLM and used to align product design, marketing campaigns, and visual merchandising narratives.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this season record was first created in the data platform. Supports audit trail, data lineage, and record lifecycle tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which all monetary planning amounts for this season are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Ensures consistent financial reporting across multi-currency operations in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO and Anaplan.']
  end_date date [note: 'The official last trading date of the merchandise season — the date on which the season\'s assortment is retired from active selling. Defines the close of the OTB budget window and triggers end-of-season markdown cadence and clearance planning.']
  first_delivery_date date [note: 'The earliest scheduled date for merchandise receipts into the distribution center or stores for this season. Defines the opening of the delivery window and is a key milestone in the Time and Action (T&A) calendar for sourcing and procurement coordination.']
  fiscal_half varchar [note: 'The fiscal half-year period (H1 or H2) to which this season is aligned. Spring/Summer and Resort seasons typically fall in H1; Fall/Winter, Pre-Fall, and Holiday seasons in H2. Used for semi-annual merchandise financial planning (MFP) and OTB budget allocation in Anaplan.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this season belongs for financial planning and reporting purposes (e.g., 2025). Aligns season-level OTB budgets and merchandise financial plans (MFP) to the corporate fiscal calendar in Anaplan and SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'The primary gender target classification for this season\'s collection, used to segment assortment planning, allocation strategies, and marketing campaigns. Aligns with product hierarchy classifications in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Centric PLM.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this season is currently active and available for use in planning, buying, and allocation transactions. Set to True when the season status is \'active\'; False for planning, closed, or archived seasons. Used to filter active seasons in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan.']
  is_nos boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this season includes a Never Out of Stock (NOS) or carry-over program, where core styles are replenished continuously regardless of seasonal boundaries. NOS seasons require separate OTB budget allocation and replenishment planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  last_delivery_date date [note: 'The latest permissible date for merchandise receipts for this season. Defines the close of the delivery window. Receipts after this date may be subject to late delivery penalties or cancellation per vendor agreements. Key milestone in the T&A calendar.']
  markdown_cadence varchar [note: 'The planned frequency of markdown (MD) events during the clearance phase of this season. Drives the markdown calendar in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management and informs maintained markup (MMU) forecasting in Anaplan.']
  markdown_start_date date [note: 'The date on which the first planned markdown (MD) event for this season begins. Initiates the end-of-season clearance cadence. Drives markdown (MD) planning in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management and Anaplan merchandise financial planning.']
  market_week_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the industry market week associated with this season. Together with market_week_start_date, defines the window during which wholesale buyers finalize orders and assortment commitments.']
  market_week_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the industry market week (trade show / buying week) associated with this season, during which buyers review collections and place wholesale orders. Key date for collection planning and wholesale distribution calendars.']
  mfp_version varchar [note: 'The version identifier of the approved merchandise financial plan (MFP) associated with this season in Anaplan (e.g., \'v1.0-Initial\', \'v2.1-Revised\'). Tracks plan revisions through the planning cycle and ensures alignment between OTB budgets and financial targets.']
  num_planned_skus varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) planned for this season across all styles, colors, and sizes. Used to manage assortment breadth, warehouse capacity planning in Manhattan Associates WMS, and OTB unit buy planning.']
  num_planned_styles varchar [note: 'The total number of distinct styles (SKU parent-level products) planned for this season\'s assortment. Set during collection planning in Centric PLM and used to manage line architecture, OTB depth-versus-breadth trade-offs, and assortment productivity analysis.']
  order_deadline_date date [note: 'The final date by which all purchase orders (POs) for this season must be submitted to suppliers to ensure on-time delivery within the season\'s delivery window. Critical T&A calendar milestone managed in SAP S/4HANA MM and Centric PLM.']
  otb_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total approved Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget in the company\'s reporting currency for this season. Represents the maximum planned merchandise spend authorized for buying activities. Set during merchandise financial planning (MFP) in Anaplan and tracked against actual PO commitments in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  planned_gross_margin_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned gross margin percentage for this season as set in the merchandise financial plan (MFP). Calculated as (planned sales minus planned cost of goods sold) divided by planned sales. Drives GMROI targets and IMU/MMU planning in Anaplan.']
  planned_imu_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned initial markup (IMU) percentage for this season, representing the difference between the cost of goods and the initial retail price (MSRP) as a percentage of retail. Set during merchandise financial planning and used to guide pricing strategy in Oracle Retail Merchandising System Price Management.']
  planned_sales_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total planned net sales revenue in the company\'s reporting currency for this season as set during merchandise financial planning (MFP). Serves as the top-line revenue target against which actual sell-through rate (STR) and average unit retail (AUR) performance are measured.']
  planned_str_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The planned sell-through rate (STR) percentage target for this season, representing the proportion of received inventory expected to be sold at full price before markdown. Used to benchmark actual in-season performance and trigger markdown cadence decisions.']
  planning_start_date date [note: 'The date on which internal merchandise planning activities formally begin for this season, including OTB budget setting, assortment planning, and buy plan development in Anaplan and Oracle Retail Merchandising System. Precedes the trading start date by several months.']
  pre_production_start_date date [note: 'The date on which pre-production (PP) activities commence for this season, including fabric and trim approvals, fit samples, and pre-production sample sign-off. Key milestone in the T&A calendar tracked in Centric PLM and Infor PLM.']
  region_scope varchar [note: 'The geographic region or market scope for which this season is defined (e.g., \'Global\', \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\'). Supports multi-regional merchandise planning where season start/end dates and delivery windows may vary by market. Used in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan regional planning.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the season across all planning and operational systems (e.g., SS25, FW24, HO24, RES25). Used as the business key in Oracle Retail Merchandising System, Anaplan, and Centric PLM for cross-system season referencing.']
  season_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description of the season, capturing key strategic intent, collection positioning, target consumer profile, or planning notes. Used by merchandising and planning teams for context in Oracle Retail Merchandising System and Anaplan.']
  season_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable full name of the merchandise season (e.g., \'Spring/Summer 2025\', \'Fall/Winter 2024\', \'Holiday 2024\', \'Resort 2025\'). Used in reporting, planning dashboards, and buyer-facing documents.']
  season_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the merchandise season. \'planning\' indicates the season is in pre-production planning; \'active\' indicates the season is currently trading; \'closed\' indicates the season has ended trading; \'archived\' indicates the season is retained for historical reference only. Controls visibility in planning tools and reporting.']
  season_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the trading season by industry-standard merchandise calendar type. Drives collection planning, OTB budget cycles, and markdown cadence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: Spring/Summer|Fall/Winter|Resort|Pre-Fall|Holiday|Cruise — promote to reference product if additional season types are introduced]']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the operational system of record from which this season record was sourced (e.g., ORM = Oracle Retail Merchandising System, ANAPLAN = Anaplan, CENTRIC = Centric PLM). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  source_system_season_code varchar [note: 'The native season identifier as recorded in the originating operational system of record (e.g., Oracle Retail Merchandising System season number, Anaplan season key). Enables traceability and reconciliation between the lakehouse Silver Layer and source systems.']
  start_date date [note: 'The official first trading date of the merchandise season — the date on which the season\'s assortment becomes available for sale in retail stores and e-commerce channels. Serves as the temporal anchor for all season-level planning entities including assortment, OTB, allocation, and MFP.']
  tna_calendar_code varchar [note: 'Reference code linking this season to its associated Time and Action (T&A) calendar template in Centric PLM or Infor PLM. The T&A calendar defines all critical path milestones from design brief through to final delivery for the season.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this season record was last modified in the data platform. Used to detect changes, support incremental data loads, and maintain audit trail compliance in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  weeks_of_supply_target decimal(5,1) [note: 'The target number of weeks of supply (WOS) to be maintained during the peak trading period of this season. Used to calibrate inventory depth in buy plans and allocation strategies, ensuring sufficient stock without excess inventory build-up.']

  Note: 'Merchandise season master reference defining trading seasons (Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter, Resort, Pre-Fall, Holiday, Cruise) with start and end dates, delivery windows, market week dates, T&A calendar milestones, financial planning periods, and markdown cadence calendar. Every planning entity in the merchandising domain (assortment, collection, OTB, buy plan, allocation, MFP) references a season as its temporal anchor. Serves as the canonical time-boundary definition for merchandise planning cycles. Managed in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.'
}

Table merchandising.price_change_event {
  price_change_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each price change event record in the merchandising system. Primary key for the price_change_event data product.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Markdown decisions are tracked against cost center budgets for accountability. Finance requires this link to allocate markdown expenses to responsible cost centers and monitor markdown budget consumpt']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee or merchandising manager who authorized and approved this price change event. Supports governance and audit trail requirements.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Price changes post to specific GL accounts (markdown expense, inventory reserve, promotional allowance). Finance requires this link to automate GL posting of markdown events and ensure proper expense ']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Price changes may result from compliance incidents (product recalls, safety violations requiring clearance, labeling corrections); incident triggers markdown and links event to root cause for financia']
  markdown_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the Open-to-Buy (OTB) markdown budget allocation against which this price change event is charged. Links the price change to the merchandise financial plan managed in Anaplan.']
  marketing_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Price changes (especially markdowns) are often timed to marketing campaigns. This link tracks which campaign drove the pricing decision, enabling analysis of campaign-driven markdown effectiveness and']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: Quality NCRs trigger clearance markdowns and vendor chargebacks. NCR documentation justifies markdown budget usage and supports vendor debit memos. Critical for linking quality failures to financial i']
  price_master_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.price_master. Business justification: Price change events modify prices; price_master is the authoritative source for pricing data. Adding price_master_id FK links transactional price changes to master pricing records and removes redundan']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Markdowns impact profit center margin performance. Financial reporting requires linking markdown events to profit centers to measure margin erosion by channel/brand and assess merchandising effectiven']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotional campaign or event that triggered this price change, if applicable. Links promotional price changes to the originating marketing or merchandising promotion.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store, channel, or distribution location where this price change event is effective. Supports location-specific pricing strategies across DTC, wholesale, and e-commerce channels.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific Stock Keeping Unit (SKU) to which this price change event applies. A SKU represents the most granular sellable unit (style-color-size combination).']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent style (product) to which this price change event applies. A price change may be applied at the style level and cascade to all associated SKUs.']
  vendor_allowance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.vendor_allowance. Business justification: Price change events (markdowns) may be funded by vendor allowances (markdown money). Adding vendor_allowance_id FK links markdown execution to vendor funding source. FK will reference merchandising.ve']
  vendor_cost_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.vendor_cost_quote. Business justification: Markdown decisions consider original vendor costs from quotes to assess margin impact and determine vendor allowance eligibility. Critical for markdown planning, vendor negotiation for markdown suppor']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the price change event was formally approved by the authorized merchandising manager or buyer. Required for governance compliance and audit trail in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  change_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code from Oracle Retail Merchandising System explaining the business rationale for the price change (e.g., SEASONAL_MD, COMPETITIVE_RESPONSE, CLEARANCE, PROMO, CORRECTION). Supports markdown (MD) analysis and GMROI reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: promote to reference product]']
  change_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative description providing additional context for the price change event beyond the standardized reason code. Entered by the merchandising planner or buyer.']
  change_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price change event by its business purpose. Permanent Markdown (MD) reduces the retail price indefinitely; Temporary Markdown is time-bound; Markup (MU) increases price; Clearance applies end-of-season liquidation pricing; Promotional applies campaign-driven pricing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: permanent_markdown|temporary_markdown|markup|clearance|promotional — promote to reference product]']
  channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel to which this price change event applies. Supports channel-specific pricing strategies across Direct-to-Consumer (DTC), wholesale, retail store, e-commerce (Salesforce Commerce Cloud), and outlet channels.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'The merchandise class code within the department hierarchy for the affected SKU or style. Used for class-level markdown (MD) analysis and GMROI optimization in Anaplan merchandise financial planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price change event record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the price change amounts are denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Supports multi-currency retail operations across international markets.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'The merchandise department classification code for the SKU or style affected by this price change event. Aligns with Oracle Retail Merchandising System department hierarchy for assortment planning and OTB reporting.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the new price becomes active and visible to customers across applicable channels. Used for scheduling future price changes in advance.']
  end_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the price change expires and the price reverts to the prior or next scheduled price. Null for permanent price changes. Critical for temporary markdowns and promotional pricing.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the price change event within the Oracle Retail Merchandising System workflow. Drives whether the price change is visible and applied at POS and e-commerce channels.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise real-world date and time when the price change event was executed or became effective in the system of record. This is the principal business event time, distinct from audit timestamps.']
  imu_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Initial Markup (IMU) percentage for the affected SKU or style at the time of this price change event. Represents the original margin built into the retail price relative to cost. Used to assess markdown (MD) impact on maintained markup (MMU).']
  is_clearance boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change event designates the SKU or style as a clearance item. Once flagged as clearance, the item is typically excluded from future regular-price replenishment and tracked separately for end-of-season liquidation reporting.']
  is_competitive_response boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change event was triggered by a competitive pricing response to a market competitor\'s price action. Supports competitive pricing analytics and brand positioning strategy.']
  is_pos_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this price change event has been successfully transmitted and applied at the Point of Sale (POS) system via SAP Customer Activity Repository. Supports reconciliation between merchandising system and store operations.']
  markdown_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating the ordinal position of this markdown (MD) event within the seasonal markdown cadence for the affected SKU or style (e.g., 1st markdown, 2nd markdown, 3rd markdown). Supports sell-through rate (STR) analysis and markdown optimization.']
  mmu_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Maintained Markup (MMU) percentage for the affected SKU or style after applying this price change event. Reflects the actual margin achieved post-markdown or post-markup. Key metric for GMROI optimization.']
  new_retail_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The updated retail selling price applied to the SKU or style as a result of this price change event. Represents the Average Unit Retail (AUR) post-change and is used in sell-through rate (STR) and GMROI analysis.']
  original_retail_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The retail selling price of the SKU or style immediately prior to this price change event. Serves as the baseline for calculating markdown (MD) depth, sell-through rate (STR) impact, and maintained markup (MMU).']
  price_change_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The absolute monetary value of the price change (new_retail_price minus original_retail_price). A negative value indicates a markdown (MD); a positive value indicates a markup (MU). Used in merchandise financial planning and OTB impact analysis.']
  price_change_level varchar [note: 'Indicates the merchandise hierarchy level at which this price change event was initiated. A style-level change cascades to all associated SKUs; a SKU-level change applies only to the specific variant.']
  price_change_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the price change event as assigned by Oracle Retail Merchandising System or SAP S/4HANA SD module. Used for cross-system reconciliation and audit traceability.']
  price_change_percent decimal(8,4) [note: 'The percentage change in retail price relative to the original retail price. Calculated as (price_change_amount / original_retail_price) * 100. Used for markdown (MD) depth reporting and sell-through rate (STR) analysis in Anaplan.']
  pricing_zone_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the geographic or channel-based pricing zone to which this price change event applies. Pricing zones group locations with identical pricing strategies in Oracle Retail Merchandising System.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The merchandise season code (e.g., SS25, FW24) associated with the collection or buy plan for the affected SKU or style. Critical for seasonal markdown (MD) cadence management and end-of-season clearance pricing.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record that originated this price change event. Supports data lineage and cross-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  str_at_change decimal(8,4) [note: 'The Sell-Through Rate (STR) for the affected SKU or style at the time of this price change event, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.65 = 65%). Provides context for the markdown decision and is used in post-event STR lift analysis.']
  subclass_code varchar [note: 'The merchandise subclass code providing the most granular product hierarchy classification for the affected SKU or style. Supports subclass-level sell-through rate (STR) and markdown (MD) depth analysis.']
  units_on_hand varchar [note: 'The inventory quantity on hand for the affected SKU at the time of this price change event. Used to calculate the total markdown (MD) dollar exposure and weeks of supply (WOS) impact in merchandise financial planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this price change event record was last modified. Tracks amendments, corrections, or status updates to the price change event.']
  wos_at_change decimal(8,2) [note: 'The Weeks of Supply (WOS) for the affected SKU at the time of this price change event, calculated as units on hand divided by average weekly sales rate. Used to assess inventory urgency and justify markdown (MD) depth decisions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of any retail price modification event including permanent markdowns, temporary markdowns, markups, clearance pricing, and promotional price changes applied to a style or SKU'
}

Table merchandising.vendor_allowance {
  vendor_allowance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor allowance record. Primary key.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Vendor allowances often tied to audit performance (compliance-based rebates, markdown support contingent on factory ratings); allowance eligibility and settlement depend on audit results.']
  corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.corrective_action. Business justification: CAPA completion is a condition for allowance reinstatement after quality failures. Corrective action costs offset vendor allowances. Critical for linking quality improvement to vendor financial terms ']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor allowances offset cost center markdown budgets. Finance requires this link to allocate vendor allowance income to responsible cost centers and track net markdown expense for departmental P&L an']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or role who approved the allowance agreement or claim.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Vendor allowances post to specific GL accounts (vendor income, markdown offset, co-op advertising). Finance requires this link to automate GL posting of vendor allowance accruals and ensure proper inc']
  markdown_budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the markdown budget that this allowance offsets or supports. Nullable if not markdown-related.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.merchandise_hierarchy. Business justification: Vendor allowances have department_id, class_id but no FK to merchandise_hierarchy master. Adding merchandise_hierarchy_id FK normalizes merchandise classification and removes redundant hierarchy attri']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: NCRs reduce vendor allowance claims and trigger dispute resolution. Quality failures offset markdown allowances and co-op funding. Essential for vendor performance management and allowance settlement ']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific purchase order (PO) associated with this allowance. Nullable if allowance is not PO-specific.']
  parent_vendor_allowance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_allowance (parent_vendor_allowance_id)']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Vendor allowances improve profit center margin performance. Financial reporting requires linking vendor allowance income to profit centers to measure net margin impact by channel/brand and assess vend']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.promotion. Business justification: Vendor allowances (co-op funds, markdown money) often support specific promotional events. Vendors provide financial allowances to fund markdowns, advertising, or promotional pricing for their product']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Vendor allowances are negotiated per season; season_code exists but no FK to season master. Adding season_id FK normalizes season reference and removes redundant season_code. FK will reference merchan']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.logistics_shipment. Business justification: Vendor allowances (co-op, markdown support, freight claims) are often tied to specific shipment performance—late delivery penalties, damage claims, or duty refunds. Linking to shipment enables allowan']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Vendor allowances tied to specific POs for markdown support, co-op advertising, or performance rebates. Essential for allowance claim validation, accrual tracking, and reconciliation of vendor funding']
  supplier_esg_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.supplier_esg_assessment. Business justification: Vendor allowances are increasingly tied to ESG performance incentives (e.g., bonus allowances for achieving sustainability milestones). Buyers negotiate allowances based on supplier ESG scores to driv']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor providing the allowance or co-op funding.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Vendor allowances often tied to specific production runs for quality claims, late delivery penalties, or markdown support. Essential for vendor performance management and cost recovery.']
  accrual_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the financial accrual period during which the allowance is earned.']
  accrual_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the financial accrual period during which the allowance is earned.']
  actual_performance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual sales or purchase amount achieved during the accrual period, used to calculate earned allowance.']
  actual_performance_units varchar [note: 'Actual unit quantity sold or purchased during the accrual period, used to calculate earned allowance.']
  agreement_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted or committed allowance amount agreed with the vendor.']
  agreement_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the vendor allowance agreement or accrual period. Nullable for open-ended agreements.']
  agreement_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the vendor allowance agreement or accrual period.']
  allowance_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier or reference number for the vendor allowance agreement, used in vendor negotiations and financial reporting.']
  allowance_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to calculate the allowance (e.g., 5% of net sales, 2% markdown allowance). Nullable if allowance is fixed amount.']
  allowance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor allowance: draft (under negotiation), active (in effect), claimed (submitted for payment), partially settled, fully settled, expired, disputed, or cancelled. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|active|claimed|partially_settled|fully_settled|expired|disputed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  allowance_type varchar [note: 'Category of vendor allowance: markdown money (MDA), advertising allowance, volume rebate, defective allowance, Return to Vendor (RTV) credit, promotional funding, or freight allowance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: markdown_money|advertising_allowance|volume_rebate|defective_allowance|rtv_credit|promotional_funding|freight_allowance — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the allowance agreement or claim was approved.']
  claim_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or claim ID provided by the vendor for tracking the allowance claim.']
  claim_submission_deadline date [note: 'Final date by which the allowance claim must be submitted to the vendor for payment.']
  claimed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of allowance formally claimed from the vendor for settlement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor allowance record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this allowance record.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for dispute if the allowance status is disputed. Nullable if no dispute exists.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date on which a disputed allowance was resolved. Nullable if no dispute or not yet resolved.']
  earned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of allowance earned based on performance criteria (sales volume, markdown taken, promotional activity) during the accrual period.']
  gmroi_impact_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated or actual impact of this allowance on Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) for the associated merchandise.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding the vendor allowance agreement, claim, or settlement.']
  outstanding_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining allowance amount not yet settled (claimed minus settled).']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms negotiated for the allowance settlement (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Upon Receipt).']
  performance_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum sales or purchase volume required to qualify for the allowance. Nullable if no threshold applies.']
  performance_threshold_units varchar [note: 'Minimum unit quantity required to qualify for the allowance. Nullable if no unit threshold applies.']
  settled_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of allowance actually paid or credited by the vendor to date.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the allowance was settled or payment was received from the vendor. Nullable if not yet settled.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the vendor will settle the allowance: credit memo, check, wire transfer, offset against future invoices, or deduction at source.']
  source_system_allowance_code varchar [note: 'Original unique identifier for this vendor allowance in the source system.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which this vendor allowance record originated (e.g., SAP, Oracle Retail, Anaplan).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor allowance record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Master record for vendor-funded financial allowances and co-op programs including markdown money (MDA), advertising allowances, volume rebates, defective allowances, RTVs (Return to Vendor credits), and promotional funding commitments. Captures vendor, allowance type, agreement terms, earned versus claimed amounts, accrual periods, claim submission deadlines, and settlement status. Critical for gross margin management and vendor negotiation leverage. Tracked in Oracle Retail Invoice Matching and SAP FI-MM.'
}

Table merchandising.seasonal_vendor_capacity_agreement {
  seasonal_vendor_capacity_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this seasonal vendor capacity agreement record. Primary key.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the merchandise season for which this capacity agreement applies']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor entity that has committed capacity for this season']
  actual_otif_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'The actual On-Time In-Full delivery performance achieved by this vendor for this season. Calculated as (on-time in-full deliveries / total deliveries) * 100. Compared against otif_target_pct for vendor scorecard.']
  actual_quality_aql decimal(3,2) [note: 'The actual defect rate (AQL) observed for shipments from this vendor for this season. Calculated from quality inspection results. Compared against quality_target_aql for vendor scorecard.']
  actual_units_ordered varchar [note: 'The actual number of units ordered from this vendor for this season to date. Tracked to monitor capacity utilization against capacity_commitment_units.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this seasonal capacity agreement. Values: draft (initial proposal), negotiation (terms being discussed), approved (signed but not yet active), active (currently in effect), completed (season ended, all deliveries received), cancelled (agreement terminated early).']
  allocated_otb_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the season\'s total OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget allocated to this vendor for this season. Represents the planned spend with this vendor in the season\'s currency_code. Sum of all vendor allocations should not exceed season.otb_budget_amount.']
  capacity_commitment_units varchar [note: 'The number of units (pieces/garments) that the vendor has committed to produce for this specific season. This is a negotiated capacity block allocation that varies by season based on the vendor\'s total capacity and the brand\'s seasonal volume forecast.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seasonal vendor capacity agreement record was created in the system.']
