  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this seasonal vendor capacity agreement expires. Typically aligns with the season\'s last_delivery_date or clearance_end_date, after which a new agreement is negotiated for the next season.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this seasonal vendor capacity agreement becomes effective and the vendor begins accepting POs under these terms. Typically aligns with the season\'s planning_start_date or order_deadline_date window.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this agreement record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this agreement record.']
  negotiated_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The agreed-upon lead time in days from PO placement to FOB delivery for orders placed under this seasonal agreement. Lead times vary by season due to factory loading, material availability, and seasonal complexity (e.g., outerwear in FW has longer lead times than tees in SS).']
  otif_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'On-Time In-Full delivery performance target percentage agreed for this season. Represents the percentage of orders that must be delivered on the committed date with the full quantity. Performance targets may vary by season based on strategic importance and vendor tier.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment terms specific to this seasonal agreement (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, LC at sight). Payment terms may vary by season based on order volume, vendor relationship strength, and cash flow considerations.']
  quality_target_aql decimal(3,2) [note: 'Acceptable Quality Limit (AQL) target for this seasonal agreement, representing the maximum acceptable defect rate. Quality standards may be tightened or relaxed by season based on product category (e.g., luxury vs. value) and vendor maturity.']
  seasonal_cmt_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'The negotiated Cut-Make-Trim (CMT) rate or cost per unit for this vendor-season combination. CMT rates are renegotiated seasonally based on order volume, product complexity, material costs, and market conditions. Expressed in the currency specified in currency_code.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the merchandising planner or sourcing manager who created this agreement record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the seasonal capacity commitment contract between a merchandise season and a vendor. It captures the negotiated commercial terms, capacity allocations, performance targets, and operational parameters that govern the vendor-season partnership. Each record links one season to one vendor with attributes that exist only in the context of this specific seasonal engagement, including capacity blocks, CMT rates, lead times, and quality targets that are renegotiated per season.. Existence Justification: In apparel merchandising operations, each season requires capacity from 10-20 vendors to diversify sourcing risk and meet volume requirements, while each vendor simultaneously commits capacity blocks across 4+ concurrent seasons (Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter, Holiday, Resort) for multiple brands. The business actively manages seasonal vendor capacity agreements as distinct operational entities with season-specific negotiated terms including capacity commitments, CMT rates, lead times, and performance targets that are renegotiated each season based on volume forecasts, factory loading, and market conditions.'
}

Table merchandising.collection_seeding {
  collection_seeding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for this collection seeding record. Primary key.']
  collection_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the seasonal collection plan being seeded to the influencer']
  influencer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the influencer receiving the collection seeding']
  attributed_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total sales revenue attributed to this specific collection-influencer seeding, tracked via promo codes, affiliate links, or UTM parameters. Performance metric for ROI calculation.']
  content_deliverable_count varchar [note: 'Number of content pieces (posts, stories, videos) the influencer committed to create for this collection seeding. Contractual obligation tracking.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this collection seeding record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for attributed_revenue and seeding_value_amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  engagement_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average engagement rate (likes, comments, shares divided by reach) across all content pieces created for this collection seeding. Performance metric specific to this collection-influencer combination.']
  exclusivity_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days during which the influencer agrees not to post content featuring competing brands or collections. Collection-specific exclusivity term.']
  expected_post_end_date date [note: 'Planned date by which all content deliverables for this collection seeding should be completed and posted.']
  expected_post_start_date date [note: 'Planned date when the influencer should begin posting content featuring this collection. Coordinated with collection launch timing.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Unique promotional code assigned to this influencer for this collection seeding. Used for attribution tracking and customer incentives.']
  seeding_date date [note: 'Date when the collection merchandise was shipped or delivered to the influencer. Critical for coordinating content timing with launch windows.']
  seeding_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units or pieces from the collection seeded to this influencer. Tracks inventory allocation for gifting programs.']
  seeding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this collection seeding record. Values: planned (seeding scheduled), shipped (merchandise in transit), delivered (influencer received), content_in_progress (creating deliverables), completed (all deliverables posted and tracked), cancelled (seeding cancelled).']
  seeding_value_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total retail value of merchandise seeded to the influencer for this collection. Used for accounting and ROI calculation.']
  total_impressions varchar [note: 'Aggregate impressions (total views) across all content pieces created for this collection seeding. Platform-reported metric.']
  total_reach varchar [note: 'Aggregate reach (unique viewers) across all content pieces created for this collection seeding. Platform-reported metric.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this collection seeding record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the seeding event between collection_plan and influencer. It captures the operational process of seeding collection merchandise to influencers for launch amplification, content creation, and performance attribution. Each record links one collection_plan to one influencer with attributes that exist only in the context of this seeding relationship, including logistics, deliverables, exclusivity terms, and performance metrics.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion, collection seeding to influencers is an operational marketing process where a single collection is seeded to multiple influencers for launch amplification, and each influencer receives merchandise from multiple collections across seasons. The business actively manages seeding programs with collection-specific logistics (quantity, timing), contractual deliverables (content count, exclusivity windows), and performance attribution (revenue, engagement). This is not an analytical correlation but an operational relationship that marketing teams create, track, and measure as part of influencer partnership management.'
}

Table merchandising.capacity_booking {
  capacity_booking_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for this seasonal factory capacity booking record. Primary key.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the manufacturing factory providing the capacity']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the merchandise season for which capacity is being booked']
  available_capacity_units varchar [note: 'The number of production units that the factory has confirmed as available for this season booking. May differ from booked capacity if merchandising has not fully committed to the available capacity.']
  booked_capacity_units varchar [note: 'The number of production units (garments, pairs, pieces) reserved/committed by merchandising for this season at this factory. Represents the capacity allocation from the factory\'s total certified capacity.']
  booking_date date [note: 'The date on which this capacity booking was created or confirmed by the merchandising and production planning teams.']
  booking_end_date date [note: 'The last date of the production window for this season at this factory. Defines the cutoff for production completion to meet the season\'s delivery deadlines.']
  booking_start_date date [note: 'The first date of the production window during which this factory will manufacture goods for this season. Aligns with the season\'s pre-production and delivery calendar.']
  commitment_level varchar [note: 'The contractual commitment status of this capacity booking. Firm = binding commitment, Tentative = soft hold pending approval, Option = reserved but not committed, Cancelled = booking withdrawn.']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this capacity booking record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special terms, conditions, or context for this season-factory capacity booking (e.g., product category focus, quality requirements, delivery terms).']
  pricing_tier varchar [note: 'The negotiated pricing tier or rate category for this season-factory booking (e.g., Standard, Volume Discount, Premium Rush). Pricing may vary by season based on volume commitments, timing, and factory utilization.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the capacity reservation contract between a merchandise season and a manufacturing factory. It captures the strategic capacity planning process where merchandising teams book production capacity at specific factories for upcoming seasons. Each record links one season to one factory with negotiated capacity commitments, availability windows, pricing tiers, and booking terms that exist only in the context of this seasonal capacity agreement.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion, seasonal capacity planning is a strategic operational process where merchandising teams book production capacity at multiple factories for each upcoming season (Spring/Summer, Fall/Winter, etc.), and each factory simultaneously serves multiple brands and seasons. This is not a reference or ownership relationship—it is an active capacity reservation contract that production planners and sourcing teams manage throughout the planning cycle, adjusting commitments, negotiating pricing, and tracking availability windows specific to each season-factory pairing.'
}

Table merchandising.price_book {
  price_book_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for price_book']
  base_price_book_id varchar [note: 'Reference to parent or base price book when this price book is derived or inherits pricing rules from another.']
  derived_from_price_book_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on price_book (derived_from_price_book_id)']
  parent_version_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the previous version of this price book, enabling version history tracking and audit trails.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Price books are seasonal pricing references. The price_book currently has season_code STRING attribute, which should be normalized to a FK to the season master table. This establishes the temporal con']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the merchandising manager or executive who approved this price book for use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price book was approved for operational use.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel this price book is designed for, enabling channel-specific pricing strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price book record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all prices in this price book.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Target customer segment for this price book, supporting segment-based pricing and promotional strategies.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price book purpose, scope, and application within merchandising operations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the price book expires and pricing rules are no longer applicable. Nullable for open-ended price books.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the price book becomes active and pricing rules become enforceable for merchandising operations.']
  initial_markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Initial markup percentage representing the difference between cost and original retail price for items in this price book.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the default price book for the organization or channel when no other price book applies.']
  maintained_markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maintained markup percentage after markdowns, representing actual margin achieved for items in this price book.']
  markdown_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default markdown percentage applied across items in this price book for promotional or clearance pricing.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this price book record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price book record was last modified.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the price book for identification and reporting purposes.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, instructions, or business rationale for this price book.']
  price_book_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the price book used in merchandising systems and reporting.']
  price_book_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price book indicating its primary merchandising purpose and pricing strategy application.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Merchandising pricing strategy applied in this price book (MSRP: Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple price books apply to the same item. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market this price book applies to, supporting regional pricing strategies.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether pricing changes in this price book require management approval before activation.']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rule applied for rounding calculated prices in this price book to achieve psychological pricing or operational simplicity.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price book indicating its operational state and availability for use.']
  tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether prices in this price book include tax or are exclusive of tax.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number tracking revisions and updates to this price book over time.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who created this price book record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for price_book. Referenced by price_book_id.'
}

Table merchandising.division {
  division_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for division']
  parent_division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent division in a hierarchical division structure. Null for top-level divisions.']
  allocation_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary allocation strategy used to distribute merchandise to stores for this division.']
  buyer_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary buyer or merchandising manager responsible for this division.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this division record was first created in the system.']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the division, used for reporting and system integration.']
  division_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the division\'s scope, product categories, and business purpose.']
  division_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the division (e.g., Men\'s Apparel, Women\'s Footwear, Accessories).']
  division_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the division by product category type.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this division was discontinued or became inactive. Null for active divisions.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this division became active in the merchandise hierarchy.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this division record was last updated.']
  lead_time_weeks varchar [note: 'Average lead time in weeks from order placement to merchandise receipt for this division.']
  markdown_cadence varchar [note: 'Typical frequency of markdown events for products in this division.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level of this division within the merchandise hierarchy (e.g., 1=Division, 2=Department, 3=Class).']
  otb_budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current open-to-buy budget amount allocated to this division for merchandise purchasing.']
  otb_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the OTB budget amount.']
  planner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary merchandise planner responsible for assortment and allocation planning for this division.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Pricing tier classification for products within this division.']
  private_label_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target percentage of private label merchandise within this division\'s assortment.']
  product_lifecycle_weeks varchar [note: 'Average product lifecycle duration in weeks for merchandise in this division.']
  replenishment_method varchar [note: 'Method used for inventory replenishment for products in this division.']
  seasonal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this division carries seasonal merchandise (True) or year-round merchandise (False).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value used to control the display order of divisions in reports and user interfaces.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the division.']
  sustainability_rating varchar [note: 'Sustainability rating for the division based on environmental and ethical sourcing practices.']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary customer demographic segment targeted by this division.']
  target_gmroi decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target GMROI for this division, calculated as gross margin dollars / average inventory cost.']
  target_gross_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target gross margin percentage for this division, used in merchandise financial planning.']
  target_initial_markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target initial markup percentage for products in this division, calculated as (retail - cost) / retail * 100.']
  target_maintained_markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target maintained markup percentage after markdowns for this division.']
  target_sell_through_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target sell-through rate percentage for this division, calculated as units sold / units received * 100.']
  vendor_concentration_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of division purchases concentrated with top vendors, used for risk assessment.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for division. Referenced by division_id.'
}

Table merchandising.channel {
  channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for channel']
  parent_channel_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent channel if this channel is a sub-channel or variant within a hierarchical channel structure. Null for top-level channels.']
  region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the geographic region where this channel primarily operates.']
  sales_organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales organization responsible for managing this channel.']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking (1=highest) used in merchandise allocation strategies when distributing inventory across channels.']
  assortment_strategy varchar [note: 'Merchandising assortment strategy defining the breadth and depth of product selection offered through this channel.']
  average_transaction_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Historical average transaction value in the channel\'s currency, used for financial planning and Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) analysis.']
  channel_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the channel by operational medium: physical (brick-and-mortar), digital (online), or omnichannel (integrated).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the channel, used for reporting and system integration (e.g., WEB, RETAIL, WHSL).']
  channel_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the business leader or manager responsible for this channel\'s performance and strategy.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the sales and distribution channel (e.g., E-Commerce, Retail Stores, Wholesale Partners).']
  channel_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the channel in the merchandising system lifecycle.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the channel based on ownership and operational model (direct-owned, indirect-partner, hybrid, franchise, marketplace, outlet).']
  commission_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Commission rate (as a decimal, e.g., 0.0500 for 5%) paid to channel partners or sales associates for transactions in this channel.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for business inquiries and operational communication related to this channel.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for business inquiries and operational communication related to this channel.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the primary country of operation for this channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for financial transactions in this channel.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the channel, including its purpose, target customer segment, and operational characteristics.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this channel ceased operations or was retired. Null for currently active channels.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this channel became operational and available for transactions.']
  fulfillment_method varchar [note: 'Primary method used to fulfill customer orders in this channel: ship from store, ship from distribution center, in-store pickup, drop-ship, or mixed.']
  inventory_visibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether real-time inventory levels are visible to customers in this channel (True) or not (False).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel record is currently active (True) or logically deleted/archived (False) in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this channel record was last updated in the system.']
  loyalty_program_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether purchases made through this channel are eligible for loyalty program points and rewards (True) or not (False).']
  markdown_policy varchar [note: 'Markdown policy governing price reductions in this channel to optimize sell-through rate (STR) and inventory turnover.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or special instructions related to this channel\'s operations or configuration.']
  omnichannel_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel is integrated into the omnichannel customer experience (True) or operates independently (False).']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for this channel (e.g., 9:00 AM - 9:00 PM Mon-Sat, 24/7 for digital channels).']
  payment_methods_accepted varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods accepted in this channel (e.g., credit card, debit card, digital wallet, cash, gift card).']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary pricing approach used in this channel: Manufacturer\'s Suggested Retail Price (MSRP), promotional, clearance, dynamic, tiered, or wholesale pricing.']
  return_policy_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the return and exchange policy applicable to purchases made through this channel.']
  seasonal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel operates seasonally (True) or year-round (False).']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary customer demographic or psychographic segment that this channel is designed to serve (e.g., premium, value-conscious, athletic, lifestyle).']
  target_initial_markup_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Initial Markup percentage for merchandise sold through this channel, used in Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget planning.']
  target_maintained_markup_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Maintained Markup percentage after markdowns, used to measure pricing effectiveness and margin preservation.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to transactions in this channel for sales tax calculation.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL for digital channels or channel-specific landing pages.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for channel. Referenced by channel_id.'
}

Table merchandising.region {
  region_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for region']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary distribution center serving this region for inventory allocation and fulfillment.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee responsible for managing merchandising operations in this region.']
  parent_region_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent region in a hierarchical region structure, enabling multi-level geographic or organizational rollups.']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for inventory allocation decisions when supply is constrained (lower number = higher priority).']
  aur_target_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target average unit retail price for merchandise sold in this region, used for assortment planning and pricing strategy.']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Climate classification of the region used for seasonal assortment planning and collection timing.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the primary country associated with this region.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this region record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency used for financial planning and reporting in this region.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this region was discontinued or became inactive. Null indicates the region is currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this region became active and available for business operations and planning.']
  gmroi_target decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target gross margin return on investment ratio for this region, measuring profitability of inventory investment.']
  imu_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target initial markup percentage for merchandise in this region, representing the difference between cost and initial retail price.']
  is_ecommerce_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether e-commerce sales channels are active and operational in this region.']
  is_wholesale_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether wholesale distribution partnerships are active in this region.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO language code representing the primary language used in this region for business communications.']
  markdown_policy varchar [note: 'Markdown policy governing price reduction strategies and timing for this region to optimize sell-through rate (STR) and gross margin return on investment (GMROI).']
  market_size_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the region based on market size and maturity for strategic planning and resource allocation.']
  otb_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Open-to-buy budget allocated to this region for merchandise purchasing, representing planned inventory investment.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary pricing strategy applied in this region based on market positioning and competitive landscape.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code representing the region, used for external identification and system integration.']
  region_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the region including its scope, coverage, and business purpose.']
  region_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the region hierarchy (e.g., 1=Global, 2=Continental, 3=Country, 4=State/Province, 5=District).']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the region (e.g., North America, EMEA, Asia Pacific).']
  region_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the region indicating whether it is actively used in business operations.']
  region_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the region based on its business purpose (geographic, sales, merchandising, distribution, operational, administrative).']
  regulatory_compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting region-specific regulatory requirements, trade restrictions, or compliance considerations.']
  sales_target_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual sales target amount set for this region, used for performance measurement and planning.']
  season_calendar_type varchar [note: 'Seasonal calendar pattern used for merchandising planning in this region (e.g., northern hemisphere follows spring/summer/fall/winter).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to this region for sales tax and VAT calculations.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'Standard time zone identifier for the region (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London) used for scheduling and reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this region record was last modified in the system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for region. Referenced by region_id.'
}

Table merchandising.price_list {
  price_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for price_list']
  base_price_list_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent or base price list from which this price list inherits default pricing, used for hierarchical price list structures.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to merchandising.season. Business justification: Price lists are seasonal pricing references. The price_list currently has season_code STRING attribute, which should be normalized to a FK to the season master table. This establishes the temporal con']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status of the price list, tracking whether it has been reviewed and authorized for use by merchandising management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the merchandising manager or authorized person who approved this price list for activation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the price list was approved, used for audit trail and compliance tracking in merchandising financial planning.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Distribution channel or sales channel for which this price list is applicable, supporting omnichannel pricing strategies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price list record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which all prices in this price list are denominated.']
  customer_segment varchar [note: 'Target customer segment or tier for which this price list is designed, enabling segment-specific pricing and promotional strategies.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the price list purpose, scope, and applicability, including target market segments and merchandising strategy context.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default discount percentage applied across all items in this price list, used for promotional or segment-specific pricing adjustments.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date on which the price list expires and is no longer applicable for pricing transactions. Nullable for open-ended price lists.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the price list becomes active and applicable for pricing transactions and merchandising operations.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the default price list to be applied when no other specific price list criteria are met.']
  is_tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether prices in this price list include applicable taxes or are exclusive of tax, affecting gross-to-net price calculations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this price list record was last updated, supporting change tracking and audit compliance.']
  markup_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default markup percentage applied to cost prices in this price list, representing the Initial Markup (IMU) or Maintained Markup (MMU) strategy.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that must be ordered for prices in this price list to apply, commonly used in wholesale and bulk pricing scenarios.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the price list, used for identification and display purposes in merchandising and planning systems.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the price list, capturing business context, special instructions, or merchandising strategy rationale.']
  price_list_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier code for the price list, used for reference in merchandising systems and business communications.']
  price_list_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the price list by its business purpose and target customer segment, determining pricing strategy and discount applicability.']
  pricing_strategy varchar [note: 'Pricing methodology or strategy applied in this price list, such as Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP), cost-plus, or competitive pricing.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used to resolve conflicts when multiple price lists are applicable to the same transaction, with lower numbers indicating higher priority.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region or market code to which this price list applies, enabling regional pricing strategies and localization.']
  rounding_rule varchar [note: 'Rule applied for rounding calculated prices in this price list, such as rounding to nearest cent, dollar, or psychological pricing thresholds.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the price list, indicating whether it is available for use in pricing calculations and transactions.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the price list, incremented with each revision to track changes and maintain pricing history.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for price_list. Referenced by price_list_id.'
}

Table merchandising.sales_organization {
  sales_organization_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for sales_organization']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the sales organization headquarters or primary location.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, floor, or building information.']
  allocation_strategy varchar [note: 'Merchandise allocation strategy used by this sales organization to distribute inventory.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the sales organization is located.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the sales organization used in external systems and reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code associated with this sales organization for budgeting and expense tracking.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the sales organization is registered or primarily operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales organization record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code used for financial transactions and reporting by this sales organization.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this sales organization ceased operations or was closed. Null for currently active organizations.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this sales organization became active and operational.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the sales organization.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the sales organization hierarchy, with 1 being the top level.']
  is_franchise boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sales organization operates as a franchise location.']
  is_outlet boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sales organization is an outlet store selling discounted or clearance merchandise.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code for the primary language used by this sales organization.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this sales organization record was last updated or modified.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official registered legal entity name associated with this sales organization for compliance and financial reporting.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the manager or director responsible for this sales organization.']
  merchandise_hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Level in the merchandise hierarchy that this sales organization is authorized to manage or sell.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full legal or business name of the sales organization.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to this sales organization.']
  parent_sales_organization_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent sales organization in a hierarchical structure, enabling multi-level organizational reporting.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the sales organization.']
  planning_group varchar [note: 'Planning group or cluster used for assortment planning and Open-to-Buy (OTB) budget allocation.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the sales organization address.']
  pricing_zone varchar [note: 'Pricing zone or tier assigned to this sales organization for Manufacturer Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) and markdown strategies.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial profit center code for revenue and profitability reporting.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or territory where the sales organization operates.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'Primary sales channel or distribution method used by this sales organization.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name or display name of the sales organization used in user interfaces and reports.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the sales organization is located.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the sales organization in its lifecycle.']
  supports_ecommerce boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sales organization supports online sales and e-commerce transactions.']
  supports_omnichannel boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this sales organization supports omnichannel capabilities such as buy online pickup in store (BOPIS) or ship from store.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number or VAT registration number for the sales organization.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the sales organization location, used for scheduling and reporting.']
  type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sales organization by business model or channel type.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for sales_organization. Referenced by sales_organization_id.'
}

Table store.retail_store {
  retail_store_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each physical retail store location operated by Apparel Fashion. Primary key for the retail_store master record, referenced by POS, inventory, workforce, and omnichannel systems as the SSOT store dimension key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Retail stores are cost centers in apparel operations. Essential for store-level P&L reporting, budget variance analysis, and operational expense allocation. Every store manager reviews cost center per']
  district_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.district. Business justification: Retail stores belong to districts for regional management hierarchy. retail_store currently has district_name (STRING) which is redundant once district_id FK is added. The district table contains dist']
  district_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: District managers oversee 8-15 store clusters, responsible for performance management, operational compliance, and escalation resolution. Essential for retail organizational hierarchy reporting, labor']
  energy_consumption_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.energy_consumption. Business justification: Stores consume energy for HVAC, lighting, and operations. Facilities teams track consumption by location for Scope 2 emissions reporting, utility cost management, renewable energy transition planning,']
  primary_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: Each store is assigned a primary DC for replenishment routing, inventory allocation planning, and freight cost modeling. Fundamental supply chain network design relationship used in allocation and tra']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Stores are profit centers for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Required for management reporting, regional performance comparison, and strategic decision-making on store portfolio. Standa']
  renewable_energy_certificate_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.renewable_energy_certificate. Business justification: Stores are allocated renewable energy certificates to offset Scope 2 emissions from electricity consumption. Sustainability teams track REC allocation by facility for carbon accounting, RE100 complian']
  store_cluster_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to store.store_cluster. Business justification: Retail stores are grouped into clusters for merchandising, pricing, and operational strategy. The visual_merchandising_plan product references store_cluster_code, indicating stores belong to clusters.']
  waste_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.waste_record. Business justification: Stores generate operational waste (packaging, hangers, damaged goods, cardboard). Waste management teams track by location for zero-waste-to-landfill programs, diversion rate reporting, circular econo']
  water_usage_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.water_usage. Business justification: Stores use water for restrooms, cleaning, and HVAC systems. Facilities teams track water consumption by location for sustainability reporting, utility management, water stewardship programs, and opera']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line of the retail store location. Used for logistics, lease documentation, customer store-finder, and regulatory filings. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the retail store (suite, floor, unit, mall name, or building identifier). Supplements address_line1 for precise location identification in complex retail environments such as shopping malls or department stores.']
  annual_base_rent decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual base rent amount payable under the store lease agreement in the store\'s local currency. Used for occupancy cost analysis, GMROI (Gross Margin Return on Investment) calculation, and store profitability modelling. Excludes turnover/percentage rent and service charges.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality in which the retail store is located. Used for geographic reporting, tax jurisdiction determination, and regional performance analytics.']
  climate_zone varchar [note: 'Köppen-based climate zone classification for the store\'s geographic location. Drives seasonal assortment planning, markdown timing, and inventory allocation decisions for weather-sensitive apparel categories (outerwear, swimwear, knitwear).']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the store permanently ceased trading operations. Nullable for active stores. Used for store network rationalization reporting, lease surrender tracking, and historical performance analysis. Populated only when operational_status is permanently_closed.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country code for the country in which the retail store operates (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA, DEU). Drives currency, tax, regulatory compliance, and international reporting hierarchies.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the retail store master record was first created in the system of record (SAP S/4HANA Real Estate / SAP Customer Activity Repository). Used for data lineage, audit trail, and record lifecycle management in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the primary trading currency of the store (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR). Used for POS transaction recording, financial consolidation, and MSRP/AUR reporting in local currency.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary operational email address for the retail store location. Used for internal communications, vendor coordination, and omnichannel order notifications. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  fitting_room_count varchar [note: 'Total number of fitting rooms (changing rooms/dressing rooms) available in the store. Used for store capacity planning, customer experience benchmarking, and visual merchandising layout design. Relevant for apparel conversion rate analytics.']
  gross_selling_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total gross interior square footage of the retail store including all selling, back-of-house, stockroom, and ancillary areas. Used for lease cost-per-square-foot analysis, capital expenditure planning, and store portfolio benchmarking.']
  headcount_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum approved headcount (number of employees) for the store as defined in the workforce plan. Used for labour scheduling, Workday HCM position management, and compliance with local employment regulations. Drives OTB labour cost budgeting.']
  is_bopis_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store is configured and operationally enabled to fulfil Buy Online Pick Up In Store (BOPIS) orders. Drives omnichannel order routing logic in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and SAP Customer Activity Repository. True = BOPIS active; False = BOPIS not available at this location.']
  is_rfid_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store is equipped with Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) infrastructure for inventory tracking, cycle counting, and loss prevention. RFID-enabled stores support real-time inventory accuracy and ATS (Available to Sell) visibility. True = RFID infrastructure active.']
  is_ship_from_store_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the store is configured to fulfil e-commerce orders by shipping directly to customers from store inventory. Enables distributed fulfilment network capabilities. Drives order routing in Salesforce Commerce Cloud and Manhattan Associates WMS. True = ship-from-store active.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the retail store location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used for store-finder applications, trade area mapping, proximity-based marketing, and omnichannel routing (BOPIS, ship-from-store).']
  lease_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the current lease agreement for the store premises. Used for lease renewal pipeline management, store closure planning, and IFRS 16 lease liability amortization schedule. Nullable for month-to-month or perpetual arrangements.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Commencement date of the current lease agreement for the store premises as recorded in SAP S/4HANA Real Estate module. Used for lease term tracking, IFRS 16 right-of-use asset calculation, and lease renewal planning.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the retail store location in decimal degrees (WGS84). Used alongside latitude for geospatial analytics, trade area delineation, and omnichannel fulfillment routing.']
  net_selling_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Net productive selling floor area in square feet, excluding stockroom, fitting rooms, cash wrap, and back-of-house. Primary denominator for sales-per-square-foot (SPF) KPI, visual merchandising density planning, and fixture capacity modelling.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date on which the store first opened for trading to customers. Used for store age calculations, comparable store (comp-store) sales analysis, and new store ramp-up performance benchmarking.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the store location within Apparel Fashion\'s retail network. Pre-open indicates the store is in fit-out or pre-launch phase; trading indicates active selling operations; temporarily_closed covers seasonal or refurbishment closures; permanently_closed indicates the store has ceased operations. Drives inclusion/exclusion in POS reporting, inventory allocation, and workforce scheduling.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary customer-facing telephone number for the retail store location. Used for customer service, click-and-collect (BOPIS) coordination, and store directory listings. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  pos_system_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the POS terminal configuration deployed in the store. Fixed terminals are traditional counter-based registers; mobile POS (mPOS) enables floor-based selling; self-checkout enables customer-operated transactions; mixed indicates a combination. Drives IT infrastructure planning and customer experience design.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the retail store location. Used for trade area analysis, catchment modelling, demographic profiling, and logistics routing.']
  region_name varchar [note: 'Name of the geographic region within Apparel Fashion\'s retail organizational hierarchy (e.g., North America, EMEA, APAC). Top tier of the region/district/territory hierarchy used for regional performance reporting and resource allocation.']
  register_count varchar [note: 'Total number of active Point of Sale (POS) terminals (registers) installed in the store. Used for transaction throughput capacity planning, queue management, and SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) terminal configuration management.']
  remodel_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent store remodel or significant capital refurbishment. Used to assess store condition age, plan future capital expenditure cycles, and correlate store investment with post-remodel sales uplift.']
  rent_structure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the rent payment structure under the store lease. Fixed rent is a flat periodic payment; turnover rent is a percentage of net sales; fixed_plus_turnover combines a base rent with a sales-linked top-up; concession_percentage applies to shop-in-shop and concession formats. Drives financial planning and OTB (Open-to-Buy) modelling.']
  standard_trading_hours varchar [note: 'Standard weekly operating hours for the store expressed as a structured string (e.g., \'Mon-Sat 09:00-21:00; Sun 11:00-18:00\'). Used for customer-facing store finder, workforce scheduling in Workday HCM, and daily sales reconciliation cut-off timing in SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region in which the retail store is located. Used for tax jurisdiction mapping, regional hierarchy assignment, and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  stockroom_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total square footage of the store\'s back-of-house stockroom area. Used for inventory capacity planning, WMS (Warehouse Management System) configuration, and the calculation of net-to-gross selling area ratio. Informs replenishment frequency and NOS (Never Out of Stock) capability.']
  store_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the store, used across SAP S/4HANA, SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR), Oracle Retail, and EDI integrations. Conforms to NRF ARTS store numbering convention. Unique across all store formats and geographies.']
  store_format varchar [note: 'Operational format classification of the store that determines merchandising strategy, assortment depth, pricing authority, and capital investment profile. Flagship stores carry full-price full-assortment; outlet stores carry clearance/off-price; pop-up stores are temporary; shop-in-shop and concession operate within a host retailer\'s space.']
  store_name varchar [note: 'Official trading name of the retail store location as displayed on signage, customer receipts, and marketing materials. Used for customer-facing communications and brand management reporting.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Tax authority jurisdiction code assigned to the store location for sales tax, VAT, or GST calculation and remittance. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA FI/CO tax configuration. Used for POS transaction tax determination and regulatory tax reporting.']
  territory_name varchar [note: 'Name of the territory or cluster within the district hierarchy. Lowest tier of the organizational hierarchy above individual store level, used for territory manager assignments and localized assortment planning.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the store\'s local operating time zone (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Critical for accurate POS transaction timestamping, daily sales reconciliation, and omnichannel order cut-off time management in SAP Customer Activity Repository.']
  trading_area_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the retail trading environment in which the store operates. Determines foot traffic patterns, trading hours, lease structure, and competitive benchmarking peer group. Used in store performance analytics and site selection models. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_street|shopping_mall|outlet_center|airport|resort|standalone|department_store — promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Master record for each physical retail store location operated by Apparel Fashion. Captures store identity, format (flagship, outlet, pop-up, shop-in-shop, concession), trading area classification, gross and net selling square footage, register inventory (terminal count, types, configurations), lease terms and rent structure, regional/district/territory hierarchy, climate zone, and operational status (pre-open, trading, temporarily closed, permanently closed). SSOT for store-level dimensional attributes referenced by POS, inventory, workforce, and omnichannel systems. Conforms to NRF ARTS RetailStore entity. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA Real Estate and SAP Customer Activity Repository.'
}

Table store.pos_transaction {
  pos_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pos_transaction']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: B2B wholesale transactions processed at retail locations generate AR invoices for trade customers. Common in apparel brands with hybrid retail/wholesale models where stores serve business accounts req']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: POS transactions track campaign attribution via promo codes and campaign-specific offers. Essential for measuring in-store campaign ROAS, conversion rates, and incremental sales lift—core retail marke']
  cashier_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales associate or cashier who processed the transaction. Used for associate-level performance tracking, commission calculation, and loss prevention audits.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to ecommerce.digital_storefront. Business justification: Omnichannel POS transactions (returns of online purchases, loyalty redemption across channels) require attribution to the originating digital storefront for unified commerce reporting and cross-channe']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program under which points were earned or redeemed in this transaction. Supports multi-program loyalty analytics and Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) measurement.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the identified customer (loyalty member or registered account) associated with this transaction. Nullable for anonymous/walk-in transactions. Enables Customer Lifetime Value (CLTV) and loyalty analytics.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary promotional offer applied to this transaction. Supports promotion effectiveness analysis, markdown (MD) attribution, and Gross Margin Return on Investment (GMROI) reporting.']
  register_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific POS register (terminal) at which the transaction was processed within the store. Supports register-level reconciliation and shrinkage analysis.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store where this POS transaction was processed. Links to the store master in the Store Operations domain.']
  return_original_transaction_pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'For RETURN or EXCHANGE transaction types, references the original sale transaction being returned against. Enables return merchandise authorization (RMA) traceability and shrinkage analysis. Null for non-return transactions.']
  rma_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.rma. Business justification: In-store returns with defects or warranty claims generate RMA records for vendor credit processing (RTV authorization). Business process: store return disposition triggers formal RMA when item require']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the associated omnichannel sales order in SAP S/4HANA SD or Salesforce Commerce Cloud for BOPIS, ship-from-store, or endless aisle transactions. Null for standard in-store transactions. Enables cross-channel order traceability.']
  business_date date [note: 'The fiscal/retail business date to which this transaction is attributed for daily sales reconciliation and End-of-Month (EOM) reporting. May differ from the wall-clock date for transactions processed after midnight during extended trading hours.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Identifies the omnichannel fulfillment mode under which the transaction was processed. IN_STORE: standard in-store purchase; BOPIS: Buy Online Pick Up In Store; SHIP_FROM_STORE: store fulfills an online order; ENDLESS_AISLE: in-store kiosk ordering; CURBSIDE: curbside pickup. Supports Direct-to-Consumer (DTC) analytics.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Alphanumeric coupon or voucher code scanned or entered at the POS to apply a discount. Supports coupon redemption tracking, campaign attribution, and fraud detection in loss prevention.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction record was first ingested into the Silver layer of the Databricks Lakehouse from SAP Customer Activity Repository. Audit trail field for data lineage and ETL reconciliation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code in which the transaction was denominated (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency retail operations and international store reporting.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total monetary value of all discounts and markdowns (MD) applied to the transaction, including promotional discounts, employee discounts, coupon redemptions, and loyalty-funded discounts. Used in Maintained Markup (MMU) and Sell-Through Rate (STR) analysis.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total pre-discount, pre-tax merchandise value of all items in the transaction at their selling price. Represents the sum of line-item extended prices before any adjustments. Used in Average Unit Retail (AUR) and Average Selling Price (ASP) calculations.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual unit items (units) included in the transaction across all line items. Used for basket size analytics, Average Order Value (AOV) benchmarking, and store traffic conversion metrics.']
  line_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct line items (SKU entries) in the transaction. Differs from item_count when multiple units of the same SKU are purchased. Used for transaction complexity analysis.']
  loyalty_points_earned varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points earned by the customer on this transaction based on the applicable loyalty program earn rate. Used for loyalty liability accrual and Customer Relationship Management (CRM) analytics.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty reward points redeemed by the customer as a form of payment or discount on this transaction. Used for loyalty liability reduction and redemption rate analytics.']
  manager_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manager authorization override was required to complete this transaction (e.g., for price overrides, high-value returns, or discount exceptions). Critical for loss prevention and exception-based reporting.']
  net_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Final amount tendered by the customer: gross_amount minus discount_amount plus tax_amount. Represents the total cash obligation of the transaction. Primary revenue figure for daily sales reconciliation and KPI reporting.']
  price_override_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a manual price override was applied to one or more items in this transaction, bypassing the system-calculated selling price. Used in loss prevention audits and markdown (MD) exception reporting.']
  receipt_delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the transaction receipt was delivered to the customer: PRINT (paper receipt), EMAIL (digital email receipt), SMS (text message receipt), NONE (no receipt requested). Supports sustainability reporting and digital engagement analytics.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Customer-facing receipt identifier printed on the physical or digital receipt. May differ from transaction_number in some POS configurations. Used for customer service, return authorization, and receipt lookup.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for a merchandise return (e.g., DEFECTIVE, WRONG_SIZE, CHANGED_MIND, GIFT_RETURN). Populated only for RETURN and EXCHANGE transaction types. Used in product quality analysis, CPSC compliance, and Return to Vendor (RTV) decisions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEFECTIVE|WRONG_SIZE|CHANGED_MIND|GIFT_RETURN|WRONG_ITEM|NOT_AS_DESCRIBED — promote to reference product]']
  rfid_verified_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the items in this transaction were verified via Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) scanning at the point of sale. Supports inventory accuracy, shrinkage reduction, and omnichannel Available to Sell (ATS) reconciliation.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total sales tax collected across all applicable tax jurisdictions for this transaction. Aggregated from jurisdiction-level tax lines. Supports tax remittance reporting and compliance with FTC and state tax authorities.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction (state, county, municipality) applicable to this transaction for sales tax calculation and remittance. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance reporting.']
  tender_cash_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the transaction settled by cash tender. Used in daily cash drawer reconciliation and loss prevention audits. Part of the tender split breakdown.']
  tender_credit_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the transaction settled by credit card tender. Supports PCI DSS compliance reporting and payment reconciliation. Part of the tender split breakdown.']
  tender_debit_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the transaction settled by debit card tender. Supports payment reconciliation and PCI DSS compliance. Part of the tender split breakdown.']
  tender_gift_card_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the transaction settled by gift card redemption. Used for gift card liability tracking, breakage analysis, and revenue recognition per IFRS/GAAP deferred revenue rules.']
  tender_mobile_wallet_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the transaction settled via mobile wallet payment (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay, Samsung Pay). Tracks digital payment adoption trends and supports omnichannel payment analytics.']
  tender_store_credit_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Amount of the transaction settled using store credit (issued from prior returns or promotions). Supports Return to Vendor (RTV) and return merchandise authorization liability tracking.']
  training_mode_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this transaction was processed in POS training/demo mode and should be excluded from financial reporting and sales analytics. Critical for data quality and accurate daily sales reconciliation.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the transaction as printed on the customer receipt and recorded in SAP Customer Activity Repository. Unique within a store and business date combination. Conforms to NRF ARTS POSLog SequenceNumber.']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the transaction in the POS workflow. COMPLETED: fully tendered and closed; VOIDED: reversed before completion; SUSPENDED: parked mid-transaction for later recall; CANCELLED: abandoned; PENDING: awaiting tender confirmation (e.g., BOPIS pickup).']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the transaction was initiated at the register, as recorded by the POS system. Principal business event timestamp. Stored in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for intraday traffic analytics and hourly sales reporting.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transaction event per NRF ARTS POSLog standard. SALE: standard purchase; RETURN: merchandise returned for refund; EXCHANGE: simultaneous return and sale; VOID: cancelled transaction before completion; NO_SALE: register opened without a sale; LAYAWAY: deferred payment purchase.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this transaction record was last modified in the Silver layer (e.g., due to post-void processing, tax adjustment, or data correction). Supports audit trail and change data capture (CDC) tracking.']
  void_reason_code varchar [note: 'Coded reason for voiding a transaction (e.g., OPERATOR_ERROR, CUSTOMER_CHANGED_MIND, SYSTEM_ERROR, MANAGER_OVERRIDE). Populated only for VOID transaction types. Used in loss prevention and exception reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: OPERATOR_ERROR|CUSTOMER_CHANGED_MIND|SYSTEM_ERROR|MANAGER_OVERRIDE|PRICE_OVERRIDE|DUPLICATE_TRANSACTION — promote to reference product]']
  workstation_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable identifier of the POS workstation/terminal as configured in SAP Customer Activity Repository (e.g., REG-01, KIOSK-03). Used for register-level reconciliation and store layout analytics.']

  Note: 'Captures every point-of-sale transaction processed at a retail store register, including sale, return, exchange, void, and no-sale events. Records transaction date/time, register ID, cashier/associate, tender split (cash, credit, debit, mobile wallet, gift card, store credit), total amount, tax by jurisdiction, discount applied, loyalty points earned/redeemed, and channel flag (in-store, BOPIS pickup, endless aisle). Conforms to NRF ARTS POSLog standard for transaction classification. Primary transactional heartbeat of the store domain. Sourced from SAP Customer Activity Repository.'
}

Table store.pos_transaction_line {
  pos_transaction_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pos_transaction_line']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Line-level campaign attribution enables SKU-level campaign performance analysis. Merchandising teams use this to understand which products respond to campaigns, informing future assortment and promoti']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_defect. Business justification: Retail stores capture quality defect codes on damaged/defective items at POS for warranty claims, vendor chargebacks, and quality feedback loops. Essential for root cause analysis and supplier perform']
  ftc_label_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.ftc_label. Business justification: POS systems validate FTC fiber content and care labeling compliance at sale to ensure consumer protection, prevent mislabeling penalties, and support regulatory audits requiring proof of compliant lab']
  pos_transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent POS transaction header. Establishes the header-detail relationship between the transaction and its individual line items. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role HEADER_REFERENCE category.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member associated with this transaction line, if the customer identified themselves. Enables customer-level purchase history, CLTV analysis, and personalized marketing attribution.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the promotion or markdown event applied to this line item, if any. Links to the promotion master for promotional effectiveness analysis and markdown event attribution.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store where this POS transaction line was recorded. Enables store-level STR, ASP, AUR, and traffic analytics at the store-SKU-day grain.']
  sales_associate_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales associate who processed or assisted with this transaction line. Used for associate-level sales performance tracking, commission calculation, and clienteling analytics.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) sold on this line. Represents the most granular sellable unit combining style, colorway, and size. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role RESOURCE_REFERENCE category.']
  class_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise class code within the department hierarchy for the SKU sold (e.g., TOPS, BOTTOMS, OUTERWEAR, SNEAKERS). Supports class-level STR, markdown analysis, and assortment planning within Oracle Retail.']
  cogs decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for this line item representing the total cost of the units sold. Used to calculate gross margin, GMROI, and MMU at the SKU-store-day grain. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA material valuation.']
  colorway_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific colorway (color variant) of the style sold on this line. Used for colorway-level sell-through analysis and markdown decision-making within collection planning.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this line item (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Required for multi-currency retail operations and financial consolidation reporting.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise department classification code for the SKU sold (e.g., MENS, WOMENS, KIDS, FOOTWEAR, ACCESSORIES). Enables department-level sales reporting, OTB management, and assortment performance analytics.']
  discount_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the business reason for any discount or markdown applied to this line item (e.g., EMPLOYEE, LOYALTY, COUPON, CLEARANCE, PROMO, DAMAGE). Used for discount analytics, shrinkage investigation, and promotional effectiveness reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EMPLOYEE|LOYALTY|COUPON|CLEARANCE|PROMO|DAMAGE|PRICE_MATCH|MANAGER_OVERRIDE — promote to reference product]']
  fulfillment_type varchar [note: 'Indicates the omnichannel fulfillment method for this line item. Distinguishes standard in-store purchases from BOPIS (Buy Online Pick Up In Store), ship-from-store, curbside pickup, and endless aisle orders. Critical for DTC omnichannel analytics.']
  imu_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Initial Markup (IMU) percentage for this SKU at the time of the transaction, representing the difference between the original retail price and COGS as a percentage of retail. Key metric for margin planning and merchandise financial performance.']
  ingestion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was ingested into the Databricks Silver layer from the source system. Used for data freshness monitoring, SLA compliance tracking, and incremental load auditing.']
  is_clearance_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU on this line was designated as a clearance item at the time of sale. Clearance items are end-of-lifecycle SKUs being liquidated. Distinct from is_markdown_item which covers all markdowns including promotional.']
  is_markdown_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU on this line was sold at a marked-down price at the time of the transaction. True if the item was on permanent or promotional markdown. Used to segment full-price vs. markdown sales for AUR and margin analytics.']
  is_rfid_scanned boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item was identified via RFID (Radio Frequency Identification) tag scan rather than traditional barcode/UPC scan at the POS. Supports RFID adoption tracking, inventory accuracy measurement, and shrinkage reduction analytics.']
  line_discount_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Additional point-of-sale discount amount applied to this specific line item beyond the ticketed markdown, such as employee discounts, loyalty rewards, or coupon redemptions. Distinct from markdown_amount which reflects price management markdowns.']
  line_extended_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total net sales amount for this line item (unit_retail_price × quantity_sold minus line_discount_amount). Represents the actual revenue recognized for this line. Used in daily sales reconciliation and financial reporting.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number identifying the position of this item within the parent POS transaction. Used for ordering and referencing individual items within a receipt. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role LINE_SEQUENCE category.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the transaction line indicating whether it represents a sale, return, exchange, void, or layaway. Drives revenue recognition logic, inventory adjustment direction, and return analytics.']
  markdown_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Total markdown (MD) dollar amount applied to this line item, calculated as the difference between the original retail price and the unit retail price multiplied by quantity. Tracks permanent and promotional markdowns for MMU and margin analytics.']
  mmu_pct decimal(8,4) [note: 'Maintained Markup (MMU) percentage for this line item, reflecting the actual margin achieved after all markdowns and discounts. Calculated as (unit_retail_price - unit_cost) / unit_retail_price. Core KPI for merchandise financial planning.']
  msrp decimal(18,4) [note: 'The manufacturer\'s suggested retail price (MSRP) for the SKU at the time of the transaction. Used as the baseline for markdown calculation, IMU computation, and price integrity reporting.']
  original_retail_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'The original ticketed retail price of the SKU before any markdowns or promotional discounts were applied in this transaction. Used to calculate markdown amount and sell-through performance relative to initial pricing.']
  quantity_sold varchar [note: 'Number of units sold on this transaction line. Positive for sales, negative for returns/voids. Core metric for sell-through rate (STR), weeks of supply (WOS), and inventory depletion analytics. Aligns with TRANSACTION_LINE role LINE_QUANTITY category.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this record in the Silver layer, reflecting corrections, late-arriving data, or restatements from the source system. Supports data quality monitoring and change data capture.']
  register_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS register or terminal where this transaction line was processed. Used for register-level reconciliation, shrinkage investigation, and store operations auditing.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for a product return when line_type is RETURN or EXCHANGE (e.g., DEFECTIVE, WRONG_SIZE, CHANGED_MIND, DAMAGED). Null for non-return lines. Used for quality analysis, vendor chargebacks, and after-sales service reporting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DEFECTIVE|WRONG_SIZE|CHANGED_MIND|DAMAGED|WRONG_ITEM|NOT_AS_DESCRIBED|OTHER — promote to reference product]']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier for the collection to which the sold SKU belongs (e.g., SS25, FW24, HO24). Used for season-level sell-through rate (STR) tracking, end-of-season markdown analysis, and collection performance reporting.']
  size_code varchar [note: 'Size designation of the SKU sold (e.g., XS, S, M, L, XL, 32x30, 8.5). Enables size-curve analytics, size-level STR reporting, and replenishment decisions at the store-SKU-size grain.']
  source_system_line_reference varchar [note: 'The native transaction line identifier from the source system (SAP Customer Activity Repository). Preserves the original system reference for data lineage, reconciliation, and audit trail purposes in the Silver layer.']
  style_number varchar [note: 'The style-level identifier for the product sold, representing the design without colorway or size differentiation. Enables style-level sell-through rate (STR) and performance analytics across colorways and sizes.']
  subclass_code varchar [note: 'Merchandise subclass code providing the most granular merchandise hierarchy classification below class (e.g., T-SHIRTS, DENIM, RUNNING_SHOES). Enables fine-grained assortment analytics and category management reporting.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'Sales tax amount applied to this line item based on the applicable tax jurisdiction and product tax classification. Required for financial reconciliation, tax reporting, and COGS analysis.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the POS transaction occurred. Primary grain dimension for daily sales reconciliation, STR reporting, and store performance KPI dashboards.']
  transaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the POS transaction line was processed at the register. Used for intraday traffic analytics, peak hour analysis, and time-of-day sales pattern reporting.']
