  fraud_screening_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the fraud detection screening process indicating whether the transaction passed, failed, or requires manual review.']
  gateway_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the payment gateway for tracking and reconciliation with external systems.']
  installment_count varchar [note: 'Total number of installments for Buy Now Pay Later or installment payment plans, if applicable.']
  installment_plan_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is part of an installment or Buy Now Pay Later plan.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this payment record was last modified or updated.']
  payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount processed in this payment transaction, representing the gross payment value before any adjustments.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'The digital interface or channel through which the payment was initiated, such as web storefront, mobile app, or call center.']
  payment_gateway varchar [note: 'The payment gateway or processor that handled the transaction, such as Stripe, Adyen, PayPal, or Authorize.Net.']
  payment_method_type varchar [note: 'The type of payment instrument used for the transaction, such as credit card, PayPal, Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL), gift card, store credit, or digital wallet. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: credit_card|debit_card|paypal|apple_pay|google_pay|bnpl|gift_card|store_credit|bank_transfer — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction indicating its processing state and outcome. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|authorized|captured|settled|failed|declined|cancelled|refunded|partially_refunded|voided — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_token varchar [note: 'Tokenized representation of the payment method provided by the payment gateway for secure storage and future transactions without exposing sensitive card data.']
  processing_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged by the payment gateway or processor for handling this transaction, used for cost analysis and reconciliation.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount refunded to the customer for this payment transaction, applicable when payment status is refunded or partially refunded.']
  refund_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was processed and initiated back to the customer\'s payment method.']
  settlement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payment was settled and funds were transferred to the merchant account.']
  three_ds_authentication_status varchar [note: 'Status of 3D Secure authentication indicating whether the cardholder was successfully authenticated through the issuer\'s authentication process.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Externally visible unique transaction identifier provided by the payment gateway or internal system for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']

  Note: 'Records payment transactions associated with digital orders, including payment method type (credit card, PayPal, BNPL, gift card, store credit, Apple Pay), payment gateway, authorization code, transaction ID, payment amount, currency, payment status (authorized, captured, failed, refunded, partially refunded), capture timestamp, and fraud screening result flag. Supports DTC revenue reconciliation and payment failure root-cause analysis.'
}

Table ecommerce.digital_return {
  digital_return_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the digital return request. Primary key for the digital return entity.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Returns generate credit memos or adjustments to original AR invoices for revenue reversal and receivables adjustment. Required for accurate revenue recognition and financial statement accuracy.']
  defect_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_defect. Business justification: Returns must link to standardized defect codes for root cause analysis, supplier scorecarding, and defect trend reporting. Maps customer return reasons to quality defect taxonomy, enabling cost-of-qua']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer service agent who processed or assisted with the return request, if applicable.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Product returns citing safety defects, labeling violations, or quality failures may trigger compliance incidents requiring investigation and regulatory notification. Returns management systems link re']
  inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.inspection. Business justification: Returns trigger re-inspection to validate customer defect claims, determine restocking vs. disposal disposition, and identify quality escapes. Inspection results drive refund approval, supplier debit ']
  non_conformance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.non_conformance. Business justification: Customer returns often generate formal NCRs when return reason codes indicate quality failures (defective, not as described). Links return claims to supplier corrective action plans, quality scorecard']
  primary_digital_sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sales order from which this return originated.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer initiating the return request.']
  sales_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order line item being returned.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Returns processing requires vendor identification for RTV (return-to-vendor) eligibility decisions, vendor chargeback calculations, quality defect escalation, and vendor scorecard impact. Critical for']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or return center where the returned item was received.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Quality feedback loop requires linking returns to originating production work orders to identify factory-specific defects, trigger corrective actions, and analyze quality trends by production batch. C']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the return request was approved by the system or customer service team.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return record was first created in the system.']
  disposition_code varchar [note: 'Final disposition decision for the returned merchandise (resell, liquidate, donate, destroy, return to vendor, repair).']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the returned item was inspected for quality and condition assessment.']
  inspection_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the quality inspection determining whether the return meets acceptance criteria.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this return record was last updated.']
  product_condition varchar [note: 'Condition of the returned product as declared by the customer or assessed upon receipt.']
  received_date date [note: 'Date when the returned merchandise was physically received at the warehouse or return center.']
  refund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount to be refunded to the customer for this return, including product value and applicable taxes.']
  refund_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the refund amount.']
  refund_date date [note: 'Date when the refund was processed and issued to the customer.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund will be issued to the customer.']
  restocking_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fee charged to the customer for processing the return, if applicable per return policy.']
  restocking_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a restocking fee was applied to this return.']
  return_channel varchar [note: 'Digital channel through which the return request was initiated (web storefront, mobile app, customer service portal).']
  return_eligibility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item met the eligibility criteria for return based on policy rules.']
  return_label_generated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a prepaid return shipping label was generated for the customer.']
  return_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the customer will return the merchandise (mail-in, drop-off location, in-store, courier pickup).']
  return_policy_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the return policy that was in effect at the time of the return request.']
  return_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units being returned for this line item.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the primary reason for the return as selected by the customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: defective|wrong_item|wrong_size|not_as_described|changed_mind|damaged_in_transit|quality_issue|fit_issue|color_mismatch|late_delivery|duplicate_order|other — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation provided by the customer detailing the reason for the return.']
  return_request_date date [note: 'Date when the customer initiated the return request through the digital channel.']
  return_request_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the return request was submitted via the e-commerce platform.']
  return_shipping_carrier varchar [note: 'Shipping carrier used for returning the merchandise (e.g., UPS, FedEx, USPS, DHL).']
  return_shipping_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Cost of return shipping, either paid by customer or absorbed by the business depending on return policy.']
  return_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the return request in the DTC after-sales workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: requested|approved|rejected|shipped|received|inspected|refunded|completed|cancelled — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  return_tracking_number varchar [note: 'Tracking number provided by the carrier for monitoring the return shipment.']
  return_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allowed for return from the original purchase date, as defined by the applicable return policy.']
  rma_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible return authorization number provided to the customer for tracking and reference.']
  rtv_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the returned item is eligible to be returned to the vendor for credit or replacement.']
  rtv_processed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item has been processed as a return to vendor.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Product SKU identifier for the item being returned.']
  upc varchar [note: 'UPC barcode of the returned product for inventory reconciliation.']

  Note: 'Captures return and refund requests initiated through the DTC e-commerce channel, including return request date, return reason code, return method (mail-in, drop-off, in-store), return status (requested, approved, received, refunded, rejected), refund amount, refund method, restocking flag, and RTV eligibility. Distinct from in-store returns — this entity owns the digital after-sales service workflow for DTC.'
}

Table ecommerce.site_promotion {
  site_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the site promotion record. Primary key for the site_promotion data product.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget_line. Business justification: Promotional discounts and campaigns must be tracked against marketing budgets for spend control, ROI analysis, and budget variance reporting. Essential for promotional effectiveness and financial plan']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotions are campaign tactics (seasonal sale, launch discount). Links promotional discount strategy to campaign budget and enables promotion effectiveness measurement within campaign context.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital storefront where this promotion is configured and active.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Promotions require employee ownership for approval workflows, budget accountability, and performance measurement. Merchandising managers need to track which employees created/manage promotions for ROI']
  qualifying_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Promotions may offer free or discounted shipping with specific carriers (e.g., "Free 2-day FedEx shipping"). Required for promotion eligibility validation, checkout display logic, and carrier cost all']
  applicable_category_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product category codes eligible for this promotion. Null if promotion applies to specific SKUs or all products.']
  applicable_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU codes eligible for this promotion. Null if promotion applies to all products or is category-based.']
  auto_apply_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion is automatically applied to eligible carts without requiring a promo code entry. True if auto-applied, False if code entry required.']
  channel_restriction varchar [note: 'Digital channel where this promotion is valid. Restricts redemption to specific customer touchpoints.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion record was first created in the system.']
  customer_segment_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the customer segment eligible for this promotion (e.g., VIP, loyalty tier, new customer). Null if promotion is available to all customers.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary discount applied when promotion type is fixed_amount. Currency is inherited from the storefront configuration.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when promotion type is percentage_off. Null for non-percentage promotions.']
  display_message varchar [note: 'Customer-facing promotional message displayed on the storefront, cart, or checkout pages to communicate the offer.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion expires and is no longer available for redemption. Null for open-ended promotions.']
  exclusion_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU codes explicitly excluded from this promotion even if they match category or general eligibility rules.']
  maximum_discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cap on the total discount value that can be applied in a single transaction, typically used with percentage-based promotions. Null if no cap applies.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum cart subtotal required for the promotion to be eligible for redemption. Null if no minimum threshold applies.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this promotion record was last updated.']
  order_count varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct orders that included this promotion.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking used to determine promotion application order when multiple promotions are eligible. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code that customers enter at checkout to redeem the promotion. Also known as promo code or coupon code.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the promotion for internal reference and customer display.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the promotion indicating whether it is available for customer redemption.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of discount mechanism applied by this promotion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: percentage_off|fixed_amount|bogo|free_shipping|bundle|loyalty_point_redemption|tiered_member_pricing|gift_with_purchase|volume_discount — promote to reference product]']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times this promotion has been successfully redeemed by customers to date.']
  revenue_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of discounts granted through this promotion to date, representing the revenue reduction attributable to the promotion.']
  stackable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promotion can be combined with other promotions in a single transaction. True if stackable, False if exclusive.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes active and available for customer redemption.']
  terms_and_conditions varchar [note: 'Legal and business terms governing the use of this promotion, including restrictions, exclusions, and customer obligations.']
  usage_limit_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single customer can redeem this promotion. Null for unlimited per-customer usage.']
  usage_limit_total varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times this promotion can be redeemed across all customers. Null for unlimited usage.']

  Note: 'Defines promotional campaigns, discount rules, and redemption tracking configured on the digital storefront, including promotion name, promotion type (percentage off, fixed amount, BOGO, free shipping, bundle, loyalty point redemption, tiered member pricing), applicable SKUs or categories, promo code, start/end dates, usage limit, minimum order value threshold, stackability flag, redemption count, revenue impact, and promotion status. Owned by the e-commerce domain for digital channel promotions; complements but does not duplicate marketing campaign records.'
}

Table ecommerce.ab_test {
  ab_test_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the A/B or multivariate test. Primary key for the ab_test data product.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: E-commerce teams A/B test concept-driven merchandising strategies (concept-based landing pages, thematic product groupings). Test results inform which design concepts resonate with digital customers, ']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital storefront where this test is executed. Links to the digital_storefront product.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: A/B tests are owned by product managers, merchandisers, or UX designers for experimentation programs. Links enable performance tracking, workload management, and accountability for test design/analysi']
  additional_variants_count varchar [note: 'Number of additional treatment variants beyond the primary treatment. Zero for simple A/B tests, one or more for A/B/n or multivariate tests. Detailed variant data stored in related variant detail table.']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level achieved by the test results, typically 95.00 or 99.00. Represents the probability that the observed difference is not due to random chance. Calculated from p-value: confidence = (1 - p_value) * 100.']
  control_aov decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average Order Value (AOV) for orders from the control variant, calculated as total revenue divided by number of orders. Measured in the storefronts default currency.']
  control_conversion_rate_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Conversion rate for the control variant, calculated as (control_conversions / control_impressions) * 100. Baseline performance metric against which treatment variants are compared.']
  control_conversions varchar [note: 'Total number of conversions (successful outcomes per primary success metric) from the control variant. For conversion rate tests, this is completed purchases; for CTR tests, this is clicks.']
  control_impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of unique visitors or sessions exposed to the control variant during the test period. Used as denominator in conversion rate calculations.']
  control_revenue_per_visitor decimal(12,2) [note: 'Revenue per visitor for the control variant, calculated as total revenue divided by impressions. Combines conversion rate and order value into a single metric. Measured in the storefronts default currency.']
  control_traffic_split_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of test traffic allocated to the control variant. In a 50/50 A/B test this would be 50.00. In multivariate tests may be lower to accommodate multiple treatment variants.']
  control_variant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the control variant (baseline experience). Typically \'Control\' or \'Original\'. This is the existing experience against which treatment variants are compared.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test record was first created in the system. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and record lifecycle tracking.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the test concluded or is scheduled to conclude. Null for tests still running. Used to calculate test duration and determine when results are final.']
  hypothesis varchar [note: 'The business hypothesis being tested, describing the expected outcome and rationale. Example: \'Changing the CTA button color from blue to orange will increase click-through rate by 15% because it creates stronger visual contrast.\'']
  lift_percent decimal(8,2) [note: 'Percentage improvement (or decline if negative) of the treatment variant over the control for the primary success metric. Calculated as ((treatment_metric - control_metric) / control_metric) * 100. Example: 15.50 means the treatment performed 15.5% better than control.']
  minimum_detectable_effect_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The smallest percentage improvement in the primary metric that the test is designed to detect with statistical confidence. Used in sample size calculation. Example: 5.00 means the test can reliably detect a 5% or greater improvement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this test record was last modified. Follows ISO 8601 format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Updated whenever test configuration or results are changed. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, learnings, or special considerations about the test. May include information about external factors (seasonality, promotions, site issues) that affected results.']
  p_value decimal(10,8) [note: 'The probability that the observed difference between variants occurred by random chance. Lower values indicate stronger evidence of a real effect. Values below the significance threshold (typically 0.05) indicate statistical significance.']
  planned_duration_days varchar [note: 'Planned duration of the test in days, determined during test design based on required sample size and traffic volume to achieve statistical significance.']
  primary_success_metric varchar [note: 'The primary Key Performance Indicator (KPI) used to determine test success. Conversion rate (CVR): percentage of visitors who complete purchase, AOV: Average Order Value, revenue per visitor (RPV): total revenue divided by visitors, click-through rate (CTR): clicks divided by impressions, add-to-cart rate: products added to cart divided by product views, bounce rate: single-page sessions, time on page: average session duration on tested page. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: conversion_rate|aov|revenue_per_visitor|click_through_rate|add_to_cart_rate|bounce_rate|time_on_page — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rollout_date date [note: 'Date when the winning variant was or will be rolled out to production for all users. Null if no rollout planned or decision pending.']
  rollout_decision varchar [note: 'Business decision on how to implement test results. Full rollout: deploy winning variant to 100% of traffic, Partial rollout: deploy to subset of traffic or segments, No rollout: keep original experience, Pending: decision not yet made, Rollback: reverse a previous rollout.']
  secondary_success_metrics varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of additional metrics monitored to understand broader test impact. Examples: engagement rate, return rate, customer satisfaction score. Used to detect unintended consequences.']
  significance_threshold decimal(5,4) [note: 'The p-value threshold required to declare a statistically significant result, typically 0.0500 (95% confidence) or 0.0100 (99% confidence). Lower values require stronger evidence but reduce false positive risk.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the test began running and traffic allocation started. Used to calculate test duration and align with business calendar events.']
  test_category varchar [note: 'Category of the test element being optimized: layout (page structure), copy (messaging/text), pricing (price display/strategy), cta (call-to-action buttons), recommendation (product suggestions), navigation (site navigation), checkout (purchase flow), personalization (targeted content). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: layout|copy|pricing|cta|recommendation|navigation|checkout|personalization — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  test_code varchar [note: 'Business-friendly unique code for the test, used for tracking and reporting purposes. Typically follows a naming convention like EXP_HOMEPAGE_CTA_Q1.']
  test_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the A/B test describing the experiment focus, such as \'Homepage Hero Banner CTA Optimization\' or \'Product Detail Page Layout Test\'.']
  test_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the test. Draft: being designed, Scheduled: approved and awaiting start, Running: actively collecting data, Paused: temporarily stopped, Completed: finished with results, Cancelled: terminated early, Archived: historical record. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|scheduled|running|paused|completed|cancelled|archived — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  test_type varchar [note: 'Type of experiment being conducted. A/B tests compare two variants, multivariate tests multiple variables simultaneously, split_url tests different page URLs, redirect tests send traffic to entirely different pages.']
  traffic_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total site traffic allocated to this test. Remaining traffic sees the default experience. Typical values range from 10% to 100% depending on risk tolerance and traffic volume.']
  treatment_aov decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average Order Value (AOV) for orders from the primary treatment variant, calculated as total revenue divided by number of orders. Measured in the storefronts default currency.']
  treatment_conversion_rate_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Conversion rate for the primary treatment variant, calculated as (treatment_conversions / treatment_impressions) * 100. Compared against control to determine test outcome.']
  treatment_conversions varchar [note: 'Total number of conversions (successful outcomes per primary success metric) from the primary treatment variant. For conversion rate tests, this is completed purchases; for CTR tests, this is clicks.']
  treatment_impressions varchar [note: 'Total number of unique visitors or sessions exposed to the primary treatment variant during the test period. Used as denominator in conversion rate calculations.']
  treatment_revenue_per_visitor decimal(12,2) [note: 'Revenue per visitor for the primary treatment variant, calculated as total revenue divided by impressions. Combines conversion rate and order value into a single metric. Measured in the storefronts default currency.']
  treatment_traffic_split_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of test traffic allocated to the primary treatment variant. In a 50/50 A/B test this would be 50.00. Sum of all variant splits must equal 100.']
  treatment_variant_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary treatment variant (new experience being tested). Examples: \'Variant A\', \'Orange CTA\', \'New Layout\'. For multivariate tests, this may represent the first treatment; additional variants tracked separately.']
  winner_variant varchar [note: 'Declared winner of the test based on statistical analysis. Control: original experience wins, Treatment: new experience wins, Inconclusive: no statistically significant difference, Ongoing: test still running or analysis incomplete.']

  Note: 'Master record for A/B and multivariate tests run on the digital storefront, covering the full test lifecycle from hypothesis through outcome. Captures test name, hypothesis, type (layout, copy, pricing, CTA, recommendation), start/end dates, traffic split, control/treatment variants, success metric, significance threshold, status, and per-variant results (impressions, conversions, CVR, AOV, revenue per visitor, confidence level, lift, winner flag). Supports continuous site optimization and evidence-based rollout decisions.'
}

Table ecommerce.wishlist {
  wishlist_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the wishlist. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Wishlists created from campaign traffic (gift guides, seasonal lookbooks). Supports campaign engagement measurement beyond immediate conversion and enables wishlist-based remarketing within campaign c']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital storefront where this wishlist was created.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who owns this wishlist.']
  archived_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist was archived by the customer. Null if never archived.']
  back_in_stock_alert_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer wants to be notified when out-of-stock wishlist items become available.']
  conversion_count varchar [note: 'Number of wishlist items that have been moved to cart or purchased.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist was first created by the customer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for wishlist value calculations.']
  default_wishlist_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the customer\'s default wishlist for quick-add actions from product pages.']
  deleted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist was soft-deleted by the customer. Null if not deleted.']
  event_date date [note: 'Target date for event-based wishlists (e.g., wedding date, birthday, holiday). Null for non-event wishlists.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of items currently in the wishlist.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist was last modified (item added, removed, or wishlist attributes updated).']
  last_share_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the wishlist was most recently shared. Null if never shared.']
  last_viewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the customer last accessed or viewed this wishlist.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'Locale code (language and region) for the wishlist, inherited from the storefront at creation time.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments added by the customer about the wishlist.']
  notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer has opted in to receive notifications for price drops, back-in-stock alerts, or promotional offers on wishlist items.']
  price_drop_alert_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer wants to be notified when any wishlist item price decreases.']
  registry_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'For registry-type wishlists, the percentage of items that have been purchased by gift-givers. Null for non-registry wishlists.']
  share_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the wishlist has been shared via social media, email, or direct link.']
  share_token varchar [note: 'Unique token embedded in the share URL for secure access control.']
  share_url varchar [note: 'Unique shareable URL for the wishlist when visibility is public or shared.']
  source_channel varchar [note: 'Digital channel through which the wishlist was originally created.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of current prices for all items in the wishlist, in the storefront currency.']
  view_count varchar [note: 'Total number of times the wishlist has been viewed by the owner or shared recipients.']
  visibility varchar [note: 'Privacy setting for the wishlist: private (only owner), public (discoverable), or shared (accessible via link).']
  wishlist_description varchar [note: 'Optional customer-provided description or notes about the wishlist purpose.']
  wishlist_name varchar [note: 'Customer-defined name for the wishlist (e.g., \'Summer Favorites\', \'Gift Ideas\', \'Wedding Registry\').']
  wishlist_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the wishlist: active (in use), archived (saved but inactive), or deleted (soft delete).']
  wishlist_type varchar [note: 'Classification of wishlist purpose: standard (general), registry (wedding/baby), gift (gift ideas), favorites (saved items), seasonal (holiday/season-specific), or event (special occasion).']

  Note: 'Captures shopper wishlists on the digital storefront including list-level attributes (name, creation date, visibility, share URL) and item-level detail (SKU, style, colorway, size, date added, price at save, current price, price drop flag, in-stock flag, move-to-cart timestamp). Supports demand sensing, NOS planning, personalized re-engagement, and back-in-stock/price-drop notification triggers. Distinct from cart — wishlist represents saved intent without active checkout commitment.'
}

Table ecommerce.product_review {
  product_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product review record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Reviews solicited via post-purchase email campaigns. Tracks review generation campaign effectiveness and supports UGC content strategy measurement. Links review acquisition to marketing investment.']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Customer reviews reporting safety issues, labeling errors, or quality defects trigger compliance incident investigations. Quality assurance teams link reviews to incidents for root cause analysis, cor']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who submitted the review. Links to the customer profile in the customer domain.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order from which this review originated, if the review is from a verified purchase.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Product reviews must link to specific SKUs for quality feedback loops, return rate analysis, fit issue identification, and product performance dashboards. Currently has only \'sku\' code field; proper F']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Customer reviews linked to production batches enable quality intelligence by factory, season, and production run. Identifies manufacturing quality issues, informs factory selection decisions, and supp']
  age_range varchar [note: 'Age bracket of the reviewer, optionally provided to help other customers assess relevance of the review. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: under_18|18_24|25_34|35_44|45_54|55_64|65_plus — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  body_type varchar [note: 'Self-reported body type of the reviewer, optionally provided to help other customers with similar body types assess fit.']
  brand_response_text varchar [note: 'Official response from the brand or customer service team addressing the customer\'s review, feedback, or concerns.']
  brand_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the brand published their response to the customer review.']
  color_purchased varchar [note: 'The color variant of the product that the customer purchased and is reviewing. Helps identify color-specific quality issues.']
  comfort_rating varchar [note: 'Customer rating of the product\'s comfort and wearability on a scale of 1 to 5. Critical metric for apparel and footwear.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the review record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  fit_rating varchar [note: 'Customer assessment of how the apparel item fits relative to standard sizing expectations. Critical for apparel purchase decisions.']
  helpful_votes_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers who marked this review as helpful. Used to surface most useful reviews and calculate review quality scores.']
  incentive_type varchar [note: 'Type of incentive provided to the customer for submitting the review, if applicable. Required for transparency and regulatory compliance.']
  incentivized_review_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer received an incentive (discount, loyalty points, free product) for submitting the review. Required for FTC compliance.']
  media_attachment_count varchar [note: 'Number of photos or videos attached to the review by the customer. Reviews with media typically receive higher engagement.']
  moderation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for rejection or flagging of the review, such as inappropriate content, spam, or policy violation.']
  moderation_status varchar [note: 'Current moderation workflow state of the review. Pending indicates awaiting review, approved means published, rejected means not published, flagged indicates requiring additional review.']
  moderation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review moderation decision was made by the content moderation team or automated system.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the review record was last modified. Tracks updates to moderation status, brand responses, or vote counts.']
  overall_rating varchar [note: 'Overall product rating provided by the customer on a scale of 1 to 5, where 1 is poor and 5 is excellent.']
  published_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review is currently visible on the public-facing digital storefront. True if published, False if hidden or unpublished.']
  published_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the review was first made visible on the digital storefront after moderation approval.']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Customer rating of product quality on a scale of 1 to 5, assessing materials, construction, and craftsmanship.']
  recommend_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer would recommend this product to others. True if yes, False if no. Contributes to Net Promoter Score (NPS) calculation.']
  review_body varchar [note: 'Full text content of the customer\'s product review, containing detailed feedback, opinions, and experiences.']
  review_language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the language in which the review was written.']
  review_source_channel varchar [note: 'The digital channel or touchpoint through which the customer submitted the review.']
  review_title varchar [note: 'Short headline or title summarizing the customer\'s review, displayed prominently on the product page.']
  reviewer_display_name varchar [note: 'Public display name or pseudonym of the reviewer shown on the product page. May be customer\'s actual name or chosen alias.']
  reviewer_location varchar [note: 'Geographic location of the reviewer, typically city and state or country, displayed to provide context for the review.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Automated sentiment analysis score derived from natural language processing of the review text, ranging from -1.00 (very negative) to +1.00 (very positive).']
  size_purchased varchar [note: 'The size variant of the product that the customer purchased and is reviewing. Provides context for fit feedback.']
  sku varchar [note: 'The SKU code of the product being reviewed. Links to the product catalog.']
  style_rating varchar [note: 'Customer rating of the product\'s style and aesthetic appeal on a scale of 1 to 5. Specific to fashion and apparel products.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer submitted the product review to the digital storefront.']
  unhelpful_votes_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers who marked this review as not helpful. Used for review quality assessment and ranking algorithms.']
  value_rating varchar [note: 'Customer assessment of product value for money on a scale of 1 to 5, comparing price paid to perceived quality and utility.']
  verified_purchase_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the review is from a verified purchase transaction. True if the reviewer purchased the product through the platform, False otherwise.']

  Note: 'Captures customer-submitted product reviews and ratings on the digital storefront, including reviewer reference, SKU reviewed, overall rating (1-5), review title, review body, fit rating, quality rating, verified purchase flag, submission date, moderation status (pending, approved, rejected, flagged), helpful votes, and response from brand. Supports social proof, product quality feedback loops, and NPS contribution.'
}

Table ecommerce.ecommerce_storefront_availability {
  ecommerce_storefront_availability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the storefront availability record. Primary key for this entity.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the digital storefront where this availability record applies.']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the primary warehouse or distribution center that will fulfill orders for this SKU on this storefront.']
  inventory_pool_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the inventory pool from which this storefront sources its available inventory. Links to the inventory allocation strategy.']
  quality_hold_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_hold. Business justification: Inventory availability must respect quality holds to prevent sale of non-conforming goods. Quality hold status drives WMS inventory blocks, ATP calculation exclusions, and storefront suppression flags']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the SKU whose availability is being tracked on the digital storefront.']
  source_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: Storefront availability records source inventory from specific DCs for ATP calculation and regional stock display. Critical for accurate online inventory visibility and preventing overselling in omnic']
  superseded_ecommerce_storefront_availability_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on ecommerce_storefront_availability (superseded_ecommerce_storefront_availability_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Availability messaging on digital storefronts includes vendor-specific lead times and restock ETAs, especially for drop-ship, made-to-order, and backorder scenarios. Enables accurate customer communic']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for inventory allocation to this storefront when inventory is constrained across multiple channels.']
  atp_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the SKU that can be promised to customers for future delivery, accounting for committed inventory and inbound supply.']
  availability_change_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the availability status last changed. Used for tracking availability transitions and triggering notifications.']
  availability_message varchar [note: 'Customer-facing message describing the current availability status of the SKU, such as in stock, low stock, or out of stock messaging.']
  availability_status varchar [note: 'Current availability status of the SKU on the digital storefront. Determines shopper visibility, listing suppression, and checkout eligibility.']
  available_quantity varchar [note: 'Current quantity of the SKU available for purchase on this storefront. Represents the Available-to-Sell (ATS) quantity visible to shoppers.']
  back_in_stock_notification_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether back-in-stock notifications are enabled for this SKU. When true, customers can subscribe to restock alerts.']
  backorder_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is eligible for backorder when out of stock. When true, customers can place orders for future fulfillment.']
  backorder_eta date [note: 'Estimated date when backordered SKUs will be available for fulfillment. Used for backorder messaging and customer expectations.']
  backorder_message varchar [note: 'Customer-facing message displayed when the SKU is on backorder, providing estimated availability and fulfillment expectations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storefront availability record was first created in the system.']
  display_availability_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether availability information should be displayed to shoppers on the product detail page. When false, availability is hidden.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this availability record became effective on the storefront. Used for historical tracking and availability lifecycle management.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this availability record expires or is no longer valid. Used for seasonal products and limited-time offerings.']
  inventory_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system providing inventory data for this availability record, such as WMS, ERP, or OMS.']
  last_inventory_sync_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the last successful inventory synchronization from the warehouse management system to the digital storefront.']
  low_stock_threshold varchar [note: 'Threshold quantity below which the SKU is considered low stock and may trigger low-stock messaging or alerts.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storefront availability record was last modified or updated.']
  notification_subscriber_count varchar [note: 'Number of customers subscribed to back-in-stock notifications for this SKU on this storefront. Used for demand forecasting.']
  perpetual_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this SKU is managed as perpetual inventory (always available) on the storefront, bypassing standard inventory checks.']
  pre_order_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU is eligible for pre-order before official release. When true, customers can reserve the SKU for future delivery.']
  pre_order_release_date date [note: 'Date when pre-ordered SKUs will be released and shipped to customers. Applicable only when availability status is pre-order.']
  previous_availability_status varchar [note: 'The availability status immediately before the most recent change. Used for availability change history and analytics.']
  reserved_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity of the SKU currently reserved in active shopping carts or pending orders, not yet available for new purchases.']
  restock_date date [note: 'Expected date when the SKU will be restocked and available for purchase again. Used for back-in-stock notifications and customer communication.']
  safety_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum safety stock quantity maintained for this SKU on this storefront to prevent stockouts and maintain service levels.']
  suppression_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the SKU listing is suppressed from the digital storefront. When true, the SKU is hidden from search and browse results.']
  suppression_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the SKU listing is suppressed from the storefront. Provides context for merchandising and operational teams. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_stock|quality_issue|seasonal|discontinued|compliance|pricing_error|merchandising_decision — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Real-time digital shelf availability record for each SKU on each storefront, representing the ecommerce platform\'s view of Available-to-Promise (ATP) and Available-to-Sell (ATS) quantities. Captures SKU, storefront, availability status (in-stock, low-stock, out-of-stock, pre-order, backorder), available quantity, restock ETA, suppression flag, backorder messaging eligibility, pre-order release date, last inventory sync timestamp, and availability change history. Distinct from warehouse inventory (owned by inventory domain) — this entity represents what the shopper sees and what drives listing suppression, back-in-stock notifications, and pre-order/backorder checkout logic on the digital storefront.'
}

Table ecommerce.search_query {
  search_query_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the search query event. Primary key for the search query product.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Search queries driven by campaign messaging (branded search from TV, product search from social). Measures campaign impact on search behavior and supports SEO-SEM-campaign integration analysis.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the digital storefront where the search was performed.']
  merchandising_rule_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the merchandising rule applied to this search query if any rule was triggered.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who performed the search. Null for guest users.']
  refined_from_search_query_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on search_query (refined_from_search_query_id)']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the web session during which this search query was executed.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'The A/B test variant identifier if this search was part of a search algorithm or merchandising experiment.']
  add_to_cart_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper added at least one product to cart from this search session.']
  autocomplete_suggestion_count varchar [note: 'The number of autocomplete suggestions displayed to the shopper as they typed the search term.']
  click_through_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper clicked on at least one product from the search results page.']
  click_through_position varchar [note: 'The position rank of the first product clicked by the shopper in the search results list. Null if no click occurred.']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the search session resulted in a completed purchase transaction.']
  corrected_search_term varchar [note: 'The spell-corrected version of the search term if automatic correction was applied. Null if no correction occurred.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this search query record was first created in the data platform.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the storefront where the search was performed.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used by the shopper to perform the search: desktop, mobile web, tablet, or native mobile app.']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'The locale code representing the language and region context of the search in ISO 639-1 and ISO 3166-1 format.']
  merchandising_rule_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any merchandising rules such as boosting, burying, or pinning were applied to influence search result ranking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this search query record was last modified or enriched with additional metrics.']
  normalized_search_term varchar [note: 'The search term after normalization processing including lowercasing, stemming, and synonym expansion for analytics consistency.']
  null_result_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the search query returned zero results, signaling a merchandising gap or catalog coverage issue.']
  page_number varchar [note: 'The deepest results page number viewed by the shopper during this search session.']
  personalization_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether search results were personalized based on customer profile, browsing history, or segment membership.']
  personalization_segment varchar [note: 'The customer segment code used to personalize search results if personalization was applied.']
  refinement_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the shopper applied any filters or refinements such as size, color, price range, or brand after the initial search.']
  refinement_count varchar [note: 'The number of refinement filters applied by the shopper to narrow search results.']
  results_count varchar [note: 'The total number of product results returned by the search query.']
  results_viewed_count varchar [note: 'The total number of individual product results viewed by the shopper across all pages during this search session.']
  search_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'The time in seconds the shopper spent on search results pages before exiting or converting.']
  search_exit_type varchar [note: 'The action taken by the shopper to exit the search results: clicked a product, navigated to category, performed new search, exited site, went to cart, or proceeded to checkout.']
  search_method varchar [note: 'How the search term was entered: manually typed, selected from autocomplete, clicked from suggested searches, selected from recent searches, or clicked from trending searches.']
  search_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total revenue attributed to this search query if a conversion occurred within the session.']
  search_source varchar [note: 'The page or interface location where the search was initiated: header search box, landing page, category page, product page, no-results page, or mobile app search.']
  search_term varchar [note: 'The raw search query text entered by the shopper in the search box.']
  search_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the search query was executed by the shopper.']
  search_type varchar [note: 'The type of search performed: keyword text search, category browse, brand filter, attribute filter, voice search, visual search, or barcode scan. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: keyword|category|brand|attribute|voice|visual|barcode — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sort_option varchar [note: 'The sorting method selected by the shopper to order search results: relevance, price ascending, price descending, newest arrivals, bestsellers, highest rating, or highest discount. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: relevance|price_low_high|price_high_low|newest|bestseller|rating|discount — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  spell_correction_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the search engine applied automatic spell correction to the original search term.']
  synonym_expansion_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the search engine expanded the query using configured synonym rules to broaden results.']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'The UTM campaign parameter associated with the session in which the search occurred, used for marketing attribution.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'The UTM medium parameter associated with the session in which the search occurred, identifying the marketing medium.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'The UTM source parameter associated with the session in which the search occurred, identifying the traffic source.']

  Note: 'Captures on-site search queries entered by shoppers including search term, results count, click-through rate, and null-result flags for merchandising optimization'
}

Table ecommerce.recommendation {
  recommendation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the product recommendation event. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Recommendations contextualized by active campaigns (seasonal, launch). Enables campaign-aware personalization (recommend campaign products) and measures recommendation engine contribution to campaign ']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the digital storefront where the recommendation was displayed.']
  fallback_recommendation_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on recommendation (fallback_recommendation_id)']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the customer who received the recommendation.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order that included a recommended product purchase.']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_sketch. Business justification: Personalization engines recommend products from similar design aesthetics using sketch attributes (silhouette, color story, design direction). Recommendation algorithms leverage design sketch metadata']
  web_session_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the web session during which the recommendation was served.']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier of the A/B test variant or experiment group to which this recommendation belongs.']
  add_to_cart_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any recommended product was added to the shopping cart.']
  added_to_cart_sku varchar [note: 'The specific SKU from the recommendation set that was added to the cart.']
  algorithm_type varchar [note: 'Type of recommendation algorithm used to generate the product suggestions.']
  algorithm_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the recommendation algorithm used.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Digital channel through which the recommendation was delivered.']
  click_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer clicked on any of the recommended products.']
  click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer clicked on a recommended product.']
  clicked_sku varchar [note: 'The specific SKU that was clicked by the customer from the recommendation set.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Algorithm confidence score for the recommendation quality, typically ranging from 0 to 1.']
  conversion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether any recommended product resulted in a completed purchase.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recommended product was purchased.']
  converted_sku varchar [note: 'The specific SKU from the recommendation set that was purchased.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recommendation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the revenue amount.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the customer when the recommendation was served.']
  impression_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the recommendation was actually displayed to the customer (true) or generated but not rendered (false).']
  locale_code varchar [note: 'Locale or language setting of the customer when the recommendation was served.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recommendation record was last updated.']
  personalization_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment or persona used to personalize the recommendation.']
  placement_position varchar [note: 'Sequential position of the recommendation widget on the page (1 for first, 2 for second, etc.).']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Location or context where the recommendation was displayed to the customer. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: homepage|product_detail_page|cart|checkout|category_page|search_results|email|mobile_app — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  primary_sku varchar [note: 'The anchor or context SKU that triggered the recommendation (e.g., the product being viewed when recommendations were shown).']
  recommendation_source varchar [note: 'Source system or service that generated the recommendation (e.g., Adobe Target, internal ML service).']
  recommendation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the recommendation event.']
  recommendation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the recommendation was generated and served to the customer.']
  recommended_sku_count varchar [note: 'Total number of SKUs included in the recommendation set.']
  recommended_sku_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of SKU codes that were recommended to the customer in display order.']
  relevance_score decimal(5,4) [note: 'Calculated relevance score indicating how well the recommendation matches customer preferences.']
  revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue generated from the purchase of recommended products in this recommendation event.']
  strategy varchar [note: 'Business strategy or campaign name associated with the recommendation (e.g., cross-sell, upsell, complete-the-look, frequently-bought-together).']
  time_to_click_seconds varchar [note: 'Number of seconds elapsed between recommendation display and customer click.']
  time_to_conversion_seconds varchar [note: 'Number of seconds elapsed between recommendation display and purchase conversion.']

  Note: 'Records product recommendations served to shoppers including algorithm type, placement, recommended SKUs, and click/conversion outcomes'
}

Table ecommerce.storefront_carrier_service {
  storefront_carrier_service_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the storefront-carrier service configuration record']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the carrier providing service to this storefront']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital storefront where this carrier service is configured']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storefront-carrier service configuration record was created in the system.']
  display_name varchar [note: 'Customer-facing display name for this carrier service option shown at checkout (e.g., \'FedEx 2-Day Shipping\', \'UPS Ground\'). May vary by storefront locale.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this carrier service configuration became effective for this storefront. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this carrier service is currently enabled and available for customer selection at checkout on this storefront. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  estimated_cost_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum estimated shipping cost for this carrier service on this storefront, used for customer cost estimation ranges.']
  estimated_cost_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum estimated shipping cost for this carrier service on this storefront, used for customer cost estimation ranges.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this carrier service configuration expires or was discontinued for this storefront. Null if still active. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  free_shipping_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Order value threshold above which this carrier service is offered for free on this storefront. Null if no free shipping promotion applies.']
  priority_rank bigint [note: 'Display priority ranking for this carrier service option at checkout on this storefront. Lower numbers appear first. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  rate_card_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the negotiated rate card or pricing agreement applicable to this storefront-carrier combination. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'The specific service level offered by this carrier on this storefront (e.g., standard, express, overnight). Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  sla_target_days bigint [note: 'Service level agreement target delivery time in days for this carrier service on this storefront. Used for customer delivery estimates. Identified in detection phase as relationship-specific data.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User or system identifier that last updated this storefront-carrier service configuration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this storefront-carrier service configuration record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the service configuration between digital storefronts and carriers. It captures the operational relationship where each storefront offers specific carrier services to customers at checkout, with distinct service levels, rates, SLAs, and availability settings per storefront-carrier combination. Each record links one digital storefront to one carrier with attributes that define how that carrier service is presented and managed within that specific storefront context.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion e-commerce operations, digital storefronts offer multiple carrier service options to customers at checkout (FedEx Express, UPS Ground, USPS Priority, regional carriers, etc.), and each carrier serves multiple storefronts across different brands, regions, and markets. The business actively manages storefront-carrier service configurations as operational entities, setting service levels, priority rankings, rate cards, SLAs, and availability flags per storefront-carrier pair. This is not a derived analytical relationship but an operational configuration that ecommerce teams create, update, and disable as part of checkout experience management and carrier performance optimization.'
}

Table ecommerce.storefront_fulfillment_assignment {
  storefront_fulfillment_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this storefront-DC fulfillment assignment record. Primary key.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital storefront that this fulfillment assignment applies to']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the distribution center assigned to fulfill orders from this storefront']
  inventory_pool_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific inventory pool within the DC that this storefront draws from for ATP calculation. Supports segmented inventory management where a DC maintains separate pools for different channels or brands.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of inventory allocated from this DC to this storefront for ATP calculation and order routing. Used in split-allocation scenarios where multiple DCs share fulfillment responsibility for a storefront.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment assignment record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this storefront-DC fulfillment assignment becomes active and begins routing orders. Supports temporal assignment changes for seasonal capacity shifts, new DC launches, or network reconfigurations.']
  enabled_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this storefront-DC fulfillment assignment is currently active and available for order routing. Allows temporary disabling of assignments without deleting the configuration (e.g., during DC maintenance or capacity constraints).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this storefront-DC fulfillment assignment expires and stops routing orders. Null if assignment is indefinite. Supports planned network changes and temporary assignments.']
  fulfillment_priority varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking for this DC when fulfilling orders from this storefront. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by order routing logic to sequence DC selection when multiple DCs can fulfill the same order.']
  shipping_cutoff_time timestamp [note: 'Time of day (in DC local time) by which orders from this storefront must be received to qualify for same-day shipment from this DC. Used for delivery promise calculation and order routing deadlines.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fulfillment assignment record was last modified.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the fulfillment assignment between digital storefronts and distribution centers in the omnichannel apparel network. It captures the operational routing rules that determine which DCs can fulfill orders from which storefronts, including priority sequencing, inventory allocation percentages, shipping cutoff times, and effective date ranges. Each record links one digital storefront to one distribution center with attributes that govern order routing, ATP calculation, and fulfillment orchestration across the logistics network.. Existence Justification: In apparel omnichannel operations, digital storefronts fulfill orders from multiple distribution centers to provide inventory breadth, geographic coverage, and redundancy, while each distribution center serves multiple storefronts across different brands, regions, and channels. The business actively manages these fulfillment assignments as operational configuration, setting priority sequences, allocation percentages, cutoff times, and effective date ranges for each storefront-DC pairing to optimize order routing, ATP calculation, and delivery promises.'
}

Table ecommerce.storefront_factory_allocation {
  storefront_factory_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this storefront-factory allocation record. Primary key.']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital storefront that sources from this factory allocation']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the manufacturing factory allocated to this storefront']
  allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority ranking for this factory when routing orders from this storefront. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by order management systems to determine factory selection sequence when multiple factories are available.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this allocation. Active = available for order routing; Inactive = historical record; Pending = approved but not yet effective; Suspended = temporarily disabled.']
  capacity_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the factory\'s total certified capacity allocated to this storefront. Used for production planning and capacity reservation. Sum of allocations across all storefronts for a factory should not exceed 100%.']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this allocation record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this storefront-factory allocation becomes active and can be used for order routing and production planning.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which this storefront-factory allocation expires or is scheduled to be terminated. Null indicates an open-ended allocation. Used for contract management and allocation planning.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this allocation record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this allocation record.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Committed production lead time in days from order placement to ex-factory date for this specific storefront-factory pairing. May differ from the factory\'s standard lead time due to storefront-specific agreements, product mix, or capacity allocation.']
  minimum_order_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum order value in the factory\'s currency required for orders from this storefront to this factory. Used for order routing validation and consolidation logic.']
  product_category_scope varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product categories that this allocation applies to. Allows storefront-factory allocations to be scoped to specific product types (e.g., \'woven,knit\' or \'footwear\'). Null indicates all categories the factory is capable of producing.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that created this allocation record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the allocation contract between digital storefronts and manufacturing factories. It captures the business rules governing which factories can fulfill orders from which storefronts, including priority sequencing, capacity allocation percentages, and lead time commitments. Each record links one digital storefront to one factory with attributes that exist only in the context of this sourcing relationship, enabling order routing logic, production planning, and multi-brand factory allocation strategies.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion multi-brand retail operations, digital storefronts (regional sites, brand-specific sites, outlet microsites) source production from multiple factories based on capacity, capability, and geographic optimization, while factories simultaneously produce for multiple storefronts across different brands and regions. The business actively manages storefront-factory allocation rules as a sourcing strategy, setting priority sequences for order routing, allocating factory capacity percentages across storefronts, and negotiating storefront-specific lead times. This is an operational relationship managed by production planning and sourcing teams, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table ecommerce.merchandising_rule {
  merchandising_rule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for merchandising_rule']
  overridden_by_merchandising_rule_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on merchandising_rule (overridden_by_merchandising_rule_id)']
  ab_test_variant varchar [note: 'Identifier for the A/B test variant this rule belongs to, used for testing different merchandising strategies.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Type of merchandising action the rule performs when triggered (e.g., boosting product visibility, excluding items, pinning to top).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the manager or system user who approved the rule for activation.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the merchandising rule was approved and authorized for use in production.']
  boost_factor decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric multiplier applied to product ranking scores when the rule boosts product visibility. Higher values increase prominence.']
  condition_logic varchar [note: 'Business logic or criteria that must be met for the merchandising rule to be triggered and applied.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the merchandising rule record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description explaining the purpose, strategy, and expected impact of the merchandising rule.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the merchandising rule expires and stops influencing site merchandising. Null indicates no expiration.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the merchandising rule becomes active and begins influencing site merchandising.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the merchandising rule applies globally across all markets and channels or is limited to specific targets.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who most recently modified the merchandising rule configuration.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the merchandising rule was last updated or modified.']
  max_products_displayed varchar [note: 'Maximum number of products that can be displayed when this merchandising rule is applied to a placement zone.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional internal notes or comments about the rule for merchandising team reference and collaboration.']
  performance_metric_target varchar [note: 'Primary key performance indicator (KPI) that the merchandising rule is designed to optimize.']
  personalization_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the rule uses personalization algorithms to tailor product recommendations based on customer behavior and preferences.']
  placement_zone varchar [note: 'Specific zone or slot on the digital storefront where the rule controls product placement (e.g., homepage hero, category top, PDP recommendations).']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric ranking used to determine rule precedence when multiple rules apply to the same product or page. Lower numbers indicate higher priority.']
  rule_category varchar [note: 'Secondary classification indicating how the rule is triggered or managed within the merchandising system.']
  rule_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the merchandising rule used in external systems and reporting.']
  rule_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the merchandising rule for display and identification purposes.']
  rule_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the merchandising rule indicating its primary purpose in the digital storefront.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system where the merchandising rule was created and is managed (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the merchandising rule indicating whether it is currently in effect.']
  target_channel varchar [note: 'Digital channel where the merchandising rule is applied within the direct-to-consumer (DTC) ecosystem.']
  target_geography varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the geographic market where the rule applies. Multiple geographies stored as comma-separated codes.']
  target_segment varchar [note: 'Customer segment or persona that the merchandising rule is designed to target for personalization purposes.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the merchandising rule configuration, incremented with each modification for change tracking.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the merchandising manager or system user who created the rule.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for merchandising_rule. Referenced by merchandising_rule_id.'
}

Table logistics.shipment {
  shipment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shipment record. Primary key for the logistics shipment entity.']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Freight transport is a major Scope 3 emission source (Category 4 upstream, Category 9 downstream). Apparel companies track carbon footprint per shipment for CDP disclosure, SBTi target tracking, and E']
  carrier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the transportation carrier or logistics service provider responsible for moving the shipment.']
  coordinator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Shipment coordinators create and manage shipments for accountability, performance tracking, and audit trails. Essential for logistics operations reporting and workforce productivity analysis in appare']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Freight costs are allocated to cost centers for departmental budgeting and P&L tracking. Monthly cost center reporting requires shipment-level freight allocation to calculate actual vs budget variance']
  destination_facility_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the facility, warehouse, store, or customer location to which the shipment is destined.']
  hs_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.hs_code. Business justification: Shipments are classified by HS code for customs and duty purposes. N:1 relationship. Normalizes hs_code string to FK for referential integrity and to enable HS code master data management.']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the purchase order associated with this inbound shipment from supplier to warehouse.']
  origin_factory_supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Inbound shipments from supplier factories require direct factory tracking for origin verification, GSP/duty calculations, factory OTIF performance metrics, and compliance audits. Multi-factory vendors']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory.
