  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this redemption.']
  discount_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the discount applied to the order as a result of the promo code redemption.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage discount applied if the promo code is percentage-based.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'The type of discount applied (percentage, fixed amount, buy-one-get-one, free shipping, tiered).']
  first_time_customer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this redemption was by a first-time customer (true) or a returning customer (false).']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this redemption was flagged as potentially fraudulent or abusive (true) or legitimate (false).']
  fraud_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason why this redemption was flagged as fraudulent, if applicable.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this redemption record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about this promo code redemption event.']
  order_subtotal_after_discount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The order subtotal amount after the promotional discount was applied.']
  order_subtotal_before_discount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The order subtotal amount before the promotional discount was applied.']
  order_total_after_discount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total order amount including tax and shipping after the promotional discount was applied.']
  order_total_before_discount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total order amount including tax and shipping before the promotional discount was applied.']
  redemption_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the promo code was redeemed (DTC, e-commerce, retail POS, mobile app, wholesale, call center).']
  redemption_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the redemption occurred.']
  redemption_device_type varchar [note: 'The type of device used to redeem the promo code (desktop, mobile, tablet, POS terminal, kiosk, unknown).']
  redemption_ip_address varchar [note: 'The IP address from which the promo code redemption was initiated, used for fraud detection and geographic analysis.']
  redemption_limit_exceeded_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the customer attempted to redeem the promo code beyond the allowed usage limit (true) or within limits (false).']
  redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the promo code redemption (applied, pending, failed, reversed, expired).']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The exact date and time when the promotional code was redeemed by the customer.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'The reason why the promo code redemption was reversed (e.g., order cancelled, return processed, fraud detected).']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the promo code redemption was reversed, if applicable (e.g., due to order cancellation or return).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this promo redemption record (e.g., Salesforce Commerce Cloud, SAP Customer Activity Repository, Adobe Experience Platform).']
  stacking_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this promo code was allowed to be stacked with other promotions (true) or not (false).']
  utm_campaign varchar [note: 'The UTM campaign parameter captured at the time of redemption for marketing attribution.']
  utm_medium varchar [note: 'The UTM medium parameter captured at the time of redemption for marketing attribution.']
  utm_source varchar [note: 'The UTM source parameter captured at the time of redemption for marketing attribution.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each promotional code redemption event. Captures redemption timestamp, promo code reference, order reference, customer reference, channel (DTC, e-commerce, retail POS), discount amount applied, order value before and after discount, and redemption status. Enables campaign effectiveness measurement, CAC calculation, and fraud detection for promo abuse.'
}

Table marketing.nps_response {
  nps_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each NPS survey response record.']
  audience_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.audience_segment. Business justification: nps_response currently stores customer_segment as STRING. NPS responses are often analyzed by marketing audience segment to understand brand perception across different customer groups. Should normali']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign if the survey was part of a campaign-specific NPS measurement.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who submitted this NPS response.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store location if the survey was triggered by an in-store experience.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order associated with this NPS survey, if applicable (post-purchase surveys).']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: NPS feedback is SKU-specific (size/fit issues, color accuracy). Granular feedback tracking required for fit optimization, size chart improvements, and quality control. Complements order_id with direct']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.style. Business justification: NPS surveys reference purchased style (product satisfaction feedback). Required for product quality tracking, style-level NPS reporting, and product development feedback loops. Natural customer feedba']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Quality feedback tied to specific production batches enables root cause analysis. Linking NPS responses to the work order that produced the purchased item supports factory performance analysis, qualit']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NPS response record was first created in the data lakehouse.']
  customer_lifetime_value_tier varchar [note: 'Customer lifetime value tier at the time of survey response.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the customer to complete the survey.']
  external_survey_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier from the external survey platform (Adobe Experience Platform) for cross-system reconciliation.']
  feedback_category varchar [note: 'Primary category or theme of the feedback (e.g., product quality, customer service, delivery experience, pricing, brand perception). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: product_quality|customer_service|delivery_experience|pricing|brand_perception|store_experience|website_usability|product_variety|sustainability|returns_process — promote to reference product]']
  feedback_sentiment varchar [note: 'Sentiment classification of the verbatim feedback derived from natural language processing or manual tagging.']
  follow_up_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether follow-up action was completed by the customer service or marketing team.']
  follow_up_date date [note: 'Date when follow-up contact with the customer was completed.']

  incentive_type varchar [note: 'Type of incentive offered to the customer for survey completion.']
  incentive_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the incentive offered, in the base currency.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this NPS response record was last modified in the data lakehouse.']
  nps_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of the respondent based on NPS score: Promoter (9-10), Passive (7-8), Detractor (0-6).']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score provided by the customer on a scale of 0 to 10, where 0 is extremely unlikely to recommend and 10 is extremely likely to recommend.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category associated with the purchase or interaction that triggered this survey.']
  response_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the country from which the customer submitted the response.']
  response_language varchar [note: 'Language in which the customer completed the survey, represented as ISO 639-1 two-letter code.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Status of the survey response indicating whether it was fully completed, partially completed, or abandoned.']
  response_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time elapsed in hours between survey send and response submission.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer submitted their NPS survey response.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Fashion season code during which the survey was conducted (e.g., SS24, FW24).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system from which this NPS response was ingested (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform).']
  survey_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the NPS survey was delivered and completed.']
  survey_send_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the NPS survey invitation was sent to the customer.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Type of NPS survey administered to the customer.']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey template used for this response, enabling tracking of survey design changes over time.']
  touchpoint_date date [note: 'Date of the customer touchpoint or interaction that triggered this NPS survey.']
  touchpoint_type varchar [note: 'Type of customer touchpoint or interaction that triggered this NPS survey.']
  verbatim_feedback varchar [note: 'Open-ended text feedback provided by the customer explaining their NPS score.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey response collected from customers across post-purchase, post-return, and brand health surveys. Captures survey type, channel (email, in-app, in-store, SMS), NPS score (0-10), promoter/passive/detractor classification, verbatim feedback text, survey send timestamp, response timestamp, customer segment reference, and associated touchpoint or order. Sourced via Adobe Experience Platform.'
}

Table marketing.brand_health_survey {
  brand_health_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the brand health survey record. Primary key for the brand health survey entity.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: brand_health_survey currently stores brand_name as STRING. Should normalize to FK relationship with brand reference table. The brand_name column becomes redundant as it can be retrieved via JOIN to br']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Brand research studies have costs (study_cost_amount field) that must be allocated to cost centers for marketing research budget tracking, vendor payment processing, and expense management in apparel ']
  aided_awareness_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents who recognize the brand when prompted with brand name or visual cues. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for brand recognition.']
  brand_affinity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score measuring emotional connection and loyalty to the brand, typically on a 0-100 scale. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for brand equity.']
  change_vs_prior_wave_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage point change in primary brand health metric compared to previous wave, enabling longitudinal trend tracking.']
  competitive_benchmark_brand varchar [note: 'Name of the primary competitor brand used as benchmark for comparative brand health analysis.']
  competitive_benchmark_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Brand health metric score for the competitive benchmark brand, enabling relative positioning assessment.']
  consideration_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents who would consider purchasing from the brand. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for brand consideration in purchase funnel.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the study cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand health survey record was first created in the system, following ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  fieldwork_end_date date [note: 'The date when data collection fieldwork concluded for this brand health survey wave.']
  fieldwork_start_date date [note: 'The date when data collection fieldwork began for this brand health survey wave.']
  innovation_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for brand innovation and trend leadership, typically on a 0-100 scale. Important for fashion and lifestyle positioning.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this brand health survey record was last modified in the system, following ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX).']
  market_geography varchar [note: 'Specific geographic segmentation or country codes for the survey market using ISO 3166 three-letter country codes (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'FRA\', \'CHN\').']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, observations, or contextual information about the brand health survey, including methodology changes or data quality issues.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score (NPS) measuring likelihood of brand recommendation, calculated as percentage of promoters minus detractors. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for brand advocacy.']
  preference_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents who prefer this brand over competitors. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for brand preference and competitive positioning.']
  prior_wave_aided_awareness_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aided awareness percentage from the previous survey wave, used for period-over-period trend analysis.']
  prior_wave_unaided_awareness_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Unaided awareness percentage from the previous survey wave, used for period-over-period trend analysis.']
  purchase_intent_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents who intend to purchase from the brand in the next 6-12 months. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for conversion likelihood.']
  quality_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for product quality and craftsmanship, typically on a 0-100 scale. Critical for luxury and lifestyle brand positioning.']
  report_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed brand health survey report document, stored in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system or document repository.']
  research_agency_contact varchar [note: 'Primary contact person or email at the research agency responsible for this brand health study.']
  research_agency_name varchar [note: 'The name of the external market research agency or vendor conducting the brand health study.']
  research_methodology varchar [note: 'The primary research methodology used for data collection (online survey, telephone interview, in-person interview, mixed mode, mobile survey, panel study).']
  sample_size_actual varchar [note: 'The actual number of completed survey responses received for this brand health study wave.']
  sample_size_target varchar [note: 'The planned or target number of survey respondents for this brand health study wave.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The fashion season or collection period associated with this brand health measurement (e.g., \'SS24\', \'FW24\', \'Holiday2024\').']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this brand health survey data originated (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform, external research platform).']
  study_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or reference code for the brand health study, used for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  study_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for conducting this brand health survey wave, including research agency fees and fieldwork expenses.']
  study_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the brand health tracking study (e.g., \'Q1 2024 Luxury Brand Perception Study\', \'Spring Collection Brand Equity Wave\').']
  study_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the brand health study (planned, in field, completed, cancelled, on hold, analysis).']
  study_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the brand health study methodology and purpose (e.g., brand tracking, perception study, equity monitor, competitive benchmark).']
  sustainability_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for environmental and social responsibility practices, typically on a 0-100 scale. Increasingly critical for apparel brand equity.']
  target_audience_segment varchar [note: 'The consumer segment or demographic profile targeted for the brand health survey (e.g., \'Luxury Consumers 25-45\', \'Athletic Lifestyle Enthusiasts\', \'High-Income Professionals\').']
  target_market varchar [note: 'The geographic market or region targeted for this brand health study (e.g., \'North America\', \'Western Europe\', \'Asia Pacific\', \'Global\').']
  target_threshold_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'The strategic target or threshold value set for the primary brand health metric, used to assess performance against objectives.']
  unaided_awareness_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of respondents who spontaneously recall the brand without prompting. Key Performance Indicator (KPI) for top-of-mind brand awareness.']
  value_perception_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Consumer perception score for price-to-value ratio and affordability, typically on a 0-100 scale.']
  variance_to_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between actual brand health metric result and target threshold, indicating performance gap or achievement.']
  wave_date date [note: 'The date when this wave of the brand health survey was conducted or completed, used for time-series analysis.']
  wave_number varchar [note: 'Sequential wave number for longitudinal tracking studies, enabling period-over-period comparison of brand health metrics.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for brand health tracking studies, consumer perception surveys, and wave-level metric results. Captures study definition (name, methodology, target market, metrics tracked — aided/unaided awareness, consideration, preference, purchase intent, brand affinity — fieldwork period, sample size, research agency, status) and wave results (date, market/geography, metric name, metric value, prior wave value, change vs prior, competitive benchmark, target threshold). Enables longitudinal brand equity monitoring, competitive positioning assessment, and seasonal brand performance tracking for luxury and lifestyle positioning.'
}

Table marketing.brand_health_result {
  brand_health_result_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the brand health survey result record. Primary key for the brand health result entity.']
  brand_health_survey_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to the specific survey wave or fielding period during which this brand health metric was captured.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand being measured in this survey result. Links to the brand master entity.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this brand health result to a specific marketing campaign if the survey wave was designed to measure campaign impact.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.collection. Business justification: Brand perception studies measure collection-level awareness, consideration, and preference; research data should link to collections for actionable insights on design direction, pricing strategy, and ']
  change_vs_prior_wave decimal(10,4) [note: 'The absolute change in metric value compared to the prior survey wave. Calculated as current metric value minus prior wave value.']
  change_vs_prior_wave_percent decimal(10,4) [note: 'The percentage change in metric value compared to the prior survey wave. Calculated as ((current value - prior value) / prior value) * 100.']
  competitive_benchmark_source varchar [note: 'The source of the competitive benchmark data, such as industry report, syndicated research provider, or internal competitive analysis.']
  competitive_benchmark_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The benchmark metric value representing competitive performance in the same market and category. Used for competitive positioning assessment.']
  confidence_level_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The statistical confidence level for this survey result, typically expressed as a percentage (e.g., 95%, 99%).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this brand health result record was first created in the data platform. Audit field for data lineage and record lifecycle tracking.']
  data_collection_end_date date [note: 'The date when data collection for this survey wave was completed.']
  data_collection_start_date date [note: 'The date when data collection for this survey wave began.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of data quality status for this survey result. Flags any quality concerns or validation issues identified during data processing.']
  demographic_segment varchar [note: 'The demographic segment or audience group for which this brand health metric was measured (e.g., age group, gender, income level, lifestyle segment).']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter in which this brand health survey wave was conducted.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this brand health survey wave was conducted.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this brand health result record was last modified or updated. Audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  margin_of_error_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The margin of error for this survey result, expressed as a percentage. Indicates the precision of the metric value.']
  market_geography varchar [note: 'The geographic market or country where the brand health survey was conducted, represented by ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|ITA|ESP|CHN|JPN|KOR|AUS|BRA|IND|SGP|NLD|BEL|SWE|NOR|DNK|AUT|CHE|POL|TUR|ZAF|ARE|SAU|RUS|ARG|CHL|COL|PER|THA|VNM|IDN|MYS|PHL|NZL|IRL|FIN|PRT|GRC|CZE|HUN|ROU|HKG|TWN|ISR|EGY|NGA|KEN|MAR|TUN|PAK|BGD — 55 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  metric_name varchar [note: 'The specific brand health metric being measured. Includes awareness, consideration, preference, Net Promoter Score (NPS), purchase intent, favorability, trust, quality perception, value perception, innovation perception, sustainability perception, loyalty, advocacy, differentiation, relevance, and momentum. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: brand_awareness|aided_awareness|unaided_awareness|brand_consideration|brand_preference|purchase_intent|nps|brand_favorability|brand_trust|brand_quality_perception|brand_value_perception|brand_innovation_perception|brand_sustainability_perception|brand_loyalty|brand_advocacy|brand_differentiation|brand_relevance|brand_momentum — 18 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  metric_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the metric value. Indicates whether the value is expressed as a percentage, index score, Net Promoter Score (NPS), or rating scale.']
  metric_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The measured value of the brand health metric for this survey wave. Typically expressed as a percentage, index score, or Net Promoter Score (NPS) value.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, observations, or commentary about this brand health result. May include insights about anomalies, market events, or methodological considerations.']
  performance_status varchar [note: 'Classification of the metric performance relative to target threshold. Indicates whether the brand health metric exceeds, meets, or falls below strategic goals.']
  prior_wave_value decimal(10,4) [note: 'The metric value from the previous survey wave for the same brand, market, and metric. Used for trend analysis and change calculation.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'The product category context for this brand health measurement. Indicates which product line or category the brand perception relates to. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: athletic_footwear|athletic_apparel|lifestyle_footwear|lifestyle_apparel|accessories|equipment|luxury_apparel|luxury_footwear|performance_wear|casual_wear|outdoor_wear|sportswear|athleisure — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  research_vendor varchar [note: 'The name of the market research vendor or agency that conducted the brand health survey.']
  respondent_sample_size varchar [note: 'The number of survey respondents included in this brand health metric calculation for this wave and market.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The seasonal collection or campaign period associated with this brand health measurement (e.g., Spring/Summer 2024, Fall/Winter 2024, Holiday 2024).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name of the source system or platform from which this brand health result was ingested (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform, Qualtrics, SurveyMonkey, custom research platform).']
  survey_methodology varchar [note: 'The research methodology used to collect the brand health data (e.g., online panel, telephone interview, in-person interview, mobile survey, mixed mode, social listening). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: online_panel|telephone_interview|in_person_interview|mobile_survey|mixed_mode|social_listening|focus_group — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_threshold decimal(10,4) [note: 'The target or goal value set for this brand health metric. Used to assess performance against strategic brand objectives.']
  variance_vs_benchmark decimal(10,4) [note: 'The difference between the brand metric value and the competitive benchmark value. Positive values indicate outperformance versus competition.']
  variance_vs_target decimal(10,4) [note: 'The difference between the actual metric value and the target threshold. Positive values indicate target achievement or overperformance.']
  wave_date date [note: 'The date when the survey wave was fielded or completed. Represents the principal business event timestamp for this brand health measurement.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of brand health metric results from each wave of a brand health survey. Captures survey wave date, market/geography, brand metric name (awareness, consideration, preference, NPS, purchase intent), metric value, prior wave value, change vs prior wave, competitive benchmark value, and target threshold. Enables brand equity trend analysis and competitive positioning assessment.'
}

Table marketing.seasonal_launch {
  seasonal_launch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the seasonal collection launch event. Primary key for the seasonal launch record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand or sub-brand for which this seasonal launch is executed. Links to the brand master record.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Seasonal launches have dedicated budgets (budget_allocated_amount field exists) that must link to formal budget records for planning, approval workflow, variance tracking, and financial control of maj']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary marketing campaign associated with this seasonal launch. Links to the campaign master record for budget, creative, and performance tracking.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product collection being launched in this seasonal event. Links to the merchandising collection master record.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: Seasonal launches are built around hero design concepts; go-to-market strategy, PR messaging, influencer briefings, and launch event planning all require concept details for cohesive storytelling and ']
  hero_style_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.style. Business justification: Launches feature hero/signature styles (key looks, statement pieces). Required for launch planning, hero product allocation, PR seeding, and influencer gifting. Complements existing collection_id with']
  launch_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Seasonal launches require dedicated cross-functional coordination. Tracking the employee owner enables accountability for launch execution, timeline management, and post-launch performance analysis. L']
  regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_change. Business justification: Seasonal product launches must track new regulations affecting marketing claims, packaging disclosures, or sustainability statements. Launch teams need to verify compliance with recent regulatory chan']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.rfq. Business justification: Seasonal launches require sourcing capacity secured through RFQs. Launch planning teams monitor RFQ status, vendor selection, and cost negotiations to ensure production feasibility and timeline alignm']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_purchase_order. Business justification: Launch execution depends on confirmed production orders. Marketing teams track PO delivery dates, production status, and OTIF performance to coordinate campaign timing, inventory availability, and go-']
  target_customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Seasonal launches target specific customer segments (luxury segment for premium collections, value segment for accessible lines). Tracks segment-specific launch performance, segment penetration, and e']
  target_stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Seasonal launches require coordinated inventory positioning across channels. Real process: launch planning teams monitor stock positions for launch SKUs to ensure sufficient inventory is allocated and']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Seasonal launches depend on production readiness. Launch teams conduct go-to-market readiness reviews that require visibility into work order status to confirm inventory availability at launch date. E']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the seasonal launch plan by executive leadership or brand management. Required before launch execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or brand leader who approved the seasonal launch plan and budget.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the seasonal launch plan received final executive approval and was authorized for execution.']
  budget_allocated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total marketing budget allocated for the seasonal launch event across all channels and activities. Includes media spend, event production, PR, and creative costs.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the budget allocated amount (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of distribution and marketing channels activated for this launch (e.g., \'retail,ecommerce,wholesale,social_media,email\'). Defines the omnichannel activation strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the seasonal launch record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  creative_concept varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the creative concept, visual direction, and storytelling approach for the launch campaign and brand assets.']
  dtc_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the launch includes direct-to-consumer channels (owned retail stores, brand e-commerce site). True if DTC is part of the channel mix.']
  ecommerce_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the launch includes e-commerce digital storefront activation. True if online sales channels are activated.']
  embargo_date date [note: 'Date and time before which press, influencers, and media partners are restricted from publishing content about the collection. Critical for coordinated PR strategy.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the seasonal launch campaign and promotional activities officially conclude. May differ from collection availability end date.']
  influencer_activation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether influencer partnerships and content creator collaborations are part of the launch strategy. True if influencer marketing is activated.']
  landing_page_url varchar [note: 'URL of the dedicated e-commerce or marketing landing page for the seasonal launch. Primary digital destination for customer acquisition and conversion.']
  launch_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the seasonal launch event, used across marketing, merchandising, and e-commerce systems for cross-functional reference.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Official public launch date when the collection becomes available or is revealed to the target audience. Primary business event timestamp for the launch.']
  launch_name varchar [note: 'Marketing name of the seasonal launch event (e.g., \'Spring Summer 2025 Runway Show\', \'Fall Winter 2024 Digital Drop\').']
  launch_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the seasonal launch event in the go-to-market workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planning|approved|in_production|ready|live|completed|cancelled|postponed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  launch_theme varchar [note: 'Creative theme or narrative concept for the seasonal launch (e.g., \'Urban Explorer\', \'Coastal Minimalism\', \'Heritage Reimagined\'). Guides creative execution across all touchpoints.']
  launch_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the launch event format and go-to-market activation strategy. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: runway_show|lookbook_drop|digital_launch|pop_up_event|wholesale_preview|influencer_preview|press_preview — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  launch_video_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the hero video content for the seasonal launch (runway show recording, campaign film, behind-the-scenes content). Hosted in DAM or video platform.']
  lookbook_url varchar [note: 'URL link to the digital lookbook or collection catalog published for the seasonal launch. Hosted in the Digital Asset Management (DAM) system or brand website.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the seasonal launch record was last updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, special instructions, or operational notes related to the seasonal launch event.']
  pr_strategy varchar [note: 'Primary public relations and media strategy employed for the launch event to generate brand awareness and editorial coverage.']
  pre_launch_date date [note: 'Date for exclusive early access events such as VIP previews, influencer unboxings, or wholesale partner previews before the public launch.']
  primary_channel varchar [note: 'Lead distribution channel for the launch event. The primary go-to-market route for collection availability.']
  primary_market varchar [note: 'Primary geographic market for the launch event (e.g., \'USA\', \'GBR\', \'FRA\'). The lead market driving launch strategy and timing.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Standard season identifier following industry convention (e.g., SS25 for Spring/Summer 2025, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024, AW24 for Autumn/Winter 2024).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record from which this seasonal launch data originated (e.g., \'Adobe Experience Platform\', \'Anaplan\', \'Salesforce Marketing Cloud\').']
  target_conversions varchar [note: 'Target number of customer conversions (purchases, sign-ups, registrations) expected from the seasonal launch campaign.']
  target_impressions varchar [note: 'Target number of media impressions (views, reach) planned for the seasonal launch campaign across all paid and owned channels.']
  target_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic markets and regions targeted for this launch (e.g., \'USA,CAN,GBR,FRA,DEU,JPN\'). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes.']
  wholesale_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the launch includes wholesale distribution partners (department stores, multi-brand retailers). True if wholesale is part of the channel mix.']

  Note: 'Master record for seasonal collection launch events and go-to-market activations. Captures season name (e.g., SS25, FW24), launch type (runway show, lookbook drop, digital launch, pop-up, wholesale preview), launch date, target markets, associated collection reference, launch channel mix, PR strategy, embargo date, and launch status. Coordinates cross-functional alignment between marketing, merchandising, and e-commerce for collection launches.'
}

Table marketing.affiliate_partner {
  affiliate_partner_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the affiliate partner record. Primary key.']
  affiliate_network varchar [note: 'Name of the affiliate network or platform through which the partner relationship is managed (e.g., CJ Affiliate, Rakuten, ShareASale, Impact).']
  brand_bidding_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affiliate partner is permitted to bid on brand keywords in paid search campaigns.']
  commission_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for commission payments.']
  commission_flat_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed commission amount per conversion for flat-fee commission structures.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission rate as a percentage of attributed revenue for percentage-based commission structures.']
  commission_structure varchar [note: 'Type of commission model applied to the affiliate partner (percentage of sale, flat fee per conversion, Cost Per Acquisition, Cost Per Click, tiered rates, or hybrid).']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Storage location URL for the signed affiliate partnership agreement document.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the affiliate partnership agreement expires or was terminated. Null for open-ended agreements.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the affiliate partnership agreement became effective.']
  cookie_window_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after a click during which a conversion can be attributed to this affiliate partner.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the affiliate partner record was first created in the system.']
  deep_linking_enabled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affiliate partner is authorized to create deep links directly to product pages.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the affiliate partner has an exclusive partnership agreement preventing promotion of competitor brands.']
  fraud_risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed fraud risk level for this affiliate partner based on traffic quality, conversion patterns, and compliance history.']
  last_conversion_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent conversion attributed to this affiliate partner.']
  last_payout_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent commission payout to this affiliate partner.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the affiliate partner record was most recently modified.']
  minimum_payout_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum accumulated commission amount required before payout is processed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context, special terms, or operational details about the affiliate partner relationship.']
  partner_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier or code assigned to the affiliate partner for tracking and reporting purposes.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'Legal or business name of the affiliate partner organization or individual.']
  partner_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the affiliate partner relationship.']
  partner_tier varchar [note: 'Performance-based tier classification of the affiliate partner determining commission rates and benefits.']
  partner_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the affiliate partner by business model and channel type.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Preferred payment method for commission disbursements to the affiliate partner.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment schedule and terms for commission payouts (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, monthly on 15th).']
  performance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite performance score (0-100) evaluating the affiliate partner based on conversion rate, revenue quality, fraud risk, and compliance.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact for partnership communications and commission notifications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary business contact at the affiliate partner organization.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary business contact at the affiliate partner organization.']
  product_categories varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product categories the affiliate partner is authorized to promote (e.g., mens_apparel, womens_footwear, accessories).']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Unique promotional discount code assigned to the affiliate partner for customer use and attribution tracking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this affiliate partner record originated (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform, affiliate network API).']
  target_markets varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of geographic markets or regions the affiliate partner operates in (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU, FRA).']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Tax identification number (EIN, VAT, or equivalent) for the affiliate partner organization for tax reporting and compliance.']
  total_attributed_revenue decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative revenue attributed to this affiliate partner across all conversions since partnership inception.']
  total_commission_paid decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cumulative commission amount paid to this affiliate partner since partnership inception.']
  total_conversions_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative number of conversions attributed to this affiliate partner since partnership inception.']
  tracking_domain varchar [note: 'Domain or subdomain used for affiliate tracking links and conversion attribution.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Primary website URL of the affiliate partner where promotional content is published.']

  Note: 'Master and transactional record for affiliate and performance marketing partners including cashback sites, comparison engines, fashion bloggers, and affiliate networks. Captures partner profile (name, type, network, commission structure, rates, cookie window, payout terms, categories, markets, tier, status) and conversion events (click timestamp, conversion timestamp, sub-affiliate ID, order reference, commission amount, commission status, attributed revenue, product category, channel, cookie match type). SSOT for affiliate channel management, ROI measurement, CAC optimization, and commission reconciliation.'
}

Table marketing.affiliate_conversion {
  affiliate_conversion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the affiliate conversion event. Primary key for the affiliate conversion record.']
  affiliate_partner_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the affiliate partner who drove this conversion event.']
  ar_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ar_invoice. Business justification: Affiliate conversions generate orders that become AR invoices. Link required for revenue recognition, commission calculation reconciliation, and understanding affiliate channel contribution to net rev']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this affiliate conversion.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer who completed the conversion action.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the order generated from this affiliate conversion, if applicable.']
  sub_affiliate_affiliate_partner_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the sub-affiliate or publisher within the affiliate partner network who generated the conversion.']
  affiliate_network_name varchar [note: 'Name of the affiliate network platform managing this conversion (e.g., Commission Junction, ShareASale, Rakuten).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the conversion was approved for commission payment after validation period.']
  attributed_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue amount attributed to this affiliate conversion, representing the order value or lead value generated.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Type of affiliate marketing channel used: content site, coupon site, loyalty program, email marketing, social media, search marketing, display advertising, or price comparison site. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: content|coupon|loyalty|email|social|search|display|comparison — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  click_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the customer clicked the affiliate link, initiating the conversion journey.']
  commission_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Commission amount owed or paid to the affiliate partner for this conversion based on agreed commission structure.']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to calculate the commission amount for this conversion.']
  conversion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the conversion in the commission lifecycle: pending validation, approved for payment, paid to affiliate, reversed due to return or fraud, rejected, or cancelled.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the conversion action was completed (purchase, lead submission, signup).']
  conversion_type varchar [note: 'Type of conversion event tracked: click, lead generation, completed sale, customer signup, content download, or add to cart action.']
  cookie_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the affiliate tracking cookie was valid for this conversion, determining the attribution window.']
  cookie_match_type varchar [note: 'Attribution model used to credit this conversion: first-click, last-click, linear, time-decay, or position-based attribution.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this affiliate conversion record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the attributed revenue and commission amounts.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used by the customer when completing the conversion: desktop, mobile web, tablet, or mobile app.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this conversion was flagged as potentially fraudulent and requires manual review or has been rejected.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Fraud risk score assigned to this conversion based on fraud detection algorithms, ranging from 0 (low risk) to 100 (high risk).']
  geo_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the geographic location of the customer at conversion time.']
  geo_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region or state where the conversion occurred, used for regional affiliate performance analysis.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this affiliate conversion record was last modified, tracking status changes and updates.']
  network_transaction_reference varchar [note: 'Unique transaction identifier assigned by the affiliate network platform for reconciliation and reporting.']
  new_customer_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this conversion represents a new customer acquisition (true) or a repeat customer purchase (false).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding this affiliate conversion, including special circumstances or manual adjustments.']
  payment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the commission was paid to the affiliate partner.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category of the items purchased in this conversion, used for category-level affiliate performance analysis.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the primary product purchased in this conversion, if applicable.']
  promo_code varchar [note: 'Promotional or coupon code used by the customer during this conversion, often unique to the affiliate partner.']
  reversal_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for conversion reversal if status is reversed: order cancelled, product returned, payment failed, or fraud detected.']
  reversal_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the conversion was reversed due to order cancellation, return, or fraud detection.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or platform that generated this conversion record (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform, affiliate network API).']
  tracking_code varchar [note: 'Unique tracking code or parameter appended to the affiliate link to identify the source and enable conversion tracking.']
  traffic_source varchar [note: 'Specific traffic source or placement within the affiliate channel (e.g., blog post URL, email campaign name, social post ID).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each affiliate-driven conversion event including clicks, leads, and sales. Captures click timestamp, conversion timestamp, affiliate partner reference, sub-affiliate ID, order reference, commission amount, commission status (pending, approved, paid, reversed), attributed revenue, product category, channel, and cookie match type. Enables affiliate ROI measurement and commission reconciliation.'
}

Table marketing.pr_placement {
  pr_placement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the PR placement record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this PR placement.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: PR placements have associated costs (agency fees, media value tracking) that must be charged to cost centers for PR budget management, expense tracking, and ROI analysis in apparel fashion communicati']
  internal_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: PR placements managed by internal PR team members require employee tracking for relationship management with journalists, workload balancing, and performance metrics on media value secured per team me']
  quality_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to quality.quality_certification. Business justification: PR placements making sustainability or compliance claims must reference valid certifications for legal compliance and fact-checking. PR teams verify certification validity before issuing press release']
  article_title varchar [note: 'Title of the article, segment, or content piece featuring the brand.']
  article_url varchar [note: 'Web link to the published article or content (if available online).']
  attributed_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue amount directly attributed to this PR placement through conversion tracking and attribution modeling.']
  attributed_traffic varchar [note: 'Number of website visits or traffic attributed to this PR placement through tracking links or referral analysis.']
  brand_prominence varchar [note: 'Level of brand visibility and prominence within the placement (primary focus, secondary mention, tertiary reference).']
  collection_featured varchar [note: 'Brand collection or seasonal line highlighted in the placement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PR placement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the media value amount.']
  estimated_impressions varchar [note: 'Estimated total impressions generated by the PR placement across all distribution channels.']
  estimated_reach varchar [note: 'Estimated number of people who were exposed to the PR placement based on publication circulation or audience size.']
  image_count varchar [note: 'Number of brand or product images included in the placement.']
  journalist_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the journalist or editor for follow-up and relationship management.']
  journalist_name varchar [note: 'Name of the journalist, editor, or content creator who authored or produced the placement.']
  key_messages varchar [note: 'Primary brand messages, themes, or talking points communicated in the placement.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this PR placement record was last modified.']
  market_geography varchar [note: 'Primary market or geographic region where the placement was published, using three-letter country code.']
  media_type varchar [note: 'Type of media channel for the placement (print, online, broadcast, podcast, social media, blog).']
  media_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated advertising value equivalency (AVE) of the earned media placement, representing what it would have cost to purchase equivalent advertising space.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, context, or observations about the PR placement.']
  pitch_date date [note: 'Date when the story or placement was initially pitched to the journalist or publication.']
  placement_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or tracking code for the PR placement.']
  placement_date date [note: 'Date when the PR placement was published or aired.']
  placement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the PR placement.']
  placement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the placement format (feature story, brand mention, product review, interview, editorial, sponsored content).']
  pr_agency_contact varchar [note: 'Primary contact person at the PR agency responsible for this placement.']
  pr_agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the external PR agency that secured or facilitated the placement.']
  product_featured varchar [note: 'Specific product, SKU, or collection featured in the PR placement.']
  publication_name varchar [note: 'Name of the media outlet, publication, or platform where the placement appeared.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier associated with the featured product or collection (e.g., SS24, FW24).']
  secondary_markets varchar [note: 'Additional markets or geographies where the placement had distribution or reach, comma-separated country codes.']
  secured_date date [note: 'Date when the PR placement was confirmed or secured with the publication.']
  sentiment varchar [note: 'Overall sentiment tone of the PR placement toward the brand (positive, neutral, negative, mixed).']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative sentiment score (typically -100 to +100 scale) measuring the tone and favorability of the placement.']
  share_of_voice_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total media coverage or mentions captured by the brand relative to competitors in this placement context.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system or platform from which this PR placement data originated (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform, PR management tool).']
  spokesperson_name varchar [note: 'Name of the brand spokesperson, executive, or representative quoted or featured in the placement.']
  video_included_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether video content was included in the placement.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of earned media and PR placements secured for the brand. Captures publication name, media type (print, online, broadcast, podcast), placement date, article/segment title, journalist/editor name, product or collection featured, estimated reach, media value (AVE), sentiment (positive/neutral/negative), market/geography, PR agency reference, and campaign association. Tracks earned media ROI and brand visibility.'
}

Table marketing.event {
  event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing event record. Primary key.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the brand hosting or featured in the event. Links to brand master data for multi-brand portfolio companies.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Events have planned and actual budgets (budget_planned_amount, budget_actual_amount fields) that must link to formal budget records for approval, allocation tracking, and variance reporting in event m']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent marketing campaign this event supports. Links event performance to broader campaign objectives and budget.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.collection. Business justification: Events showcase collections (fashion shows, trunk shows, pop-ups). Essential for event planning, inventory allocation, and experiential marketing attribution. Replaces denormalized collection_name wit']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: Fashion events (runway shows, trunk shows, pop-ups) showcase hero concepts; event planning, styling, press kits, and ROI analysis require concept attribution to measure design impact and customer resp']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Events incur costs (venue, production, sponsorship) tracked to cost centers for expense management, budget variance analysis, and ROI calculation. Standard practice for managing event marketing spend ']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fashion events (runway shows, trunk shows, pop-ups) require dedicated employee coordination for venue, logistics, guest lists, and budget management. Essential for event portfolio planning and staff p']
  reserved_stock_position_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_position. Business justification: Fashion events (trunk shows, pop-ups, fashion weeks) require specific inventory allocation and reservation. Real process: event planning teams reserve stock positions for event merchandise, tracking w']
  sample_request_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sample_request. Business justification: Fashion events (trunk shows, buyer meetings, runway shows) require physical samples. Event planning depends on sample request status, delivery dates, and approval timelines to ensure product availabil']
  seasonal_launch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.seasonal_launch. Business justification: event has season_code (STRING) and collection_name (STRING) fields. Marketing events (runway shows, pop-up activations, launch parties) are often part of seasonal collection launches. While event alre']
  social_impact_program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.social_impact_program. Business justification: Brand events (fashion shows, pop-ups, community activations) frequently partner with social impact programs (artisan cooperatives, workforce development, community investment) for CSR storytelling, st']
  target_customer_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.segment. Business justification: Events target specific customer segments (VIP events, loyalty member exclusives, new customer welcomes). Enables segment-based invitation lists, attendance tracking by segment, and segment-specific ev']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Brand events (factory tours, sustainability showcases, craftsmanship demonstrations) feature specific vendors. Business need: event planning for transparency initiatives, supplier summits, factory sho']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_order. Business justification: Fashion events (runway shows, pop-ups, trunk shows) often feature exclusive or preview styles requiring dedicated production runs. Event merchandise planning and sample production coordination depend ']
  actual_attendance varchar [note: 'Confirmed number of attendees who participated in the event. Captured post-event for performance analysis and ROI calculation.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the event proposal. Events must be approved before budget allocation and execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or manager who approved the event proposal and budget.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when the event proposal was formally approved. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  attributed_order_count varchar [note: 'Number of customer or wholesale orders directly attributed to the event through tracking mechanisms.']
  attributed_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue directly attributed to the event through tracked conversions, promo codes, or wholesale orders placed during/after the event.']
  budget_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual expenditure for the event after completion. Used for variance analysis and future event budget calibration.']
  budget_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all budget amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, JPY).']
  budget_planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total planned budget allocated for the event during initial planning phase. Includes venue, production, staffing, marketing, and contingency costs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when the event concludes. For single-day events, matches start_date. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the event officially concludes, including time zone. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the event, used for external reference and tracking across systems. Typically alphanumeric code assigned during event planning.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the marketing event (e.g., Spring 2024 Runway Show, NYC Pop-Up Launch, Paris Fashion Week Press Day).']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the event. Tracks progression from planning through execution to completion.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the marketing event format and purpose. Determines audience, venue requirements, and success metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: runway_show|pop_up_activation|trade_show|press_day|brand_experience|wholesale_preview|influencer_event|product_launch|store_opening|virtual_event|fashion_week|trunk_show — 12 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expected_attendance varchar [note: 'Projected number of attendees based on invitations sent and historical response rates. Used for capacity planning and budget forecasting.']
  is_virtual_event boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the event is conducted entirely online/virtually (True) or has a physical venue component (False).']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the event record was last modified. Tracks data freshness and audit trail. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  media_impressions varchar [note: 'Total estimated media impressions generated by the event across press coverage, social media, and digital channels. Captured post-event.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional event details, special requirements, lessons learned, or post-event observations.']
  nps_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Net Promoter Score collected from event attendees post-event. Measures attendee satisfaction and likelihood to recommend. Scale: -100 to +100.']
  press_coverage_count varchar [note: 'Number of press articles, blog posts, or media mentions generated by the event. Indicates earned media success.']
  registration_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individuals who registered or RSVP\'d for the event. Used to calculate attendance conversion rate.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Fashion season or collection code associated with the event (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024). Links event to merchandising calendar.']
  social_engagement_count varchar [note: 'Total social media engagements (likes, shares, comments, mentions) attributed to the event across all platforms. Measured during and post-event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which this event record originated (e.g., Adobe Experience Platform, Salesforce Marketing Cloud, custom event management system).']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary external sponsor or partner co-hosting the event. Null if no external sponsorship.']
  sponsorship_tier varchar [note: 'Level of external sponsorship secured for the event. Impacts budget offset and co-branding requirements.']
  sponsorship_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of sponsorship contribution or in-kind services provided by external sponsor. Reduces net event cost.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when the event begins. For multi-day events, this is the first day. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the event officially begins, including time zone. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary audience segment the event is designed to engage. Determines invitation strategy, content, and success metrics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: press|buyers|consumers|vips|influencers|trade|internal|mixed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  venue_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the event venue. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  venue_city varchar [note: 'City where the event venue is located. Used for market segmentation and regional performance analysis.']
  venue_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the event venue location (e.g., USA, FRA, GBR, ITA, JPN).']
  venue_name varchar [note: 'Name of the physical or virtual venue where the event takes place (e.g., Javits Center, Brand Flagship Store, Zoom Virtual Studio).']
  venue_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the event venue. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  venue_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province of the event venue. Relevant for regional compliance and logistics planning.']
  virtual_platform_name varchar [note: 'Name of the digital platform used for virtual events (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, custom branded platform). Null for physical-only events.']

  Note: 'Master record for physical and virtual marketing events including runway shows, pop-up activations, trade shows, press days, brand experiences, and wholesale previews. Captures event name, event type, venue, city/market, event date(s), target audience (press, buyers, consumers, VIPs), expected attendance, actual attendance, budget, associated season/collection, sponsorship details, and event status. Coordinates brand experience activations across markets.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_flight {
  campaign_flight_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this campaign flight execution. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the parent marketing campaign being executed']
  digital_storefront_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the digital storefront where this campaign flight is executed']
  budget_allocated_to_storefront decimal(18,2) [note: 'Marketing budget allocated specifically to this campaign flight for this storefront/market. Subset of the parent campaign\'s total budget.']
  cac_actual decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual Customer Acquisition Cost for this campaign flight on this storefront, calculated as budget spent divided by new customers acquired.']
  clicks_delivered varchar [note: 'Actual number of clicks delivered for this campaign flight on this storefront. Performance metric.']
  conversions_delivered varchar [note: 'Actual number of conversions (purchases, sign-ups) attributed to this campaign flight on this storefront. Performance metric.']
  flight_end_date date [note: 'Date when this campaign flight concludes on this specific storefront. May differ from parent campaign end_date due to market-specific timing.']
  flight_start_date date [note: 'Date when this campaign flight becomes active on this specific storefront. May differ from parent campaign start_date due to market-specific timing.']
  flight_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this campaign flight on this storefront.']
  impressions_delivered varchar [note: 'Actual number of impressions delivered for this campaign flight on this storefront. Performance metric.']
  landing_page_url_override varchar [note: 'Storefront-specific landing page URL override. If null, uses the parent campaign\'s landing_page_url.']
  revenue_attributed decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue attributed to this campaign flight on this storefront using the campaign attribution model.']
  storefront_specific_creative varchar [note: 'Reference to the creative asset variant or localized creative used for this storefront (e.g., language-specific imagery, regional messaging, cultural adaptation).']
  traffic_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of storefront traffic targeted or allocated to this campaign flight (used for A/B testing, personalization targeting, or media buy planning).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the execution of a marketing campaign on a specific digital storefront. It captures the market-specific deployment of campaigns including budget allocation, flight timing, creative adaptation, and performance tracking. Each record links one campaign to one storefront with attributes that exist only in the context of this market-specific execution.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion multi-market operations, marketing campaigns are executed across multiple regional storefronts (e.g., US site, UK site, EU site) with market-specific budget allocations, localized creative, and region-specific flight timing. Simultaneously, each storefront runs multiple concurrent campaigns (seasonal launches, brand awareness, promotional campaigns). The business actively manages these campaign flights as operational entities with distinct budgets, performance tracking, and creative variants per storefront-campaign combination.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_initiative_promotion {
  campaign_initiative_promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this campaign-initiative promotion relationship. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign that is promoting the sustainability initiative']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sustainability initiative being promoted in the campaign']
  sustainability_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the sustainability initiative being promoted in this campaign.']
  attributed_impact_metric_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measurement for the attributed impact metric (e.g., \'percentage points\', \'new customers\', \'impressions\', \'engagement rate\').']
  attributed_impact_metric_value decimal(18,3) [note: 'Quantified impact metric attributed to this campaign-initiative pairing, such as incremental brand awareness lift, customer acquisition attributed to sustainability messaging, or engagement metrics specific to this initiative within the campaign.']
  contribution_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the campaign budget or messaging allocation dedicated to promoting this specific sustainability initiative. Used for attribution and ROI analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign-initiative promotion relationship was created in the system.']
  creative_asset_count varchar [note: 'Number of creative assets (images, videos, copy variants) produced specifically featuring this sustainability initiative within the campaign.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when this sustainability initiative ceased being featured in the campaign messaging and creative assets.']
  messaging_theme varchar [note: 'Campaign-specific messaging theme or narrative used to communicate this sustainability initiative to the target audience (e.g., \'Renewable Energy Leadership\', \'Circular Fashion Pioneer\', \'Water Stewardship\').']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this initiative\'s promotion within the campaign lifecycle.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when this sustainability initiative began being featured in the campaign messaging and creative assets.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign-initiative promotion relationship was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the promotional relationship between marketing campaigns and sustainability initiatives. It captures how specific sustainability initiatives are featured, messaged, and attributed within marketing campaigns. Each record links one campaign to one sustainability initiative with campaign-specific messaging themes, contribution allocation percentages, and attributed impact metrics that exist only in the context of this promotional relationship.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion marketing operations, campaigns routinely promote multiple sustainability initiatives simultaneously (e.g., a holiday campaign features circular program, carbon neutrality, and fair trade sourcing), and each sustainability initiative is promoted across multiple campaigns throughout the year (e.g., renewable energy initiative featured in spring launch, back-to-school, and holiday campaigns). Marketing teams actively manage which initiatives are featured in which campaigns, allocate messaging budget percentages, create campaign-specific creative themes, and measure attributed impact metrics for each campaign-initiative pairing.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_certification_usage {
  campaign_certification_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this campaign certification usage record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign that features the certification']
  certification_compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance certification being featured in the campaign']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the certification being featured']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval status for using this certification in this specific campaign. Tracks whether legal/compliance has approved the use of certification marks and claims in campaign materials.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the compliance or legal team member who approved the use of this certification in this campaign.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when approval was granted to use this certification in this campaign.']
  certification_mark_used boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the official certification logo or mark is displayed in campaign materials (true) or only textual claims are made (false).']
  claim_type varchar [note: 'The type of sustainability or compliance claim being made in the campaign using this certification (e.g., organic, fair trade, recycled content, sustainable manufacturing, ethical labor).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign certification usage record was created in the system.']
  featured_products_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product SKUs or product line identifiers that are specifically highlighted as certified in this campaign usage.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this campaign certification usage record was last updated.']
  usage_context varchar [note: 'Free-text description of how and where the certification is featured in the campaign (e.g., \'GOTS certified organic cotton featured in email hero image and product detail pages\', \'Fair Trade logo on social media posts for spring collection\').']
  usage_end_date date [note: 'The date when this certification stopped being featured in this campaign. May differ from campaign end date if certification expires or is removed early.']
  usage_start_date date [note: 'The date when this certification began being featured or promoted in this campaign. May differ from campaign start date if certification is added mid-campaign.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the usage of compliance certifications within marketing campaigns. It captures which certifications are featured in which campaigns, the approval workflow for using each certification in marketing materials, the specific claim context, and the time period during which the certification is actively promoted. Each record links one campaign to one compliance certification with attributes that exist only in the context of this marketing usage relationship.. Existence Justification: In apparel fashion marketing, campaigns routinely feature multiple sustainability and compliance certifications (GOTS organic, Fair Trade, GRS recycled content, etc.) to support brand positioning and consumer trust. Each certification can be featured across multiple campaigns throughout its validity period. The business actively manages which certifications are used in which campaigns, requiring approval workflows, tracking usage periods, and documenting claim contexts for legal compliance and brand consistency.'
}

Table marketing.influencer_seeding {
  influencer_seeding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this influencer seeding record. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the broader marketing campaign this seeding activity supports, if applicable.']
  influencer_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the influencer receiving the seeded products']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the work order from which seeded products are allocated']
  actual_post_date date [note: 'Actual date when the influencer published content featuring the seeded products.']
  content_deadline date [note: 'Target date by which the influencer is expected to create and publish content featuring the seeded products. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  content_post_url varchar [note: 'URL of the content post created by the influencer featuring the seeded products, captured once published.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seeding record was created in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes about this seeding event, special instructions, or follow-up actions.']
  product_return_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the influencer is required to return the seeded products after content creation (true for high-value items, false for gifted items). Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  seeding_date date [note: 'Date when the seeded products were shipped or delivered to the influencer. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  seeding_quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units from this work order allocated and shipped to the influencer for seeding purposes. Explicitly identified in detection phase relationship data.']
  seeding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this seeding event: planned (allocated but not shipped), shipped (in transit), delivered (received by influencer), content_posted (influencer published content), completed (all obligations fulfilled), cancelled.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number for the seeded products sent to the influencer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this seeding record was last updated.']
