  Note: 'This association product represents the operational tracking of quality defects observed at specific vendors in the apparel-fashion supply chain. It captures vendor-specific defect patterns, frequency, cost impact, and corrective action effectiveness for supplier scorecarding and quality improvement programs. Each record links one quality defect type to one vendor with attributes that exist only in the context of this vendor-defect relationship, enabling Pareto analysis, supplier quality rating, and targeted CAPA initiatives.. Existence Justification: In apparel-fashion supply chain operations, quality defects are tracked at the vendor level to enable supplier scorecarding, quality improvement programs, and sourcing decisions. A single defect type (e.g., \'seam puckering\') occurs across multiple vendors with different frequencies and root causes, and each vendor exhibits multiple defect types across their production. The business actively manages vendor-specific defect patterns through CAPA workflows, cost tracking, and recurrence analysis—this is not a derived analytical correlation but an operational quality management process.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_quality_compliance {
  vendor_quality_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this vendor-quality standard compliance record. Primary key.']
  quality_standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to quality_standard identifying which standard applies']
  standard_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the quality standard that applies to this vendor compliance record']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor entity subject to this quality standard compliance']
  audit_frequency_override varchar [note: 'Vendor-specific override of the default audit frequency defined in the quality standard, allowing more frequent audits for high-risk vendors or less frequent for consistently compliant vendors (e.g., Quarterly, Semi-Annual, Annual, Biennial)']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score from the most recent audit of this vendor against this quality standard, typically expressed as a percentage or on a defined scale']
  certification_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendors certification for this quality standard, after which recertification is required']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Official certification or registration number issued by the certifying body for this vendor-standard combination (e.g., GOTS certificate number, WRAP registration ID)']
  certification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this specific vendor must obtain formal third-party certification for this quality standard, which may differ from the standards general certification requirements based on vendor tier, product categories supplied, or target markets served']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party organization that issued the certification to this vendor for this standard (may differ from the standards issuing body)']
  compliance_owner varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the internal quality manager or compliance officer responsible for managing this vendor-standard compliance relationship']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this vendor for this specific quality standard, tracking whether the vendor meets all requirements, has open corrective actions, or is pending certification']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this compliance record was created in the system']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date from which this quality standard became applicable to this specific vendor, which may differ from the standards global effective date based on vendor onboarding, contract terms, or product category changes']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent quality audit conducted for this vendor against this specific quality standard']
  last_updated_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this compliance record']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next quality audit of this vendor for this quality standard, calculated based on audit frequency and last audit date']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting vendor-specific compliance considerations, historical issues, or special requirements for this quality standard']
  open_corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Number of open corrective action requests (CARs) for this vendor related to this specific quality standard']
  waiver_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the granted waiver expires and full compliance is required']
  waiver_granted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a temporary waiver or exception has been granted for this vendor for this quality standard, allowing production despite non-compliance under specific conditions']
  waiver_justification varchar [note: 'Business justification and approval documentation for the granted waiver']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance relationship between quality standards and vendors in the apparel-fashion supply chain. It captures vendor-specific compliance status, audit schedules, certification requirements, and quality performance tracking for each quality standard that applies to each vendor. Each record links one quality standard to one vendor with attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance relationship, enabling tracking of multi-standard compliance per vendor and multi-vendor applicability per standard.. Existence Justification: In apparel-fashion supply chain operations, vendors must comply with multiple quality standards simultaneously (ISO 9001, WRAP, GOTS, OEKO-TEX, brand-specific standards), and each quality standard applies to multiple vendors across the supply base. The business actively manages vendor-specific compliance status, audit schedules, certification tracking, and corrective actions for each standard-vendor combination. This is an operational compliance management process, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_relationship {
  vendor_relationship_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this vendor-employee relationship assignment record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the employee master record. Identifies which internal employee is assigned to work with this vendor.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor master record. Identifies which vendor this relationship assignment is for.']
  assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of this vendor-employee relationship assignment. Active assignments are currently in effect. Inactive assignments have ended. Suspended assignments are temporarily paused. Pending assignments are scheduled to begin in the future.']
  authorization_level varchar [note: 'The level of authority or access this employee has in their interactions with this vendor. Determines what actions the employee can take (e.g., place orders, approve samples, negotiate terms, access confidential data). Examples: View Only (read-only access), Standard (routine operations), Elevated (can approve exceptions), Full Authorization (can modify vendor terms).']
  created_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor relationship assignment record was created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this employee-vendor relationship assignment ended or is scheduled to end. Nullable for currently active relationships. Used to track relationship lifecycle and historical changes in vendor management assignments.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this employee-vendor relationship assignment became active. Used to track historical assignments and determine current vs. past relationships.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of who last modified this vendor relationship assignment record.']
  last_modified_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor relationship assignment record was last updated.']
  primary_contact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this employee is the primary point of contact for this vendor in this role type. Used to identify the main responsible party when multiple employees have relationships with the same vendor. Only one employee per role_type should be marked as primary for a given vendor at any time.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'The functional role or capacity in which the employee interacts with the vendor. Determines the nature of the working relationship and authorization scope. Examples: Buyer (commercial negotiations), Quality Manager (QA/QC oversight), Compliance Auditor (social/environmental compliance), Technical Designer (product development), Costing Analyst (pricing analysis).']
  specialization_area varchar [note: 'The product category, material type, or functional area that defines the scope of this employee-vendor relationship. Examples: Mens Outerwear, Womens Denim, Knit Fabrics, Trims & Accessories, Footwear. Allows employees to specialize in specific vendor relationships by product line or material expertise.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier of who created this vendor relationship assignment record.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the operational assignment between internal employees and external vendors in the apparel-fashion supply chain. It captures which employees are authorized to interact with which vendors, in what capacity, and for what time period. Each record links one vendor to one employee with role-specific attributes that exist only in the context of this working relationship. This is distinct from vendor_contact (which tracks external vendor personnel) and represents internal employee-to-vendor assignments for sourcing, quality, compliance, and technical collaboration.. Existence Justification: In apparel-fashion sourcing operations, vendors are managed by multiple internal employees in different functional capacities simultaneously (a buyer negotiates commercial terms, a quality manager oversees QA, a compliance auditor conducts social audits, a technical designer collaborates on product development). Each employee manages a portfolio of multiple vendors, often specialized by product category or material type. The business actively manages these assignments with role definitions, authorization levels, and time-bound effective periods.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_certification {
  vendor_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this vendor certification holding record. Primary key.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the compliance certification standard held by the vendor']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor entity that holds this certification']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate number issued to this vendor for this certification. Vendor-specific certificate identifier.']
  certification_scope varchar [note: 'The scope at which this vendor holds this specific certification (e.g., facility-level WRAP, organization-level ISO 9001, product-line-level GOTS). Scope varies by vendor-certification combination.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this vendor\'s certification holding. Tracks lifecycle state for this specific vendor-certification combination.']
  certified_facility_location varchar [note: 'The specific facility or site location covered by this vendor certification, if the scope is facility-level. May differ from vendor headquarters.']
  cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cost incurred by this vendor (or paid by Apparel Fashion on behalf of the vendor) to obtain or renew this specific certification. Cost varies by vendor, certification type, and scope.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the certification cost amount.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor certification record was created in the system.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this vendor\'s holding of this certification became effective. May differ from the certification\'s original issue date if the vendor obtained it later.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date on which this vendor\'s certification expires and must be renewed. Vendor-specific expiry tracking for sourcing eligibility.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next surveillance or renewal audit for this vendor\'s certification. Used for compliance calendar management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context about this vendor\'s certification holding, such as special conditions, audit findings, or renewal negotiations.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'The date on which this vendor renewed this certification. Tracks renewal history for this vendor-certification relationship.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'The name of the individual or team at Apparel Fashion responsible for managing this vendor\'s certification relationship and renewal tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor certification record was last updated.']

  Note: 'This association product represents the certification holdings of vendors in the apparel-fashion supply chain. It captures which certifications each vendor holds, the scope of certification at the vendor level, validity periods, renewal schedules, and costs. Each record links one vendor to one compliance certification with attributes that exist only in the context of this vendor-specific certification holding.. Existence Justification: In apparel-fashion supply chain operations, vendors hold multiple certifications (WRAP, BSCI, GOTS, ISO 9001, bluesign, etc.) simultaneously, and each certification standard is held by multiple vendors across the supply base. The business actively manages vendor-specific certification holdings with distinct effective dates, expiry dates, renewal schedules, costs, certificate numbers, and scope definitions (facility-level vs organization-level). Sourcing teams query which vendors hold which active certifications to determine order placement eligibility and compliance risk.'
}

Table supplier.vendor_substance_compliance {
  vendor_substance_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for this vendor-substance compliance record. Primary key.']
  restricted_substance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the restricted substance being tested for in vendor materials']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the vendor being tested for substance compliance']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for compliance team notes specific to this vendor-substance relationship, including corrective actions, special conditions, or monitoring requirements']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this vendor for this specific restricted substance based on test results and regulatory requirements']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the measured_concentration value']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor-substance compliance record was first created in the system']
  exemption_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor has been granted an exemption or derogation for this specific substance based on material type, product category, or regulatory conditions']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this vendor-substance compliance monitoring relationship is currently active and requires ongoing testing']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor-substance compliance record was last updated']
  last_test_date date [note: 'Date when this vendor was last tested for this specific restricted substance']
  measured_concentration decimal(12,6) [note: 'Actual measured concentration of the restricted substance found in the vendor\'s materials during the most recent test']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the compliance team member who last modified this record']
  next_test_due_date date [note: 'Calculated date when the next compliance test is due for this vendor-substance combination based on test_frequency']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk assessment level for this vendor-substance combination based on material type, product category, and historical test results']
  test_frequency varchar [note: 'Required frequency of testing for this vendor-substance combination based on risk assessment and contractual agreements']
  test_method varchar [note: 'Specific test method or protocol used by this vendor to detect and quantify this substance (may differ from standard test_method_standard in restricted_substance based on vendor capabilities and agreements)']
  test_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document ID for the most recent test report for this vendor-substance combination']
  test_result varchar [note: 'Most recent test result for this vendor-substance combination indicating whether the vendor\'s materials comply with threshold limits']
  testing_lab_name varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party testing laboratory used by this vendor for this substance testing']

  Note: 'This association product represents the compliance testing and monitoring relationship between vendors and restricted substances in the apparel-fashion supply chain. It captures vendor-specific testing protocols, test results, and compliance status for chemical management and product safety clearance. Each record links one vendor to one restricted substance with attributes that exist only in the context of this compliance relationship, enabling tracking of which vendors have been tested for which substances, when, and with what results.. Existence Justification: In apparel-fashion supply chain operations, vendors must comply with multiple restricted substance regulations (REACH, Prop 65, CPSIA), and each restricted substance applies to multiple vendors. The business actively manages vendor-specific testing protocols, test schedules, test results, and compliance status for each vendor-substance combination as part of chemical management and product safety clearance processes. This is an operational compliance monitoring relationship, not an analytical correlation.'
}

Table sourcing.rfq {
  rfq_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the request for quotation record. Primary key.']
  awarded_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or mill that was selected and awarded the business as a result of this RFQ evaluation.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product collection or line this RFQ supports.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: RFQs are issued to source materials/production for approved design concepts. Enables concept-to-sourcing traceability, cost validation against design targets, and sourcing performance reporting by con']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: RFQs are created and owned by sourcing employees. Tracks accountability for RFQ issuance, vendor selection decisions, and award approvals. Essential for sourcing performance metrics and audit trails i']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: RFQs specify compliance obligations (labor standards, sustainability certifications, chemical restrictions) that vendors must meet. Buyers use this to enforce compliance requirements in sourcing decis']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: RFQs specify target sustainable materials for new product development. Buyers reference sustainable material specifications when issuing RFQs to ensure sustainability requirements are met. Critical fo']
  award_date date [note: 'The date when the RFQ was awarded to the selected vendor.']
  awarded_quote_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The final quoted unit price from the awarded vendor.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RFQ was formally closed, either through award or cancellation.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Any regulatory, labor, or ethical compliance requirements that vendors must meet (e.g., WRAP certification, FLA compliance, CPSC safety standards).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RFQ record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this RFQ (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  destination_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the destination country or primary market for the goods.']
  estimated_volume varchar [note: 'The anticipated total order volume or quantity that may be placed if the RFQ results in an award.']
  fabric_composition varchar [note: 'The fiber content and blend percentages for fabric RFQs (e.g., 80% Cotton 20% Polyester).']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System tariff classification code for the goods being sourced, used for customs and duty calculation.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms defining the responsibilities, costs, and risks between buyer and seller (e.g., FOB, CIF, EXW). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: exw|fca|cpt|cip|dat|dap|ddp|fas|fob|cfr|cif — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date when the RFQ was formally issued and sent to vendors or mills.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The requested or expected production and delivery lead time in days from order placement to delivery.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'The type or category of material being sourced through this RFQ (e.g., cotton fabric, polyester blend, leather, zipper, button).']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this RFQ record was last modified or updated.']
  moq_quantity varchar [note: 'The minimum order quantity being requested or negotiated as part of this RFQ.']
  moq_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the MOQ (e.g., pieces, yards, meters, kilograms).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, special instructions, or comments related to this RFQ that vendors should be aware of.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms and conditions specified in the RFQ (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Letter of Credit, 50% deposit).']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product category classification for the items covered by this RFQ.']
  quality_standard varchar [note: 'The quality specifications, testing standards, or acceptance criteria that vendors must meet (e.g., AQL 2.5, ISO 9001, specific performance requirements).']
  quote_received_count varchar [note: 'The number of vendor quotations received in response to this RFQ.']
  rfq_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the RFQ, used in vendor communications and sourcing documentation.']
  rfq_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RFQ: draft (being prepared), issued (sent to vendors), under evaluation (quotes received and being reviewed), awarded (vendor selected), closed (process complete), cancelled (RFQ withdrawn).']
  rfq_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the RFQ based on sourcing scope: CMT (Cut Make Trim), FOB (Free on Board), material procurement, trim sourcing, fabric sourcing, or full package manufacturing.']
  sample_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether vendors are required to submit physical samples as part of their quotation response.']
  sample_submission_deadline date [note: 'The deadline by which vendors must submit physical samples if required.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The fashion season or collection period this RFQ is associated with (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024).']
  shipping_origin_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the country from which goods will be shipped (e.g., CHN for China, VNM for Vietnam, IND for India).']
  sourcing_region varchar [note: 'The geographic region being targeted for sourcing through this RFQ.']
  style_reference varchar [note: 'The style number or design reference code that this RFQ is requesting quotes for.']
  submission_deadline date [note: 'The final date by which vendors must submit their quotations in response to this RFQ.']
  sustainability_requirement varchar [note: 'Any sustainability or certification requirements specified in the RFQ (e.g., GOTS certified, BCI cotton, OEKO-TEX Standard 100, recycled materials, organic).']
  target_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The desired or budgeted unit cost that the business is aiming to achieve through this RFQ process.']
  technical_pack_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the technical specification document or tech pack that provides detailed construction, measurement, and material specifications for the RFQ.']
  vendor_count varchar [note: 'The number of vendors or mills to whom this RFQ has been issued.']

  Note: 'Request for Quotation master record capturing the formal solicitation sent to vendors or mills for CMT, FOB, or material pricing. Tracks RFQ number, season, style references, target cost, submission deadline, currency, incoterms, and lifecycle status (draft, issued, under evaluation, awarded, closed). SSOT for all sourcing quote requests.'
}

Table sourcing.rfq_line {
  rfq_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the RFQ line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent RFQ header document. Links this line item to the overall RFQ request.']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_sketch. Business justification: RFQ lines reference specific design sketches when sourcing materials/production for that design. Enables design-to-sourcing handoff tracking, ensures vendor quotes align with design specifications, su']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.style. Business justification: RFQ lines quote specific styles for vendor cost estimation. Critical for sourcing decisions, vendor selection, and cost comparison. Apparel buyers always link RFQ line items to style master for accura']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Individual RFQ lines specify sustainable material requirements at the style/colorway level. Enables line-level sustainable material specification and tracking for product development. Essential for ma']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Internal approval workflow status for this RFQ line. Tracks progression through sourcing, merchandising, and finance approval gates.']
  bom_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the Bill of Materials document listing all components, fabrics, and trims required for this style.']
  buyer_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes from the buyer or sourcing team regarding special requirements, constraints, or context for this line item.']
  colorway_code varchar [note: 'Color variant code for the style being quoted. Represents specific color combination or finish.']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all cost values on this line. Typically USD, EUR, CNY, or other manufacturing region currencies.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Expected country of manufacture for this line item. Impacts duty rates, trade agreements (GSP), and compliance labeling requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this RFQ line item was first created in the system. Audit trail for sourcing process tracking.']
  gender_classification varchar [note: 'Target gender demographic for the product. Used for merchandising planning and assortment allocation.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System tariff classification code for customs and duty calculation. Required for international trade compliance.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Incoterms code defining the delivery terms and transfer of risk between buyer and vendor. Most common in apparel is FOB (Free on Board). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FOB|CIF|DDP|FCA|CPT|DAP|DPU — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Requested production lead time in days from order placement to ex-factory date. Critical for Time and Action (TNA) calendar planning.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the RFQ document. Used for ordering and referencing specific line items in vendor communications.']
  material_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the fabric, trim, or material being sourced. Includes composition, weight, finish, and construction details.']
  material_type varchar [note: 'Category of material being quoted. Classifies the component type for sourcing and costing purposes. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fabric|trim|accessory|packaging|label|thread|zipper|button — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to this RFQ line. Tracks changes during negotiation and vendor response cycles.']
  moq_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum order quantity requested from vendors for this line item. Critical for vendor capability assessment and cost negotiation.']
  packaging_requirement varchar [note: 'Packaging specifications for this line item. Includes polybag requirements, hangtags, labels, carton specifications, and shipping marks.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'Expected port of origin for shipment. Critical for logistics planning and landed cost calculation.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level for this RFQ line. Critical items require expedited vendor response and faster decision cycles.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product category classification for merchandising and sourcing segmentation. Examples: tops, bottoms, outerwear, footwear, accessories.']
  quality_standard varchar [note: 'Quality and compliance standards required for this line item. May reference OEKO-TEX, GOTS, BCI, or internal quality specifications.']
  quantity_break_tier_1 varchar [note: 'First quantity threshold for tiered pricing. Vendors provide different unit costs based on volume commitments.']
  quantity_break_tier_2 varchar [note: 'Second quantity threshold for tiered pricing. Enables cost comparison across different volume scenarios.']
  quantity_break_tier_3 varchar [note: 'Third quantity threshold for tiered pricing. Supports complex volume-based sourcing strategies.']
  requested_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity being requested in the RFQ for this line item. Used for volume-based pricing negotiations.']
  response_due_date date [note: 'Date by which vendors must submit their quotations for this line item. Critical for Time and Action (TNA) calendar management.']
  sample_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether physical samples are required from vendors as part of the RFQ response. Critical for SMS (Sample Management System) workflow.']
  sample_type varchar [note: 'Type of sample requested from vendors. Includes proto (prototype), fit sample, PP (pre-production), SMS (salesman sample), strike-off (fabric), or lab dip (color). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: proto|fit|pp|sms|production|strike_off|lab_dip — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Fashion season or collection code this RFQ line belongs to. Examples: SS24 (Spring/Summer 2024), FW24 (Fall/Winter 2024), Holiday 2024.']
  size_range varchar [note: 'Size range or size breakdown for the style being quoted. May include standard sizing (XS-XXL) or numeric sizing depending on product category.']
  sustainability_requirement varchar [note: 'Sustainability or ethical sourcing requirements for this line. May include organic certification, recycled content, fair labor standards, or carbon footprint targets.']
  target_cmt_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Target CMT cost per unit for manufacturing services only. Excludes material costs, represents labor and production overhead.']
  target_fob_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Target FOB cost per unit that the buyer is seeking from vendors. Represents the price at the port of origin before shipping.']
  tech_pack_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier to the technical specification package in PLM system. Contains construction details, measurements, and quality standards.']
  testing_requirement varchar [note: 'Laboratory testing requirements for materials or finished goods. May include colorfastness, shrinkage, pilling, flammability, or chemical content testing.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantities and pricing. Varies by material type (pieces for garments, yards for fabric, etc.). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: piece|yard|meter|kilogram|dozen|gross|roll|set — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vendor_notes varchar [note: 'Notes or comments provided by vendors in their quotation response. May include production constraints, alternative suggestions, or clarification requests.']
  vendor_response_status varchar [note: 'Current status of vendor responses for this RFQ line. Tracks the quotation lifecycle from request through vendor selection.']

  Note: 'Individual line item within an RFQ representing a specific style, colorway, or material being quoted. Captures style number, material description, target FOB/CMT cost, MOQ, lead time requested, quantity break tiers, and vendor response status. Enables granular cost comparison across multiple vendor responses for vendor selection.'
}

Table sourcing.vendor_quote {
  vendor_quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor quote record. Primary key.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_certification. Business justification: Vendor quotes must reference certifications they hold to qualify for RFQ evaluation. Sourcing teams use certification status as a scoring criterion in vendor selection. Quotes without required certifi']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Vendor quotes must be assigned to cost centers for RFQ budget tracking, procurement planning, and cost benchmarking - enables financial control over sourcing decisions before PO commitment.']
  evaluator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Vendor quotes are evaluated by sourcing analysts who benchmark pricing, assess capacity, and recommend awards. Tracks who performed quote analysis for accountability in cost negotiations and vendor se']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the RFQ that this quote responds to. Links the vendor response back to the sourcing request.']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.style. Business justification: Vendor quotes price specific styles. Essential for cost analysis, vendor comparison, and award decisions. Sourcing teams must link quotes to style master for accurate cost tracking and historical anal']
  supplier_esg_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.supplier_esg_assessment. Business justification: Vendor quotes are evaluated against current ESG assessments for responsible sourcing decisions. Sourcing teams must verify ESG compliance before awarding business. Mandatory for ethical sourcing polic']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier submitting this quote.']
  acceptance_date date [note: 'Date the buyer formally accepted this quote. Triggers conversion to purchase order or contract.']
  acceptance_status varchar [note: 'Buyer decision status on this quote. Indicates whether the quote has been accepted, rejected, or is under negotiation.']
  benchmark_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Comparative score assigned to this quote during vendor selection analysis. Enables objective comparison across multiple vendor quotes.']
  capacity_available_units varchar [note: 'Production capacity available from the vendor for this quote period. Indicates maximum units the vendor can produce within the lead time window.']
  cmt_rate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quoted CMT rate per unit. Represents the manufacturing labor cost for cutting fabric, making the garment, and trimming/finishing, excluding material costs.']
  counter_terms varchar [note: 'Any counter-proposals, exceptions, or deviations from the original RFQ terms proposed by the vendor. Captures negotiation points.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor quote record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this quote (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  duty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated import duty rate as a percentage of FOB value. Based on Harmonized System (HS) code classification and destination country tariff schedule.']
  fob_price decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quoted FOB price per unit. Represents the cost of goods loaded onto the shipping vessel at the port of origin, excluding freight and insurance.']
  freight_cost_estimate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated freight cost per unit from port of loading to destination. May be provided by vendor or calculated internally.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System tariff classification code for the quoted product. Used to determine applicable duties, taxes, and trade compliance requirements.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) rule defining the division of costs, risks, and responsibilities between buyer and seller for shipment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ldp_estimate decimal(18,4) [note: 'Estimated total landed cost per unit including FOB price, freight, insurance, duties, and taxes. Provides a complete cost view for comparison.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from purchase order confirmation to shipment readiness. Critical for Time and Action (TNA) calendar planning.']
  material_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Quoted cost of raw materials (fabric, trims, accessories) per unit. May be itemized separately from CMT in some quotes.']
  moq varchar [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the vendor to honor the quoted price and terms. Orders below this threshold may not be accepted or may incur surcharges.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or clarifications related to this quote. Captures context for sourcing decisions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Vendor-specified payment terms and conditions (e.g., Net 30, 50% deposit + 50% on shipment, Letter of Credit). Defines when and how payment is due.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'Port or location where goods will be loaded for shipment. Relevant for FOB and other origin-based Incoterms.']
  quality_certification varchar [note: 'Quality certifications or standards the vendor commits to for this quote (e.g., OEKO-TEX Standard 100, GOTS, ISO 9001, WRAP). Ensures compliance with quality and ethical standards.']
  quote_date date [note: 'Date the vendor submitted or issued this quote.']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-assigned unique identifier for this quotation. Used for external reference and vendor communication.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the vendor quote in the sourcing workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|accepted|rejected|expired|withdrawn|superseded — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quote_version varchar [note: 'Version number of this quote. Increments when vendor revises or resubmits the quote in response to negotiation or clarification.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for rejecting this quote. Provides feedback to vendor and supports future sourcing decisions.']
  sample_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'Cost per sample unit for pre-production (PP) samples. Samples are typically required for approval before bulk production.']
  sample_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to produce and deliver pre-production samples for approval. Critical for Sample Management System (SMS) workflow.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Sustainability or environmental certifications applicable to the quoted materials or production process (e.g., Better Cotton Initiative, Global Organic Textile Standard).']
  tooling_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'One-time cost for tooling, molds, dies, or setup required for production. May be amortized across order quantity or charged separately.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this vendor quote record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Start date of the quote validity period. Quote pricing and terms are effective from this date.']
  valid_until_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the quote. After this date, the vendor is not obligated to honor the quoted terms.']

  Note: 'Vendor response to an RFQ capturing the quoted FOB price, CMT rate, MOQ, lead time, payment terms, validity period, and any counter-terms. Tracks quote version, currency, landed duty paid (LDP) estimate, and acceptance status. Enables cost negotiation, benchmark comparison, and vendor selection decisions.'
}

Table sourcing.vendor_cost_quote {
  vendor_cost_quote_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor cost quote record. Primary key for the vendor cost quote entity.']
  colorway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.colorway. Business justification: Cost quotes vary by colorway due to dye costs, material availability, and complexity. Critical for accurate colorway-level costing and margin analysis. Sourcing teams price by colorway for profitabili']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost quotes require cost center assignment for detailed costing analysis, budget validation, and procurement financial planning - supports target cost vs. actual cost variance reporting in apparel sou']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Cost quotes are created by sourcing coordinators who compile vendor pricing. Tracks quote originator for audit trail. Complements existing approved_by field to show full quote lifecycle ownership in c']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.rfq. Business justification: Vendor cost quotes are typically generated in response to RFQs. This FK establishes the parent-child relationship between the RFQ solicitation and the detailed cost breakdown response. No bidirectiona']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_sketch. Business justification: Vendor cost quotes are validated against design sketches to ensure costing aligns with design specifications before style finalization. Critical for design-costing validation, target margin analysis, ']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the style or product design for which this cost quote applies. Links to the product master in PLM.']
  supplier_esg_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.supplier_esg_assessment. Business justification: Cost quotes must be evaluated alongside ESG performance for holistic sourcing decisions. Enables total cost of ownership analysis including sustainability risk. Required for responsible sourcing appro']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or manufacturer providing this cost quote. Links to the vendor master data.']
  vendor_quote_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.vendor_quote. Business justification: A vendor_cost_quote provides the detailed cost breakdown that supports a vendor_quote (the formal quote response). This FK links the detailed costing to the summary quote. No bidirectional conflict ex']
  agent_commission decimal(15,4) [note: 'Commission paid to sourcing agent or buying office per unit. Typically a percentage of FOB cost, expressed as absolute amount per unit.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date when this cost quote was approved for production. Marks transition from negotiation to execution phase.']
  cmt_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'The manufacturing labor cost for cutting, making, and trimming the garment. Core component of production cost in CMT sourcing model.']
  cogs_estimate decimal(15,4) [note: 'Estimated COGS per unit including all landed costs and internal allocation of overhead. Used for financial planning and margin calculation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost quote record was first created in the system. Part of audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this quote (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Critical for multi-currency sourcing operations.']
  duty_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Estimated import duty and tariff cost per unit based on HS code classification and country of origin. Critical for landed cost calculation.']
  embellishment_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Additional cost for embellishments such as embroidery, printing, appliqué, or special finishes. Optional cost component for decorated styles.']
  fabric_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'The cost of fabric materials per unit for this style. Major component of total COGS. Sourced from fabric mills or material suppliers.']
  fob_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Total Free On Board cost per unit at the port of origin. Sum of fabric, trim, CMT, embellishment, packaging, testing, and agent commission. Key metric for sourcing decisions.']
  freight_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Estimated freight and logistics cost per unit from factory to destination port or warehouse. May vary by incoterm (FOB, CIF, DDP).']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'The international commercial term defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. Determines which costs are included in the quoted price. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FOB|CIF|CFR|DDP|DDU|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  ldp_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Total landed duty paid cost per unit at destination warehouse. Includes FOB cost plus freight, duty, and other landed charges. Used for COGS and margin analysis.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Production lead time in days from PO confirmation to ex-factory date. Critical input for TNA calendar and delivery planning.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this cost quote record was last modified. Tracks changes through negotiation and revision cycles.']
  moq varchar [note: 'The minimum order quantity required by the vendor for this style at the quoted price. Key constraint for buy plan and allocation decisions.']
  moq_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the MOQ (e.g., pieces, dozens, cartons). Clarifies how MOQ is counted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing negotiation details, special conditions, quality requirements, or other contextual information relevant to this cost quote.']
  other_charges decimal(15,4) [note: 'Miscellaneous charges not captured in other cost components (e.g., bank fees, inspection fees, special handling). Catch-all for additional costs.']
  packaging_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost of packaging materials per unit including polybags, boxes, tissue paper, and branded packaging. Part of total unit cost.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment terms for this quote (e.g., 30% deposit, 70% on shipment; LC at sight; Net 60). Impacts cash flow and working capital planning.']
  quote_date date [note: 'The date when the vendor submitted or the sourcing team created this cost quote. Key timestamp for TNA calendar tracking.']
  quote_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the cost quote, typically generated by the PLM system or sourcing team. Used for external communication with vendors and internal tracking.']
  quote_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost quote in the sourcing workflow. Tracks progression from initial draft through approval or rejection. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|submitted|under_review|negotiating|approved|rejected|superseded — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  quote_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the cost quote. Increments with each revision as negotiations progress or specifications change. Supports cost history tracking.']
  sample_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost charged by vendor for producing pre-production samples (PP samples, SMS samples). May be deducted from bulk order or charged separately.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The fashion season for which this cost quote applies (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024). Critical for collection planning and merchandising.']
  target_imu_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target initial markup percentage for this style based on merchandising strategy. Used to validate if the quoted cost supports desired retail price and margin.']
  target_retail_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target retail price for this style. Used with cost quote to calculate achievable margin and validate pricing strategy.']
  testing_certification_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost per unit for product testing and certifications (OEKO-TEX, GOTS, CPSC compliance testing, etc.). Amortized across production quantity.']
  trim_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'The cost of trims and accessories (buttons, zippers, labels, hangtags, etc.) per unit. Part of the BOM cost breakdown.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this cost quote becomes effective. Used for time-bound pricing agreements.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date until which this cost quote remains valid. After this date, a new quote may be required. Nullable for open-ended quotes.']

  Note: 'Detailed cost breakdown record for a style or material capturing CMT cost components (cut, make, trim), fabric cost, accessories cost, freight, duty, agent commission, and total FOB/LDP cost. Tracks IMU target, COGS estimate, season, cost version, and approval status. Authoritative costing record for pre-production financial planning and margin analysis.'
}

  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for purchase_order']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_agreement. Business justification: Sourcing POs can be issued under framework sourcing agreements. This FK links tactical purchase orders to the strategic agreements that govern pricing, terms, and commitments. No bidirectional conflic']
