Business justification: Materials must be screened against restricted substance lists (REACH SVHC, California Prop 65, ZDHC MRSL). Material approval workflow requires RSL compliance verification before supplier qualification']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.rfq. Business justification: Materials can be sourced via the RFQ process. This FK links material sourcing records to the RFQ that procured them, providing traceability from solicitation to material master. No bidirectional confl']
  sourcing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Materials are sourced by fabric/trim buyers who negotiate with mills. Tracks accountability for material selection, sustainability compliance, and cost negotiations. Essential for performance metrics ']
  supplier_material_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the approved mill or supplier from whom this material is sourced. Links to supplier master data.']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Material sourcing records reference sustainable material master data for procurement specifications. Natural alignment between sourcing execution and sustainability material library. Enables consisten']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material is currently active and available for use in new product development and production.']
  bci_cotton boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material contains cotton sourced through the Better Cotton Initiative for sustainable cotton farming.']
  care_instructions varchar [note: 'Care and maintenance instructions for the material (e.g., washing, drying, ironing). Used for product labeling and consumer information.']
  color_standard varchar [note: 'Reference color standard or Pantone code for the material. Used for color matching and quality control in production.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  cost_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the unit cost (e.g., per yard, per meter, per kilogram, per piece).']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the country where the material is manufactured. Required for customs and trade compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the material sourcing record was first created in the system.']
  development_season varchar [note: 'Season or collection for which this material was developed (e.g., \'SS24\', \'FW24\', \'Holiday 2024\'). Links material to merchandising calendar.']
  development_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the material in the product development and sourcing workflow.']
  fiber_content varchar [note: 'Composition of the material by fiber type and percentage (e.g., \'100% Organic Cotton\', \'65% Polyester 35% Cotton\', \'Metal: Brass\'). Required for FTC textile labeling compliance.']
  fob_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Free on Board cost including material cost and delivery to port of shipment. Used for total landed cost calculation.']
  gots_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material meets Global Organic Textile Standard (GOTS) requirements for organic fiber content and sustainable processing.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'Harmonized System code for customs classification and international trade. Required for import/export documentation and duty calculation.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the material sourcing record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard lead time in days from purchase order placement to material delivery. Used in Time and Action (TNA) calendar planning.']
  moq_quantity decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the mill or supplier for this material. Critical for procurement planning and costing.']
  moq_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the MOQ (e.g., yards, meters, kilograms, pieces).']
  oeko_tex_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the material has OEKO-TEX Standard 100 certification for textile safety and absence of harmful substances.']
  pp_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the material received Pre-Production approval. Key milestone in the Time and Action (TNA) calendar.']
  pp_approval_status varchar [note: 'Pre-Production approval status for the material. Indicates whether the material has passed quality and specification review for production use.']
  sample_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a physical sample of this material is available in the Sample Management System (SMS) for design review and approval.']
  sample_reference_number varchar [note: 'Reference number for the physical sample in the Sample Management System (SMS) if available.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite sustainability score for the material based on environmental impact, certifications, and sourcing practices. Scale 0-100.']
  test_report_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether lab test reports (e.g., colorfastness, shrinkage, tensile strength) are available for this material.']
  test_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or document identifier for the lab test report if available.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost per unit of the material in the base currency. Used for Bill of Materials (BOM) costing and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation.']
  weight_gsm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the fabric material measured in grams per square meter. Key specification for fabric performance and costing.']
  width_inches decimal(10,2) [note: 'Usable width of the fabric material measured in inches. Critical for yield calculation and cutting efficiency.']

  Note: 'Master record for all raw materials, fabrics, trims, and components used in apparel production. Captures material code, description, material type (woven, knit, trim, lining, hardware, button, zipper, label, elastic, interlining), fiber content, weight (GSM), width, color standard, development status, development season, OEKO-TEX certification status, GOTS compliance flag, HS code, MOQ, lead time, unit cost, and approved mill/supplier references. SSOT for material definitions including trim catalog entries within sourcing.'
}

Table sourcing.fabric_development {
  fabric_development_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fabric development record. Primary key for tracking a fabric through its entire development lifecycle from concept to bulk approval.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product collection for which this fabric is being developed. Links fabric development to specific merchandising collections.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: Fabric development projects originate from design concepts requiring custom fabric creation. Enables concept-to-fabric development traceability, concept-level fabric innovation tracking, and design co']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Fabric development costs must be allocated to cost centers for R&D expense tracking, product development budgeting, and innovation cost management - standard practice in apparel product development fi']
  developer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Fabric development is led by textile developers who manage lab dips, strike-offs, and bulk approvals. Tracks ownership for fabric innovation, quality issues, and development timelines. Normalizes deno']
  environmental_impact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.environmental_impact. Business justification: New fabric developments require environmental impact assessments (LCA) for sustainability evaluation and material selection decisions. Enables comparison of development alternatives based on environme']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.material. Business justification: Fabric development results in approved materials added to material library. Critical for BOM integration and material master data. Sourcing teams link approved fabrics to material master for reuse.']
  product_safety_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_safety_test. Business justification: New fabric developments require safety testing (azo dyes, formaldehyde, phthalates, lead) before bulk approval. Fabric approval gate requires passing test results. Development teams track test status ']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the textile mill or fabric supplier responsible for developing and producing this fabric. Links to supplier master data.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.rfq. Business justification: Fabric development can be initiated via RFQs sent to mills. This FK links fabric development to the RFQ that initiated it, providing traceability from solicitation to fabric approval. No bidirectional']
  sourcing_material_sourcing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.material_sourcing. Business justification: Fabric development records track the lifecycle of new fabrics, which should reference the material sourcing master record. This FK links fabric development to the material master, enabling consolidate']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.style. Business justification: Fabric development is initiated for specific styles. Needed for style-fabric linkage, development tracking, and fabric approval workflow. Design and sourcing teams link fabric development to target st']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Fabric development projects target sustainable material specifications for new collections. Links R&D efforts to sustainability material library. Essential for tracking sustainable innovation pipeline']
  bulk_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the fabric received final bulk approval. Enables procurement to issue Purchase Orders (PO) for production yardage.']
  bulk_approval_status varchar [note: 'Final approval status for bulk production of the fabric. Indicates whether the fabric is cleared for large-scale manufacturing and Purchase Order (PO) placement.']
  bulk_yardage_commitment decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total yardage or meterage of fabric committed for bulk production. Used for capacity planning and Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) negotiations with the mill.']
  colorway varchar [note: 'Color or color combination being developed for this fabric. May include Pantone references, color names, or internal color codes.']
  cost_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the fabric cost (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY). Required for multi-currency procurement and landed cost calculations.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(15,4) [note: 'Negotiated cost per unit (yard or meter) of fabric from the mill. Used for Bill of Materials (BOM) costing and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fabric development record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  development_season varchar [note: 'The target season for which this fabric is being developed. Format: SS (Spring/Summer), FW (Fall/Winter), SP (Spring), FA (Fall) followed by two-digit year (e.g., SS24, FW25).']
  development_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fabric development process. Tracks progression from initial concept through mill submissions, lab dip approval, strike-off approval, and final bulk approval. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|submitted|lab_dip_pending|lab_dip_approved|strike_off_pending|strike_off_approved|bulk_approved|rejected|on_hold — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  fabric_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the fabric for system identification and cross-referencing across Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems.']
  fabric_construction varchar [note: 'Technical description of the fabric structure including weave type, knit pattern, or construction method (e.g., plain weave, twill, jersey knit, rib knit, interlock).']
  fabric_name varchar [note: 'Business name or marketing name assigned to the fabric being developed. Used for identification in collection planning and merchandising.']
  fabric_weight decimal(10,2) [note: 'Weight of the fabric measured in grams per square meter (GSM) or ounces per square yard. Critical specification for product costing and performance characteristics.']
  fabric_width decimal(10,2) [note: 'Width of the fabric measured in inches or centimeters. Standard widths impact cutting efficiency and Bill of Materials (BOM) calculations.']
  fiber_blend varchar [note: 'Composition of fibers used in the fabric with percentages (e.g., 60% Cotton 40% Polyester, 100% Organic Cotton). Must comply with Federal Trade Commission (FTC) fiber content labeling requirements.']
  finish_treatment varchar [note: 'Chemical or mechanical finishing processes applied to the fabric (e.g., enzyme wash, stone wash, water repellent, anti-microbial, wrinkle-free). Impacts hand-feel and performance.']
  lab_dip_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the lab dip color sample was approved by the design or product development team. Critical milestone in the TNA calendar.']
  lab_dip_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the lab dip color sample. Lab dip is the initial small-scale color sample submitted by the mill for color approval before bulk production.']
  lab_dip_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the lab dip color sample was submitted by the mill for review. Used to track Time and Action (TNA) calendar milestones.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required from Purchase Order (PO) placement to fabric delivery. Critical for Time and Action (TNA) calendar planning and On Time In Full (OTIF) performance.']
  mill_reference_number varchar [note: 'The mills internal reference or article number for this fabric. Used for communication and ordering with the supplier.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this fabric development record was last modified. Audit trail for tracking changes throughout the development lifecycle.']
  moq_quantity decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required by the mill for this fabric. Critical for procurement planning and cost negotiations.']
  moq_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) - may be linear measure (yards/meters) or weight (kg) depending on mill requirements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or observations during the fabric development process. Captures iterative feedback and mill communications.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation if the fabric was rejected at any stage (lab dip, strike-off, or bulk). Captures quality issues, color mismatch, hand-feel deviation, or test failures.']
  strike_off_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the strike-off fabric sample was approved. Approval triggers progression to bulk yardage commitment and Pre-Production (PP) sample phase.']
  strike_off_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the strike-off sample. Strike-off is a larger fabric sample (typically 1-2 yards) that demonstrates the final fabric construction, color, and finish.']
  strike_off_submission_date date [note: 'Date when the strike-off fabric sample was submitted by the mill for review. Key TNA milestone before bulk production commitment.']
  submission_round varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which iteration of fabric submission this represents. Tracks the number of times the mill has resubmitted samples for approval.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or ethical certifications held by the fabric (e.g., GOTS, BCI, OEKO-TEX, Bluesign, GRS). Supports sustainability reporting and compliance.']
  target_hand_feel varchar [note: 'Desired tactile characteristics and drape of the fabric as specified by the design team (e.g., soft, crisp, fluid, structured, brushed).']
  test_results_summary varchar [note: 'Summary of physical and chemical test results performed on the fabric (e.g., colorfastness, shrinkage, pilling resistance, tensile strength). Links to detailed lab test reports.']
  weight_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for fabric weight. GSM (grams per square meter) is metric standard; oz per square yard is imperial standard.']
  width_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for fabric width (inches or centimeters).']
  yardage_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for bulk yardage commitment (yards or meters).']

  Note: 'Fabric development record tracking the lifecycle of a new fabric from concept through mill approval. Captures fabric name, construction, fiber blend, colorway, finish, target hand-feel, lab dip status, strike-off approval, bulk yardage commitment, and development season. Manages the iterative mill development process including submission rounds, test results, and final bulk approval.'
}

Table sourcing.lab_dip {
  lab_dip_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lab dip approval record. Primary key for tracking color development and approval process for fabric or material.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Lab dips are approved by color managers or product developers who verify Pantone accuracy and color fastness. Tracks approval authority for color standards, critical for quality control and vendor acc']
  chemical_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.chemical_compliance. Business justification: Lab dips must be tested for chemical compliance (ZDHC MRSL, restricted substances) before bulk production approval. Quality gate for color development. Links color approval process to chemical testing']
  colorway_development_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.colorway_development. Business justification: Lab dips are color approval samples for specific colorway development projects. Direct 1:1 business relationship. Enables colorway-to-lab-dip traceability, color approval workflow management, and colo']
  colorway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.colorway. Business justification: Lab dips are submitted for specific colorways to achieve color standards. Fundamental to color approval process and bulk production authorization. Colorists link lab dips to colorway master for approv']
  fabric_development_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific fabric or material for which the color is being matched.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.material. Business justification: Lab dips test specific materials for color accuracy and fastness. Needed for material-color approval tracking and dye recipe validation. Sourcing teams link lab dips to material master for quality con']
  print_colorway_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.print_colorway. Business justification: Lab dips validate print colorway color accuracy for printed fabrics. Natural design-to-sourcing validation process. Enables print colorway approval tracking, print color conformance validation, and pr']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the textile mill or dye house that submitted this lab dip for approval.']
  sourcing_material_sourcing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.material_sourcing. Business justification: Lab dips are color approval records for materials. This FK links lab dip approvals to the material sourcing master record, enabling consolidated material color management. No bidirectional conflict ex']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product style for which this lab dip color is being developed.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary supplier managing the fabric sourcing and color development process.']
  actual_lab_a decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured green-red axis value of the submitted lab dip in CIELAB color space.']
  actual_lab_b decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured blue-yellow axis value of the submitted lab dip in CIELAB color space.']
  actual_lab_l decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured lightness value of the submitted lab dip in CIELAB color space.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the lab dip received final approval for bulk production. Null if not yet approved. Critical milestone for TNA calendar and PO (Purchase Order) release.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the lab dip in the color development workflow. Determines whether bulk fabric production can proceed.']
  collection_name varchar [note: 'Name of the product collection or line to which this color belongs. Used for merchandising and assortment planning.']
  color_fastness_rating varchar [note: 'Rating (1-5 scale) indicating the color fastness performance of the dyed fabric. Higher values indicate better resistance to fading. Based on ISO 105 testing standards.']
  color_standard_type varchar [note: 'Type of color standard used for matching specification (Pantone, LAB values, RGB, CMYK, or physical swatch).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, observations, or special instructions related to the lab dip evaluation and approval process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lab dip record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and process tracking.']
  delta_e_value decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated color difference (Delta E) between target and actual color. Lower values indicate closer match. Typically acceptable range is Delta E < 1.0 for critical color matching.']
  digital_image_url varchar [note: 'URL link to digital photograph or scan of the lab dip sample. Enables remote review and digital archiving.']
  dye_lot_number varchar [note: 'Batch identifier for the dye lot used in this lab dip submission. Critical for traceability and consistency in bulk production.']
  dye_recipe_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the specific dye formulation and recipe used for this color. Links to dye house technical specifications.']
  evaluation_date date [note: 'Date when the lab dip was evaluated by the design or quality team.']
  is_strike_off_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a larger strike-off sample (printed or dyed fabric yardage) is required after lab dip approval before bulk production commitment.']
  lab_dip_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the lab dip submission. Externally-known reference number used across PLM systems and vendor communications.']
  light_fastness_grade varchar [note: 'Grade (1-8 scale) measuring resistance to color change from light exposure. Grade 8 is excellent, grade 1 is poor.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lab dip record was last modified. Tracks the most recent update to any field in the record.']
  pantone_reference varchar [note: 'Pantone color standard reference code used as the target color specification for matching.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the lab dip was rejected or requires resubmission. Includes specific color matching issues or quality defects.']
  sample_physical_location varchar [note: 'Physical storage location of the lab dip sample swatch (e.g., design studio, quality lab, archive). Used for sample retrieval and reference.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the fashion season for which this color is being developed (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024).']
  spectrophotometer_reading_file varchar [note: 'File path or reference to the spectrophotometer measurement data file. Contains detailed color measurement data for technical analysis.']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date when the mill submitted this lab dip sample for evaluation. Critical for Time and Action (TNA) calendar tracking.']
  submission_round varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which submission attempt this represents (1 for first submission, 2 for resubmit, etc.). Tracks iteration count in color approval process.']
  target_bulk_production_date date [note: 'Planned date for bulk fabric production to begin. Drives urgency of lab dip approval process within TNA calendar.']
  target_lab_a decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target green-red axis value in CIELAB color space. Negative values indicate green, positive indicate red.']
  target_lab_b decimal(6,2) [note: 'Target blue-yellow axis value in CIELAB color space. Negative values indicate blue, positive indicate yellow.']
  target_lab_l decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target lightness value in CIELAB color space (0-100 scale). Used for precise color measurement and matching.']
  wash_fastness_grade varchar [note: 'Grade (1-5 scale) measuring resistance to color change from washing. Grade 5 is excellent, grade 1 is poor.']

  Note: 'Lab dip approval record tracking the color development and approval process for a fabric or material. Captures colorway reference, Pantone/color standard, dye lot, mill submission date, evaluation result (approved, rejected, resubmit), delta-E measurement, submission round number, and final approval date. Critical for color accuracy compliance before bulk fabric production commitment.'
}

Table sourcing.lead_time_profile {
  lead_time_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lead time profile record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.sourcing_agreement. Business justification: Lead time profiles can be defined and governed by sourcing agreements. This FK links lead time profiles to the agreements that define them, enabling agreement-based lead time management. No bidirectio']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Lead time profiles require cost center assignment for supply chain cost modeling, lead time cost analysis, and procurement planning expense allocation - supports apparel sourcing financial optimizatio']
  destination_distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.distribution_center. Business justification: Lead time profiles specify target in-DC dates for specific destination facilities; critical for vendor onboarding, PO placement timing, and ensuring sourcing commitments align with DC capacity and ret']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sourcing manager or planner who approved this lead time profile for operational use.']
  primary_carrier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.carrier. Business justification: Lead time profiles include carrier-specific transit times for different lanes and service levels; essential for accurate TNA planning, routing guide alignment, and vendor lead time commitments in appa']
  superseded_lead_time_profile_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lead_time_profile (superseded_lead_time_profile_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier for which this lead time profile is defined.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when this lead time profile was formally approved and activated for planning purposes.']
  buffer_days varchar [note: 'Additional safety buffer days added to the total lead time to account for variability, delays, and risk mitigation.']
  capacity_constraint_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has capacity constraints for this product category that may affect lead time variability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lead time profile record was first created in the system.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this lead time profile is on the critical path for Time and Action (TNA) calendar planning.']
  customs_clearance_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required for customs clearance, duty payment, and import documentation processing.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code representing the destination country for shipment.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this lead time profile becomes active and applicable for planning and sourcing decisions.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this lead time profile remains valid; nullable for open-ended profiles.']
  fob_port_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the port of loading where goods are handed over to the carrier under FOB terms.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) defining the delivery terms and responsibility transfer point between supplier and buyer.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when this lead time profile was last reviewed and validated against actual performance data.']
  material_sourcing_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to source and procure raw materials, fabrics, and trims from mills and vendors.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lead time profile record was last modified or updated.']
  moq_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum order quantity required by the supplier for this product category, negotiated during sourcing.']
  moq_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity (e.g., units, pieces, dozens, cartons).']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next review and update of this lead time profile to ensure accuracy.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this lead time profile.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment terms with the supplier (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Letter of Credit) that may influence lead time planning.']
  plm_system_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier linking this lead time profile to the corresponding record in Centric PLM or Infor PLM system.']
  pp_approval_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required for pre-production sample approval workflow including fit, construction, and material compliance evaluation.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the product category (e.g., tops, bottoms, footwear, accessories) for which this lead time profile applies.']
  production_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required for bulk production including cutting, making, and trimming (CMT) operations.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name for the lead time profile to facilitate identification and reporting.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lead time profile indicating whether it is actively used in planning.']
  quality_inspection_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days allocated for quality inspection and compliance testing before shipment.']
  quality_standard varchar [note: 'Quality standard or certification requirement applicable to this lead time profile (e.g., ISO 9001, AQL 2.5).']
  sample_development_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required for sample development, submission, and evaluation through the Sample Management System (SMS).']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the fashion season or collection period for which this lead time profile is applicable (e.g., SS24, FW24).']
  shipping_transit_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required for freight transportation from Free on Board (FOB) port to destination warehouse or distribution center.']
  sustainability_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether sustainability certifications (OEKO-TEX, GOTS, BCI) are required for this supplier-category combination, which may impact lead time.']
  total_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total end-to-end lead time in days from order placement to delivery at destination, including all component lead times and buffers.']

  Note: 'Master record defining standard lead time parameters for a supplier-product category combination including total lead time, component lead times, and buffer days'
}

Table sourcing.rtv_order {
  rtv_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the return to vendor order.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: RTV orders must be allocated to cost centers for quality cost tracking, return expense management, and vendor performance cost analysis - enables financial accountability for defective goods.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the buyer or sourcing manager responsible for the RTV order.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: RTV orders require GL account assignment for accounts payable credit processing, inventory return accounting, and vendor credit memo reconciliation - fundamental to apparel financial transaction proce']
  incident_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_incident. Business justification: Returns to vendor for compliance failures (restricted substances detected, missing certifications, labeling violations) must link to incident records. RTV root cause tracking for regulatory violations']
  original_rtv_order_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on rtv_order (original_rtv_order_id)']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the factory or manufacturing facility that produced the returned goods.']
  return_shipment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to logistics.return_shipment. Business justification: RTV orders generate outbound return shipments that must be tracked through logistics network for carrier coordination, tracking, delivery confirmation, and freight cost reconciliation in apparel retur']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Returns are processed at SKU level for inventory accuracy and defect tracking. Essential for vendor accountability, quality analysis, and cost recovery. Warehouse and QA teams link RTVs to SKU master.']
  sourcing_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original sourcing purchase order from which goods are being returned.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor to whom goods are being returned.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the warehouse or distribution center initiating the return.']
  waste_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.waste_record. Business justification: Returns to vendor generate waste that must be tracked for waste management, circular economy programs, and landfill diversion reporting. Links reverse logistics to waste accounting. Required for zero-']
  actual_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual credit amount issued by the vendor after inspection and approval of returned goods.']
  actual_ship_date date [note: 'Date when the returned goods were actually shipped back to the vendor.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the RTV order was approved for execution.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person who approved the RTV order for processing.']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the logistics carrier or freight forwarder handling the return shipment.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RTV order record was first created in the system.']
  credit_disposition varchar [note: 'Vendor decision on how the return will be credited or resolved.']
  credit_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor issued the credit memo for the returned goods.']
  credit_memo_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-issued credit memo reference number for the return transaction.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the return value and credit amounts. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USD|EUR|GBP|CNY|JPY|INR|CAD|AUD — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  expected_credit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Expected credit amount to be issued by the vendor for the returned goods.']
  freight_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'Cost of freight and logistics for returning goods to the vendor.']
  freight_responsibility varchar [note: 'Party responsible for bearing the freight cost of the return shipment.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Incoterms governing the return shipment and transfer of risk and cost responsibility.']
  inspection_report_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number of the quality inspection report documenting the defects or non-conformance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the RTV order record was last modified or updated.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the RTV order.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product category classification of the returned goods.']
  quality_defect_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the quality defect type when return is due to quality issues.']
  requested_pickup_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor is requested to pick up or receive the returned goods.']
  return_authorization_number varchar [note: 'Vendor-issued return merchandise authorization (RMA) number approving the return.']
  return_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the specific reason for returning goods to the vendor.']
  return_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the reason for returning goods including quality issues, specification mismatches, or other defects.']
  rtv_date date [note: 'Date when the RTV order was created and initiated in the system.']
  rtv_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the RTV order used in vendor communication and tracking.']
  rtv_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the RTV order in the return workflow process. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|approved|in_transit|received_by_vendor|credit_issued|closed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  rtv_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the return type indicating the primary reason category for the return.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier for the returned goods linking to the collection planning cycle.']
  shipping_method varchar [note: 'Method of transportation used to return goods to the vendor.']
  total_return_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Total quantity of units being returned to the vendor across all line items.']
  total_return_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of all goods being returned to the vendor.']
  tracking_number varchar [note: 'Shipment tracking number provided by the carrier for monitoring return transit.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit of measure for the returned goods quantity. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|PCS|CTN|BOX|PLT|YD|MTR|KG|LB — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  vendor_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the vendor contact for return coordination and communication.']
  vendor_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the vendor contact person handling the return transaction.']
  vendor_receipt_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor confirmed receipt of the returned goods.']

  Note: 'Return to Vendor order managing the return of defective, excess, or non-conforming goods to the supplier including RTV reason, quantity, and credit disposition'
}
