Business justification: Long-term sourcing agreements align with corporate carbon reduction targets and may include supplier-specific emission reduction commitments. Links commercial strategy to climate goals. Essential for ']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: Sourcing agreements define pricing, capacity, and terms for product categories. Critical for category-level capacity planning, pricing strategy, and vendor management. Sourcing directors link agreemen']
  collaboration_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_collaboration. Business justification: Sourcing agreements are established for design collaboration partnerships requiring dedicated vendor relationships and capacity allocation. Enables collaboration-specific sourcing terms management, co']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Sourcing agreements must be allocated to cost centers for multi-year contract expense tracking, committed spend management, and long-term procurement budget planning in apparel operations.']
  master_agreement_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on agreement (master_agreement_id)']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Long-term sourcing agreements specify ongoing compliance obligations (labor standards, environmental certifications, audit frequency) that vendors must maintain. Master service agreements include comp']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Agreements must be assigned to profit centers for strategic vendor profitability analysis, brand-specific sourcing cost tracking, and long-term margin planning in apparel brand management.']
  rfq_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sourcing.rfq. Business justification: Sourcing agreements can result from RFQ processes (e.g., strategic RFQs leading to framework agreements). This FK links agreements to the RFQ that initiated them, providing traceability from solicitat']
  supplier_esg_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.supplier_esg_assessment. Business justification: Sourcing agreements require valid ESG assessments and reference sustainability requirements as contract terms. Links long-term commercial relationships to ESG performance. Mandatory for responsible so']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor party with whom this sourcing agreement is established.']
  agreement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the sourcing agreement for easy identification and reference by business users.']
  agreement_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the sourcing agreement, used in vendor communications and contract references.']
  agreement_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the sourcing agreement indicating its operational state and enforceability. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|pending_approval|active|suspended|expired|terminated|renewed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  agreement_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sourcing agreement based on its strategic purpose and scope. Master agreements cover multiple seasons, seasonal agreements are collection-specific, spot agreements are one-time purchases, blanket agreements provide volume commitments, framework agreements establish terms for future orders, and exclusive agreements grant sole sourcing rights.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when this sourcing agreement received final internal approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval status of this sourcing agreement within the internal governance workflow.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the executive or manager who approved this sourcing agreement.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement automatically renews at the end of its term unless either party provides termination notice.']
  base_cmt_rate decimal(15,4) [note: 'The baseline Cut Make Trim labor rate per unit negotiated in this agreement for manufacturing services.']
  base_fob_price decimal(15,4) [note: 'The baseline Free On Board unit price negotiated in this agreement before volume discounts or surcharges are applied.']
  capacity_allocation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the vendors production capacity allocated or reserved for the buyer under this agreement.']
  committed_volume_quantity decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total volume or quantity of goods that the buyer commits to source from the vendor under this agreement, used for volume guarantee negotiations.']
  compliance_certification_required varchar [note: 'List of compliance certifications that the vendor must maintain and provide under this agreement (e.g., WRAP, SA8000, ISO 14001, CPSC compliance).']
  contract_document_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier or file path to the signed legal contract document stored in the document management system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this sourcing agreement record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary values in this agreement (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY).']
  destination_port_code varchar [note: 'The destination port or distribution center where goods will be delivered under this agreement.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'The date when the sourcing agreement becomes legally binding and operational.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'The date when the sourcing agreement expires or terminates. Nullable for open-ended agreements with no fixed end date.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this agreement grants the buyer exclusive sourcing rights for specified products or categories from this vendor.']
  exclusivity_scope varchar [note: 'Description of the scope of exclusivity if applicable, detailing which products, categories, or geographic regions are covered by the exclusive arrangement.']
  fob_port_code varchar [note: 'The port of origin from which goods will be shipped under FOB terms, typically the vendors shipping port.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'The Incoterms rule governing the transfer of risk and responsibility for shipping costs in this agreement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'The standard total lead time in days from purchase order placement to ex-factory shipment under this agreement.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum order quantity per purchase order or per style that the vendor requires under this agreement.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this sourcing agreement record was last modified or updated.']
  moq_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the minimum order quantity.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special terms, or contextual information about this sourcing agreement.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The negotiated payment terms for orders placed under this agreement (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, 50% deposit + 50% on shipment, Letter of Credit).']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Description of penalties or liquidated damages applicable for breach of agreement terms, such as failure to meet volume commitments or quality standards.']
  pricing_tier_structure varchar [note: 'Description of the volume-based pricing tiers negotiated in this agreement, detailing quantity breaks and corresponding unit prices or discount percentages.']
  quality_standard varchar [note: 'The quality standards and specifications that the vendor must meet under this agreement (e.g., ISO 9001, AQL 2.5, ASTM standards).']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'The number of days advance notice required to terminate or opt out of auto-renewal of this agreement.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The fashion season or collection period covered by this sourcing agreement. Format examples: SS24 (Spring/Summer 2024), FW24 (Fall/Winter 2024), HO24 (Holiday 2024), SP24 (Spring 2024).']
  sustainability_requirement varchar [note: 'Sustainability and ethical sourcing requirements mandated in this agreement, such as GOTS certification, BCI cotton usage, OEKO-TEX compliance, or Fair Labor Association standards.']
  termination_clause varchar [note: 'Description of the conditions and procedures under which either party may terminate this agreement before its natural expiration.']
  volume_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the committed volume quantity (e.g., units, pieces, dozens, cases, pallets).']

  Note: 'Master record for strategic sourcing framework agreements with vendors covering seasonal commitments, volume guarantees, pricing tiers, and exclusivity terms distinct from individual purchase orders'
}

Table production.work_order {
  work_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work order. Primary key for the work order entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Work orders link to factory audit records to verify production facility compliance status. Brands require valid audit (social, environmental) before issuing production orders - mandatory gate in ethic']
  bom_id varchar [note: 'FK to product.bom.bom_id — MUST-HAVE: Links production work orders to the authoritative BOM in the product domain, enabling material requirements planning and cost tracking. Critical after production.bom removal.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Production orders consume OTB (Open-to-Buy) budget for manufacturing spend control, enabling real-time budget utilization tracking and production cost commitment management—core apparel financial plan']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product collection this work order belongs to. Links production to merchandising assortment strategy.']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_certification. Business justification: Work orders require valid compliance certifications (GOTS, OEKO-TEX, Fair Trade) before production approval. Apparel manufacturers gate production start on certification status - standard compliance w']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: Work orders track originating design concept for collection performance reporting, design ROI analysis, and concept-to-production success metrics. Enables design team performance evaluation.']
  environmental_impact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.environmental_impact. Business justification: Product-level LCA and EPD (Environmental Product Declaration) reporting require linking production orders to environmental impact assessments. Apparel brands publish carbon footprints per style/order ']
  factory_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier.factory.factory_id — MUST-HAVE: Links production work orders to the factory master for capacity planning, compliance verification, and production allocation decisions.']
  letter_of_credit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.letter_of_credit. Business justification: International production orders require LC tracking for payment terms, trade finance management, and supplier payment security—standard in global apparel sourcing operations with overseas factories.']
  packaging_sustainability_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.packaging_sustainability. Business justification: Production orders specify packaging requirements (retail vs. wholesale, channel-specific). Linking to packaging sustainability enables EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) compliance tracking, plast']
  production_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Production orders must allocate manufacturing costs to cost centers for standard costing, variance analysis, and factory overhead allocation—core requirement in apparel manufacturing accounting.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility assigned to execute this work order. Links to the supplier/factory master.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Work orders require profit center assignment for margin analysis by brand/channel/region, enabling P&L reporting at collection and season level—standard apparel financial reporting requirement.']
  routing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.routing. Business justification: Every work order executes a specific manufacturing routing that defines the sequence of operations, work centers, and standard times. This is core SAP PP functionality. The routing defines HOW to manu']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the merchandising season this work order supports. Links production to collection planning and retail calendar.']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_sketch. Business justification: Work orders reference approved design sketches for manufacturing specifications, pattern instructions, and construction details. Core production workflow linking design intent to factory execution.']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the style being manufactured in this work order. Links to the product master style definition.']
  trade_compliance_record_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_record. Business justification: Work orders for export require trade compliance classification (HS codes, country of origin, duty rates). Links production order to trade compliance data - mandatory for international shipments and cu']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Work orders must track the executing vendor for vendor performance scorecards, payment processing, compliance audits, and OTIF reporting. Essential for vendor management and production accountability ']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the distribution center or warehouse where finished goods will be received. Links production to supply chain fulfillment.']
  wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.wholesale_account. Business justification: B2B wholesale accounts commission production runs for their orders. Work orders track which account the production is for, enabling account-level production tracking, capacity allocation, and fulfillm']
  actual_ex_factory_date date [note: 'Actual date when finished goods departed the factory. Used to measure OTIF performance and update logistics schedules.']
  aql_level varchar [note: 'Acceptable Quality Limit standard applied for inspection (e.g., 2.5, 4.0). Defines the maximum acceptable defect rate per ISO 2859 sampling standards.']
  cmt_type varchar [note: 'Manufacturing service model defining the scope of work performed by the factory. Cut-Make-Trim includes all production steps; Make-Trim excludes cutting; Full Package includes material sourcing.']
  compliance_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this work order requires compliance certifications (e.g., OEKO-TEX, GOTS, WRAP, FLA). True if certifications must be provided before shipment.']
  confirmed_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity confirmed by the factory as feasible for production. May differ from ordered quantity based on capacity constraints or material availability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work order record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this work order. Typically USD for international apparel manufacturing.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code for the final destination of finished goods. Used for customs, compliance, and logistics planning.']
  incoterms varchar [note: 'Incoterms code defining the division of costs and risks between buyer and seller. FOB (Free on Board) is common in apparel manufacturing.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing special instructions, production requirements, or coordination details for this work order.']
  ordered_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity of units authorized for production in this work order. Represents the target production volume.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms agreed with the factory for this work order (e.g., 30% deposit, 70% on shipment). Defines cash flow and financial settlement.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level for production scheduling. Critical orders receive expedited processing and resource allocation.']
  produced_quantity varchar [note: 'Actual quantity completed and confirmed through production execution. Updated as production progresses and goods are received.']
  production_end_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when production execution is completed. Marks readiness for final inspection and shipment preparation.']
  production_start_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when production execution begins at the factory. Key milestone in Time and Action (TNA) calendar.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Status of quality inspection for this work order. Passed status is required before goods can be shipped.']
  rejected_quantity varchar [note: 'Quantity rejected during quality inspection. Represents units that failed quality standards and cannot be shipped.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date by which the customer or distribution center requires delivery of finished goods. Drives production scheduling and logistics planning.']
  sap_pp_order_number varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA PP module production order number. System of record identifier linking this work order to the ERP production planning system.']
  shipping_mode varchar [note: 'Primary transportation mode for delivering finished goods from factory to destination. Impacts lead time and logistics cost.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit identifier for the specific color-size combination being manufactured. Represents the sellable unit at retail.']
  target_ex_factory_date date [note: 'Target date for goods to leave the factory. Critical milestone for logistics planning and On Time In Full (OTIF) performance measurement.']
  total_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total manufacturing cost for this work order (unit cost × ordered quantity). Represents the Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for this production run.']
  unit_cost decimal(15,4) [note: 'Cost per unit for manufacturing this work order. Includes CMT charges, materials (if applicable), and factory overhead. Used for COGS calculation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for all quantity fields in this work order. Typically pieces for apparel manufacturing.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this work order. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this work order record was last modified. Audit trail for record changes.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known business identifier for the work order. Human-readable unique reference used across manufacturing systems and communications with factory partners.']
  work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work order. Tracks progression from initial creation through production execution to final closure. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|released|confirmed|in_progress|completed|closed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this work order. Audit trail for accountability.']

  Note: 'Core manufacturing work order authorizing production of a specific quantity of a style/SKU at a designated factory. Captures work order number, style reference, factory assignment, ordered and confirmed quantities, production start/end dates, CMT type (Cut/Make/Trim), status lifecycle (draft → released → in-progress → completed → closed), SAP PP order number, season, collection, priority, customer PO reference, and target ex-factory date. SSOT for all bulk production execution instructions. Links downstream to operations, confirmations, cut orders, T&A milestones, and production lots.'
}

Table production.work_order_operation {
  work_order_operation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the work order operation. Primary key for this entity.']
  operation_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Individual operations require cost center assignment for activity-based costing, labor cost allocation by department, and detailed production cost variance analysis—standard in apparel manufacturing c']
  primary_work_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production operator or worker who performed or is assigned to this operation. Used for performance tracking and labor management.']
  tertiary_work_confirmed_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user ID of the person who confirmed the operation completion in the ERP system.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work center (production resource or machine) where this operation is performed. Links to factory floor resources.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent work order that contains this operation. Links this operation to the overall production order.']
  actual_duration_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual time taken to complete this operation, measured in minutes. Used to calculate efficiency variance against SAM.']
  actual_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the operation execution was completed on the factory floor, captured from production confirmation.']
  actual_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the operation execution began on the factory floor, captured from production confirmation.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Unique confirmation document number generated when the operation is confirmed in the system, linking to goods movement and time confirmation.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operation was confirmed in the system, marking the official completion record.']
  confirmed_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Actual quantity of units successfully processed and confirmed for this operation. Used to track production progress and yield.']
  control_key varchar [note: 'SAP control key that determines how the operation is processed, including scheduling, costing, and confirmation requirements (e.g., PP01, PP02, PP03).']
  cost_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the operation cost amount (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY, INR, BDT).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this operation record was first created in the system, typically when the work order was released.']
  efficiency_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Operation efficiency calculated as (standard_allowed_minutes / actual_duration_minutes) * 100. Measures operator and work center performance against standard.']
  factory_location_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter code identifying the manufacturing facility or factory where this operation is performed (e.g., BDH for Bangladesh Dhaka, CNS for China Shenzhen).']
  inspection_lot_number varchar [note: 'Quality inspection lot number assigned to this operation for tracking quality control results and defects.']
  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether quality inspection is required after completing this operation. True if inspection is mandatory, False otherwise.']
  labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total labor hours consumed for this operation, including direct production time, setup, and teardown. Used for labor costing and capacity analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this operation record was last updated in the system, tracking the most recent change to any field.']
  machine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total machine or equipment hours utilized for this operation. Used for machine costing and utilization tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this operation, including special instructions, issues encountered, or deviations from standard process.']
  operation_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the type of manufacturing operation (e.g., CUT, SEW, FINISH, PRESS, PACK, INSPECT, EMBROIDER, PRINT).']
  operation_cost_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred for this operation, including labor, machine, overhead, and material costs. Used for production costing and variance analysis.']
  operation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the manufacturing operation, including specific instructions, techniques, or requirements for execution.']
  operation_number varchar [note: 'The unique operation number within the routing sequence, typically a 4-digit code (e.g., 0010, 0020, 0030) that defines the order of manufacturing steps.']
  operation_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this operation within the work order routing. Determines the execution order of manufacturing steps.']
  operation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the manufacturing operation indicating its execution state in the production workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|released|in_progress|completed|confirmed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when this operation is planned to be completed based on standard times and production scheduling.']
  planned_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Target quantity of units planned to be processed in this operation based on the work order requirements.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date when this operation is planned to begin execution based on production scheduling and capacity planning.']
  production_line varchar [note: 'Identifier for the production line or assembly line where this operation is executed. Used for line balancing and capacity planning.']
  quality_status varchar [note: 'Quality inspection result status for this operation indicating whether output meets quality standards.']
  rework_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units requiring rework or reprocessing due to quality issues identified during or after this operation.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity of units rejected or scrapped during this operation due to quality defects or production errors.']
  setup_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time required to prepare the work center, tools, and materials before starting production for this operation, measured in minutes.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Production shift during which this operation was executed. Used for shift performance analysis and labor scheduling.']
  standard_allowed_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Standard time allocated for completing this operation per unit, measured in minutes. Used for capacity planning, costing, and efficiency measurement in apparel manufacturing.']
  teardown_time_minutes decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time required to clean up, reset, or prepare the work center for the next operation after completing this operation, measured in minutes.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantities in this operation (EA=Each, PC=Piece, DZ=Dozen, PR=Pair, SET=Set, KG=Kilogram, M=Meter, YD=Yard). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|DZ|PR|SET|KG|M|YD — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of good output relative to input for this operation, calculated as (confirmed_quantity / planned_quantity) * 100. Key metric for production efficiency.']

  Note: 'Individual manufacturing operation or routing step within a work order, representing each discrete production activity (cutting, sewing, finishing, pressing, packing). Captures operation sequence number, operation code, work center, standard time (SAM — Standard Allowed Minutes), actual time, planned quantity, confirmed quantity, operation status, and yield percentage. Derived from SAP PP routing operations.'
}

Table production.order_confirmation {
  order_confirmation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production order confirmation record. Primary key for the goods confirmation transaction.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center to which labor and overhead costs from this confirmation are allocated. Used for management accounting.']
  material_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the finished good or semi-finished material being produced in this operation. Links to the product master data.']
  primary_order_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production operator or worker who performed and confirmed this operation. Used for labor tracking and performance management.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility or plant where this production confirmation occurred. Used for multi-site production tracking.']
  reversed_confirmation_order_confirmation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original confirmation record that this reversal transaction is cancelling. Creates audit trail for corrections.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing work center or production line where this operation was executed. Represents the physical production resource.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production work order against which this confirmation is recorded. Links to the manufacturing order being executed.']
  work_order_operation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific operation within the work order routing that this confirmation applies to. Represents a discrete manufacturing step.']
  activity_type_code varchar [note: 'Classification of the production activity performed during this operation. Used for activity-based costing and capacity planning.']
  actual_labor_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of labor hours consumed to complete this operation. Used for labor cost calculation and capacity planning.']
  actual_machine_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of machine or equipment hours utilized during this operation. Used for equipment utilization analysis and maintenance scheduling.']
  backflush_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether component materials were automatically consumed via backflushing when this confirmation was posted. Used for automated material consumption.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'The production batch or lot number assigned to the output from this confirmation. Used for traceability and quality control.']
  confirmation_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the production activity was completed and confirmed. Represents the actual manufacturing event date.']
  confirmation_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing confirmation document number generated by the production system. Used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  confirmation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the confirmation record indicating whether it is in draft, submitted for approval, posted to inventory, cancelled, or reversed.']
  confirmation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the confirmation transaction was recorded in the system. Used for audit trail and real-time production tracking.']
  confirmation_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the confirmation transaction indicating whether this is a partial completion, final completion, milestone achievement, rework confirmation, or scrap reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this confirmation record was first created in the database. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  final_confirmation_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the final confirmation for the operation, signaling that no further output is expected and the operation can be closed.']
  goods_movement_posted boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the confirmed quantities have been posted to inventory through a goods receipt transaction. Links confirmation to inventory update.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'The material document number generated when confirmed output was posted to inventory. Used for inventory reconciliation and traceability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when this confirmation record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the confirmation was posted to inventory and financial ledgers. May differ from confirmation date for period-end adjustments.']
  quality_inspection_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this confirmation triggers a mandatory quality inspection before the output can be moved to finished goods inventory.']
  quality_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the quality inspection associated with this confirmation. Determines whether output can be released to inventory.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this confirmation has been reversed or cancelled. Used to track corrections and adjustments to previously posted confirmations.']
  rework_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units that require rework or reprocessing to meet quality standards. Represents non-conforming output that can be salvaged.']
  scrap_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of material or units that were scrapped or rejected during this production operation due to defects or quality failures.']
  setup_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The time spent preparing equipment, tooling, and materials before production could begin. Separate from actual production time.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'The production shift during which this confirmation was recorded. Used for shift-based performance analysis and labor scheduling.']
  teardown_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The time spent cleaning, resetting, or breaking down equipment after the production operation was completed.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The standard unit in which quantities are measured and reported for this confirmation. Common values include EA (each), PC (piece), KG (kilogram), M (meter).']
  variance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation providing additional context about production variances, quality issues, or operational challenges encountered during this confirmation.']
  variance_reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for any variance between planned and actual quantities or times. Used for root cause analysis of production inefficiencies.']
  yield_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'The actual quantity of good output produced and confirmed for this operation. Represents conforming units that passed quality inspection.']

  Note: 'Goods confirmation event recording actual output quantities, scrap, rework, and labor hours against a work order operation. Captures confirmation date, confirmed yield quantity, scrap quantity, rework quantity, actual machine time, actual labor time, shift, operator ID, and confirmation type (partial, final). Represents the SAP PP goods confirmation transaction (CO11N/CO15).'
}

Table production.production_factory {
  production_factory_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturing facility. Primary key for the factory entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Factories operate as cost centers for overhead allocation, factory performance analysis, and manufacturing cost tracking—fundamental to apparel manufacturing accounting and factory profitability analy']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Factories require operational managers for production oversight, capacity planning, compliance audits, and vendor relationship management. Links factory operations to internal employee accountability ']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Factories map to profit centers for regional/brand P&L segmentation in multi-brand apparel operations, enabling factory-level margin analysis and strategic sourcing decisions.']
  active_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the factory is currently active and available for new production orders. Inactive factories are retained for historical reference but excluded from sourcing and planning.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the factory facility. Used for shipping, audits, and official correspondence.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as building name, floor, or suite number.']
  audit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score from the most recent audit, typically on a 0-100 scale. Used for factory performance benchmarking and risk assessment.']
  certified_capacity_units_per_week varchar [note: 'Maximum production capacity in units per week as certified by the factory and validated through capacity audits. Used for production planning and allocation decisions.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the factory is located. Used for logistics planning and regional risk assessment.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the factory is located. Used for duty calculation, trade compliance, and origin marking requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this factory record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code used for pricing and invoicing with this factory (e.g., USD, EUR, CNY, BDT, VND).']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for factory communications, Purchase Order (PO) transmission, and Time and Action (TNA) updates.']
  employee_count varchar [note: 'Total number of employees working at the factory. Used for capacity assessment, social compliance monitoring, and risk evaluation.']
  facility_size_sqm varchar [note: 'Total floor area of the factory in square meters. Used for capacity planning and facility assessment.']
  factory_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the factory, used across systems and documentation. Typically alphanumeric code assigned during factory onboarding.']
  factory_name varchar [note: 'Common business name of the manufacturing facility as used in operational communications and planning.']
  factory_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the manufacturing relationship model. CMT (Cut Make Trim) = factory provides labor only; OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturer) = factory manufactures to buyer specs; ODM (Original Design Manufacturer) = factory provides design and manufacturing; INTERNAL = company-owned facility; HYBRID = mixed model; SUBCONTRACTOR = secondary tier supplier.']
  incoterm varchar [note: 'Standard International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) used for shipments from this factory. Defines responsibility for shipping costs, insurance, and risk transfer. FOB (Free on Board) is most common in apparel. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FOB|FCA|CIF|DDP|DAP|CPT|CIP — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance or quality audit conducted at the factory. Used to track audit recency and schedule follow-up audits.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard production lead time in days from Purchase Order (PO) confirmation to ex-factory shipment readiness. Used in Time and Action (TNA) calendar planning.']
  legal_entity_name varchar [note: 'Official registered legal name of the factory entity as it appears on contracts, licenses, and regulatory filings.']
  moq_units varchar [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity in units that the factory requires per style or per order to accept production. Critical for buy plan feasibility and allocation planning.']
  next_audit_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next compliance or quality audit. Used for audit planning and vendor compliance monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional factory information, special handling instructions, strategic notes, or historical context not captured in structured fields.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date when the factory was approved and onboarded into the company\'s vendor network. Used for relationship tenure analysis and vendor lifecycle tracking.']
  otif_performance_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of orders delivered On Time In Full over the trailing 12 months. Key performance indicator for factory reliability and used in allocation decisions.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms negotiated with the factory (e.g., Net 30, Net 60, Letter of Credit (LC), 30% deposit + 70% on shipment). Used for cash flow planning and Purchase Order (PO) processing.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the factory. Used for operational coordination and emergency communication.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the factory location. Used for logistics and regional analysis.']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this factory has preferred vendor status based on performance, quality, compliance, and strategic relationship. Preferred vendors receive priority allocation and longer-term commitments.']
  production_capabilities varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of product categories and manufacturing techniques the factory is equipped to produce (e.g., woven, knit, denim, footwear, accessories, leather goods, outerwear, activewear, swimwear, intimate apparel).']
  quality_rating varchar [note: 'Letter grade quality rating based on defect rates, inspection results, and customer returns. A = excellent (defect rate <1%), B = good (1-2%), C = acceptable (2-4%), D = needs improvement (4-6%), F = unacceptable (>6%).']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification for the factory based on compliance history, audit findings, geographic risk, and operational stability. Used for sourcing decisions and monitoring frequency.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region where the factory is located. Used for regional compliance and logistics routing.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite sustainability score (0-100) based on environmental practices, energy efficiency, waste management, water usage, and chemical compliance. Used for ESG reporting and sustainable sourcing initiatives.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the business relationship with the factory was terminated or the factory was deactivated. Null for active factories.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the factory location (e.g., Asia/Shanghai, Asia/Dhaka, Europe/Istanbul). Used for scheduling meetings, coordinating shipments, and Time and Action (TNA) planning.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this factory record was last modified. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']

  Note: 'Master record for each manufacturing facility (CMT factory, OEM, ODM) engaged in production. Captures factory code, factory name, legal entity name, country, city, address, factory type (CMT/OEM/ODM/internal), production capabilities (woven, knit, denim, footwear, accessories), certified capacity (units/week), compliance certifications (WRAP, BSCI, FLA, OEKO-TEX, SA8000), audit status, lead time profile, minimum order quantity, and active flag. SSOT for factory identity within the production domain; supplier domain owns the broader vendor relationship and commercial terms.'
}

Table production.factory_capacity {
  factory_capacity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the factory capacity planning record.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Capacity planning ties to factory overhead budgets for resource allocation decisions, enabling capacity cost planning and factory investment budget tracking in apparel manufacturing operations.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.product_category. Business justification: Capacity planning by product category (knits vs wovens, footwear vs apparel) reflects different machine/skill requirements. Enables category-level capacity allocation and booking. Removes denormalized']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manufacturing facility or CMT (Cut Make Trim) partner to which this capacity plan applies.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchandise season for which capacity is reserved or allocated.']
  work_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.work_center. Business justification: Factory capacity is often planned and tracked at the work center or production line level. The table currently has production_line_id (STRING) but no formal FK to work_center. Adding work_center_id en']
  allocated_capacity_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Production capacity units already allocated to confirmed purchase orders (PO) or work orders during the planning period.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the production planner or manager who approved this capacity plan.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity plan was approved for execution.']
  available_capacity_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining production capacity units available for new bookings, calculated as total capacity minus allocated and reserved capacity.']
  available_labor_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor hours available for production during the planning period, based on workforce headcount and shift schedules.']
  available_machine_hours decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total machine hours available for production during the planning period, accounting for scheduled maintenance and downtime.']
  booking_cutoff_date date [note: 'Last date by which new production orders can be booked for this capacity period.']
  booking_status varchar [note: 'Current booking status of the capacity period, indicating whether additional orders can be accepted.']
  capacity_allocation_priority varchar [note: 'Priority level for capacity allocation, used to manage competing demands from multiple customers or product lines.']
  capacity_buffer_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Safety buffer percentage applied to capacity planning to account for variability, quality issues, or unexpected delays.']
  capacity_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the factory or production line during the planning period.']
  capacity_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure used to express production capacity (e.g., pieces, garments, dozens, pairs).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity planning record was first created in the system.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Manufacturing department or process stage to which this capacity allocation applies (e.g., cutting, sewing, finishing). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: cutting|sewing|finishing|packing|embroidery|printing|dyeing|quality_control — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  efficiency_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Factory or production line efficiency rate as a percentage, representing actual output versus theoretical maximum capacity.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this capacity plan is currently active and in use for production scheduling.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Standard production lead time in days from order confirmation to shipment for orders booked in this capacity period.']
  machine_count varchar [note: 'Total number of production machines or equipment units available during the planning period.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum order quantity (MOQ) in units that the factory requires for accepting new production bookings during this period.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments regarding capacity constraints, special conditions, or planning assumptions for this period.']
  overtime_hours_available decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional overtime hours available for production if needed to meet demand during the planning period.']
  planned_downtime_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total hours of planned downtime for maintenance, changeovers, or scheduled breaks during the planning period.']
  planning_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the capacity planning period (weekly or monthly bucket).']
  planning_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the capacity planning period (weekly or monthly bucket).']
  planning_period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the capacity planning period (daily, weekly, or monthly).']
  reserved_capacity_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Production capacity units reserved for specific seasons, collections, or customers but not yet confirmed by purchase order.']
  shift_count varchar [note: 'Number of production shifts per day during the planning period (e.g., 1, 2, or 3 shifts).']
  standard_minute_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average standard minute value (SMV) per unit for the product mix planned during this capacity period, used for capacity calculation.']
  total_capacity_units decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total production capacity expressed in units (pieces, garments, or SKUs) that can be manufactured during the planning period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity planning record was last modified.']
  utilization_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Capacity utilization rate as a percentage, calculated as allocated capacity divided by total available capacity.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the capacity plan, incremented with each revision to support change tracking and audit trail.']
  workforce_headcount varchar [note: 'Total number of production workers assigned to the factory or production line during the planning period.']
  working_days varchar [note: 'Number of working days available during the planning period, excluding holidays and factory closures.']

  Note: 'Weekly or monthly capacity plan and reservation record for a factory, capturing available machine hours, labor hours, allocated capacity (units), reserved capacity by season/booking, efficiency rate, utilization percentage, and booking status by production line or department. Supports OTB capacity planning, T&A scheduling, forward capacity booking, and load balancing across manufacturing partners.'
}

Table production.schedule {
  schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the production schedule record. Primary key for the master production schedule (MPS) entity.']
  delivery_window_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the target delivery window or time bucket for finished goods. Aligns production schedule with retail distribution calendar and store delivery requirements.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production planner responsible for this schedule. Identifies the person accountable for schedule creation and maintenance.']
  factory_capacity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to production.factory_capacity. Business justification: Production schedules allocate capacity from factory capacity plans. The MPS (Master Production Schedule) record should reference the specific capacity plan/slot it is consuming. This enables capacity ']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility where production is scheduled. Identifies the factory partner or internal plant assigned to execute this production run.']
  production_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific production line or work center within the factory allocated for this schedule. Represents the capacity slot assignment.']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fashion season or collection period this production schedule supports. Links schedule to merchandising calendar for seasonal product delivery.']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_sketch. Business justification: Production schedules reference design sketches for visual production planning boards, design-driven scheduling, and design complexity-based capacity allocation. Enables design-aware production plannin']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the work order being scheduled. Links this schedule record to the production work order that defines what is being manufactured.']
  actual_cut_date date [note: 'Actual date when fabric cutting operations began. Used for On-Time In-Full (OTIF) performance tracking and schedule variance analysis.']
  actual_ex_factory_date date [note: 'Actual date when finished goods left the factory. Critical for OTIF performance measurement and supply chain delivery tracking.']
  actual_finishing_date date [note: 'Actual date when finishing operations completed. Used for quality gate tracking and final production milestone confirmation.']
  actual_sew_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when sewing operations completed. Used for cycle time analysis and production efficiency measurement.']
  actual_sew_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when sewing operations commenced. Used for production milestone tracking and factory performance measurement.']
  change_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for the most recent schedule change or revision. Captures rationale for date shifts, quantity adjustments, or factory reassignments to support root cause analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production schedule record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and schedule age analysis.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule is on the critical path for seasonal delivery. True for schedules with no slack time that directly impact retail delivery deadlines.']
  factory_capacity_utilization_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of factory production line capacity consumed by this schedule. Used for capacity planning and factory loading optimization.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who most recently updated this production schedule record. Used for accountability and change management.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this production schedule record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days from cut date to ex-factory date. Represents the complete production cycle time for this schedule.']
  locked_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the planner or manager who locked this schedule. Locked schedules are committed to factory and cannot be changed without approval.']
  locked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this schedule was locked. Marks the point when the schedule became committed and change-controlled.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about this production schedule. Captures special instructions, constraints, or coordination details for factory partners.']
  otif_target_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this schedule met the On-Time In-Full (OTIF) delivery target. True if actual ex-factory date matched planned date and full quantity was delivered.']
  planned_cut_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for fabric cutting operations to begin. First milestone in the Cut-Make-Trim (CMT) production process, marking when raw materials are cut into garment pieces.']
  planned_ex_factory_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for finished goods to leave the factory. Critical milestone for Free On Board (FOB) shipment and supply chain handoff to logistics partners.']
  planned_finishing_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for finishing operations including washing, pressing, quality inspection, and packaging. Final production milestone before ex-factory shipment.']
  planned_sew_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for sewing operations to complete. Marks the end of garment assembly before finishing operations begin.']
  planned_sew_start_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for sewing operations to commence. Marks the beginning of garment assembly on the production line.']
  planning_group varchar [note: 'Planning group or team responsible for this schedule. Used for workload distribution and organizational reporting.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for production scheduling and resource allocation. Rush and urgent priorities receive expedited handling and preferential capacity allocation.']
  production_method varchar [note: 'Manufacturing model for this production schedule. Cut-Make-Trim (CMT) is contract manufacturing, Free On Board (FOB) is full-package manufacturing, Original Design Manufacturer (ODM) provides design and manufacturing, Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) manufactures to buyer specifications, and in-house is internal production.']
  production_type varchar [note: 'Classification of production run type. Pre-production (PP) samples are for approval, bulk production is main volume manufacturing, pilot runs are small test batches, and reorders are repeat production of proven styles.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the production schedule record. Human-readable schedule number used in communications with factory partners and internal planning teams.']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the production schedule. Draft schedules are under planning, confirmed schedules are committed to factory, locked schedules cannot be changed, in-progress schedules are actively executing, completed schedules are finished, cancelled schedules are voided, and on-hold schedules are temporarily suspended. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|locked|in_progress|completed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  scheduled_quantity varchar [note: 'Total quantity of units scheduled for production in this schedule record. Represents the planned output volume for the production run.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of this schedule record. Increments with each revision to track schedule changes and maintain audit trail of planning iterations.']
  change_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent schedule change was made. Used for change frequency analysis and schedule stability measurement.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who created this production schedule record. Used for accountability and audit purposes.']

  Note: 'Master production schedule (MPS) record linking work orders to factory capacity slots, defining planned cut dates, sew start dates, sew end dates, finishing dates, and ex-factory dates by production line. Captures schedule version, schedule status (draft, confirmed, locked), season, delivery window, critical path flags, and schedule change reason. Supports T&A calendar management, on-time delivery tracking (OTIF), and weekly production planning meetings with factory partners.'
}

Table production.production_tna_milestone {
  production_tna_milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Time and Action milestone record. Primary key for tracking critical production lifecycle events.']
  approver_contact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.contact. Business justification: Critical TNA milestones (fabric approval, sample approval, shipment authorization) require buyer contact approval in B2B workflows. Linking enables approval routing, notification, and compliance track']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.concept. Business justification: TNA tracks concept approval milestones, concept review gates, and concept-to-sketch transition events for collection development timeline management. Enables concept-phase critical path tracking.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific party (factory, supplier, vendor, team) responsible for executing this milestone. Links to the appropriate master data entity based on responsible_party_type.']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing factory or production facility where this milestone is executed. Critical for factory performance analysis and capacity planning.']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: TNA (Time and Action) milestones include regulatory filing deadlines (customs declarations, import permits, certificates of origin). Links production timeline to compliance filing requirements - criti']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_sketch. Business justification: TNA milestones track design sketch approval gates, design handoff events, and design revision completion in the critical path. Essential for design-phase timeline management.']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product style or design this milestone tracks. Enables milestone tracking at the style level across multiple production runs.']
  work_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the production work order or manufacturing order this milestone belongs to. Links milestone to the production execution context.']
  actual_date date [note: 'The actual date when the milestone was achieved or completed. Used for OTIF (On Time In Full) performance measurement and variance analysis.']
  actual_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the milestone was completed, including time of day. Provides granular tracking for time-sensitive milestones and shift-level analysis.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the milestone was formally approved. Null if approval is not required or not yet approved.']
  approval_required_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this milestone requires formal approval before proceeding to the next stage (e.g., PP sample approval, fabric approval). True if approval gate exists.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the approval process for this milestone if approval is required. Indicates whether approval is pending, approved, rejected, or conditionally approved. Not_required if no approval needed.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments or notes about this milestone execution. Captures additional context, issues, or observations from the responsible party or production team.']
  days_variance varchar [note: 'Number of days difference between planned (or revised) date and actual date. Positive values indicate delay, negative values indicate early completion. Key metric for OTIF performance.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for milestone delay if the milestone was not achieved on time. Used for root cause analysis and process improvement. Null if milestone was on time.']
  delay_reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description or notes explaining the reason for the delay. Provides context beyond the standardized delay reason code. Null if milestone was on time.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the destination market of this production order. Used for logistics milestone tracking and country-specific lead time analysis.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this milestone record is currently active and relevant. False if the milestone was cancelled or superseded by a revised plan.']
  is_critical_path boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this milestone is on the critical path for the production schedule. True if any delay to this milestone will delay the overall ex-factory date.']
  is_customer_visible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this milestone is visible to the customer or buyer in external milestone tracking reports. True for customer-facing milestones like PP sample approval and ex-factory.']
  milestone_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential order of this milestone within the production lifecycle for the work order or style. Used to establish the chronological flow of production events.']
  milestone_status varchar [note: 'Current execution status of the milestone. Indicates whether the milestone is pending, in-progress, completed, overdue, cancelled, or on hold.']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'The type of production milestone being tracked. Defines the specific critical event in the Time and Action calendar (fabric approval, PP sample submission/approval, bulk fabric receipt, cut start, sew start, finishing, QC inspection, ex-factory, cargo receipt). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: fabric_approval|pp_sample_submission|pp_sample_approval|bulk_fabric_receipt|cut_start|sew_start|finishing_start|qc_inspection|ex_factory|cargo_receipt — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  planned_date date [note: 'Original planned date for achieving this milestone as defined in the initial Time and Action calendar. Baseline for measuring schedule adherence.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'External purchase order number from the buyer or customer associated with this production milestone. Used for customer-facing milestone reporting.']
  quantity_at_milestone decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units (pieces, garments, SKUs) that reached this milestone. Enables tracking of partial completions and yield analysis across production stages.']
  recorded_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user or system that recorded this milestone completion. Provides audit trail for data entry accountability.']
  recorded_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was created or updated in the system. Distinct from actual_timestamp which represents the real-world event time.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party responsible for this milestone. Denormalized for reporting convenience and historical tracking even if party master data changes.']
  responsible_party_type varchar [note: 'The type of party responsible for completing this milestone (internal team, factory, supplier, vendor, logistics provider, quality agency). Categorizes accountability.']
  revised_date date [note: 'Updated target date for the milestone if the original plan was revised. Captures schedule adjustments due to delays, expedites, or other changes. Null if no revision occurred.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The fashion season or collection code this milestone belongs to (e.g., SS24, FW24). Enables season-level milestone performance analysis.']
  shipping_mode varchar [note: 'The mode of transportation for shipment-related milestones (ex-factory, cargo receipt). Impacts lead time expectations and milestone planning.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity at milestone (pieces, units, dozens, cartons, pairs). Standardizes quantity reporting across different production contexts.']

  Note: 'Time and Action (T&A) calendar milestone record tracking each critical event in the production lifecycle for a style or work order, including both planned and actual execution data. Captures milestone type (fabric approval, PP sample submission/approval, bulk fabric receipt, cut start, sew start, finishing, QC inspection, ex-factory, cargo receipt), planned date, revised date, actual date, actual timestamp, days variance, quantity at milestone, responsible party, milestone status (pending, in-progress, completed, overdue), delay reason code, and recorded-by reference. Core tool for production milestone tracking and OTIF performance measurement.'
}

Direct 1:1 link between sample submission and test results - standard ']

Linking to contact enables approval workflow routing, notification delivery, and audit trail of who approved what. re']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to design.design_sketch. Business justification: Pre-production samples are physical manifestations of specific design sketches for fit, construction, and aesthetic approval. Fundamental sample approval workflow linking design intent to physical val']

Business justification: Pre-production samples are submitted to wholesale buyers for approval before bulk production.   colorway_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the specific color variant of the style for this sample.']

  review_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the sample review and approval decision must be completed.']
  revision_round varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which iteration of the sample this represents (1 = first submission, 2 = first revision, etc.).']
