  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company\'s local reporting currency used in financial consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this payment record was last modified or updated. Critical for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  net_payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The final net amount transferred after applying discounts and deducting bank charges. Represents the actual cash movement.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The business date when the payment transaction was executed or processed. Distinct from value date and posting date.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The instrument or mechanism used to execute the payment: wire transfer, ACH (Automated Clearing House), SEPA (Single Euro Payments Area), check, Letter of Credit (LC), electronic funds transfer, or cash. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wire_transfer|ach|sepa|check|lc|electronic_funds_transfer|cash — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_run_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the automated payment run batch in SAP S/4HANA FI that grouped multiple payments for processing. Used for batch reconciliation and audit trails.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment: pending approval, cleared through bank, reconciled with bank statement, failed processing, cancelled, or in transit.']
  payment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the payment transaction: outgoing vendor payments, incoming customer receipts, intercompany netting settlements, Letter of Credit (LC) settlements, bank transfers, or refunds.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The accounting period date when the payment was posted to the General Ledger (GL) in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used for period-end close (EOM) and financial consolidation.']
  priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to the payment for processing sequence in payment runs: urgent, high, normal, or low.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when this payment was successfully reconciled with the bank statement, completing the cash management cycle.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of bank statement reconciliation: matched to bank statement, unmatched, partially matched, or under review by treasury team.']
  reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or remittance advice identifier provided by the payer or payee for payment tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The tax amount (VAT, GST, withholding tax) associated with the payment transaction, if applicable. Used for tax reporting and compliance.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code applied to the payment transaction in SAP S/4HANA FI, determining tax calculation rules and reporting requirements.']
  terms varchar [note: 'The agreed payment terms between buyer and seller (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30, FOB, DDP). Influences discount eligibility and cash flow timing.']
  value_date date [note: 'The effective date when funds are available or debited for interest calculation and cash positioning purposes. Critical for treasury management and cash flow analysis.']

  Note: 'Records outgoing vendor payments and incoming customer receipts processed through SAP S/4HANA FI, including wire transfers, Letters of Credit (LC), ACH/SEPA, checks, and intercompany netting settlements. Captures payment method, house bank and bank account, value date, clearing document reference, exchange rate at payment date, payment run ID, reconciliation status, and bank statement matching result. Supports AP/AR clearing, cash positioning, LC settlement for apparel import transactions, and bank reconciliation.'
}

Table finance.budget {
  budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the budget record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budgets are allocated to cost centers for cost planning. Normalizing STRING cost_center_code to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_code STRING column.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budgets are planned by GL account for financial planning. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainability_initiative. Business justification: Links initiative funding to financial planning. Enables ESG capex tracking, sustainability ROI analysis, and budget variance reporting for decarbonization projects—standard for integrated financial/su']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Annual compliance budgets are planned by obligation type (CPSIA testing, REACH compliance, factory audits) in apparel. Links budget allocation to specific regulatory requirements for compliance spend ']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Budget ownership must link to employee for accountability, approval workflows, and budget variance responsibility. Apparel companies require budget_owner FK for budget approval routing and performance']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budgets are allocated to profit centers for profitability planning. Normalizing STRING profit_center_code to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profit_center_code S']
  actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual amount spent or incurred against this budget to date. Updated from financial actuals in the general ledger.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of budget that has been allocated or distributed to specific cost centers, projects, or line items.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this budget was formally approved by the authorized approver or budget committee.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual who approved this budget (e.g., CFO, Finance Director, Budget Committee Chair).']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the budget plan (e.g., FY2024 OPEX Budget, Spring Collection COGS Budget).']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget plan in the approval and execution workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DRAFT|SUBMITTED|APPROVED|REJECTED|ACTIVE|CLOSED|REVISED — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Classification of budget type: OPEX (Operating Expenses), CAPEX (Capital Expenditures), COGS (Cost of Goods Sold), OTB (Open-to-Buy), REVENUE, or EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization).']
  channel varchar [note: 'The sales or distribution channel for which this budget applies. DTC stands for Direct-to-Consumer.']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of budget that has been committed through purchase orders or contractual obligations but not yet spent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget record was first created in the system. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  end_date date [note: 'The effective end date of the budget period (e.g., 2024-12-31 for FY2024 annual budget).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which this budget applies (e.g., 2024).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this budget is currently active and in use for financial tracking and reporting. True for active, False for inactive or superseded budgets.']
  is_baseline boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the baseline (original approved) budget version. True for baseline, False for revised versions.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this budget record was last modified. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the budget, including assumptions, constraints, or special considerations.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'The granularity of budget period allocation: MONTHLY (12 periods), QUARTERLY (4 periods), or ANNUAL (1 period).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for budget allocation (e.g., USA, EUR, APAC). Uses ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country codes or regional abbreviations.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number tracking changes to the budget. Starts at 0 for original budget, increments with each revision.']
  revision_reason varchar [note: 'Business justification or reason for budget revision (e.g., Market Conditions Change, Strategic Initiative, Cost Overrun, Revenue Shortfall).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The system of record from which this budget data originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA CO for controlling budgets, Anaplan for merchandise financial planning).']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this budget record in the source system (e.g., SAP budget document number, Anaplan budget ID).']
  start_date date [note: 'The effective start date of the budget period (e.g., 2024-01-01 for FY2024 annual budget).']
  total_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total planned budget amount for the entire fiscal period, in the specified currency. Sum of all period-level allocations.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The variance between budgeted and actual amounts (calculated as total_budget_amount minus actual_amount). Positive indicates under-budget, negative indicates over-budget.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The variance expressed as a percentage of the total budget amount. Calculated as (variance_amount / total_budget_amount) * 100.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the budget (e.g., V1, V2, REVISED, FINAL). Supports multiple iterations of budget planning cycles.']

  Note: 'Financial budget plan in SAP S/4HANA CO and Anaplan capturing budget header (version, fiscal year, type, owner, approval status) and granular period-level line allocations (monthly/quarterly amounts by GL account, cost center, profit center, channel). Supports budget types including OPEX, CAPEX, COGS, and OTB (Open-to-Buy). Each line stores planned, revised, committed, and actual amounts with variance calculations at the period level. Enables merchandise financial planning, OTB management, budget vs. actual tracking, and EBITDA target-setting for apparel fashion operations.'
}

Table finance.budget_line {
  budget_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the budget line item. Primary key.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent budget plan that contains this line item.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Budget lines are allocated to cost centers at granular level. Normalizing STRING cost_center_code to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_code STRING column.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Budget lines are planned by GL account at granular level. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column.']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Budget lines track monthly/quarterly compliance spending by obligation (certifications, audits, testing programs). Enables period-level compliance budget tracking, variance reporting, and spend author']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Budget lines are allocated to profit centers at granular level. Normalizing STRING profit_center_code to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profit_center_code STRIN']
  actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual amount spent to date against this budget line, based on posted financial transactions.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for this budget line (pending management review, approved, rejected).']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who approved this budget line.']
  approved_date date [note: 'Date when this budget line was approved.']
  available_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Remaining budget available for spending (revised_amount - committed_amount - actual_amount). Used for Open-to-Buy (OTB) management.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'High-level budget category classification (Cost of Goods Sold, operating expense, capital expenditure, marketing, payroll, overhead).']
  budget_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget line (draft, approved, active, frozen for no further changes, closed at period end).']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Type of budget allocation (operational, capital, project-based, discretionary).']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Sales channel to which this budget line applies (retail stores, wholesale, e-commerce, direct-to-consumer, outlet).']
  committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount committed through purchase orders or contractual obligations but not yet spent.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget line record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this budget line (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period within the year (monthly M01-M12, quarterly Q1-Q4, or half-yearly H1-H2).']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which this budget line is planned (e.g., 2024).']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget line is locked from further edits or reallocations (True/False).']
  is_otb_controlled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this budget line is subject to Open-to-Buy controls for merchandise purchasing (True/False).']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the budget plan for ordering and reference.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this budget line record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context, justification, or instructions for this budget line.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the fiscal period covered by this budget line.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the fiscal period covered by this budget line.']
  planned_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original planned budget amount allocated to this line for the period.']
  revised_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revised budget amount after adjustments, reallocations, or amendments during the fiscal period.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Variance between revised budget and actual spend (revised_amount - actual_amount). Positive indicates under-spend, negative indicates over-spend.']
  variance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Variance as a percentage of the revised budget ((variance_amount / revised_amount) * 100).']

  Note: 'Granular budget line item within a budget plan, capturing period-level (monthly/quarterly) allocations by GL account, cost center, profit center, and channel. Stores planned amount, revised amount, committed amount, actual spend to date, variance, and period. Enables detailed budget vs. actual tracking, OTB management, and financial planning drill-down for apparel merchandise and operations.'
}

Table finance.cogs_entry {
  cogs_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the COGS accounting entry. Primary key for the COGS entry record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: COGS entries are allocated to cost centers for cost tracking. Normalizing STRING cost_center_code to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_code STRING column.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: COGS entries post to GL accounts for financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: COGS recognition requires traceability to production lot for FIFO/LIFO costing methods and margin analysis by batch quality/origin. Essential for apparel cost accounting and inventory valuation compli']
  original_entry_cogs_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original COGS entry ID if this entry is a reversal or adjustment, supporting audit trail and reconciliation.']
  product_safety_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_safety_test. Business justification: Safety testing costs (lead, flammability, chemical restrictions) are allocated to COGS for specific SKUs in apparel. Enables accurate product costing including mandatory compliance testing expenses fo']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: COGS entries are allocated to profit centers for profitability analysis. Normalizing STRING profit_center_code to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profit_center_c']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the retail store where the sale occurred, if applicable to retail channel transactions.']
  sales_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order that triggered this COGS recognition event.']
  sales_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific sales order line item for which COGS is being recognized.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or manufacturer who produced this product, supporting supplier profitability analysis.']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the warehouse or distribution center from which the product was fulfilled.']
  actual_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The actual cost per unit incurred for this SKU, including all manufacturing and procurement costs.']
  collection_code varchar [note: 'The collection or line code for the product, supporting collection-level margin and IMU/MMU tracking.']
  colorway_code varchar [note: 'The color variant code for the product style.']
  cost_variance decimal(18,4) [note: 'The difference between standard cost and actual cost per unit, used for cost control and variance analysis.']
  costing_method varchar [note: 'The inventory costing method applied to calculate the COGS for this entry (standard, average, FIFO, LIFO, or specific identification).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or system process that created this COGS entry record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this COGS entry record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this COGS entry.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The accounting document number in the financial system for this COGS posting, supporting audit and reconciliation.']
  duty_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The customs duty and tariff cost component allocated to this unit for international shipments.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for this COGS entry, supporting EOM close and period reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this COGS entry is recognized for financial reporting purposes.']
  fob_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The FOB cost component representing the cost of goods at the point of shipment from the supplier or manufacturer.']
  freight_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The freight and transportation cost component allocated to this unit for shipping from origin to destination.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or system process that last modified this COGS entry record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this COGS entry record was last modified or updated.']
  ldp_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The total landed cost including FOB, freight, duty, and all other costs to deliver the product to the final destination.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which this COGS entry was posted to the general ledger, which may differ from recognition date due to period-end adjustments.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'The merchandise category code for the product, enabling category-level profitability analysis.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number under which this product was procured, linking COGS to procurement transactions.']
  quantity_sold decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of units sold for this SKU in this transaction, triggering the COGS recognition.']
  recognition_date date [note: 'The accounting date on which the COGS was recognized, typically aligned with revenue recognition per IFRS 15 or GAAP ASC 606.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this COGS entry is a reversal or adjustment of a previous entry.']
  sales_channel varchar [note: 'The sales channel through which the product was sold, enabling channel-specific GMROI and margin analysis.']
  sales_region_code varchar [note: 'The geographic region code where the sale occurred, enabling regional profitability analysis.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The merchandising season code for the product sold, enabling seasonal profitability and GMROI analysis.']
  size_code varchar [note: 'The size code for the product (e.g., XS, S, M, L, XL, or numeric sizing).']
  sku varchar [note: 'The unique SKU identifier for the apparel product sold, representing the specific style, colorway, and size combination.']
  standard_cost decimal(18,4) [note: 'The predetermined standard cost per unit for this SKU as maintained in the cost accounting system, used for variance analysis.']
  style_code varchar [note: 'The style identifier for the apparel product, representing the base design before colorway and size variations.']
  total_cogs_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total COGS amount for this entry, calculated as actual cost multiplied by quantity sold.']
  valuation_class varchar [note: 'The SAP valuation class used for inventory valuation and COGS calculation, supporting material ledger accounting.']

  Note: 'Cost of Goods Sold accounting entry capturing the COGS recognition event for each apparel product sold, derived from SAP S/4HANA MM/PP and SD integration. Stores SKU, style, colorway, size, sales order reference, quantity sold, standard cost, actual cost, cost variance, landed cost components (FOB, freight, duty, LDP), and COGS GL account. Foundational for gross margin analysis, GMROI calculation, and IMU/MMU tracking by product and channel.'
}

Table finance.landed_cost {
  landed_cost_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for landed_cost']
  asn_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.asn. Business justification: Landed cost calculation (duty, freight, insurance) is triggered by ASN receipt and must trace to specific advance shipment notice for accurate cost allocation to received goods. Standard apparel impor']
  carbon_emission_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.carbon_emission. Business justification: Links freight/duty costs to transportation emissions. Enables Scope 3 Category 4 (upstream transportation) allocation to specific shipments for CDP disclosure and carbon-adjusted landed cost analysis ']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Landed cost records represent imported goods cost breakdowns and must be tied to a legal entity (company code) for proper financial accounting, statutory reporting, and consolidation. This FK is essen']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt document that triggered the landed cost calculation. Links to the inbound inventory receipt event in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  lot_batch_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_batch. Business justification: Landed costs must be allocated to production lots for accurate unit cost calculation and COGS recognition. Apparel costing requires lot-level traceability for dye lots, factory batches, and origin-spe']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the purchase order for which this landed cost is calculated. Links to the originating procurement transaction in SAP S/4HANA MM.']
  product_safety_test_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.product_safety_test. Business justification: Testing costs are a component of total landed cost for apparel imports. Links safety test expenses to shipment-level landed cost calculations for accurate duty-paid cost determination and inventory va']
  shipment_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the shipment or consignment that transported the goods. Links to logistics and transportation records.']
  sku_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific SKU for which the landed cost is being calculated. Identifies the product at the most granular inventory level.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor from whom the goods were sourced. Links to the supplier master record.']
  air_freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of air freight transportation if goods were shipped by air. Component of landed cost for expedited shipments.']
  calculation_date date [note: 'The date on which the landed cost calculation was performed. Used for financial period assignment and audit purposes.']
  cost_allocation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to allocate shared costs (freight, insurance) across multiple SKUs in a shipment. Common methods: weight-based, volume-based, value-based, unit count.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this landed cost record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this landed cost record. Typically the functional currency of the importing entity.']
  customs_brokerage_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The fee paid to customs brokers for handling import documentation and customs clearance procedures.']
  customs_duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The customs duty paid to import the goods into the destination country. Calculated based on HS code tariff rate and customs value.']
  customs_value_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The declared value of the goods for customs purposes. Basis for calculating customs duty and import taxes.']
  destination_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the destination country where the goods are imported. Determines applicable tariff rates and customs regulations.']
  exchange_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate applied to convert supplier invoice amounts to the functional currency. Used when FOB price is in a different currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the landed cost was recognized. Used for monthly financial close and reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the landed cost was recognized for financial reporting purposes.']
  fob_price_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The FOB price of the goods as agreed with the supplier. Represents the cost of goods before international freight and insurance.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the landed cost was posted to the general ledger. Determines the financial period for COGS recognition and inventory valuation.']
  goods_receipt_date date [note: 'The date on which the goods were physically received into inventory. Determines the accounting period for cost recognition.']
  gsp_preference_applied boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a GSP tariff preference was applied to reduce or eliminate customs duty. True if GSP benefits were utilized.']
  gsp_preference_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The reduced tariff rate percentage applied under GSP preference. Null if no GSP preference was applied.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System tariff classification code for the imported goods. Determines the applicable customs duty rate and trade regulations.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'The Incoterms 2020 code defining the terms of delivery and cost responsibility between buyer and seller. Common values: FOB, CIF, EXW, FCA, CPT, CIP, DAP, DPU, DDP. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: FOB|CIF|EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  inland_freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of domestic transportation from the port of discharge to the final warehouse or distribution center.']
  insurance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of cargo insurance covering the goods during international transit. Component of landed cost.']
  landed_cost_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the landed cost calculation. Human-readable reference number used in financial reporting and audit trails.']
  landed_cost_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the landed cost record in the financial workflow. Values: draft (initial calculation), calculated (computation complete), approved (reviewed and approved), posted (recorded to general ledger), reversed (corrected or cancelled).']
  letter_of_credit_number varchar [note: 'The letter of credit number used to secure payment for this shipment. Links to trade finance instruments.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this landed cost record was last modified. Audit trail for record updates and corrections.']
  ocean_freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of ocean freight transportation from the port of loading to the port of discharge. Component of landed cost.']
  origin_country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of manufacture or origin for the goods. Used for customs duty calculation and trade compliance.']
  other_charges_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Miscellaneous charges not categorized elsewhere, such as inspection fees, fumigation costs, or special handling charges.']
  port_handling_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cost of port handling services including unloading, storage, and terminal fees at the port of discharge.']
  port_of_discharge varchar [note: 'The seaport or airport where the goods were unloaded upon arrival. Used for customs clearance and port handling cost allocation.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'The seaport or airport from which the goods were shipped. Used for freight cost allocation and logistics tracking.']
  quantity_received decimal(15,3) [note: 'The quantity of goods received in this shipment. Used to calculate unit landed cost and allocate costs across units.']
  tariff_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The customs duty rate percentage applied to the goods based on the HS code and origin country. Used to calculate the customs duty amount.']
  total_landed_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total landed duty paid cost per unit or shipment. Sum of FOB price, freight, insurance, customs duty, and all other charges. Authoritative cost basis for inventory valuation and COGS.']
  unit_landed_cost_amount decimal(18,4) [note: 'The landed cost per individual unit of the SKU. Calculated by dividing total landed cost by quantity received. Used for per-unit inventory valuation.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for the quantity received. Standard units include EA (each), PC (piece), KG (kilogram), LB (pound), M (meter), YD (yard), PAIR, SET, BOX, CASE. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|PC|KG|LB|M|YD|PAIR|SET|BOX|CASE — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Captures the full landed duty paid (LDP) cost breakdown for imported apparel goods, including FOB price, ocean/air freight, insurance, customs duty, HS code tariff rate, GSP preference utilization, port handling, and inland freight. Linked to purchase orders and goods receipts in SAP S/4HANA MM. Provides the authoritative LDP cost basis for inventory valuation, COGS recognition, and margin analysis by sourcing origin.'
}

Table finance.company_code {
  company_code_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the company code record. Primary key. Inferred role: MASTER_RESOURCE (legal entity as a managed organizational resource).']
  esg_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_report. Business justification: Links legal entities to ESG disclosures. Enables consolidated ESG reporting aligned with financial reporting boundaries—required for SEC climate disclosure, CSRD, and ensuring ESG scope matches financ']
  accounting_principle varchar [note: 'Primary accounting standard applied for statutory financial reporting. Options include IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), US GAAP (Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), local GAAP, or hybrid approaches for dual reporting.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date on which the company code was activated in the SAP S/4HANA FI system and began posting financial transactions.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the registered legal address for the company code. Includes street number and street name.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the registered legal address for the company code. Includes building name, suite, or floor information.']
  business_segment varchar [note: 'Primary business segment classification for this company code within the apparel fashion group. Used for segment reporting, profitability analysis, and strategic planning.']
  chart_of_accounts varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the chart of accounts assigned to this company code. The COA defines the structure of General Ledger (GL) accounts used for recording financial transactions.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the registered legal address for the company code.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the legal entity within the SAP S/4HANA FI system. This is the business identifier used across all financial transactions and statutory reporting.']
  company_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the company as registered with statutory authorities. Used for financial statements, tax filings, and legal documentation.']
  company_name_short varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or trading name of the company used for internal reporting and operational displays.']
  company_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the company code. Active indicates the entity is operational and posting transactions. Inactive, dormant, liquidation, or merged indicate non-operational states.']
  consolidation_entity_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code is included in group-level financial consolidation. True indicates the entity participates in consolidated financial statements.']
  consolidation_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the consolidation group or reporting entity to which this company code belongs. Used for multi-level consolidation hierarchies and segment reporting.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the Controlling (CO) area to which this company code is assigned. The controlling area is the organizational unit for cost accounting, profitability analysis, and internal management reporting.']
  cost_of_sales_accounting_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code uses cost-of-sales accounting (income statement by function) or period accounting (income statement by nature). True indicates cost-of-sales method.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country of domicile for the legal entity. Determines tax jurisdiction, statutory reporting requirements, and local GAAP applicability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this company code record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'Four-character code identifying the credit control area responsible for managing customer credit limits and credit exposure for this company code.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the local currency of the company code. All financial transactions for this entity are recorded in this currency before consolidation.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date on which the company code was deactivated or ceased operations. Null if the entity is still active.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for official correspondence with the company code.']
  fax_number varchar [note: 'Fax number for the company code, if applicable. Used for formal legal and regulatory correspondence.']
  field_status_variant varchar [note: 'Four-character code defining the field status variant that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed during financial document posting for this company code.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Two-character code defining the fiscal year structure for this company code, including the number of posting periods, special periods for year-end adjustments, and fiscal year start/end dates.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region classification for this company code. Used for regional performance analysis, geographic segment reporting, and market-based strategic decisions.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date on which the legal entity was incorporated or registered with the statutory authority. Used for legal compliance and entity lifecycle tracking.']
  intercompany_clearing_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator specifying whether this company code participates in automated intercompany clearing and reconciliation processes. True indicates intercompany transactions are tracked and cleared.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter ISO 639-1 language code indicating the primary language used for financial reporting and communication for this company code.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who last modified this company code record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp indicating when this company code record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the company code headquarters or registered office.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the registered legal address for the company code.']
  posting_period_variant varchar [note: 'Four-character code defining the posting period variant that controls which posting periods are open or closed for transaction posting in this company code.']
  profit_center_standard_hierarchy varchar [note: 'Code identifying the standard profit center hierarchy used for profitability analysis and internal management reporting for this company code.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or administrative region of the registered legal address for the company code.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction applicable to this company code. Determines tax calculation rules, tax reporting requirements, and compliance with local tax authorities.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the company code location. Used for timestamp normalization and cross-entity transaction reconciliation.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'VAT or sales tax registration number assigned by the local tax authority. Required for VAT reporting and cross-border transactions within VAT jurisdictions.']

  Note: 'Master record for each legal entity (company code) within the apparel fashion group as defined in SAP S/4HANA FI. Captures company code, company name, country, currency, chart of accounts, fiscal year variant, controlling area, tax jurisdiction, and IFRS/local GAAP accounting principle. Serves as the organizational anchor for statutory reporting, financial consolidation, and intercompany accounting.'
}

Table finance.intercompany_transaction {
  intercompany_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the intercompany transaction record. Primary key for the intercompany transaction entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Intercompany transactions can be allocated to cost centers for cost tracking. Normalizing STRING cost_center_code to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_code S']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Intercompany transactions post to GL accounts for financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column']
  sending_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Intercompany transactions track sending legal entity for intercompany reconciliation. Normalizing STRING sending_company_code to FK sending_company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id (first FK to ']
  sending_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Intercompany transactions track sending profit center for segment reporting. Normalizing STRING sending_profit_center_code to FK sending_profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id (first FK to ']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'The brand code associated with the intercompany transaction, particularly relevant for royalty and license fee transactions. Used for brand-level financial analysis.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'The sales or distribution channel associated with the intercompany transaction. Wholesale for B2B distribution; retail for owned stores; ecommerce for digital; franchise for licensed operations; outlet for clearance channels.']
  consolidation_unit_code varchar [note: 'The consolidation unit or reporting entity code used in the financial consolidation system. Identifies the level at which this transaction is eliminated in the consolidation hierarchy.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who created the intercompany transaction record in the system. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the intercompany transaction record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  elimination_date date [note: 'The date when the intercompany transaction was eliminated during the financial consolidation process. Null if elimination is still pending.']
  elimination_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the intercompany transaction in the consolidation elimination process. Pending indicates awaiting elimination; eliminated means fully removed from consolidated statements; partially eliminated for complex multi-step transactions; exception for transactions requiring manual review.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'The foreign exchange rate used to convert the transaction amount from transaction currency to group currency. Typically the rate effective on the transaction date.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the intercompany transaction is recorded, typically 1-12 for monthly periods or 1-13 including adjustments.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the intercompany transaction is recorded, used for financial reporting and consolidation purposes.']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'The geographic region code associated with the intercompany transaction, used for regional segment reporting and transfer pricing documentation by jurisdiction.']
  group_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the intercompany transaction translated into the group reporting currency using the applicable exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the corporate group reporting currency. All intercompany transactions are translated to this currency for consolidation purposes.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who last modified the intercompany transaction record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when the intercompany transaction record was last modified or updated. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  netting_position varchar [note: 'Indicates the settlement position of the intercompany transaction from the group perspective. Payable means the sending entity owes the receiving entity; receivable means the opposite; settled indicates payment completed; offset means netted against other transactions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments providing additional context about the intercompany transaction, including special circumstances, adjustments, or audit findings.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when the intercompany transaction was posted to the general ledger in the financial system. May differ from transaction date due to period-end processing or adjustments.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'The product category or merchandise hierarchy code associated with the intercompany transaction, particularly relevant for goods transfers. Used for category-level profitability analysis.']
  receiving_company_code varchar [note: 'The company code of the legal entity that is the receiver or consumer in the intercompany transaction. Represents the entity being charged or receiving goods/services. Typically a 4-character alphanumeric code per SAP standard.']
  receiving_profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The profit center code within the receiving company that is charged for the intercompany transaction. Used for internal profitability analysis and segment reporting.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date when the intercompany transaction was reconciled and confirmed between the sending and receiving entities. Null if reconciliation is pending or disputed.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'The current reconciliation status between the sending and receiving entities. Matched indicates both sides agree; unmatched for discrepancies; disputed for contested amounts; resolved for cleared disputes.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'The reference number of the source document that triggered the intercompany transaction, such as a purchase order, invoice, or service agreement number. Used for audit trail and reconciliation.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'The date when the intercompany transaction was settled through cash payment, netting, or other settlement mechanism between the legal entities. Null if settlement is pending.']
  settlement_method varchar [note: 'The mechanism used to settle the intercompany transaction. Cash payment for direct fund transfer; netting for multilateral settlement; offset against other balances; loan conversion for capitalization; waiver for forgiveness.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'The tax jurisdiction code applicable to the intercompany transaction, used for transfer pricing compliance and tax reporting. Critical for OECD BEPS documentation.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary value of the intercompany transaction in the transaction currency. Represents the gross amount charged or transferred before any adjustments.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the intercompany transaction was originally denominated and executed between the two legal entities.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The business date when the intercompany transaction occurred or was recognized. This is the accounting effective date used for financial period assignment and consolidation.']
  transaction_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique transaction number for the intercompany transaction, typically following format IC followed by 10 digits. Used for external reference and audit trails.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the intercompany transaction indicating the nature of the business activity between legal entities. Goods transfer represents finished goods or materials moved between entities; service charge for shared services; royalty and license fees for intellectual property usage; management fees for corporate overhead; loan and interest for financing arrangements; dividend for profit distribution; allocation for cost sharing. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: goods_transfer|service_charge|royalty|license_fee|management_fee|loan|interest|dividend|allocation — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  transfer_price_adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any adjustment amount applied to the intercompany transaction to align with transfer pricing policy and arms-length principles. Positive values indicate upward adjustments; negative values indicate downward adjustments.']
  transfer_pricing_method varchar [note: 'The OECD-compliant transfer pricing methodology applied to determine the arms-length price for this intercompany transaction. Comparable uncontrolled price uses market benchmarks; resale price based on resale margin; cost plus adds markup to cost; transactional net margin compares net margins; profit split allocates combined profits.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The amount of withholding tax applied to the intercompany transaction, particularly relevant for cross-border royalty, interest, and service fee payments. Recorded in transaction currency.']
  withholding_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'The withholding tax rate percentage applied to the intercompany transaction based on applicable tax treaties and local regulations. Expressed as a percentage (e.g., 5.00 for 5%).']

  Note: 'Records intercompany financial transactions between legal entities within the apparel fashion group, including intercompany sales of finished goods (e.g., sourcing entity to retail entity), royalty and license fee charges, shared service allocations, management fees, and intercompany loans. Captures sending and receiving company codes, transaction type, amount in transaction and group currency, elimination status, netting position, transfer pricing method, and IFRS 10 consolidation treatment. Essential for financial consolidation, transfer pricing documentation (OECD guidelines), and group statutory reporting.'
}

Table finance.tax_item {
  tax_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tax line item record in the financial system.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Tax items are posted to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Tax items can be allocated to cost centers for cost tracking. Normalizing STRING cost_center_number to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_number STRING column']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tax items post to GL accounts for financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column.']
  original_tax_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original tax item record if this entry is a reversal or adjustment, enabling audit trail and reconciliation.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer associated with this tax item, relevant for sales tax and output tax scenarios.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Tax items can be allocated to profit centers for profitability analysis. Normalizing STRING profit_center_code to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profit_center_c']
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_filing. Business justification: Regulatory filing fees and compliance service taxes generate tax items in apparel operations. Enables tax accounting for compliance expenditures and supports VAT/GST recovery on certification and test']
  transaction_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent financial transaction (invoice, payment, purchase order) to which this tax item applies.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor or supplier associated with this tax item, relevant for withholding tax and input tax scenarios.']
  wholesale_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to customer.wholesale_account. Business justification: Tax items for B2B wholesale transactions need wholesale account reference for VAT exemption certificate validation, resale certificate tracking, cross-border tax compliance (EU intra-community supply,']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the business area or segment (e.g., retail, wholesale, e-commerce) to which this tax item is allocated for internal reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where the tax obligation arises (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person or system process that created this tax item record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax item record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the tax amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  customs_entry_number varchar [note: 'The customs declaration or entry number associated with import duty tax items, used for customs clearance tracking.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date on the source document (invoice date, receipt date) that triggered this tax obligation.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known financial document number (invoice number, receipt number, or accounting document number) associated with this tax item.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current status of this tax item in the filing and payment lifecycle: pending (not yet filed), filed (submitted to authorities), paid (payment completed), reconciled (confirmed by authorities), or disputed (under review or appeal).']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period number within the fiscal year (1-12 for monthly, 1-4 for quarterly) when this tax item is recorded.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this tax item is recorded for financial and statutory reporting purposes.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System code used for customs classification of goods, relevant for import duty and customs tax calculations.']
  landed_cost_component varchar [note: 'Identifies which component of landed cost this tax item represents: duty (import tax), freight (shipping tax), insurance (insurance premium tax), or handling (processing fees).']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person or system process that last modified this tax item record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tax item record was last updated or modified.']
  line_item_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line item number within the parent financial document, used for ordering and referencing individual tax entries.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments, explanations, or special instructions related to this tax item.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date when the tax amount was paid to the tax authority, if applicable.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date when this tax item was posted to the general ledger for accounting purposes.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this tax item is a reversal or correction of a previously posted tax entry.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The calculated tax amount for this line item, derived from taxable base amount multiplied by tax rate.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The tax code identifier that determines the tax type, rate, and calculation method applied to this transaction line (e.g., V1 for standard VAT, I0 for import duty).']
  tax_determination_date date [note: 'The date used to determine the applicable tax rate and rules, typically the transaction date or delivery date.']
  tax_direction varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this is input tax (tax paid on purchases, recoverable) or output tax (tax collected on sales, payable to authorities).']
  tax_exemption_certificate_number varchar [note: 'The certificate or authorization number supporting the tax exemption claim, required for audit and compliance verification.']
  tax_exemption_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the reason for tax exemption or reduced rate, if applicable (e.g., export exemption, diplomatic exemption, zero-rated goods).']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the tax authority or jurisdiction where this tax is applicable (e.g., country code, state code, or customs territory).']
  tax_jurisdiction_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the tax jurisdiction or authority (e.g., United States - California, European Union, HM Revenue & Customs UK).']
  tax_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate applied to calculate the tax amount (e.g., 20.00 for 20% VAT, 5.50 for 5.5% import duty).']
  tax_reporting_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period or reporting period to which this tax item belongs (e.g., 2024-Q1, 2024-03, 2024-FY).']
  tax_return_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the tax return filing in which this tax item is included for statutory reporting to tax authorities.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'The category of tax applied: VAT (Value Added Tax), sales tax, import duty, excise duty, withholding tax, or GST (Goods and Services Tax).']
  taxable_base_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The base amount on which the tax is calculated, before tax is applied. Represents the net value subject to taxation.']
  withholding_tax_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this tax item represents withholding tax deducted at source from vendor payments.']

  Note: 'Captures tax line items associated with financial transactions in SAP S/4HANA FI, including VAT, sales tax, import duty, withholding tax, and GST. Stores tax code, tax type, taxable base amount, tax amount, tax jurisdiction, reporting period, tax return reference, and filing status. Supports indirect tax compliance, statutory tax reporting, and customs duty reconciliation for apparel fashion global operations.'
}

Table finance.fixed_asset {
  fixed_asset_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fixed asset record. Primary key for the fixed asset master data in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Factory equipment and facilities are verified during safety and social compliance audits in apparel manufacturing. Links asset records to audit findings for capital expenditure planning and compliance']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Fixed assets belong to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier for the person responsible for the fixed asset. Used for asset custody tracking and accountability.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier.vendor']
  warehouse_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.warehouse_location. Business justification: Fixed assets (racking systems, conveyors, RFID gates, sortation equipment) are assigned to specific warehouse locations for depreciation allocation, asset tracking, and maintenance scheduling. Standar']
  accumulated_depreciation decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total depreciation posted to date since acquisition. Used to calculate net book value and track asset consumption over time.']
  acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Original acquisition cost of the fixed asset including purchase price, installation costs, and any directly attributable costs to bring the asset to working condition. Basis for depreciation calculation.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date when the fixed asset was acquired or placed in service. Start date for depreciation calculation and useful life tracking.']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'Classification code defining the type of fixed asset (e.g., store fixtures, manufacturing equipment, warehouse racking, IT infrastructure, leasehold improvements). Determines depreciation method, useful life, and GL account assignment in SAP FI-AA.']
  asset_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the fixed asset including make, model, specifications, and purpose. Used for asset identification and audit trail.']
  asset_group_code varchar [note: 'Grouping code for aggregating similar assets for reporting and analysis. Supports portfolio management and CAPEX planning by asset category.']
  asset_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the fixed asset. The externally-known unique asset number used across SAP S/4HANA FI-AA for asset tracking, depreciation runs, and financial reporting.']
  asset_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fixed asset. Determines whether depreciation is active and whether the asset is included in financial reporting.']
  asset_subnumber varchar [note: 'SAP asset subnumber for tracking components or sub-assets within a main asset. Enables detailed tracking of complex assets with multiple depreciable components.']
  asset_tag_number varchar [note: 'Physical asset tag or barcode number affixed to the asset for identification during physical inventory counts and asset verification audits.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area code for segment reporting and profitability analysis. Enables CAPEX tracking and asset analysis by channel, product line, or region.']
  capitalization_date date [note: 'Date when the asset was capitalized and added to the fixed asset register. May differ from acquisition date for assets under construction.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who created the fixed asset record. Supports audit trail and data governance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fixed asset record was first created in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA. Provides audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for acquisition cost and net book value. Used for multi-currency consolidation and IFRS reporting.']
  depreciation_area varchar [note: 'SAP depreciation area code (e.g., 01 for book depreciation, 15 for tax depreciation). Enables parallel valuation for IFRS, GAAP, and local statutory reporting requirements.']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Depreciation calculation method applied to the fixed asset. Determines how acquisition cost is systematically allocated over useful life per IAS 16.']
  impairment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total impairment loss recognized on the fixed asset per IAS 36. Reduces net book value when recoverable amount falls below carrying amount.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Insurance policy number covering the fixed asset. Supports risk management and insurance claim processing.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who last modified the fixed asset record. Supports audit trail and accountability for data changes.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fixed asset record was last modified. Tracks data currency and supports change audit trail.']
  last_physical_inventory_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent physical inventory verification for the fixed asset. Supports audit compliance and asset existence verification.']
  lease_contract_number varchar [note: 'Contract number for leased assets. Links fixed asset to lease liability and supports IFRS 16 compliance reporting for right-of-use assets.']
  lease_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the asset is held under a lease agreement. Used for IFRS 16 lease accounting and right-of-use asset tracking.']
  maintenance_schedule_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the preventive maintenance schedule for the fixed asset. Links to maintenance planning and asset reliability programs.']
  net_book_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current net book value of the fixed asset calculated as acquisition cost minus accumulated depreciation. Represents the carrying amount on the balance sheet per IAS 16.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the fixed asset. Captures additional context, special handling instructions, or historical information.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'Purchase order number used to acquire the fixed asset. Links asset acquisition to procurement process and provides audit trail for CAPEX tracking.']
  retirement_date date [note: 'Date when the fixed asset was retired, disposed, or scrapped. Marks the end of the asset lifecycle and depreciation posting.']
  retirement_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for asset retirement. Supports audit trail and asset lifecycle analysis for CAPEX planning and replacement strategies.']
  salvage_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated residual value of the fixed asset at the end of its useful life. Subtracted from acquisition cost to determine depreciable amount per IAS 16.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number or unique identifier for the physical asset. Used for warranty tracking, maintenance scheduling, and physical inventory verification.']
  tax_depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Depreciation method used for tax reporting purposes. May differ from book depreciation method to comply with local tax regulations.']
  useful_life_years decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected useful life of the fixed asset in years. Used for depreciation calculation and asset lifecycle planning. May include fractional years for precision.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty expires. Used for maintenance planning and cost forecasting.']

  Note: 'Master record and complete lifecycle history for fixed assets in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA, including retail store fixtures, manufacturing equipment, warehouse racking, IT infrastructure, and leasehold improvements. Captures asset master attributes (asset class, acquisition date, cost, useful life, depreciation method, net book value, location, company code) and all lifecycle transactions (acquisitions, transfers, retirements, write-downs, depreciation postings with transaction type, posting date, amount, depreciation area, and document reference). Provides the complete audit trail for fixed asset movements. Supports CAPEX tracking, depreciation runs, IFRS 16 lease accounting, and IAS 16 compliance reporting for apparel fashion.'
}

Table finance.asset_transaction {
  asset_transaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the asset transaction record. Primary key for the asset transaction entity.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Asset transactions are posted to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Asset transactions can be allocated to cost centers. Normalizing STRING cost_center_code to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_code STRING column.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who created or posted the asset transaction. Supports audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset master record involved in this transaction. Links to the asset master data in FI-AA.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Asset transactions post to GL accounts for financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Asset transactions can be allocated to profit centers. Normalizing STRING profit_center_code to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profit_center_code STRING column.']
  asset_class_code varchar [note: 'Classification code defining the type of fixed asset (e.g., buildings, machinery, vehicles, IT equipment, leasehold improvements). Determines depreciation rules and GL account assignment.']
  asset_sub_number varchar [note: 'Sub-asset number for assets with multiple components tracked separately (e.g., building structure vs. HVAC system). Supports component depreciation per IFRS IAS 16.']
  asset_value_date date [note: 'Date from which the asset transaction becomes effective for asset valuation and depreciation calculation purposes. May differ from posting date for backdated transactions.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area classification for cross-company code segment reporting and internal financial statements.']
  customer_code varchar [note: 'Customer code for asset retirements involving sale or transfer to external parties. Links to accounts receivable.']
  depreciation_area_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the depreciation area (book depreciation, tax depreciation, IFRS, group consolidation) for which this transaction is recorded. Supports parallel valuation per IFRS and local GAAP.']
  document_line_item varchar [note: 'Line item number within the financial document that corresponds to this asset transaction entry.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Unique financial document number assigned by SAP S/4HANA FI for this asset transaction. Provides audit trail linkage to source accounting documents.']
  entry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the asset transaction record was created in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA. Provides audit trail for transaction entry time.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,5) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate used to convert transaction currency to local or group currency at the time of posting.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the transaction was posted. Supports monthly financial close and EOM (End of Month) reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the asset transaction was posted, used for period-based financial reporting and statutory compliance.']
  group_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount converted to the corporate group reporting currency for consolidated financial statements and EBITDA analysis.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp when the asset transaction record was last modified. Tracks changes for audit and compliance purposes.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount converted to the company code local currency for statutory reporting and consolidation.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the asset transaction was posted to the general ledger. This is the accounting date that determines the fiscal period for financial reporting.']
  quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Physical quantity of assets involved in the transaction (e.g., number of units acquired, transferred, or retired). Used for quantity-based asset tracking.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Code indicating the business reason for the asset transaction (e.g., normal depreciation, impairment, damage, obsolescence, sale, donation, scrap).']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference document number such as purchase order (PO), invoice number, or vendor document that originated the asset transaction.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this transaction is a reversal of a previously posted asset transaction. True if reversal, False otherwise.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original transaction that this reversal transaction is canceling. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the source system from which the asset transaction originated (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, legacy ERP, interface system). Supports data lineage and multi-system integration.']
  transaction_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the asset transaction in the transaction currency. Represents acquisition cost, depreciation expense, write-down amount, or retirement proceeds depending on transaction type.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  transaction_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the asset transaction in the financial system workflow.']
  transaction_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description or memo providing additional context about the asset transaction. Used for audit trail and user notes.']
  transaction_type_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the type of asset transaction: ACQ (Acquisition), TRF (Transfer), RET (Retirement), DEP (Depreciation), WDN (Write-Down), REV (Revaluation), COR (Correction), ADJ (Adjustment). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACQ|TRF|RET|DEP|WDN|REV|COR|ADJ — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the asset quantity (e.g., EA for each, M2 for square meters, KG for kilograms).']
  vendor_code varchar [note: 'Vendor or supplier code for asset acquisitions. Links to accounts payable and procurement records.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing all asset lifecycle events in SAP S/4HANA FI-AA, including acquisitions, transfers, retirements, write-downs, and depreciation postings. Stores transaction type, posting date, asset value date, amount, depreciation area, document reference, and GL account. Provides the complete audit trail for fixed asset movements and supports IFRS IAS 16/IFRS 16 compliance reporting.'
}

Table finance.cost_allocation {
  cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost allocation record. Primary key for the cost allocation entity.']
  allocation_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the allocation cycle or run executed in SAP S/4HANA CO. Groups multiple allocation records processed together in a single execution.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Cost allocations are posted to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  original_allocation_cost_allocation_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original cost allocation record if this is a reversal or correction. Links reversal documents to their original transactions.']
  receiver_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'FK to finance.cost_center']
  receiver_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Cost allocations can be allocated to profit centers for profitability analysis. Normalizing STRING receiver_profit_center_code to FK receiver_profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove ']
  sender_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost allocations track sender cost center for internal cost distribution. Normalizing STRING sender_cost_center_code to FK sender_cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id (first FK to cost_center w']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount allocated from sender to receiver in the transaction currency. Represents the portion of shared cost assigned to the receiver.']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to distribute costs from sender to receiver. Percentage allocates by fixed ratio, quantity by volume or count, equivalence by weighted factors, direct by explicit assignment, activity-based by activity driver consumption, driver-based by cost driver metrics.']
  allocation_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments explaining the allocation rationale, special circumstances, or business justification. Used for audit and review purposes.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sender cost allocated to this receiver when using percentage-based allocation method. Value between 0 and 100.']
  allocation_quantity decimal(15,3) [note: 'Quantity or volume used as the basis for allocation when using quantity-based or driver-based methods. Unit depends on allocation driver.']
  allocation_run_date date [note: 'Date when the allocation cycle was executed. May differ from posting date if allocation is run retrospectively or in batch.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the cost allocation record. Posted indicates successfully posted to CO/FI, reversed indicates reversal document created, pending indicates awaiting approval, error indicates posting failure, cancelled indicates allocation was voided.']
  allocation_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the allocation quantity (e.g., hours, square meters, headcount, transactions). Defines the driver metric.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Brand code to which the allocated cost is assigned. Supports brand-level profitability and GMROI analysis.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Sales or distribution channel code (e.g., retail, wholesale, e-commerce, DTC) to which the allocated cost is assigned. Enables channel-level profitability analysis.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'Controlling area in which the cost allocation is executed. Represents the organizational unit for cost accounting and internal reporting.']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'Cost element (account assignment) under which the allocated cost is posted. Represents the nature of the cost being distributed (e.g., overhead, logistics, marketing).']
  cost_element_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the cost element being allocated. Provides business-readable context for the type of cost.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system account that created the cost allocation record. Part of audit trail.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost allocation record was created in the system. Part of audit trail for compliance and data lineage.']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Controlling document number generated in SAP S/4HANA CO when the allocation is posted. Provides audit trail and traceability.']
  equivalence_number decimal(10,4) [note: 'Weighting factor used in equivalence-based allocation. Represents the relative consumption or benefit received by the receiver.']
