  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year when the allocation is posted. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, supports EOM close processes.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the cost allocation is posted. Used for period-based financial reporting and EBITDA analysis.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person or system account that last modified the cost allocation record. Part of audit trail.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the cost allocation record was last modified. Part of audit trail for compliance and change tracking.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Allocated amount converted to the company code local currency for statutory reporting and consolidation.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company code local currency.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date when the cost allocation was posted to the general ledger and controlling documents. Used for period assignment and audit trail.']
  product_line_code varchar [note: 'Product line or category code to which the allocated cost is assigned. Enables product-level profitability and COGS analysis.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External or cross-reference document number for traceability. May reference source documents, approval workflows, or related transactions.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code to which the allocated cost is assigned. Enables regional profitability and landed cost analysis.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this allocation record is a reversal of a previous allocation. True if this is a reversal document.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Business segment code for segment reporting under IFRS 8. Enables profitability analysis by reportable segment.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the source system from which the cost allocation record originated. Typically SAP S/4HANA CO module identifier.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the allocated amount is denominated. Typically the controlling area currency.']

  Note: 'Records internal cost allocation and assessment cycles executed in SAP S/4HANA CO, distributing shared costs (overhead, logistics, marketing) across cost centers, profit centers, and product lines. Captures allocation cycle, sender cost center, receiver cost center or profit center, allocation method (percentage, quantity, equivalence), allocated amount, and fiscal period. Enables accurate product-level and channel-level profitability analysis and EBITDA reporting.'
}

Table finance.margin_record {
  margin_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the margin record. Primary key for the authoritative profitability analysis record capturing the complete cost-to-margin chain from landed cost through COGS to realized margin at SKU/transaction level.']
  environmental_impact_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.environmental_impact. Business justification: Links product profitability to lifecycle environmental footprint. Enables true-cost accounting (financial + environmental), sustainable product portfolio optimization, and eco-margin analysis—emerging']
  actual_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Actual cost per unit based on real landed cost inputs. Used to calculate cost variance against standard cost.']
  air_freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Air shipping cost component of landed cost. Used when expedited shipping is required, impacting margin significantly.']
  asp_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average selling price per unit at gross revenue level before markdowns. Used to track pricing strategy effectiveness.']
  aur_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Average selling price per unit after markdowns and discounts. Key metric for pricing effectiveness and markdown impact analysis.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Brand identifier for multi-brand portfolio margin analysis. Enables brand-level profitability comparison and GMROI benchmarking.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Sales channel through which the transaction occurred. Critical dimension for channel-level profitability analysis and GMROI calculation.']
  cogs_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total cost of goods sold recognized for this transaction. The cost basis subtracted from net revenue to calculate gross margin.']
  contribution_margin_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Gross margin minus variable selling expenses. Used for break-even analysis and channel profitability assessment.']
  cost_variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between actual cost and standard cost. Positive variance indicates cost overrun impacting margin negatively.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this margin record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this margin record. Essential for multi-currency consolidation.']
  customs_duty_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Import duty paid at customs based on HS code classification and tariff rate. Critical component of landed cost impacting gross margin.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Promotional discount or customer-specific discount applied to the transaction. Reduces net revenue and impacts gross margin.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for this margin record. Used for EOM close and period-level profitability analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which this margin record is recognized for financial reporting purposes.']
  fob_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Factory gate cost before freight, insurance, and duties. First component of the landed duty paid cost breakdown. Sourced from purchase order or standard costing.']
  gmroi_ratio decimal(10,2) [note: 'Gross margin dollars returned for every dollar of inventory investment. Critical metric for inventory productivity and buying decisions.']
  gross_margin_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net revenue minus COGS. The primary profitability metric for product-level margin analysis.']
  gross_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Gross margin as a percentage of net revenue. Key performance indicator for profitability analysis by SKU, channel, and region.']
  gross_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total revenue before discounts and markdowns. The starting point for margin calculation.']
  gsp_preference_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether GSP tariff preference was applied to reduce or eliminate customs duty. Impacts landed cost and margin calculation.']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'International product classification code used to determine customs duty rates. Essential for accurate landed cost calculation and tariff management.']
  imu_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Initial markup percentage from cost to original retail price. Measures the starting margin before markdowns.']
  inland_freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Domestic transportation cost from port to distribution center or store. Final component of the full LDP cost breakdown.']
  insurance_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cargo insurance cost component of landed cost. Part of the full LDP cost breakdown.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this margin record was last updated. Tracks changes to cost components or margin calculations due to adjustments or corrections.']
  markdown_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Price reduction from original retail price. Critical for calculating maintained markup and understanding margin erosion.']
  mmu_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maintained markup percentage after markdowns and discounts. The realized margin percentage that accounts for all price reductions.']
  net_revenue_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Revenue after markdowns and discounts. The revenue figure used to calculate gross margin and contribution margin.']
  ocean_freight_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Ocean shipping cost component of landed cost. Part of the full LDP cost breakdown for margin calculation.']
  port_handling_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Port and terminal handling charges component of landed cost. Includes unloading, storage, and customs clearance fees.']
  product_category_code varchar [note: 'Product category classification for category-level margin analysis. Links to merchandise hierarchy for aggregated profitability reporting.']
  quantity_sold decimal(15,3) [note: 'Number of units sold in this transaction. Used to calculate unit-level margin metrics and contribution to sell-through rate.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where the sale occurred. Enables regional profitability analysis and landed cost variance tracking by destination market.']
  sales_order_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the sales order document that triggered the COGS recognition event. Links to the originating transaction in SAP SD.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Fashion season identifier (e.g., SS2024, FW2024) for the product. Essential for seasonal margin analysis, markdown tracking, and sell-through rate contribution.']
  sku_code varchar [note: 'Unique product identifier at the SKU level for which margin is calculated. Links to the specific product variant (style-color-size combination) in the product master.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that provided the margin record data. Typically SAP S/4HANA CO-PA or Oracle Retail for profitability analysis.']
  standard_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Predetermined standard cost per unit used for inventory valuation and initial COGS recognition. Basis for cost variance analysis.']
  str_contribution decimal(5,2) [note: 'Contribution of this transaction to the overall sell-through rate for the SKU/season. Used to assess inventory turn and markdown risk.']
  tariff_rate_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Customs duty rate percentage applied to this product based on HS code and country of origin. Used to calculate customs duty amount.']
  total_ldp_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Complete landed cost including FOB, freight, insurance, duties, and inland transportation. The total cost basis for COGS and margin calculation.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The business date when the revenue and COGS were recognized for this margin record. Represents the point-of-sale or shipment date depending on revenue recognition policy.']

  Note: 'Authoritative profitability analysis record in SAP S/4HANA CO-PA capturing the complete cost-to-margin chain from landed cost inputs through COGS recognition to realized margin outputs. At the SKU/transaction level by channel, region, and season, stores: full landed duty paid (LDP) cost breakdown (FOB, ocean/air freight, insurance, customs duty, HS code tariff rate, GSP preference, port handling, inland freight), COGS recognition event (standard cost, actual cost, cost variance, quantity sold, sales order reference), and margin outputs (net revenue, gross margin, contribution margin, AUR, ASP, IMU, MMU, STR contribution, GMROI, markdown amount, discount amount). The single source of truth for product-level profitability from sourcing origin through gross margin analysis in apparel fashion.'
}

Table finance.period_close {
  period_close_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the period close record. Primary key.']
  close_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Period close ownership must link to employee for close process accountability, task assignment, and audit trail. Apparel companies require close_owner FK for SOX compliance and close process managemen']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Period close is performed per legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Period close is performed per ledger for parallel accounting. Normalizing STRING ledger_group to FK ledger_id → ledger.ledger_id. Remove redundant ledger_group STRING column.']
  accrual_posting_status varchar [note: 'Status of accrual postings for expenses and revenues that need to be recognized in the current period per IFRS/GAAP accrual accounting principles.']
  actual_close_date date [note: 'The actual date on which the period close process was completed and signed off. Null if close is not yet complete.']
  blocked_steps varchar [note: 'The number of checklist steps that are currently blocked and cannot proceed due to issues or dependencies.']
  blocking_reason varchar [note: 'A description of the reason why the period close is blocked or delayed, such as missing data, unreconciled accounts, or pending approvals. Null if close is not blocked.']
  checklist_template_code varchar [note: 'The code or identifier of the close checklist template used for this period close, defining the standard steps and milestones (e.g., depreciation run, accruals, intercompany reconciliation, statutory reporting).']
  close_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the period close process was marked as complete and all checklist steps were finished.']
  close_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the period close process was initiated and status changed from open to in_progress.']
  close_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the period close process. Open indicates not started, in_progress indicates close activities underway, complete indicates all steps finished and signed off, blocked indicates close cannot proceed due to issues, reopened indicates a previously closed period has been reopened for adjustments.']
  close_type varchar [note: 'The purpose or scope of the close: operational for internal management reporting, statutory for regulatory and external reporting per IFRS/GAAP, management for internal KPI and EBITDA analysis, consolidation for group-level financial consolidation.']
  cogs_recognition_status varchar [note: 'Status of COGS recognition and inventory valuation adjustments for the period, ensuring proper matching of costs to revenues.']
  completed_steps varchar [note: 'The number of checklist steps that have been completed and signed off as of the current state.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'The controlling area code in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO that governs cost accounting and profitability analysis for this period close.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this period close record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the reporting currency for this period close (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  depreciation_run_status varchar [note: 'Status of the depreciation calculation and posting run for fixed assets as part of the period close process.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period number within the fiscal year (e.g., 1-12 for monthly periods, 1-4 for quarterly periods). Represents the accounting period being closed.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which the period close is being performed, typically a four-digit year (e.g., 2024).']
  intercompany_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of intercompany account reconciliation and elimination entries required for group consolidation.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who last modified this period close record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this period close record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form text field for additional comments, observations, or special instructions related to this period close process.']
  period_lock_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether the fiscal period has been locked in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO to prevent further postings. True if locked, False if open for postings.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'The type of accounting period being closed: monthly for regular month-end close, quarterly for quarter-end, annual for year-end, or special for ad-hoc adjustments.']
  planned_close_date date [note: 'The target date by which the period close process is scheduled to be completed, as defined in the close calendar.']
  reopen_approved_by varchar [note: 'The name of the senior finance executive who approved the reopening of a previously closed period. Null if period has not been reopened.']
  reopen_reason varchar [note: 'The business justification or reason for reopening a previously closed period, such as error correction, audit adjustment, or late invoice receipt. Null if period has not been reopened.']
  reopen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the period was reopened for additional postings or adjustments. Null if period has not been reopened.']
  revenue_recognition_status varchar [note: 'Status of revenue recognition adjustments and deferrals to ensure compliance with IFRS 15 or GAAP ASC 606 revenue recognition standards.']
  sign_off_approver_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the sign-off approver who provided final approval for the period close.']
  sign_off_approver_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the senior finance executive (e.g., CFO, Controller) who provided final sign-off approval for the period close.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the sign-off approver formally approved and signed off on the period close, marking it as officially complete.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source ERP system from which this period close record originated, typically SAP S/4HANA FI/CO.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or key of this period close record in the source ERP system.']
  statutory_reporting_status varchar [note: 'Status of statutory financial statement preparation and regulatory reporting submissions required for this period.']
  total_checklist_steps varchar [note: 'The total number of checklist steps or tasks defined for this period close process.']
  variance_explanation varchar [note: 'Explanation or commentary on significant variances identified during the close process, such as budget vs actual variances, prior period comparisons, or unusual transactions.']

  Note: 'Tracks the EOM (End of Month) and period-end financial close process for each company code and fiscal period in SAP S/4HANA FI/CO. Captures close checklist step, responsible owner, planned close date, actual completion date, status (open, in-progress, complete, blocked), blocking reason, and sign-off approver. Manages the structured close calendar including depreciation runs, accruals, intercompany reconciliation, and statutory reporting milestones.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bank account record. Primary key for the bank account master data entity.']
  account_id varchar [note: 'Internal account identifier within the house bank structure. Used in combination with house_bank_id to uniquely identify the account in SAP.']
  cash_pool_header_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on bank_account (cash_pool_header_bank_account_id)']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Bank accounts belong to a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Bank accounts are linked to GL accounts for reconciliation and financial reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code ']
  account_closing_date date [note: 'Date when the bank account was officially closed. Null for active accounts.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the account holder as registered with the bank. Typically matches the company legal entity name.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The actual bank account number as assigned by the financial institution. Format varies by country and banking system.']
  account_opening_date date [note: 'Date when the bank account was officially opened and activated with the financial institution.']
  account_purpose_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the business purpose and usage of this bank account. Provides context for treasury management and audit purposes.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the bank account. Active accounts are in use, inactive are temporarily suspended, closed are permanently terminated, blocked are restricted by bank or company, dormant have no recent activity.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account based on its business purpose. Operating accounts handle day-to-day transactions, payroll accounts for employee payments, Letter of Credit (LC) accounts for trade finance, cash pooling for treasury optimization, escrow for held funds, and transit for in-process payments.']
  available_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Available balance after accounting for holds, pending transactions, and reserved funds. Represents actual spendable cash.']
  bank_address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the bank branch physical address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the bank branch physical address (suite, floor, building). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_branch_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific bank branch where the account is maintained.']
  bank_city varchar [note: 'City where the bank branch is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_key varchar [note: 'Country-specific bank identifier code (e.g., routing number in USA, sort code in UK, bank code in other countries). Used for domestic payment processing.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'Full legal name of the financial institution where the account is held.']
  bank_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the bank branch. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the bank branch location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  bank_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province where the bank branch is located. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  cash_pooling_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this account participates in a cash pooling arrangement for treasury optimization across multiple entities or accounts.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the bank account is domiciled. Important for regulatory reporting and tax compliance.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account master record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code representing the primary currency denomination of the bank account.']
  current_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Current available balance in the bank account as of the last reconciliation or statement date. Used for cash flow management and liquidity analysis.']
  house_bank_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the house bank in SAP S/4HANA FI. Represents the company\'s banking partner where the account is maintained.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number used for cross-border payments and international transactions. Standardized format for global banking.']
  interest_rate_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate percentage applied to the account balance. May be positive for interest-bearing accounts or negative for certain treasury accounts.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account master record was last updated in the system.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'Date when the last bank reconciliation was completed for this account. Critical for End of Month (EOM) close and financial reporting accuracy.']
  last_statement_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent bank statement received and processed for this account. Used for reconciliation tracking.']
  lc_facility_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this account is designated for Letter of Credit (LC) issuance and settlement. Critical for apparel fashion global sourcing and Free on Board (FOB) payment terms.']
  lc_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum Letter of Credit facility limit available on this account. Used for managing trade finance capacity for international supplier payments.']
  minimum_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum balance requirement set by the bank or company policy to avoid fees or maintain account status.']
  overdraft_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum overdraft or credit facility limit extended by the bank for this account. Zero if no overdraft facility exists.']
  payment_method_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods supported by this account (e.g., wire transfer, ACH, check, EDI, SWIFT). Used for payment run configuration.']
  relationship_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the bank relationship manager. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  relationship_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the bank relationship manager or account officer responsible for this account. Key contact for banking services and issue resolution.']
  relationship_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the bank relationship manager. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'SWIFT/BIC code identifying the bank and branch for international wire transfers and Letter of Credit (LC) transactions.']

  Note: 'Master record for bank accounts maintained in SAP S/4HANA FI (house banks and account IDs) used for payment processing, cash pooling, and treasury management. Captures bank key, account number, IBAN, SWIFT/BIC, currency, GL reconciliation account, account type (operating, payroll, LC), daily balance, and bank relationship details. Essential for cash flow management, payment run configuration, and Letter of Credit settlement in apparel fashion global operations.'
}

Table finance.consolidation_entry {
  consolidation_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the consolidation entry record. Primary key for the consolidation entry entity.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Consolidation entries can be allocated to cost centers for cost tracking. Normalizing STRING cost_center_code to FK cost_center_id → cost_center.cost_center_id. Remove redundant cost_center_code STRIN']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Consolidation entry creation must link to employee for audit trail and SOX compliance. Apparel companies with multiple entities require created_by FK for consolidation journal entry audit and maker-ch']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Consolidation entries post to GL accounts for group reporting. Normalizing STRING gl_account_code to FK gl_account_id → gl_account.gl_account_id. Remove redundant gl_account_code STRING column.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.ledger. Business justification: Consolidation entries are posted to specific ledgers (group ledger, IFRS ledger, GAAP ledger, etc.) in SAP S/4HANA for parallel accounting and group reporting. Each consolidation entry must be associa']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Consolidation entries can be allocated to profit centers for segment reporting. Normalizing STRING profit_center_code to FK profit_center_id → profit_center.profit_center_id. Remove redundant profit_c']
  reversal_consolidation_entry_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on consolidation_entry (reversal_consolidation_entry_id)']
  reversed_entry_consolidation_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original consolidation entry that is being reversed by this entry. Null if not a reversal.']
  source_company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Consolidation entries track source legal entity for intercompany eliminations. Normalizing STRING source_company_code to FK source_company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id (first FK to company_c']
  accounting_principle varchar [note: 'Accounting standard or principle under which the consolidation entry is prepared. Determines the treatment and disclosure requirements.']
  approved_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who approved the consolidation entry for posting. Null if not yet approved.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consolidation entry was approved. Null if not yet approved. Supports segregation of duties controls.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Brand code associated with the consolidation entry. Enables brand-level profitability analysis in multi-brand apparel fashion groups.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area code for segment or divisional reporting. Enables cross-company code reporting by business line.']
  channel_type varchar [note: 'Sales or distribution channel associated with the consolidation entry. Used for channel-level segment reporting and analysis.']
  consolidation_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the consolidation group or reporting entity hierarchy to which this entry belongs. Used for multi-level consolidation structures.']
  consolidation_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the consolidation unit or group entity for which this entry is recorded. Represents the reporting entity in the group structure.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consolidation entry record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the consolidation entry is a debit or credit posting. Determines the direction of the accounting impact.']
  elimination_method varchar [note: 'Method used for eliminating intercompany transactions or consolidating subsidiary results. Determines the calculation approach for the consolidation entry.']
  entry_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of the consolidation entry in the transaction currency. Represents the value of the adjustment or elimination.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date on which the consolidation entry was created or entered into the system. May differ from posting date.']
  entry_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the consolidation entry explaining the business purpose and nature of the adjustment or elimination.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the consolidation entry document. Externally-known reference number used for tracking and audit purposes.']
  entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consolidation entry in the approval and posting workflow.']
  entry_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the consolidation entry indicating the nature of the adjustment. Determines the accounting treatment and reporting requirements.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Foreign exchange rate used to translate the transaction currency amount to the group currency amount. Applied at the time of consolidation.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Type of exchange rate applied for currency translation. Determines the rate selection methodology per accounting standards.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period within the fiscal year for which the consolidation entry is recorded. Typically represents a month or quarter.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which the consolidation entry is recorded. Aligns with the group reporting calendar.']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for the consolidation entry. Supports geographic segment reporting per IFRS 8 and GAAP ASC 280.']
  group_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Consolidation entry amount translated into the group reporting currency. Used for consolidated financial statement preparation.']
  group_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code of the group reporting currency. Typically the parent company\'s functional currency.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the consolidation entry. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consolidation entry was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.']
  minority_interest_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of non-controlling interest in the subsidiary for which the consolidation entry is recorded. Used for minority interest calculations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to the consolidation entry. Used for documentation and audit purposes.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of ownership interest held by the parent in the subsidiary. Determines the consolidation method and elimination scope.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the consolidation entry is posted to the group ledger. Represents the accounting effective date for the adjustment.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference document number or source transaction identifier that triggered the consolidation entry. Provides audit trail linkage.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this consolidation entry is a reversal of a previously posted entry. True if reversal, False otherwise.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Segment code for IFRS 8 or GAAP segment reporting. Identifies the operating segment to which the consolidation entry relates.']
  target_company_code varchar [note: 'Company code of the receiving entity in an intercompany transaction or consolidation adjustment. Represents the receiving or crediting entity.']
  transaction_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which the consolidation entry amount is denominated.']

  Note: 'Records financial consolidation adjustments and elimination entries for group reporting in SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting or BPC. Captures consolidation unit, elimination method, adjustment type (IC elimination, currency translation, minority interest), source and target company codes, consolidation group, reporting period, and IFRS/GAAP treatment. Supports statutory group financial statements and segment reporting for multi-entity apparel fashion groups.'
}

Table finance.letter_of_credit {
  letter_of_credit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the letter of credit record. Primary key.']
  amended_letter_of_credit_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on letter_of_credit (amended_letter_of_credit_id)']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Letters of credit are issued by a legal entity (company code). Normalizing STRING company_code to FK company_code_id → company_code.company_code_id. Remove redundant company_code STRING column.']
  issuing_bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Letters of credit are issued through a specific bank account maintained in SAP S/4HANA FI. Currently the issuing bank is captured as denormalized STRING fields (issuing_bank_name, issuing_bank_swift_c']
  order_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.purchase_order. Business justification: Letters of credit secure payment for international purchase orders in apparel sourcing. This link enables LC utilization tracking, compliance verification that shipments match LC terms, and procure-to']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the supplier master data. Links the LC beneficiary to the internal supplier record for procurement and payment tracking.']
  advising_bank_name varchar [note: 'The name of the bank that advises the letter of credit to the beneficiary, typically located in the beneficiary\'s country.']
  advising_bank_swift_code varchar [note: 'The SWIFT/BIC code of the advising bank for international communication and document handling.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent amendment to the letter of credit terms.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number tracking amendments made to the original letter of credit. Zero indicates the original LC with no amendments.']
  applicant_address varchar [note: 'The full business address of the applicant party requesting the letter of credit.']
  applicant_name varchar [note: 'The name of the party (buyer/importer) who requests the issuing bank to open the letter of credit.']
  available_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The remaining credit amount available for utilization under the letter of credit.']
  beneficiary_address varchar [note: 'The full business address of the beneficiary party entitled to receive payment under the letter of credit.']
  beneficiary_name varchar [note: 'The name of the party (seller/exporter/supplier) in whose favor the letter of credit is issued and who is entitled to draw under it.']
  charges_account varchar [note: 'Indicates which party (applicant or beneficiary) is responsible for paying the letter of credit charges and fees.']
  confirming_bank_name varchar [note: 'The name of the bank that adds its confirmation to the letter of credit, providing additional payment guarantee to the beneficiary.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who created this letter of credit record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this letter of credit record was first created in the system. Record audit trail for data governance.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the letter of credit amount.']
  document_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed description of all documents required to be presented for negotiation under the letter of credit (e.g., commercial invoice, packing list, bill of lading, certificate of origin, inspection certificate).']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date when the letter of credit expires and is no longer valid for presentation of documents. Represents the effective-until date of the agreement.']
  goods_description varchar [note: 'Description of the goods or merchandise covered by the letter of credit, including product details, quantities, and specifications.']
  incoterm_code varchar [note: 'The International Commercial Terms (Incoterms) code defining the delivery terms, risk transfer point, and cost responsibilities between buyer and seller. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EXW|FCA|CPT|CIP|DAP|DPU|DDP|FAS|FOB|CFR|CIF — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  issue_date date [note: 'The date when the letter of credit was officially issued by the issuing bank. This is the principal business event timestamp marking when the LC becomes effective.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID or name of the person who last modified this letter of credit record in the system.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this letter of credit record was last updated in the system. Record audit trail for data governance.']
  latest_shipment_date date [note: 'The last permissible date for shipment of goods under the terms of the letter of credit.']
  lc_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The maximum monetary value of the letter of credit. This is the principal quantitative fact representing the credit limit available to the beneficiary.']
  lc_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known documentary credit number issued by the bank. This is the business identifier used in trade finance communications and documentation.']
  lc_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the letter of credit in the trade finance workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|advised|confirmed|amended|utilized|expired|closed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  lc_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the letter of credit based on its terms and conditions. Determines the level of commitment and transferability.']
  partial_shipment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether partial shipments of goods are permitted under the letter of credit terms.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'The payment terms specified in the letter of credit indicating when payment will be made (at sight, deferred, or upon acceptance).']
  port_of_discharge varchar [note: 'The port or place where goods are unloaded at destination under the letter of credit terms.']
  port_of_loading varchar [note: 'The port or place where goods are loaded for shipment under the letter of credit terms.']
  presentation_period_days varchar [note: 'The maximum number of days after shipment date within which documents must be presented to the bank for negotiation.']
  revolving_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the letter of credit automatically renews for additional shipments after each utilization.']
  special_conditions varchar [note: 'Any additional special terms, conditions, or instructions specific to this letter of credit that are not covered by standard fields.']
  tenor_days varchar [note: 'The number of days after sight or shipment date when payment is due under deferred payment or usance terms.']
  tolerance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The permissible percentage variance (plus or minus) allowed in the LC amount and quantity as per UCP 600 Article 30.']
  transferable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the letter of credit can be transferred in whole or in part to one or more second beneficiaries.']
  transshipment_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether transshipment (transfer of goods from one vessel to another during transit) is permitted under the letter of credit terms.']
  utilized_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The cumulative amount that has been drawn or utilized against the letter of credit to date.']

  Note: 'Letter of Credit documentary credit record managing trade finance for international supplier payments including LC type, amount, beneficiary, and document requirements'
}

Table finance.cost_center_initiative_participation {
  cost_center_initiative_participation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost center initiative participation record. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the cost center participating in the sustainability initiative. References the organizational cost object (store, warehouse, DC, office, production facility) involved in the program.']
  initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the sustainability initiative in which the cost center is participating. References the discrete sustainability improvement program or project.']
  sustainability_initiative_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to sustainability.sustainability_initiative. The sustainability program in which the cost center participates.']
  actual_carbon_reduction_mt_co2e decimal(15,3) [note: 'Measured annual greenhouse gas emissions reduction in metric tons of CO2 equivalent achieved by this cost center through participation in the initiative. Enables rollup of initiative impact by cost center hierarchy and location.']
  actual_energy_savings_mwh decimal(15,2) [note: 'Measured annual energy consumption reduction in megawatt hours achieved by this cost center through participation in the initiative. Supports energy efficiency reporting by facility type and geography.']
  actual_water_savings_m3 decimal(15,2) [note: 'Measured annual water consumption reduction in cubic meters achieved by this cost center through participation in the initiative. Supports water stewardship reporting by facility and region.']
  allocated_investment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The portion of the initiative\'s total investment amount allocated to this cost center, calculated as initiative investment_amount × cost_allocation_percentage. Used for cost center budget impact analysis and ESG investment tracking by organizational unit.']
  cost_allocation_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of the initiative\'s total investment amount allocated to or borne by this cost center, expressed as a decimal percentage (0.00 to 100.00). Used for distributing initiative costs across participating organizational units and for cost center budget tracking. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection reasoning as relationship data (cost share).']
  end_date date [note: 'The date when this cost center completed or exited participation in the sustainability initiative. Null if participation is ongoing. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection reasoning as relationship data (timeline).']
  initiative_phase varchar [note: 'The current phase of initiative implementation at this specific cost center. PLANNING: initiative approved but not yet started at this location. IMPLEMENTATION: active rollout in progress. MEASUREMENT: implementation complete, measuring impact. COMPLETED: initiative fully implemented and measured at this location. SUSPENDED: participation paused or cancelled. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection reasoning as relationship data (phase).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing implementation details, challenges, lessons learned, or other context specific to this cost center\'s participation in the initiative.']
  participation_role varchar [note: 'The role of the cost center in the sustainability initiative. LEAD: primary responsible party driving implementation at this location. PARTICIPANT: active participant implementing the program. PILOT: test site for initiative before broader rollout. OBSERVER: monitoring or learning from the initiative without active implementation. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection reasoning as relationship data.']
  participation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this cost center\'s participation in the initiative. ACTIVE: currently participating. COMPLETED: participation finished successfully. SUSPENDED: temporarily paused. CANCELLED: participation terminated before completion.']
  start_date date [note: 'The date when this cost center began participating in the sustainability initiative. May differ from the initiative\'s overall start date if cost centers join in phases. This attribute was explicitly identified in the detection reasoning as relationship data (timeline).']

  Note: 'This association product represents the participation relationship between cost centers and sustainability initiatives in Apparel Fashion operations. It captures the cross-functional involvement of organizational cost centers (stores, warehouses, DCs, offices) in discrete sustainability improvement programs (renewable energy, waterless dyeing, repair services, packaging elimination). Each record links one cost center to one sustainability initiative with attributes that track the participation role (lead vs. participant), cost allocation responsibility, timeline boundaries, and implementation phase. This enables portfolio management of sustainability programs across the organizational cost structure and supports ESG reporting by cost center hierarchy.. Existence Justification: In Apparel Fashion sustainability operations, cost centers (stores, warehouses, DCs, offices, production facilities) participate in multiple sustainability initiatives simultaneously (LED retrofit, renewable energy procurement, waterless dyeing, packaging elimination), and each initiative involves multiple cost centers across the organizational hierarchy. The business actively manages these participation relationships as part of ESG program portfolio management, tracking which locations are involved in which programs, their roles (lead vs. participant), cost allocation responsibility, implementation timelines, and measured impact by location.'
}

Table finance.allocation_cycle {
  allocation_cycle_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for allocation_cycle']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Allocation cycles represent cost allocation and assessment runs in SAP S/4HANA CO. These must be scoped to a legal entity (company code) for proper cost accounting and financial reporting. Each alloca']
  parent_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent allocation cycle if this cycle is a revision or sub-cycle of another. Nullable for top-level cycles.']
  prior_allocation_cycle_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on allocation_cycle (prior_allocation_cycle_id)']
  source_cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary cost center from which costs are being allocated.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'The actual date when the allocation cycle execution was completed. Nullable if not yet completed.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'The actual date when the allocation cycle execution began. Nullable if not yet started.']
  allocation_basis varchar [note: 'The basis or driver used for allocation calculations (e.g., headcount, square footage, revenue, units sold).']
  allocation_frequency varchar [note: 'The frequency at which this allocation cycle is executed (e.g., monthly, quarterly, annual).']
  allocation_method varchar [note: 'The methodology used to allocate costs or revenues during this cycle (e.g., direct allocation, step-down, activity-based costing).']
  allocation_scope varchar [note: 'The organizational scope to which this allocation cycle applies (e.g., global, regional, divisional).']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation cycle was formally approved by authorized personnel.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved this allocation cycle.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this allocation cycle record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code in which allocation amounts are denominated.']
  cycle_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the allocation cycle, used for external reference and reporting.']
  cycle_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the allocation cycle, typically indicating the period or purpose (e.g., Q1 2024 Cost Allocation, Annual Budget Allocation).']
  cycle_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the allocation cycle indicating its purpose within the financial planning process.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the allocation cycle, including its purpose, scope, and any special considerations.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this allocation cycle ceases to be effective. Nullable for open-ended cycles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this allocation cycle becomes effective and applicable for financial transactions.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period within the year (e.g., Q1, M01, H1, FY) to which this allocation cycle applies.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this allocation cycle applies.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation cycle is currently active (True) or has been deactivated (False).']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation cycle is executed through automated processes (True) or requires manual intervention (False).']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who last modified this allocation cycle record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this allocation cycle record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments related to this allocation cycle, including any exceptions or special handling instructions.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'The planned date for completing the allocation cycle execution activities.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'The planned date for beginning the allocation cycle execution activities.']
  requires_approval boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this allocation cycle requires formal approval before execution (True) or can proceed without approval (False).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the allocation cycle, indicating its progression through the approval and execution workflow.']
  total_allocation_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The total monetary amount being allocated in this cycle across all cost centers, products, or channels.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this allocation cycle, incremented when the cycle is revised or rerun.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the user who created this allocation cycle record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for allocation_cycle. Referenced by allocation_cycle_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the employee record. Primary key. System-generated surrogate key for all workforce identity data across retail stores, corporate offices, and distribution centers.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: Employees are hired into standardized job profiles. Employee currently has denormalized job_code, job_family, job_level attributes. Adding job_profile_id FK normalizes this relationship and allows acc']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee ID of the direct manager or supervisor. Establishes organizational reporting hierarchy.']
  cost_center varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code for labor expense allocation. Links employee payroll costs to General Ledger (GL) accounts.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Date of birth of the employee. Used for age verification, benefits eligibility, and compliance reporting.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional area to which the employee is assigned (e.g., Merchandising, Store Operations, Finance, IT).']
  eeo_classification varchar [note: 'EEO-1 job category classification for regulatory reporting (e.g., Executive/Senior Officials, Professionals, Sales Workers, Service Workers). Used for diversity and compliance reporting.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary corporate email address for the employee. Used for internal communications and system access.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee record. Active employees are on payroll and eligible for work assignments.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment arrangement. Determines benefits eligibility, scheduling rules, and labor compliance requirements.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the employee as recorded in HR system.']
  flsa_status varchar [note: 'Classification under FLSA determining overtime eligibility. Exempt employees are salaried and not eligible for overtime; non-exempt employees are eligible for overtime pay.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee was originally hired or rehired. Used for tenure calculations, benefits eligibility, and anniversary tracking.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position name assigned to the employee. Used for organizational reporting and role-based access control.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name (surname) of the employee as recorded in HR system.']
  middle_name varchar [note: 'Middle name or initial of the employee. Nullable.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit trail.']
  national_id_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued national identification number (e.g., Social Security Number in USA, National Insurance Number in UK). Used for tax reporting and payroll.']
  pay_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for employee compensation (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR).']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation band or grade level. Defines salary range and progression path for the role.']
  pay_rate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current base pay rate for the employee. Represents hourly rate for hourly employees or annual salary for salaried employees. Currency determined by work location.']
  pay_type varchar [note: 'Compensation structure for the employee. Determines payroll calculation method and time tracking requirements.']
  performance_rating varchar [note: 'Most recent annual performance review rating. Used for talent management, succession planning, and compensation decisions.']
  performance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal performance review. Used for tracking review cycles and next review scheduling.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee. May be mobile or landline.']
  preferred_name varchar [note: 'Preferred or chosen name used by the employee in workplace communications. May differ from legal name.']
  rehire_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is eligible for rehire based on termination circumstances and performance history.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employees employment ended. Nullable for active employees. Used for offboarding, final payroll, and compliance reporting.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Classification of the reason for employment termination. Used for turnover analysis and compliance reporting. Nullable for active employees.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the labor union to which the employee belongs. Nullable for non-union employees.']
  union_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee is a member of a labor union. Determines applicable collective bargaining agreements and labor rules.']
  work_authorization_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of work authorization (e.g., visa expiry). Nullable for citizens and permanent residents. Used for compliance monitoring and renewal alerts.']
  work_authorization_status varchar [note: 'Legal authorization status for the employee to work in the jurisdiction. Used for I-9 compliance and visa tracking.']
  work_location_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the employees primary work location (store number, office code, or distribution center ID).']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Category of primary work location. Determines applicable labor policies, scheduling systems, and operational workflows.']
  workday_employee_code varchar [note: 'External employee identifier from Workday HCM system. Business key for integration with source HR system.']

  Note: 'Master record for all employees across retail stores, corporate offices, and distribution centers. Captures personal details, employment type (full-time, part-time, seasonal, contractor), job classification, hire date, termination date, employment status, work authorization, EEO classification, and Workday HCM employee ID. SSOT for all workforce identity data in the apparel-fashion enterprise.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the authorized headcount position within the organization. Primary key.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Positions are budgeted against cost centers for headcount planning and salary budgeting. Apparel companies track position costs (approved headcount budget) by cost center for OTB headcount management ']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the department or cost center to which this position is assigned for budgeting and organizational reporting.']
  incumbent_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee currently occupying this position. Null if the position is vacant.']
  job_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.job_profile. Business justification: Positions are defined by job profiles. Position currently has denormalized job_family, job_level, flsa_classification attributes. Adding job_profile_id FK normalizes this relationship and ensures posi']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical work location (store, distribution center, corporate office) where this position is based.']
  supervisor_position_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the position that this position reports to in the organizational hierarchy. Null for top-level executive positions.']
  talent_requisition_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the active job requisition for this position if it is currently vacant and approved for hiring. Null if position is filled or not approved for recruitment.']
  annual_salary_max decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum annual salary for the position based on the assigned pay grade. Used for compensation planning and budgeting.']
  annual_salary_min decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum annual salary for the position based on the assigned pay grade. Used for compensation planning and budgeting.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year for which this position is budgeted and approved as part of the Open-to-Buy (OTB) headcount planning process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for salary and compensation amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this position was eliminated or is scheduled to be eliminated. Null for active positions with no planned end date.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this position was created or became effective in the organizational structure.']
  eligible_for_bonus boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for annual performance bonuses or incentive compensation. True if eligible; False otherwise.']
  eligible_for_commission boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for sales commission or variable pay based on performance metrics. True if eligible; False otherwise.']
  fte_allocation decimal(5,4) [note: 'Full-time equivalent allocation for the position. 1.0000 represents a full-time position; values less than 1.0 represent part-time positions (e.g., 0.5000 for half-time).']
  is_critical_position boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this position is designated as business-critical, requiring priority succession planning and talent development. True for critical positions; False otherwise.']
  is_filled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is currently occupied by an employee. True if filled; False if vacant.']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a seasonal position typically filled during peak retail periods (e.g., holiday season, back-to-school). True for seasonal positions; False for year-round positions.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was last updated in the system.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum educational qualification required for the position as defined in the job profile.']
  minimum_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for the position.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation band assigned to the position, defining the salary range and eligibility for incentive programs.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Business-assigned unique code for the position, used for external reporting and integration with payroll and HR systems.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the position responsibilities, key accountabilities, and scope of work. Used for job postings and performance management.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position. Active positions are available for hiring; Frozen positions are temporarily on hold; Eliminated positions have been removed from the headcount plan; Pending Approval positions are awaiting budget authorization.']
  position_type varchar [note: 'Employment type classification for the position. Regular positions are permanent; Temporary positions have a defined end date; Contract positions are filled by external contractors; Intern positions are for student or graduate programs.']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements. True if remote work is permitted; False if the position requires on-site presence.']
  seasonal_end_date date [note: 'End date for seasonal positions, indicating when the position is typically eliminated each year. Null for non-seasonal positions.']
  seasonal_start_date date [note: 'Start date for seasonal positions, indicating when the position becomes active each year. Null for non-seasonal positions.']
  standard_hours_per_week decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected number of working hours per week for the position. Typically 40.00 for full-time positions; varies for part-time positions based on FTE allocation.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official job title for the position as it appears in organizational charts and job postings.']
  travel_requirement_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of time the position requires business travel. 0.00 for no travel; values up to 100.00 for positions requiring extensive travel.']
  union_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the labor union representing this position, if applicable. Null for non-unionized positions.']
  work_shift varchar [note: 'Primary work shift assigned to the position, relevant for retail store associates and distribution center teams.']

  Note: 'Defines authorized headcount positions within the organization, including job title, job family, job level, FLSA classification (exempt/non-exempt), pay grade, department, location, FTE allocation, and whether the position is filled or vacant. Supports open-to-buy headcount planning and seasonal staffing models for retail and DC teams.'
}

Table workforce.org_unit {
  org_unit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the organizational unit. Primary key.']
  company_code_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Org units must link to legal entities for organizational hierarchy aligned to company structure and entity-level reporting. Apparel companies with multiple brands/regions require org_unit→company_code']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Org units must link to finance cost centers for labor cost allocation, departmental budgeting, and P&L reporting. Apparel retailers track store/DC labor costs via org_unit→cost_center. Replaces denorm']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who is the responsible manager or supervisor for this organizational unit.']
  parent_org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent organizational unit in the hierarchy. Enables hierarchical roll-up for workforce reporting and labor cost allocation.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical location or facility where this organizational unit operates.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocation for this organizational unit in local currency. Used for labor cost planning and variance analysis.']
  business_unit_code varchar [note: 'Strategic business unit code for executive reporting and performance management. Aligns with KPI dashboards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for budget and financial reporting (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\').']
  division_code varchar [note: 'Business division code for divisional reporting and strategic planning (e.g., \'RETAIL\', \'WHOLESALE\', \'ECOMM\').']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit becomes inactive or is closed. Null for currently active units. Supports historical reporting.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this organizational unit becomes active and available for workforce assignment. Supports temporal hierarchy management.']
  gl_segment varchar [note: 'General ledger segment code for financial reporting and consolidation. Used for EBITDA and P&L roll-up.']
  headcount_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum planned headcount capacity for this organizational unit. Used for workforce planning and seasonal staffing models.']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the organizational hierarchy (e.g., 1=corporate, 2=division, 3=region, 4=store). Enables hierarchical roll-up queries.']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Full hierarchical path from root to this unit (e.g., \'/CORP/RETAIL/NORAM/STORE001\'). Supports recursive queries and reporting.']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit operates on a seasonal basis (e.g., pop-up stores, seasonal distribution centers). Supports seasonal workforce planning.']
  labor_union_code varchar [note: 'Labor union or collective bargaining agreement code applicable to this organizational unit. Supports labor compliance and FLA reporting.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who last modified this organizational unit record. Supports change management and audit compliance.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this organizational unit record was last modified. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  org_unit_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the organizational unit\'s purpose, scope, and operational responsibilities.']
  org_unit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the organizational unit. Indicates operational state and availability for workforce assignment.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center code for P&L reporting and profitability analysis. Used for GMROI and financial performance tracking.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code for regional reporting and workforce planning (e.g., \'NORAM\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\').']
  sla_compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this organizational unit is subject to SLA compliance requirements for operational performance tracking.']
  store_cluster_code varchar [note: 'Store cluster or group code for merchandising and allocation planning. Used for assortment planning and inventory allocation.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for this organizational unit (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Used for shift scheduling and payroll processing.']
  unit_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the organizational unit. Used for external reporting and system integration.']
  unit_name varchar [note: 'Full business name of the organizational unit (e.g., \'North America Retail Operations\', \'Distribution Center East\', \'Corporate Marketing\').']
  unit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the organizational unit type. Determines reporting structure and operational characteristics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: store|distribution_center|corporate|regional|division|department|cost_center|business_unit — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier who created this organizational unit record. Supports audit trail and GDPR compliance.']

  Note: 'Organizational hierarchy node representing departments, cost centers, divisions, regions, and store clusters within the apparel-fashion enterprise. Captures unit name, unit type (store, DC, corporate, regional), parent unit, cost center code, GL segment, effective dates, and responsible manager. Enables hierarchical roll-up for workforce reporting and labor cost allocation.'
}

Table workforce.job_profile {
  job_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the job profile. Primary key. Serves as the template identifier for position creation and talent acquisition in Workday HCM.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the HR business partner or executive who approved this job profile for use. Required for governance and compliance.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile was formally approved for use in position creation and talent acquisition.']
  bonus_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this job profile are eligible for performance-based bonus compensation.']
  career_path_progression varchar [note: 'Typical next-level job profiles in the career progression path. Used for succession planning and employee development.']
  certifications_required varchar [note: 'Professional certifications or licenses required or preferred for this job profile. Examples include CPA, PHR, PMP, or industry-specific certifications.']
  commission_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this job profile are eligible for commission-based compensation, common in retail and wholesale sales roles.']
  competency_requirements varchar [note: 'Detailed list of competencies required for this job profile including behavioral, technical, and leadership competencies. Used for talent assessment and development planning.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  critical_role_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile is designated as business-critical requiring succession planning and talent pipeline development.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the pay range amounts. Supports multi-country operations.']
  education_level_required varchar [note: 'Minimum education level required for this job profile. Used for candidate screening and talent acquisition. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: high_school|associate|bachelor|master|doctorate|professional_certification|none — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile is no longer active for new position creation. Null for open-ended profiles. Supports job profile retirement.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this job profile becomes active and available for position creation and hiring. Supports temporal validity.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Standard employment type associated with this job profile indicating work schedule and duration expectations.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Classification under the Fair Labor Standards Act determining overtime eligibility. Exempt employees are not eligible for overtime pay; non-exempt employees are eligible.']
  job_family varchar [note: 'Broad occupational category grouping similar roles together for career pathing and compensation planning in apparel fashion business. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: retail_operations|merchandising|design|supply_chain|corporate|distribution|e_commerce|marketing|finance|human_resources|information_technology|legal|customer_service — 13 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Hierarchical level of the job profile within the organization indicating seniority and scope of responsibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: entry|intermediate|senior|lead|manager|senior_manager|director|senior_director|vice_president|senior_vice_president|executive — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  job_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job profile indicating whether it is available for position creation and hiring.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this job profile record was last updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  management_level varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile includes people management responsibilities and at what level.']
  pay_frequency varchar [note: 'Standard pay frequency for positions created from this job profile indicating how often compensation is disbursed.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade level defining the pay range band for this job profile. Used for salary planning and equity analysis.']
  pay_range_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum annual salary or hourly wage for this job profile in the base currency. Represents the ceiling for this role.']
  pay_range_midpoint decimal(15,2) [note: 'Midpoint annual salary or hourly wage for this job profile representing the target compensation for fully competent performance.']
  pay_range_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum annual salary or hourly wage for this job profile in the base currency. Used for compensation planning and offer generation.']
  physical_requirements varchar [note: 'Physical demands and requirements of the job including lifting, standing, mobility, and environmental conditions. Required for ADA compliance and workplace safety.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the job profile used across HR systems and organizational documentation. Externally-known unique code.']
  profile_description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive description of the job profile including purpose, scope, and key responsibilities.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Official name of the job profile as it appears in organizational documentation and job postings.']
  seasonal_role_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this job profile is used for seasonal workforce planning, common in retail store operations during peak periods.']
  skill_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific skills required for successful performance in this job profile including technical skills, software proficiency, and domain expertise.']
  span_of_control_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of direct reports typically managed by positions in this job profile. Used for organizational planning.']
  span_of_control_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of direct reports typically managed by positions in this job profile. Null for individual contributor roles.']
  stock_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions in this job profile are eligible for equity compensation such as stock options or restricted stock units.']
  supervisory_organization varchar [note: 'Default supervisory organization or department where positions from this job profile typically reside in the organizational hierarchy.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'Expected percentage of time spent traveling for business purposes. Used for candidate expectations and expense planning.']
  union_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether positions created from this job profile are eligible for union membership or collective bargaining representation.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of this job profile record. Incremented with each significant update to track profile evolution over time.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Standard work location arrangement for this job profile indicating whether the role is performed on-site, remotely, or in a hybrid model.']
  years_experience_required varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant work experience required for this job profile. Used for candidate qualification.']
