  quaternary_labor_remediation_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or compliance officer assigned to own and execute the remediation action.']
  tertiary_labor_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or supervisor responsible for the affected employee or location at the time of the event.']
  audit_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or identifier from FLA, WRAP, or other third-party audit reports linking this event to a formal audit finding.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor compliance event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the financial impact amount.']
  detected_by varchar [note: 'Source or method by which the compliance event was detected (e.g., automated system alert, manager report, employee complaint, audit finding). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: system_automated|manager_report|employee_report|internal_audit|external_audit|whistleblower|regulatory_inspection — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  event_date date [note: 'Date when the labor compliance event or violation occurred.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the labor compliance event was recorded or detected in the system.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Category of labor compliance event or violation. Includes wage-and-hour incidents, missed meal/rest break violations, overtime threshold breaches, child labor checks, work authorization expiry alerts, and FLA/WRAP audit findings. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wage_hour_violation|missed_meal_break|missed_rest_break|overtime_threshold_breach|child_labor_check|work_authorization_expiry|fla_audit_finding|wrap_audit_finding|safety_violation|discrimination_incident — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  financial_impact_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the financial impact or penalty associated with the compliance event, including back wages, fines, or settlements. Expressed in USD.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this labor compliance event record was last updated or modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, comments, or context related to the compliance event, remediation progress, or resolution.']
  recurrence_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance event is a repeat occurrence for the same employee or location, signaling a pattern requiring escalated intervention.']
  regulatory_filing_date date [note: 'Date when the compliance event was formally reported or filed with the applicable regulatory authority. Null if no filing is required or filing is pending.']
  regulatory_filing_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this compliance event requires formal reporting or filing with a regulatory body (e.g., FTC, OSHA, state labor department).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The governing regulatory framework or standard under which the compliance event is classified (e.g., FLSA for wage-hour, FLA for labor rights, WRAP for production standards, OSHA for safety). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flsa|fla|wrap|osha|state_labor_law|local_ordinance|international_labor_standards|company_policy — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  remediation_action varchar [note: 'Description of the corrective or remediation action taken or planned to address the compliance event and prevent recurrence.']
  remediation_completed_date date [note: 'Actual date when the remediation action was completed and verified. Null if remediation is still in progress.']
  remediation_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the remediation action must be completed to achieve compliance.']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the compliance event in the resolution workflow, indicating whether it is open, being addressed, resolved, or closed.']
  root_cause_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the underlying root cause of the compliance event, used for trend analysis and systemic remediation planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: policy_gap|training_deficiency|system_error|management_oversight|workload_pressure|inadequate_staffing|process_failure — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity classification of the compliance event indicating the level of risk or impact to the business and affected employees.']
  violation_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the labor compliance event or violation, including circumstances and context.']

  Note: 'Records labor law compliance events and violations including wage-and-hour incidents, missed meal/rest break violations, overtime threshold breaches, child labor checks, work authorization expiry alerts, and FLA/WRAP audit findings. Captures event type, event date, employee affected, location, severity, remediation action, and resolution status. Critical for FTC, FLA, and WRAP regulatory compliance.'
}

Table workforce.benefit_plan {
  benefit_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for benefit_plan']
  superseded_benefit_plan_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on benefit_plan (superseded_benefit_plan_id)']
  carrier_name varchar [note: 'Name of the insurance carrier or third-party administrator providing the benefit plan.']
  carrier_policy_number varchar [note: 'Unique policy or contract number assigned by the insurance carrier for this benefit plan.']
  coinsurance_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of covered expenses the employee pays after meeting the deductible.']
  contribution_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which employee and employer contributions are collected for the benefit plan.']
  copay_amount decimal(8,2) [note: 'Fixed dollar amount the employee pays for specific covered services (e.g., doctor visits, prescriptions).']
  coverage_level varchar [note: 'Scope of coverage indicating who is eligible under the plan (employee only, family, etc.).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit plan record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this benefit plan.']
  deductible_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual deductible amount the employee must pay before the plan begins coverage. Applicable to health and insurance plans.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan is no longer available for new enrollments. Nullable for open-ended plans.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the benefit plan becomes active and available for employee enrollment.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Description of employee eligibility requirements for enrollment in this benefit plan (e.g., full-time status, waiting period).']
  employee_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard monetary amount the employee contributes per pay period for this benefit plan.']
  employee_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the employee group or division eligible for this benefit plan (e.g., corporate, retail, distribution center).']
  employer_contribution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Standard monetary amount the employer contributes per pay period for this benefit plan.']
  enrollment_period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the annual open enrollment period for this benefit plan.']
  enrollment_period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the annual open enrollment period when employees can elect or change this benefit plan.']
  is_aca_compliant boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan meets Affordable Care Act minimum essential coverage and affordability requirements.']
  is_cobra_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan is subject to COBRA continuation coverage requirements.']
  is_fsa_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan allows employees to contribute to a Flexible Spending Account for eligible expenses.']
  is_hsa_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this benefit plan qualifies as a high-deductible health plan eligible for Health Savings Account contributions.']
  is_pre_tax_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employee contributions to this benefit plan are eligible for pre-tax payroll deduction under IRS Section 125.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this benefit plan record was last modified in the system.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Type of provider network for health plans (PPO, HMO, EPO, POS, or indemnity).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or administrative comments about the benefit plan.']
  out_of_pocket_maximum decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum amount the employee will pay out-of-pocket in a plan year before the plan covers 100% of costs.']
  plan_administrator_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the plan administrator for employee inquiries and plan management.']
  plan_administrator_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual or department responsible for administering this benefit plan.']
  plan_administrator_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the plan administrator for employee support and plan inquiries.']
  plan_category varchar [note: 'Broader classification grouping related benefit types for reporting and administration.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code identifying the benefit plan in HR systems and employee communications.']
  plan_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the official plan document, summary plan description, or benefits guide.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the benefit plan as displayed to employees and in HR documentation.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of benefit plan indicating the primary coverage area.']
  plan_year_end_date date [note: 'End date of the benefit plan year for coverage and contribution calculations.']
  plan_year_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the benefit plan year for coverage and contribution calculations.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the benefit plan indicating availability for enrollment and administration.']
  summary_plan_description varchar [note: 'Brief textual summary of the benefit plan features, coverage, and key terms for employee communication.']
  waiting_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days a new employee must wait before becoming eligible to enroll in this benefit plan.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for benefit_plan. Referenced by benefit_plan_id.'
}

Table workforce.course {
  course_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for course']
  prerequisite_course_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on course (prerequisite_course_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the external training vendor in the procurement or vendor management system.']
  assessment_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether learners must complete a formal assessment or examination to pass the course.']
  average_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'The mean rating score provided by learners who have completed the course, typically on a 1-5 scale.']
  category varchar [note: 'Business classification grouping courses by subject area or functional domain.']
  certification_awarded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether successful completion of the course results in a formal certification.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'The name of the certification awarded upon successful course completion, if applicable.']
  certification_validity_months varchar [note: 'The number of months the certification remains valid before renewal or recertification is required.']
  compliance_framework varchar [note: 'The regulatory or industry compliance framework that mandates this course (e.g., OSHA, SOX, GDPR).']
  compliance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course is mandatory for regulatory or organizational compliance purposes.']
  content_owner varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the department, team, or individual responsible for course content and maintenance.']
  content_url varchar [note: 'The web address or link to access the course content in the learning management system or content repository.']
  cost_per_learner decimal(10,2) [note: 'The standard cost charged or budgeted per learner for course delivery, in USD.']
  course_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique alphanumeric code for the course, used in catalogs and registration systems.']
  course_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the course delivery method and format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the course record was first created in the system.']
  credit_hours decimal(4,2) [note: 'The number of continuing education or professional development credits awarded upon successful completion.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'The instructional delivery channel or platform through which the course is provided.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the course content, objectives, and learning outcomes.']
  difficulty_level varchar [note: 'The skill or knowledge level required or targeted by the course content.']
  duration_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'The total instructional time required to complete the course, measured in hours.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date after which the course is no longer available for new enrollments or delivery.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the course becomes available for enrollment and delivery.']
  is_mobile_compatible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the course content is accessible and optimized for mobile devices.']
  language varchar [note: 'The primary language in which the course content is delivered, using ISO 639-2 three-letter codes.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'The date when the course content was last reviewed for accuracy, relevance, and compliance.']
  learning_objectives varchar [note: 'Structured list of specific skills, knowledge, or competencies learners will acquire upon completion.']
  max_enrollment varchar [note: 'The maximum number of learners that can be enrolled in a single offering of the course.']
  min_enrollment varchar [note: 'The minimum number of learners required for a course offering to proceed.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the course record was last updated or modified.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next periodic review of course content and relevance.']
  passing_score_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage score required on assessments to successfully complete the course.']
  prerequisites varchar [note: 'Description of required prior knowledge, skills, or completed courses necessary before enrolling.']
  retired_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the course was retired or archived from active use.']
  short_title varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or shortened version of the course title for display in constrained UI spaces.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the course indicating availability and operational readiness.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated keywords or labels used for course search, filtering, and categorization.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended learner population or job roles for which the course is designed.']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'The web address of the course thumbnail or preview image used in catalogs and search results.']
  title varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the course as displayed to learners and in catalogs.']
  version varchar [note: 'The version number of the course content, used to track revisions and updates.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for course. Referenced by course_id.'
}

Table workforce.department {
  department_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for department']
  division_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the business division to which this department belongs. Used for high-level organizational segmentation and financial reporting.']
  location_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary physical location or facility where this department operates. Used for workforce planning, space management, and logistics.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who serves as the department manager or head. Used for organizational hierarchy, approval workflows, and reporting lines.']
  parent_department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent department in the organizational hierarchy. Enables multi-level department structures and roll-up reporting. Null for top-level departments.']
  budget_amount_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total annual operating budget allocated to this department in the organization\'s base currency. Includes payroll, operating expenses, and capital expenditures.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial accounting code used to track expenses and budget allocation for this department. Links to general ledger and financial planning systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this department record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the department\'s budget and financial reporting. Typically matches the currency of the department\'s primary location.']
  department_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the department in business operations and reporting. Typically used in payroll, time tracking, and financial systems.']
  department_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the department based on its primary function within the organization. Distinguishes between corporate functions, retail operations, distribution centers, manufacturing facilities, and support services.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the department\'s purpose, responsibilities, and scope of operations within the organization.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this department ceased operations or when the current configuration ended. Null for currently active departments. Used for historical analysis and organizational restructuring tracking.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this department became operational or when the current configuration became effective. Used for historical tracking and organizational change management.']
  headcount_actual varchar [note: 'Current number of active employees assigned to this department. Used for staffing analysis and variance reporting against planned headcount.']
  headcount_planned varchar [note: 'Approved number of full-time equivalent positions budgeted for this department. Used for workforce planning and budget management.']
  is_customer_facing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this department has direct customer interaction. True for retail associates, customer service, and sales; false for back-office functions.']
  is_revenue_generating boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this department directly generates revenue for the organization. True for retail stores, sales teams, and e-commerce; false for support functions like HR, IT, and finance.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this department record was last updated. Used for change tracking, audit compliance, and data synchronization.']
  name varchar [note: 'Full official name of the department as recognized in organizational structure and human resources systems.']
  requires_seasonal_staffing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this department requires seasonal or temporary workforce augmentation during peak periods such as holidays, back-to-school, or fashion season launches.']
  safety_certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this department must complete safety training and certification. Typically true for distribution centers, manufacturing, and warehouse operations.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or shortened name of the department used in reports, dashboards, and user interfaces where space is limited.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the department in the organizational hierarchy. Active departments are fully operational, inactive are temporarily non-operational, suspended are under review, planned are future departments, and closed are permanently discontinued.']
  union_representation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this department are represented by a labor union or collective bargaining agreement. Used for labor relations, contract compliance, and workforce planning.']
  work_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Primary work schedule pattern for employees in this department. Standard for 9-5 office work, shift for retail and distribution centers, flexible for variable hours, remote for work-from-home, hybrid for mixed office/remote, and on-call for emergency response.']
  workday_department_code varchar [note: 'External identifier for this department in the Workday HCM system. Used for data integration, synchronization, and cross-system reconciliation between the lakehouse and the source HR system.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for department. Referenced by department_id.'
}

Table workforce.role {
  role_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for role']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the financial cost center that budgets and tracks expenses for employees in this role.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary department or organizational unit where this role is typically positioned.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved this role definition or most recent changes.']
  reports_to_role_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supervisory role that this role typically reports to in the organizational hierarchy. Nullable for executive roles.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state for new or modified role definitions.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this role definition was approved through the governance workflow.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this role are eligible for performance-based bonus compensation.']
  commission_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether employees in this role are eligible for sales commission or incentive compensation.']
  compensation_band_maximum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum salary or hourly wage for this role within the established compensation structure.']
  compensation_band_minimum decimal(15,2) [note: 'Minimum salary or hourly wage for this role within the established compensation structure.']
  compensation_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the compensation band values.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this role record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Comprehensive narrative describing the purpose, scope, and key responsibilities of the role.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this role definition was retired or superseded. Null for currently active roles.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this role definition became active and available for employee assignment.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Standard employment classification indicating expected work schedule and duration for this role.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'Classification under US labor law determining overtime eligibility and wage requirements.']
  headcount_target varchar [note: 'Planned number of employees to be assigned to this role across the organization for workforce planning purposes.']
  is_customer_facing boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role involves direct interaction with external customers, used for training and performance management.']
  is_safety_sensitive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the role involves responsibilities that could impact workplace safety, requiring additional screening or compliance measures.']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this role is typically filled on a seasonal basis to support peak business periods such as holiday retail seasons.']
  job_grade varchar [note: 'Compensation grade level assigned to the role for salary band determination and career progression mapping.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Numeric representation of the role\'s hierarchical position within the organization, used for reporting structure and career pathing.']
  key_responsibilities varchar [note: 'Detailed list of primary duties and accountabilities expected from individuals in this role.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this role record was most recently updated.']
  last_reviewed_date date [note: 'Date when this role definition was last reviewed for accuracy and relevance by HR or management.']
  minimum_education_level varchar [note: 'Lowest level of formal education required for eligibility to hold this role.']
  minimum_years_experience varchar [note: 'Minimum number of years of relevant professional experience required for this role.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of this role definition to ensure continued alignment with business needs.']
  physical_requirements varchar [note: 'Description of physical demands and working conditions associated with the role, including lifting, standing, or environmental factors.']
  preferred_qualifications varchar [note: 'Additional desirable qualifications that enhance candidate suitability but are not mandatory for the role.']
  required_qualifications varchar [note: 'Mandatory education, certifications, experience, and skills necessary to perform the role successfully.']
  role_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the role used across HR systems and organizational charts.']
  role_family varchar [note: 'High-level categorization grouping similar roles together for workforce planning and talent management purposes.']
  role_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating the level of leadership responsibility and organizational hierarchy position.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the role indicating whether it is available for assignment, retired, or awaiting approval.']
  title varchar [note: 'Official job title or role name as displayed on organizational charts, job postings, and employee records.']
  travel_requirement_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of work time requiring business travel, used for candidate expectations and expense planning.']
  work_location_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the role requires physical presence at a company location, can be performed remotely, or follows a hybrid model.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for role. Referenced by role_id.'
}

Table workforce.shift_swap_request {
  shift_swap_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for shift_swap_request']
  counterpart_shift_swap_request_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on shift_swap_request (counterpart_shift_swap_request_id)']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Department or functional area within the location where the shift swap applies (e.g., sales floor, stockroom, customer service).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Store manager or supervisor responsible for reviewing and approving the shift swap request.']
  original_shift_id varchar [note: 'The shift assignment that the requesting employee wants to swap away from.']
  proposed_shift_id varchar [note: 'The shift assignment that the requesting employee is willing to take in exchange. May be null for one-way swap requests.']
  requesting_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who is initiating the shift swap request and seeking to exchange their assigned shift.']
  retail_store_id varchar [note: 'Retail store or distribution center location where the shift swap would occur.']
  target_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who is being asked to swap shifts with the requesting employee. May be null if request is open to any qualified employee.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided by the requesting employee for why they cancelled the shift swap request.']
  cancellation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift swap request was cancelled by the requesting employee. Null if not cancelled.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift swap was fully executed and schedule changes were finalized. Null until completed.']
  compliance_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of automated compliance validation ensuring the swap meets labor law requirements (rest periods, maximum hours, etc.).']
  compliance_violation_notes varchar [note: 'Details of any labor compliance violations detected during automated validation of the shift swap request.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift swap request record was first created in the system.']
  expiration_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift swap request will automatically expire if not acted upon.']
  impacts_overtime boolean [note: 'Indicates whether approving this shift swap would result in overtime hours for either employee involved.']
  is_reciprocal_swap boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is a mutual two-way swap where both employees exchange shifts, versus a one-way coverage request.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this shift swap request record was most recently updated.']
  manager_approval_status varchar [note: 'Whether the manager has approved, rejected, or not yet reviewed the shift swap request.']
  manager_approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the manager approved or rejected the shift swap request. Null if pending manager review.']
  manager_notes varchar [note: 'Comments or feedback provided by the approving manager regarding their decision on the swap request.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether notification has been sent to the target employee and manager about this swap request.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when notification was sent to relevant parties about the shift swap request.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the shift swap request based on urgency and business impact.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation provided by the requesting employee for why they need to swap shifts.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable business identifier for the shift swap request, formatted as SSR-YYYYMMDD sequence.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift swap request in the approval workflow.']
  request_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the shift swap request was originally submitted by the requesting employee.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Classification of swap request: direct (specific employee targeted), open (any qualified employee), or partial (portion of shift).']
  requires_seniority_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target employee must have comparable seniority level for the swap to be approved.']
  requires_skill_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the target employee must possess the same skills or certifications as the requesting employee for the swap to be valid.']
  swap_reason_code varchar [note: 'Categorized reason why the employee is requesting the shift swap.']
  system_source varchar [note: 'The system or channel through which the shift swap request was submitted (Workday HCM, mobile app, web portal).']
  target_response_status varchar [note: 'Whether the target employee has accepted, declined, or not yet responded to the swap request.']
  target_response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the target employee accepted or declined the swap request. Null if no response yet.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for shift_swap_request. Referenced by shift_swap_request_id.'
}

Table workforce.job_family {
  job_family_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for job_family']
  competency_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the competency model or framework that defines the skills, knowledge, and behaviors required for success in this job family.']
  parent_job_family_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent job family in a hierarchical job family structure, enabling multi-level organizational taxonomy.']
  bonus_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether positions in this job family are typically eligible for performance-based bonus or incentive compensation.']
  career_path_type varchar [note: 'The typical career progression model for this job family, indicating whether roles follow an individual contributor, management, or dual-track advancement path.']
  category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the job family by business function or operational area within the apparel and fashion organization.']
  code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code representing the job family, used for external identification and integration with payroll and HR systems.']
  commission_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether positions in this job family are eligible for commission-based compensation, common in sales and retail roles.']
  compensation_grade_maximum varchar [note: 'The maximum compensation grade or pay band associated with this job family, defining the upper limit of compensation progression.']
  compensation_grade_minimum varchar [note: 'The minimum compensation grade or pay band associated with this job family, used for salary structure and equity analysis.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who created this job family record, supporting audit and accountability requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this job family record was first created in the system, used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  critical_skill_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this job family represents critical skills or hard-to-fill positions requiring specialized talent acquisition strategies.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the job family, including the nature of work, typical responsibilities, and scope of roles included within this family.']
  eeo_category varchar [note: 'The Equal Employment Opportunity job category used for regulatory reporting and workforce diversity analysis.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date when this job family definition is no longer active, used for historical tracking and workforce analytics. Null indicates the job family is currently active.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date when this job family definition became or will become active and available for use in workforce planning and hiring.']
  flsa_classification varchar [note: 'The Fair Labor Standards Act classification indicating whether roles in this job family are typically exempt or non-exempt from overtime pay requirements.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the user who last modified this job family record, supporting change management and audit requirements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this job family record was last updated, used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  level_range_maximum varchar [note: 'The maximum job level or grade associated with positions in this job family, defining the career ceiling within this family.']
  level_range_minimum varchar [note: 'The minimum job level or grade associated with positions in this job family, used for compensation and career progression planning.']
  name varchar [note: 'The official name of the job family, representing a group of related jobs with similar work content and skill requirements.']
  remote_work_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether positions in this job family are eligible for remote or hybrid work arrangements.']
  seasonal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this job family typically includes seasonal or temporary positions, common in retail and distribution operations during peak periods.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the source system from which this job family record originated, typically Workday HCM, used for data lineage and integration tracking.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier for this job family in the source system, used for reconciliation and bidirectional synchronization with Workday HCM.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the job family indicating whether it is actively used for workforce planning and hiring.']
  stock_option_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether positions in this job family are eligible for equity compensation such as stock options or restricted stock units.']
  succession_planning_priority varchar [note: 'The priority level assigned to this job family for succession planning and leadership development initiatives.']
  travel_requirement_percentage varchar [note: 'The typical percentage of time employees in this job family are expected to travel for business purposes, expressed as an integer from 0 to 100.']
  typical_education_requirement varchar [note: 'The typical minimum education level required or preferred for positions within this job family.']
  typical_experience_years varchar [note: 'The typical number of years of relevant work experience required or preferred for entry into this job family.']
  union_eligible boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether positions in this job family are eligible for union membership or collective bargaining representation.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for job_family. Referenced by job_family_id.'
}

Table workforce.budget_period {
  budget_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for budget_period']
  parent_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent budget period in a hierarchical structure (e.g., a quarterly period may reference its parent annual period).']
  prior_budget_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on budget_period (prior_budget_period_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status indicating whether the budget period has been reviewed and approved by management.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person or role who approved this budget period.']
  approved_date date [note: 'The date when the budget period was formally approved by authorized management.']
  budget_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the budget associated with this period, supporting multiple budget scenarios (e.g., Original, Revised, Forecast).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget period record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for budget amounts in this period (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description or notes about this budget period, including special considerations, planning assumptions, or business context.']
  duration_days varchar [note: 'The total number of calendar days in this budget period.']
  end_date date [note: 'The effective end date when this budget period concludes (inclusive).']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'The fiscal month within the fiscal year (1-12), if applicable to this budget period type.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter within the fiscal year (1-4), if applicable to this budget period type.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this budget period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget period is currently active and available for use in budget planning and tracking processes.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the current active budget period for operational planning and tracking.']
  is_locked boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this budget period is locked and cannot be modified.']
  lock_date date [note: 'The date when this budget period was locked to prevent further modifications, typically after approval or period close.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this budget period record was last modified.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the budget period, used for external reference and reporting (e.g., FY2024Q1, BP2024H1).']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the budget period (e.g., Fiscal Year 2024 Quarter 1, Spring 2024 Budget Cycle).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget period by duration and recurrence pattern.']
  planning_cycle varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the broader planning cycle or budget process to which this period belongs (e.g., Annual Operating Plan 2024, Mid-Year Reforecast).']
  reporting_entity varchar [note: 'The organizational entity or business unit for which this budget period applies (e.g., Corporate, North America Region, Retail Division).']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Numeric value used to control the display order of budget periods in reports and user interfaces.']
  start_date date [note: 'The effective start date when this budget period begins (inclusive).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the budget period indicating whether it is in planning, active use, or historical state.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for budget_period. Referenced by budget_period_id.'
}

Table workforce.pay_period {
  pay_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for pay_period']
  prior_pay_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pay_period (prior_pay_period_id)']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month (1-12) in which the pay period primarily falls.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which this pay period falls (e.g., 2024).']
  check_date date [note: 'The date printed on physical paychecks or used as the effective date for direct deposits.']
  closed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay period was officially closed and locked for further time entry or changes.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the jurisdiction where this pay period applies (e.g., USA, CAN, GBR).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay period record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date included in this pay period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter (1-4) to which this pay period belongs.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this pay period belongs (e.g., 2024).']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this pay period is designated for payroll adjustments or corrections (True/False).']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the current active pay period (True/False).']
  is_year_end boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is the final pay period of the fiscal or calendar year (True/False).']
  legal_entity_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the legal entity or subsidiary to which this pay period applies.']
  locked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay period was locked for audit or compliance purposes, preventing any further modifications.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this pay period record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about this pay period (e.g., special processing instructions, holiday impacts).']
  pay_frequency_type varchar [note: 'The frequency at which employees are paid during this period (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly).']
  pay_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the pay group or employee population to which this pay period applies (e.g., \'RETAIL\', \'CORPORATE\', \'DC\').']
  pay_period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the pay period (e.g., \'PP01-2024\', \'January 2024 - Period 1\').']
  pay_period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the pay period within the fiscal or calendar year (e.g., 1, 2, 3...26 for bi-weekly).']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which employees are paid for this pay period.']
  payroll_processing_date date [note: 'The date on which payroll calculations and processing are executed for this pay period.']
  start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date included in this pay period.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the pay period (open for time entry, in-progress for payroll processing, closed after payment, locked for audit, archived).']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'The time zone in which this pay period operates (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'America/Los_Angeles\').']
  timesheet_due_date date [note: 'The deadline by which employees must submit timesheets for this pay period.']
  total_days varchar [note: 'The total number of calendar days in this pay period.']
  working_days varchar [note: 'The number of standard working days (excluding weekends and holidays) in this pay period.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pay_period. Referenced by pay_period_id.'
}

Table workforce.location {
  location_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for location']
  parent_location_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on location (parent_location_id)']
  accessibility_features varchar [note: 'Description of accessibility features available at the location (e.g., wheelchair access, elevators, accessible restrooms). Supports ADA compliance and inclusive workplace practices.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the location (street number, street name, building name). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for the location (suite, floor, unit, apartment). Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality where the location is situated. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date when the location permanently ceased operations. Null for active locations.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center code associated with the location for accounting and budget allocation purposes.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code where the location is situated (e.g., USA, GBR, FRA, CHN, IND).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the location record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for financial transactions at this location (e.g., USD, GBP, EUR, CNY).']
  district varchar [note: 'Business district or sub-region within a region to which the location belongs. Used for district manager assignments and localized operations.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary contact email address for the location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  is_seasonal boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the location operates on a seasonal basis (e.g., pop-up stores, seasonal outlets). True if seasonal, False if year-round.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent safety or compliance inspection conducted at the location.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the location in decimal degrees. Used for mapping, logistics routing, and proximity analytics.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement for the location expires. Applicable only to leased locations. Used for lease renewal planning.']
  lease_or_owned varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the location is leased, owned, or subleased by the organization.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement for the location became effective. Applicable only to leased locations.']
  location_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the location (e.g., store code, warehouse code, office code). Used for operational reference and reporting.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the location (e.g., \'Manhattan Flagship Store\', \'Portland Distribution Center\', \'Corporate Headquarters\').']
  location_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the location in its lifecycle.']
  location_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the location by its primary business function within the apparel and fashion operations.']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the location in decimal degrees. Used for mapping, logistics routing, and proximity analytics.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the location manager or site lead responsible for day-to-day operations.']
  monthly_rent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monthly rent payment amount for leased locations in the location\'s local currency. Used for financial planning and cost allocation.']
  next_inspection_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next safety or compliance inspection at the location.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date when the location first opened for business operations.']
  operating_hours varchar [note: 'Standard operating hours for the location (e.g., \'Mon-Fri 9:00-18:00\', \'Mon-Sat 10:00-21:00, Sun 11:00-19:00\'). Used for staffing and customer communication.']
  parking_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of parking spaces available at the location for employees and visitors.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the location. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the location address. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  region varchar [note: 'Business region or geographic market segment to which the location belongs (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'Asia Pacific\'). Used for regional reporting and organizational hierarchy.']
  safety_certification varchar [note: 'Safety and compliance certifications held by the location (e.g., OSHA compliance, fire safety certification, building code compliance).']
  seasonal_close_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when a seasonal location typically closes for the off-season. Applicable only to seasonal locations.']
  seasonal_open_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) when a seasonal location typically opens for operations. Applicable only to seasonal locations.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of employees or workstations that can be accommodated at the location. Applicable to corporate offices and design studios.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total floor area of the location in square feet. Used for capacity planning, lease management, and operational cost allocation.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region where the location is situated. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the location (e.g., \'America/New_York\', \'Europe/London\'). Used for scheduling, payroll, and operational reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the location record was last modified in the system. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for location. Referenced by location_id.'
}

Table workforce.competency_model {
  competency_model_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for competency_model']
  parent_competency_model_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on competency_model (parent_competency_model_id)']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the competency model was formally approved. Null if not yet approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow status for the competency model before it can be activated.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executive or HR leader who approved this competency model for organizational use. Null if not yet approved.']
  business_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific business unit or division this competency model is designed for. Null for enterprise-wide models.']
  competency_count varchar [note: 'Total number of individual competencies included in this competency model.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who originally created this competency model record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this competency model record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the competency model purpose, scope, and intended application within the organization.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this competency model is no longer active. Null for open-ended models.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this competency model becomes active and available for assignment to roles and employees.']
  external_reference_url varchar [note: 'Web link to external documentation, industry frameworks, or certification standards this competency model aligns with.']
  is_global boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this competency model applies globally across all regions or is region-specific.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this competency model is mandatory for all employees in the target audience or optional for development purposes.']
  job_family_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the job family this competency model is associated with for role-based competency mapping.']
  job_level_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the organizational job level (e.g., entry, mid, senior, executive) this model applies to.']
  last_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who last modified this competency model record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this competency model record was last updated.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the competency model was last reviewed for accuracy and relevance.']
  model_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier code for the competency model used in HR systems and documentation.']
  model_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the competency model (e.g., Retail Store Manager Competencies, Distribution Center Leadership Model).']
  model_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the competency model by its primary focus area.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next periodic review of the competency model.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about the competency model for internal reference.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the HR business partner or talent management leader responsible for maintaining this competency model.']
  parent_model_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a parent or predecessor competency model if this model is derived from or supersedes another model. Null for standalone models.']
  proficiency_scale_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the proficiency rating scale used to assess competency levels within this model (e.g., 1-5 scale, beginner-expert).']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code indicating the specific region this competency model applies to. Null for global models.']
  review_frequency_months varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval in months for periodic review and update of the competency model to ensure relevance (e.g., 12, 24).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the competency model indicating whether it is available for use in talent management processes.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary employee population or workforce segment for which this competency model is designed.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of active employee assignments or job profiles currently using this competency model.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the competency model to track revisions and updates over time (e.g., 1.0, 2.1).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for competency_model. Referenced by competency_model_id.'
}

Table compliance.regulatory_filing {
  regulatory_filing_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the regulatory filing record. Primary key for the regulatory filing entity.']
  audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.audit. Business justification: Regulatory filings report audit results to governing bodies (e.g., factory audit score submitted to FLA). This FK links the filing to the audit being reported. audit_score on filing is redundant with ']
  compliance_certification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_certification. Business justification: Regulatory filings reference certifications as supporting evidence (e.g., OEKO-TEX certification submitted with EU market entry filing). This FK links the filing to the certification it references. ce']
  esg_report_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.esg_report. Business justification: Regulatory filings (California Transparency Act, UK Modern Slavery Act, EU CSRD) require ESG data from sustainability reporting systems. Linking enables automated regulatory disclosure generation from']
  obligation_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.compliance_obligation. Business justification: Regulatory filings are submitted to satisfy compliance obligations (e.g., annual ESG report filing satisfies ESG reporting obligation). This FK links the filing transaction to the obligation it addres']
  production_factory_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the manufacturing facility or production site that is the subject of or associated with this regulatory filing (e.g., for factory audits, safety certifications).']
  regulatory_change_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to compliance.regulatory_change. Business justification: Regulatory filings may be triggered by regulatory changes (e.g., new REACH regulation requires updated substance disclosure filing). This FK links the filing to the regulatory change that triggered it']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to product.style. Business justification: Regulatory filings (CPSC submissions, flammability certifications, safety declarations) are often style-specific. Compliance teams file regulatory submissions for new styles before market launch. Crit']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or manufacturing partner associated with the products or materials covered by this regulatory filing.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory filing was officially approved by the governing body or certification authority.']
  compliance_level varchar [note: 'The assessed level of compliance with the regulatory framework or standard, indicating whether requirements are fully met, conditionally met, or not met.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'The deadline date by which required corrective actions must be completed and documented to achieve compliance.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether corrective actions are required to address identified non-conformances or deficiencies before approval can be granted.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO country code representing the country where the products covered by this filing were manufactured or produced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory filing record was first created in the system.']
  due_date date [note: 'The deadline date by which the regulatory filing must be submitted to remain compliant with regulatory requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date from which the regulatory filing or certification becomes legally effective and enforceable.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory filing, certification, or approval expires and requires renewal or re-submission.']
  filing_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total cost incurred for preparing, submitting, and processing the regulatory filing, including fees, audit costs, and consulting expenses.']
  filing_currency varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the currency in which the filing cost is denominated.']
  filing_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the regulatory filing, including its purpose, scope, and key content or requirements being addressed.']
  filing_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique filing number or reference code assigned by the business or regulatory body for tracking and identification purposes.']
  filing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the regulatory filing indicating its position in the submission and approval workflow.']
  filing_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the regulatory filing indicating the nature of the submission (e.g., textile labeling, product safety certification, trade compliance, ESG reporting, audit report).']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'The name of the regulatory authority, certification body, or governing organization to which the filing was submitted (e.g., FTC, CPSC, OEKO-TEX, GOTS, FLA, WRAP, BCI).']
  hs_code varchar [note: 'The Harmonized System code used for international trade classification of the products covered by this filing, required for customs and trade compliance.']
  internal_notes varchar [note: 'Internal business notes, comments, or observations related to the regulatory filing for internal tracking and knowledge management purposes.']
  jurisdiction varchar [note: 'The geographic or legal jurisdiction under which the regulatory filing applies, typically represented by country code or regional authority (e.g., USA, EUR, CHN).']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the system user who last modified this regulatory filing record.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this regulatory filing record was last updated or modified in the system.']
  non_conformance_count varchar [note: 'The number of non-conformances, violations, or deficiencies identified during the regulatory review or audit process.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The business priority assigned to this regulatory filing based on risk, urgency, and business impact.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'The category or classification of products covered by this regulatory filing (e.g., apparel, footwear, accessories, textiles).']
  regulatory_framework varchar [note: 'The specific regulatory standard, law, or framework under which the filing is made (e.g., OEKO-TEX Standard 100, GOTS, ISO 9001, GDPR, CCPA, FTC Act Section 5).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation or reason provided by the regulatory body for rejecting the filing, including specific deficiencies or non-conformances.']
  renewal_frequency_months varchar [note: 'The number of months between required renewals or re-certifications for this regulatory filing (e.g., 12 for annual, 36 for triennial).']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this regulatory filing or certification requires periodic renewal to maintain compliance status.']
  responsible_party varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the individual, department, or organizational unit responsible for preparing and submitting the regulatory filing.']
  responsible_party_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the responsible party for communication and follow-up regarding the regulatory filing.']
  review_completion_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory body completed its review and assessment of the filing.']
  reviewer_name varchar [note: 'The name of the regulatory body representative or auditor who reviewed and assessed the filing.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'The primary risk category or domain that this regulatory filing addresses (e.g., product safety, environmental compliance, labor standards, trade compliance, financial reporting, data privacy).']
  submission_date date [note: 'The date on which the regulatory filing was officially submitted to the governing body or regulatory authority.']
  supporting_document_count varchar [note: 'The number of supporting documents, attachments, or evidence files submitted as part of the regulatory filing package.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or identifier of the system user who created this regulatory filing record.']

  Note: 'Master record for all regulatory filings submitted to governing bodies (FTC, CPSC, OEKO-TEX, GOTS, FLA, WRAP, etc.) across all jurisdictions. Captures filing type, jurisdiction, submission date, due date, status, responsible party, and associated regulatory framework. Serves as the authoritative log of all compliance submissions for apparel and fashion regulatory obligations including textile labeling, product safety, and trade compliance filings.'
}

  compliance_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the compliance certification record. Primary key for the compliance certification entity.']

  certificate_document_url varchar [note: 'The URL or file path to the digital copy of the certification certificate document. Provides direct access to the official certificate for verification, audit, and reporting purposes.']
  certificate_number varchar [note: 'The unique certificate number or identifier issued by the certifying body. This is the externally-known business identifier for the certification (e.g., OEKO-TEX certificate number, GOTS license number).']
  certification_category varchar [note: 'The high-level category or domain of the certification. Groups certifications by their primary focus area: product safety (e.g., OEKO-TEX Standard 100), sustainability (e.g., bluesign, Cradle to Cradle), ethical manufacturing (e.g., WRAP, FLA, SA8000), quality management (e.g., ISO 9001), environmental management (e.g., ISO 14001), occupational health and safety (e.g., ISO 45001), social accountability (e.g., SA8000), organic standards (e.g., GOTS, OCS), recycled content (e.g., GRS, RCS), or chemical safety (e.g., OEKO-TEX, bluesign).   certification_scope varchar [note: 'The scope or level at which the certification applies.
