  approval_status varchar [note: 'Overall approval status of the design review session outcome (pending, approved, conditionally approved, rejected).']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design review outcomes were formally approved.']
  brand_alignment_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00 to 5.00) representing how well the reviewed designs align with brand identity and strategic direction.']
  commercial_feedback varchar [note: 'Feedback from the commercial team on sales potential, customer demand, and distribution strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design review record was first created in the system.']
  end_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design review session concluded.']
  follow_up_review_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the follow-up design review session, if applicable.']
  follow_up_review_scheduled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a follow-up review session has been scheduled to re-evaluate revised styles.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the design review session and its outcomes are confidential and subject to restricted access.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design review record was last updated.']
  location varchar [note: 'Physical or virtual location where the design review session was held (e.g., \'Design Studio NYC\', \'Virtual - Zoom\', \'Milan Showroom\').']
  meeting_minutes_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the detailed meeting minutes document for the design review session.']
  merchandising_feedback varchar [note: 'Feedback provided by the merchandising team regarding commercial viability, pricing, and market fit.']
  next_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the next actions or revisions must be completed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'General notes and observations captured during the design review session.']
  participant_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of participant names or IDs who attended the design review session (design team, merchandising, commercial teams).']
  presentation_file_path varchar [note: 'File path or URL to the presentation materials used during the design review session.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the design review outcomes were rejected or not approved, if applicable.']
  review_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the design review session, used for external reference and tracking.']
  review_date date [note: 'The date on which the design review session took place. This is the principal business event timestamp for the review.']
  review_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the design review session (e.g., \'Spring 2025 Concept Review\', \'Fall Line Adoption Review\').']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the design review session (scheduled, in progress, completed, cancelled, postponed).']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Type of design review session indicating the stage in the design calendar (concept review, line review, final adoption review, pre-production review, sample review, technical review).']
  revision_instructions varchar [note: 'Consolidated revision instructions and feedback provided to designers for styles requiring changes.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier for the collection under review (e.g., SS25 for Spring/Summer 2025, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024).']
  start_time timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design review session started.']
  styles_adopted_count varchar [note: 'Number of styles approved for adoption and progression to the next stage.']
  styles_dropped_count varchar [note: 'Number of styles rejected and removed from the collection pipeline.']
  styles_reviewed_count varchar [note: 'Total number of styles or designs evaluated during this review session.']
  styles_revised_count varchar [note: 'Number of styles requiring revisions before re-submission.']
  sustainability_assessment varchar [note: 'Assessment of the sustainability aspects of the designs reviewed, including material sourcing, environmental impact, and compliance with sustainability standards (GOTS, BCI, OEKO-TEX).']
  technical_feedback varchar [note: 'Technical feedback on feasibility, construction, materials, and production considerations.']
  trend_alignment_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0.00 to 5.00) representing how well the reviewed designs align with current and forecasted market trends.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a formal design review session where sketches, CAD files, or samples are evaluated by the design director, merchandising, and commercial teams. Captures review date, review type (concept review, line review, final adoption review), participants, styles reviewed, decisions per style (adopted, revised, dropped), revision instructions, and next action owner. Design reviews are the critical gate events in the seasonal design calendar.'
}

Table design.review_item {
  review_item_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design review item record. Primary key for tracking individual style-level decisions within design review sessions.']
  assigned_designer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the designer responsible for executing any required revisions or advancing this style to the next development phase.']
  previous_review_item_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on review_item (previous_review_item_id)']
  primary_review_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead reviewer or decision-maker who provided the final decision for this item. Typically a design director or merchandising leader.']
  review_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent design review session in which this style was evaluated. Links this item to the broader review gate event.']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design sketch being reviewed if the item is still in conceptual phase and not yet a finalized style. Nullable for finalized styles.']
  style_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the style or design being reviewed. May link to a finalized style master record or a design sketch depending on review stage.']
  tertiary_review_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who most recently modified this design review item record. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  brand_alignment_score varchar [note: 'Numeric assessment of how well this style aligns with brand identity, aesthetic standards, and strategic positioning. Scale typically 1-10.']
  color_story varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the color palette and colorway strategy for this style. Captures the creative vision and seasonal color direction.']
  commercial_feedback varchar [note: 'Input from merchandising and commercial teams regarding market viability, pricing potential, target customer fit, and sales forecasts for this style.']
  competitive_benchmark varchar [note: 'Reference to competitor products or market comparables used to evaluate this styles positioning, pricing, and differentiation strategy.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this design review item record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage and compliance.']
  decision_rationale varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the reviewer decision was made. Captures strategic, commercial, or creative reasoning behind adopt, revise, drop, or hold outcomes.']
  decision_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reviewer decision was finalized for this item. Critical for tracking review gate progression and Time and Action (TNA) calendar adherence.']
  estimated_cogs decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for this style including materials, labor, and manufacturing overhead. Critical for margin analysis and pricing decisions.']
  estimated_retail_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected Manufacturers Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) for this style. Used to evaluate price tier positioning and competitive landscape fit.']
  estimated_unit_volume varchar [note: 'Projected production quantity or sales volume for this style. Used to assess commercial scale and manufacturing feasibility.']
  estimated_wholesale_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected wholesale price point for this style based on cost estimates and target margin. Used for commercial viability assessment during review.']
  fabric_type varchar [note: 'Primary fabric or material composition for this style. Critical for cost estimation, sustainability assessment, and technical feasibility evaluation.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'Intended gender demographic for this style. Used for collection segmentation and merchandising planning.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this design review item record is currently active and valid. Supports soft-delete patterns and historical record management.']
  item_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of this item within the design review session. Used to maintain presentation order and facilitate discussion flow.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this design review item record was most recently updated. Supports change tracking and audit requirements.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Market positioning tier for this style based on pricing strategy. Aligns with brand segmentation and customer targeting.']
  priority_ranking varchar [note: 'Relative priority of this style within the collection line. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used for resource allocation and production sequencing decisions.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product classification for this style such as tops, bottoms, outerwear, footwear, accessories. Used for portfolio balance analysis.']
  requires_new_tooling boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this style requires investment in new manufacturing tooling, molds, or equipment. Impacts cost and timeline feasibility.']
  review_notes varchar [note: 'General notes and observations captured during the review discussion for this item. Provides context and rationale for future reference.']
  reviewer_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision outcome for this style at the review gate. Adopt means approved for production, revise requires design changes, drop means rejected, hold means deferred to future review, pending means decision not yet finalized.']
  revision_comments varchar [note: 'Specific feedback and instructions for design revisions when decision is revise. Details what changes are required before the style can be adopted.']
  risk_assessment varchar [note: 'Overall risk level associated with adopting this style considering technical, commercial, supply chain, and brand factors.']
  sample_status varchar [note: 'Current status of physical sample development for this style. Tracks progression through Sample Management System (SMS) workflow.']
  silhouette_type varchar [note: 'Design silhouette classification such as fitted, relaxed, oversized, A-line, or other style-specific shape descriptors. Key design attribute for aesthetic evaluation.']
  sustainability_rating varchar [note: 'Assessment of the styles environmental and social sustainability based on materials, production methods, and supply chain considerations. Aligns with GOTS, BCI, and OEKO-TEX standards.']
  target_launch_date date [note: 'Planned market introduction date for this style if adopted. Drives Time and Action (TNA) calendar planning and production scheduling.']
  target_margin_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected gross margin percentage for this style based on estimated cost and pricing. Critical metric for commercial decision-making at review gates.']
  technical_feasibility_score varchar [note: 'Numeric assessment of how technically feasible this design is to manufacture given current capabilities, materials, and production constraints. Scale typically 1-10.']
  trend_relevance_score varchar [note: 'Numeric assessment of how well this style captures current or emerging market trends and consumer preferences. Scale typically 1-10.']

  Note: 'Individual style-level decision record within a design review session. Captures the design review reference, style or sketch being reviewed, reviewer decision (adopt, revise, drop, hold), revision comments, priority ranking within the line, commercial feedback from merchandising, and the designer responsible for any revisions. Enables granular tracking of which styles were adopted, revised, or dropped at each review gate.'
}

Table design.brief {
  brief_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design brief record. Primary key.']
  assigned_designer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the lead designer or design team member assigned to execute this design brief.']
  brief_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the design brief record.']
  brief_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user or stakeholder who approved the design brief for execution.']
  brief_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the design brief record.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Design briefs reference approved collection budgets for design spend authorization and budget consumption tracking. Real process: design budget control and variance reporting by collection.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent collection or product line that this design brief supports.']
  parent_brief_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on brief (parent_brief_id)']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Design briefs may target specific vendor capabilities (e.g., \'design collection for Vendor X\'s advanced embroidery capacity\') - strategic design planning aligning creative direction with manufacturing']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date on which the design brief was formally approved and authorized for execution.']
  brand_alignment_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Qualitative or quantitative score (0.00 to 5.00) assessing how well the design brief aligns with brand identity and positioning.']
  brief_code varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the design brief, used for cross-functional reference and communication.']
  brief_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the design brief, tracking its progression from draft through completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|issued|in_progress|under_review|completed|cancelled|on_hold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  budget_allocated decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total budget allocated for design development activities under this brief, expressed in the company\'s base currency.']
  collaboration_notes varchar [note: 'Notes on cross-functional collaboration requirements, stakeholder involvement, or coordination needs for executing the design brief.']
  commercial_objectives varchar [note: 'Business and commercial goals for the design brief, including revenue targets, market share objectives, or strategic positioning goals.']
  competitive_benchmarks varchar [note: 'Competitive products, brands, or market references used as benchmarks or inspiration for positioning and differentiation.']
  completed_date date [note: 'Date on which all design work under the brief was completed and delivered.']
  concept_submission_deadline date [note: 'Deadline by which initial design concepts must be submitted for review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design brief record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_milestone_date date [note: 'Target date by which the design work under this brief must be completed and delivered.']
  design_direction varchar [note: 'High-level creative direction and design philosophy for the brief, outlining aesthetic vision, style themes, and creative intent.']
  design_team_size varchar [note: 'Number of designers or team members allocated to work on this design brief.']
  distribution_channel_target varchar [note: 'Primary distribution channel or sales channel that the design brief targets (e.g., Direct-to-Consumer, Wholesale, E-Commerce).']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'Target gender segment for the design brief.']
  geographic_market_focus varchar [note: 'Target geographic markets or regions for which the design brief is intended (e.g., North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific).']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the design brief contains confidential or proprietary information requiring restricted access.']
  issued_date date [note: 'Date on which the design brief was formally issued to the design team.']
  issuing_department varchar [note: 'Department or functional area that originated the design brief.']
  issuing_stakeholder varchar [note: 'Name or role of the stakeholder who issued the design brief (e.g., Design Director, Merchandising Lead, Brand Manager).']
  key_trend_references varchar [note: 'List or description of key fashion trends, consumer insights, or market movements that inform the design brief.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update or modification to the design brief record.']
  merchandising_feedback varchar [note: 'Feedback or input from merchandising teams on commercial viability, assortment fit, or market alignment.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the design brief, indicating urgency and resource allocation.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or rationale if the design brief was rejected or cancelled, capturing business context for the decision.']
  sample_delivery_date date [note: 'Target date for delivery of physical samples or prototypes developed under this design brief.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier for which this design brief is issued (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024).']
  sustainability_requirements varchar [note: 'Sustainability goals, material requirements, or environmental standards that must be met in the design execution (e.g., organic cotton, recycled polyester, GOTS certification).']
  target_category varchar [note: 'Product category or classification that the design brief targets (e.g., Outerwear, Footwear, Accessories, Activewear).']
  target_customer_persona varchar [note: 'Description of the target customer archetype or persona that the design brief is intended to serve, including demographic and psychographic characteristics.']
  target_margin_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target gross margin percentage that products developed under this brief should achieve, used for commercial viability assessment.']
  target_price_point decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target retail price point or price range for products developed under this design brief, expressed in the company\'s base currency.']
  target_volume_units varchar [note: 'Target production or sales volume in units that the design brief aims to support.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the design brief that summarizes the creative project or initiative.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the design brief, incremented with each revision or update.']

  Note: 'Master record for a formal design brief issued to the design team for a season, category, or specific project. Captures brief title, issuing stakeholder (design director, merchandising, brand), season, target category, commercial objectives (price point, target margin, volume), design direction, key trend references, competitive benchmarks, delivery milestones, and brief status (draft, issued, in-progress, completed). The design brief is the commercial-to-creative handoff document.'
}

Table design.calendar {
  calendar_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design calendar record. Primary key for the design calendar entity.']
  budget_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.budget. Business justification: Season calendars are tied to approved design budgets for phased spend tracking and milestone-based budget consumption reporting in apparel collection development operations.']
  calendar_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created the design calendar record.']
  calendar_designer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design director who approves and oversees the calendar at the executive level.']
  calendar_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who approved the design calendar.']
  calendar_lead_designer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead designer responsible for overseeing the design calendar execution.']
  calendar_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified the design calendar record.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection this design calendar supports (e.g., Women\'s Sportswear, Men\'s Luxury).']
  previous_version_calendar_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on calendar (previous_version_calendar_id)']
  season_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the season this design calendar is associated with (e.g., Spring/Summer 2025, Fall/Winter 2025).']
  actual_sku_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of SKUs that were developed and approved through this design calendar.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the design calendar was officially approved.']
  calendar_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the design calendar used for external reference and tracking.']
  calendar_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for the design calendar (e.g., \'SS25 Women\'s Sportswear Design Calendar\').']
  calendar_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the design calendar indicating its operational state.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of calendar milestones completed (0.00 to 100.00).']
  concept_kickoff_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when the concept kick-off meeting occurred.']
  concept_kickoff_planned_date date [note: 'Planned date for the initial concept kick-off meeting to launch the design process.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design calendar record was first created in the system.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this calendar is on the critical path for the season launch (True=critical, False=non-critical).']
  delay_days varchar [note: 'Total number of days the calendar is delayed compared to planned milestones. Negative values indicate ahead of schedule.']
  end_date date [note: 'Official end date of the design calendar period when all milestones should be completed.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'Target gender demographic for the design calendar (e.g., Men\'s, Women\'s, Unisex, Kids).']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the design calendar is currently active and in use (True=active, False=inactive/archived).']
  line_review_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when the line review meeting occurred.']
  line_review_planned_date date [note: 'Planned date for the line review meeting to evaluate and select designs for the collection.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design calendar record was last modified.']
  mood_board_review_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when the mood board review meeting took place.']
  mood_board_review_planned_date date [note: 'Planned date for mood board presentation and review with stakeholders.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the design calendar, including risks, dependencies, and special considerations.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'Primary product category this design calendar covers (e.g., Apparel, Footwear, Accessories).']
  proto_sample_request_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when prototype sample requests were submitted.']
  proto_sample_request_planned_date date [note: 'Planned date for requesting prototype samples from manufacturers.']
  responsible_team varchar [note: 'Name of the design team or department responsible for executing this calendar (e.g., \'Women\'s Design Team\', \'Footwear Innovation\').']
  season_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year associated with the season (e.g., 2025 for SS25).']
  sketch_submission_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when design sketches were submitted.']
  sketch_submission_planned_date date [note: 'Planned date for designers to submit initial design sketches.']
  start_date date [note: 'Official start date of the design calendar period.']
  target_sku_count varchar [note: 'Target number of SKUs planned to be developed through this design calendar.']
  tech_pack_handoff_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when tech packs were handed off to product development.']
  tech_pack_handoff_planned_date date [note: 'Planned date for handing off completed technical design packages to product development and sourcing teams.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number or identifier for the calendar to track revisions and updates (e.g., \'v1.0\', \'v2.1\', \'Final\').']

  Note: 'Master record for the seasonal design calendar defining the critical path milestones from concept kick-off through design handoff to product development. Captures season, calendar version, key milestone names (concept kick-off, mood board review, sketch submission, line review, tech pack handoff, proto sample request), planned dates, actual dates, responsible team, and calendar status. The design calendar is the operational backbone of the design process.'
}

Table design.milestone {
  milestone_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design milestone record. Primary key for the design milestone entity.']
  calendar_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent design calendar that contains this milestone. Links milestone to the seasonal design planning schedule.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection this milestone belongs to. Enables tracking of milestones across seasonal collections.']
  milestone_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who created this milestone record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']
  milestone_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the individual authorized to approve milestone completion. Typically a design director, merchandiser, or product manager.']
  milestone_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this milestone record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  org_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design team responsible for this milestone. Enables team-level workload and performance tracking.']
  predecessor_milestone_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the milestone that must be completed before this milestone can begin. Defines dependency relationships in the design critical path.']
  responsible_designer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead designer accountable for completing this milestone. Primary owner for milestone deliverables.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the milestone was completed and signed off. Null until milestone is marked complete. Used for performance tracking and historical analysis.']
  actual_deliverable_count varchar [note: 'Actual number of deliverable items completed at milestone closure. Used to measure scope completion and productivity.']
  actual_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Actual number of labor hours spent completing this milestone. Used for productivity analysis and future estimation accuracy.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the milestone deliverable was formally approved. Null until approval is granted.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Approved baseline date for the milestone established at project kickoff. Serves as the reference point for measuring schedule performance.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the milestone. Tracks progress through the design critical path and identifies at-risk milestones.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this milestone record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  critical_path_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone is on the critical path for the collection launch. True means any delay directly impacts the final launch date.']
  delay_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days the milestone is delayed compared to the planned date. Negative values indicate early completion. Used for schedule variance reporting.']
  delay_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the root cause of milestone delay if overdue. Used for delay pattern analysis and process improvement. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: design_change|material_unavailable|resource_constraint|approval_delay|vendor_delay|technical_issue|scope_change — promote to reference product]']
  deliverable_count varchar [note: 'Quantitative target for the number of deliverable items expected at this milestone (e.g., 15 designs, 5 mood boards, 3 prototypes).']
  deliverable_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the expected deliverable or output for this milestone (e.g., 20 concept sketches, approved tech pack, final colorway selection).']
  dependency_type varchar [note: 'Type of scheduling dependency relationship with the predecessor milestone. Defines how the two milestones are linked in the project schedule.']
  estimated_effort_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated number of labor hours required to complete this milestone. Used for resource planning and capacity management.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'Current forecasted completion date based on project progress and known constraints. Updated regularly to reflect realistic expectations.']
  gate_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the milestone as a formal stage-gate checkpoint. Gates require cross-functional approval before proceeding to next phase.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this milestone is currently active in the design calendar. False for cancelled or archived milestones.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this milestone record. Enables change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  milestone_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the milestone indicating which business function owns the checkpoint (creative design, technical development, commercial review, compliance approval).']
  milestone_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code identifying the milestone type for system integration and reporting (e.g., MS-CONCEPT-01, MS-SKETCH-02).']
  milestone_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the design milestone event (e.g., Concept Presentation, First Sketch Review, Final Design Approval).']
  milestone_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the milestone event type. Categorizes the nature of the design phase checkpoint.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Description of actions planned to mitigate identified risks or recover from delays. Documents contingency strategies.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing important context, decisions, issues, or feedback related to this milestone. Provides audit trail for design decisions.']
  planned_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled target date for milestone completion as defined in the Time and Action (T&A) calendar. Used for baseline planning and variance analysis.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to this milestone. Guides resource allocation and escalation decisions when conflicts arise.']
  review_meeting_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the formal review meeting associated with this milestone. Used for calendar coordination and stakeholder alignment.']
  review_outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the milestone review meeting. Determines whether the design can proceed to the next phase or requires rework.']
  rework_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the milestone deliverable requires rework or revision before final approval. True triggers additional design cycles.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Current risk assessment for milestone completion. Considers factors like resource availability, complexity, and dependency risks.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the fashion season this milestone belongs to (e.g., SS24, FW24, HOLIDAY24). Aligns milestone to seasonal planning cycles.']
  stakeholder_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of stakeholder roles or individuals who must participate in or approve this milestone (e.g., Design Director, Merchandiser, Sourcing Manager).']

  Note: 'Individual milestone record within a design calendar, tracking each critical event in the seasonal design critical path. Captures milestone name, milestone type (concept, sketch, review, handoff, approval), planned date, actual completion date, responsible designer or team, completion status (pending, in-progress, completed, overdue), and any delay reason codes. Enables T&A tracking within the design phase and feeds into the broader product development T&A calendar.'
}

Table design.collaboration {
  collaboration_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design collaboration record. Primary key.']
  collaboration_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created this collaboration record.']
  collaboration_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the executive or decision-maker who approved the collaboration.']
  collaboration_merchandising_contact_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the merchandising team member coordinating the commercial aspects of the collaboration.']
  collaboration_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified this collaboration record.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the collection that this collaboration is part of or contributes to.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Designer collaboration fees, royalties, and advance payments are allocated to specific design cost centers for collaboration program cost tracking and budget management.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Collaboration royalty payments, flat fees, and advances are posted to specific GL accounts for financial reporting, accrual accounting, and royalty expense tracking in apparel operations.']
  lead_designer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal lead designer responsible for managing the collaboration project.']
  related_collaboration_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on collaboration (related_collaboration_id)']
  actual_launch_date date [note: 'The actual date when the collaboration products were launched to market.']
  advance_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The upfront advance payment made to the partner against future royalties, if applicable.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date when the collaboration was officially approved.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'The approval status of the collaboration proposal or contract.']
  collaboration_code varchar [note: 'Internal unique code or identifier for the collaboration project used for tracking and reference.']
  collaboration_name varchar [note: 'The official name or title of the design collaboration project (e.g., \'Artist X Spring Collection\', \'Celebrity Y Capsule\').']
  collaboration_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the collaboration project. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: concept|negotiation|contracted|in_design|in_production|launched|completed|cancelled — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  collaboration_type varchar [note: 'The category of creative partnership: artist collaboration, guest designer, brand co-branding, celebrity endorsement, influencer co-design, or licensing deal.']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the signed collaboration contract document.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'The expiration or termination date of the collaboration contract.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'The effective start date of the collaboration contract.']
  contracted_deliverables varchar [note: 'Description of the agreed-upon deliverables from the partner (e.g., \'Design 12 SKUs\', \'Provide artwork for prints\', \'Attend 3 marketing events\').']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this collaboration record was first created in the system.']
  design_ownership_terms varchar [note: 'Legal terms defining intellectual property ownership of the designs created through the collaboration (e.g., \'Joint ownership\', \'Company retains all rights\', \'Partner retains IP\').']
  distribution_channels varchar [note: 'The sales channels through which collaboration products will be distributed (e.g., \'DTC only\', \'Wholesale + E-commerce\', \'Exclusive retail partners\').']
  estimated_sku_count varchar [note: 'The estimated number of unique SKUs (Stock Keeping Units) to be produced as part of this collaboration.']
  exclusivity_terms varchar [note: 'Description of any exclusivity clauses (e.g., \'Partner cannot work with competitors for 12 months\', \'Non-exclusive agreement\').']
  fee_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the collaboration fees and royalties.']
  flat_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The fixed fee amount paid to the partner for the collaboration, if applicable.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'The target gender demographic for the collaboration products.']
  geographic_market_focus varchar [note: 'The primary geographic markets or regions targeted for the collaboration (e.g., \'North America\', \'Global\', \'Asia-Pacific\').']
  ip_rights_structure varchar [note: 'The structure of intellectual property rights granted or licensed through the collaboration.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the collaboration details are subject to confidentiality or non-disclosure agreements.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this collaboration record was last updated.']
  marketing_campaign_code varchar [note: 'Code or identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the collaboration launch.']
  minimum_guarantee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The minimum guaranteed payment to the partner regardless of sales performance, if applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes, comments, or context about the collaboration project.']
  partner_legal_entity varchar [note: 'The legal entity name of the partner organization or individual for contractual purposes.']
  partner_name varchar [note: 'The name of the external creative partner, artist, designer, brand, celebrity, or influencer involved in the collaboration.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'The primary product category focus of the collaboration (e.g., \'Footwear\', \'Apparel\', \'Accessories\', \'Outerwear\').']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage rate of sales revenue paid as royalty to the partner, if applicable.']
  royalty_structure varchar [note: 'The type of royalty or compensation structure agreed upon (percentage of sales, flat fee, hybrid model, or no royalty).']
  season_code varchar [note: 'The season identifier for which this collaboration is planned (e.g., \'SS24\', \'FW24\', \'Holiday23\').']
  season_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year associated with the collaboration season.']
  sustainability_focus varchar [note: 'Description of any sustainability or ethical sourcing commitments specific to this collaboration.']
  target_launch_date date [note: 'The planned market launch date for the collaboration products.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing a design collaboration event or external creative partnership — e.g., a guest designer collaboration, artist licensing deal, brand collaboration (co-branding), or influencer co-design project. Captures collaboration name, partner name, collaboration type (artist, designer, brand, celebrity), season, contracted deliverables, design ownership terms, royalty or fee structure, approval status, and associated styles. Distinct from supplier agreements — this is a creative IP partnership.'
}

Table design.embellishment {
  embellishment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the embellishment design record. Primary key.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design collection or product line for which this embellishment was originally created, enabling traceability to merchandising plans.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Embellishment development costs including sampling, digitization, and vendor setup are allocated to design cost centers for product development cost tracking and budget management.']
  derived_from_embellishment_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on embellishment (derived_from_embellishment_id)']
  designer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the designer or design team member who created the embellishment design, used for attribution and workflow management.']
  embellishment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the embellishment design for production use, used for audit trail and accountability.']
  embellishment_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the embellishment design record, used for audit trail and change management.']
  embellishment_supplier_vendor_id varchar [note: 'FK to supplier.vendor']
  embellishment_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the external supplier or vendor providing the embellishment if origin_type is supplier. Used for procurement and vendor management.']
  supplier_factory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.supplier_factory. Business justification: Embellishments may be factory-specific (e.g., \'Factory A\'s beading capability\') - technical feasibility assessment where designers specify which factory can execute specialized embellishment technique']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the embellishment design was approved for production use, following the format yyyy-MM-dd.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the embellishment design within the Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) workflow, indicating readiness for production use.']
  care_instructions varchar [note: 'Special care or handling instructions for garments with this embellishment, used for labeling compliance and customer communication per Federal Trade Commission (FTC) requirements.']
  color_specification varchar [note: 'Detailed color specification for the embellishment, including Pantone codes, thread colors, or ink formulations. May include multiple colors for multi-color designs.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the embellishment design record was first created in the system, following the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the unit cost, enabling multi-currency cost tracking for global sourcing.']
  digital_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or cloud storage location for the digital design file of the embellishment (e.g., embroidery DST file, vector artwork, print-ready PDF).']
  embellishment_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code for the embellishment design, used across Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) and Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) systems for cross-referencing and sourcing.']
  embellishment_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the embellishment design, used for identification in design briefs, tech packs, and production orders.']
  embellishment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the embellishment technique or method applied to the garment or accessory. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: embroidery|applique|beading|screen_print|heat_transfer|woven_label|sequin|rhinestone|patch|foil_print|digital_print — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  file_format varchar [note: 'Digital file format of the embellishment design file, such as DST for embroidery, AI/EPS for vector graphics, or PDF for print-ready artwork. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: DST|PES|AI|EPS|PDF|PNG|SVG|JPG — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  height_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Height dimension of the embellishment design in centimeters, used for sizing specifications in tech packs and Bill of Materials (BOM).']
  ip_ownership varchar [note: 'Defines the intellectual property rights ownership for the embellishment design, critical for legal compliance and licensing agreements.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the embellishment design is currently active and available for use in new product development, or has been retired or archived.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the embellishment design record was last updated or modified, following the format yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to produce or procure the embellishment from order placement to delivery, critical for Time and Action (TNA) calendar planning.']
  license_agreement_reference varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier for the licensing agreement if the embellishment design is licensed from a third party, used for legal and financial tracking.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum Order Quantity (MOQ) required by the supplier or production facility for this embellishment, used for procurement planning and inventory management.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, comments, or special instructions related to the embellishment design, production considerations, or usage guidelines.']
  origin_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the embellishment design was created in-house by the design team, sourced from an external supplier, licensed from a third party, or custom-developed for a specific collection.']
  placement_position varchar [note: 'Designated location on the garment or accessory where the embellishment is applied, critical for tech pack specifications and production instructions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: chest_left|chest_right|chest_center|back_upper|back_lower|sleeve_left|sleeve_right|hem|collar|pocket|cuff|hood|side_panel|full_front|full_back — 15 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reusable_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the embellishment design is a reusable asset that can be applied across multiple styles and collections, or is specific to a single product.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier for which the embellishment design was created, following industry convention (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024).']
  stitch_count varchar [note: 'Total number of stitches in an embroidered embellishment design, used for production time estimation and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Sustainability or environmental certifications applicable to the embellishment materials or production process, such as OEKO-TEX, GOTS, or recycled content certifications.']
  tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of descriptive tags or keywords for the embellishment design, used for search, filtering, and categorization in Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) systems.']
  technique varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the specific production technique or process used to create the embellishment, including stitch type for embroidery, application method for appliqués, or printing technology for screen prints.']
  thread_count varchar [note: 'Number of thread colors or individual threads used in embroidery or stitched embellishments, relevant for costing and production planning.']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'Uniform Resource Locator (URL) or path to a thumbnail image of the embellishment design, used for visual reference in Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) systems and merchandising tools.']
  unit_cost decimal(12,4) [note: 'Cost per unit to produce or procure the embellishment, used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation and pricing strategy. Expressed in the company\'s base currency.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct Stock Keeping Units (SKUs) or styles that currently use this embellishment design, used for asset utilization analysis.']
  width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width dimension of the embellishment design in centimeters, used for sizing specifications in tech packs and Bill of Materials (BOM).']

  Note: 'Master record for a decorative embellishment design used on apparel or accessories — including embroidery motifs, appliqués, beading patterns, screen print placements, heat transfer designs, and woven labels. Captures embellishment name, type, technique, placement position (chest, back, sleeve, hem), size dimensions, color specification, supplier or in-house origin, IP ownership, and approval status. Embellishments are reusable design assets applied across multiple styles.'
}

Table design.color_palette {
  color_palette_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the color palette record. Primary key.']
  assigned_designer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lead designer responsible for creating and curating this color palette.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent collection that this color palette supports. Links palette to broader collection planning and merchandising strategy.']
  derived_from_color_palette_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on color_palette (derived_from_color_palette_id)']
  primary_color_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user (typically Creative Director or Merchandising Lead) who approved this color palette for production use.']
  tertiary_color_modified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user who last modified this color palette record.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the color palette received final approval. Used for tracking design-to-production timelines and governance compliance.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the color palette in the design governance workflow: draft (in development), submitted (under review), approved (ready for production), rejected (not approved), archived (historical record).']
  collaboration_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing cross-functional feedback, design rationale, or collaboration comments from merchandising, marketing, and sourcing teams.']
  color_story_narrative varchar [note: 'Descriptive narrative explaining the inspiration, mood, and strategic intent behind the color palette. Communicates the emotional and aesthetic direction to design, merchandising, and marketing teams.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the color palette record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and design timeline analysis.']
  design_team varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the design team or studio that developed this palette (e.g., \'Womens Apparel Design\', \'Footwear Innovation Lab\').']
  digital_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or cloud storage URI where the authoritative digital palette file is stored (e.g., Adobe Swatch Exchange .ase file, Pantone Color Manager file).']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'Gender category this palette is designed for: mens, womens, unisex, kids, or all genders.']
  geographic_market_focus varchar [note: 'Primary geographic markets or regions this palette is tailored for (e.g., \'North America\', \'EMEA\', \'APAC\'). Reflects regional color preferences and cultural sensitivities.']
  hex_codes_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of all hexadecimal color codes in the palette. Used for digital design, e-commerce product imagery, and web applications.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this palette is currently active and available for use in new designs. False if archived or superseded.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the color palette record. Tracks design iteration and change activity.']
  ncs_codes_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of Natural Color System codes for colors in the palette. Used in European markets and for architectural/interior design applications.']
  number_of_colors varchar [note: 'Total count of distinct colors included in this palette. Typically ranges from 5 to 30 colors depending on palette type and collection scope.']
  palette_category varchar [note: 'Product category scope for which this palette is intended: apparel, footwear, accessories, or all categories.']
  palette_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code assigned to the color palette for system identification and cross-referencing in PLM, ERP, and merchandising systems.']
  palette_name varchar [note: 'Business name of the color palette (e.g., \'Spring Bloom 2024\', \'Urban Edge Neutrals\'). Human-readable identifier used by design and merchandising teams.']
  palette_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the palette\'s strategic role within the collection: core (foundational colors), accent (highlight colors), neutral (base colors), trend (fashion-forward colors), seasonal (time-bound colors), capsule (limited edition).']
  pantone_codes_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of all Pantone codes included in the palette. Authoritative reference for material sourcing and supplier communication.']
  primary_color_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the primary or hero color in the palette (e.g., \'Sunset Coral\', \'Midnight Navy\'). Used in marketing and merchandising communications.']
  primary_hex_code varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code for the primary color, used in digital design, e-commerce, and web applications (e.g., \'#FF6347\').']
  primary_pantone_code varchar [note: 'Pantone Matching System (PMS) code for the primary color, ensuring consistent color reproduction across materials and suppliers (e.g., \'18-1664 TPX\').']
  primary_rgb_code varchar [note: 'RGB color code for the primary color, used in digital rendering and screen-based applications (e.g., \'rgb(255, 99, 71)\').']
  ral_codes_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of RAL color standard codes for colors in the palette. Used for industrial and hardware applications (e.g., zippers, buttons, trims).']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation for why the palette was rejected, if applicable. Captures feedback for design iteration and learning.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier for which this palette is designed (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024). Follows industry-standard season coding.']
  season_year varchar [note: 'Calendar year associated with the season (e.g., 2024, 2025). Used for multi-year trend analysis and archival.']
  secondary_color_names varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of business-friendly names for secondary colors in the palette. Supports merchandising and marketing communication.']
  sustainability_focus boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this palette prioritizes sustainable or eco-friendly dyeing processes and materials. True if sustainability is a core design principle.']
  sustainability_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing sustainability considerations, such as low-impact dyes, natural pigments, or water-saving processes associated with this palette.']
  target_customer_segment varchar [note: 'Primary customer demographic or psychographic segment this palette is designed for (e.g., \'Gen Z Urban\', \'Active Lifestyle\', \'Luxury Consumer\').']
  thumbnail_url varchar [note: 'URL to a visual thumbnail or preview image of the color palette, used in PLM systems and design collaboration tools.']
  trend_alignment_tags varchar [note: 'Comma-separated tags linking this palette to macro trends, cultural movements, or trend forecasting themes (e.g., \'sustainability\', \'retro revival\', \'digital nomad\').']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of products, SKUs, or designs that reference this color palette. Indicates palette adoption and business impact.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the palette, incremented with each revision. Supports design iteration tracking and change management.']

  Note: 'Master record for a seasonal or thematic color palette defined by the design team. Captures palette name, season, palette type (core palette, accent palette, neutral palette, trend palette), number of colors, Pantone/NCS/RAL codes for each color, color names, color story narrative, and approval status. Color palettes are the authoritative color direction documents that govern colorway development, print design, and material sourcing across a season.'
}

Table design.handoff {
  handoff_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design handoff transaction. Primary key for the design handoff record.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design concept that is being handed off. Links to the originating concept record.']
  handoff_approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the design handoff. Typically a product development manager or technical design lead.']
  handoff_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific user or team lead who acknowledged receipt of the design handoff package.']
  handoff_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who last modified the design handoff record. Provides accountability for changes.']
  reissued_handoff_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on handoff (reissued_handoff_id)']
  submitting_designer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the designer who submitted the design handoff package. Responsible party from the design team.']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Design handoffs specify target vendor for tech pack execution - production planning workflow where design team designates which vendor will manufacture the style, enabling vendor-specific technical sp']
  acknowledgment_date date [note: 'Date when the receiving team acknowledged receipt of the design handoff package. Marks the start of the product development phase.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the design handoff was formally approved by the receiving team. Indicates readiness to proceed to sourcing and production.']
  bom_draft_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a draft Bill of Materials listing all components, fabrics, trims, and hardware is included in the handoff.']
  bom_draft_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the draft BOM included in the handoff (e.g., draft_v1, draft_v2). Tracks BOM iterations during design phase.']
  cad_file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the CAD files included (e.g., AI for Adobe Illustrator, CLO for CLO3D, ZPRJ for Browzwear). Indicates the software compatibility. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: AI|PDF|DXF|CLO|ZPRJ|OBJ|FBX|none — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  cad_files_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether CAD design files (e.g., Adobe Illustrator, CLO3D, Browzwear) are included in the handoff package.']
  collaboration_platform varchar [note: 'Digital platform or tool used to facilitate the design handoff and collaboration between teams (e.g., Centric PLM, Infor PLM, Microsoft Teams).']
  collection_name varchar [note: 'Name of the collection to which this design belongs. Groups related styles under a cohesive theme or brand story.']
  colorway_count varchar [note: 'Number of colorway variations included in the design handoff. Each colorway represents a different color combination for the same style.']
  colorway_specs_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed colorway specifications (Pantone codes, fabric swatches, trim colors) are included in the handoff.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design handoff record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  feedback_notes varchar [note: 'General feedback and comments from the receiving team regarding the design handoff. May include suggestions for improvement or clarification requests.']
  gender_target varchar [note: 'Target gender segment for the design. Determines merchandising placement and marketing strategy.']
  handoff_date date [note: 'The date when the design package was formally handed off from the design team to the receiving team. Critical gate event marking the end of the design phase.']
  handoff_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the design handoff, typically formatted as DH-NNNNNN. Used for tracking and reference in cross-functional communications.']
  handoff_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the design handoff. Tracks the progression from submission through acknowledgment and approval or rejection.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the design handoff contains confidential or proprietary information requiring restricted access. Used for high-profile collaborations or innovative designs.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the design handoff record was last updated. Tracks the most recent change to the record.']
  material_library_references varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of material codes or references from the PLM material library that are specified in the design. Links to approved fabric and trim options.']
  outstanding_design_items varchar [note: 'List of any outstanding design items, open questions, or pending decisions that need to be resolved by the receiving team. Tracks incomplete elements at handoff.']
  package_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to the complete design handoff package stored in the PLM system or shared drive. Provides access to all handoff materials.']
  price_tier varchar [note: 'Price tier classification for the design (entry, core, premium, luxury). Aligns with brand positioning and target customer segment.']
  print_files_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether print and pattern artwork files are included in the handoff package. Essential for styles with custom prints or graphics.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification for the design handoff. Critical and high priority items receive expedited processing by the receiving team.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product category for the design (e.g., tops, bottoms, dresses, outerwear). Used for merchandising and assortment planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: tops|bottoms|dresses|outerwear|activewear|footwear|accessories|swimwear — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  receiving_team varchar [note: 'The functional team receiving the design handoff package. Typically product development, sourcing, or technical design team.']
  rejection_date date [note: 'Date when the design handoff was rejected by the receiving team. Triggers rework by the design team.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Detailed explanation of why the design handoff was rejected. Provides feedback to the design team for revisions.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier for the design handoff (e.g., SS24 for Spring/Summer 2024, FW24 for Fall/Winter 2024). Indicates the target selling season.']
  silhouette_description varchar [note: 'Description of the design silhouette and fit (e.g., slim fit, relaxed, oversized, A-line). Key design characteristic communicated to product development.']
  style_code varchar [note: 'The style or sketch reference code for the design being handed off. Unique identifier for the style within the collection.']
  sustainability_notes varchar [note: 'Notes on sustainability considerations for the design, including use of organic materials, recycled content, or sustainable manufacturing processes. Supports compliance with GOTS, BCI, and OEKO-TEX standards.']
  target_launch_date date [note: 'Target date for the style to be available for sale. Drives the Time and Action calendar for product development and production.']
  target_price_point decimal(10,2) [note: 'Target retail price point for the design as specified by the design team. Used by product development and sourcing to guide costing decisions.']
  tech_pack_included boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a technical specification package (tech pack) is included in the handoff. Tech packs contain detailed construction, measurement, and material specifications.']
  tech_pack_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the tech pack included in the handoff (e.g., v1.0, v2.3). Tracks iterations and revisions.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal handoff of a completed design package from the design team to the product development or sourcing team. Captures handoff date, style or sketch reference, handoff package contents (tech pack, CAD files, print files, colorway specs, BOM draft), receiving team, handoff status (pending, submitted, acknowledged, rejected with feedback), and any outstanding design items. The design handoff is the critical gate event marking the end of the design phase.'
}

Table design.revision {
  revision_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the design revision record. Primary key.']
  assigned_designer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the designer assigned to execute the revision. Tracks workload distribution and designer accountability.']
  collection_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the product collection this design is part of. Enables analysis of revision patterns by collection.']
  fit_session_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fit session that generated feedback requiring this revision. Tracks revisions driven by fit and construction issues.']
  prior_revision_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on revision (prior_revision_id)']
  review_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the design review session where this revision was discussed or decided. Links revision to formal review process.']
  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person responsible for reviewing and approving the completed revision. Typically a senior designer or design director.']
  revision_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or role who requested the design revision. May be a merchandiser, product manager, design director, or technical designer.']
  sketch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent design being revised. Links to the original design concept or CAD file that is undergoing revision.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the revision was formally approved by the reviewer. Marks the completion of the revision lifecycle.']
  collaboration_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for team collaboration and communication during the revision process. Captures discussions, decisions, and context.']
  colorway_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of color palette or colorway modifications. Applicable when revision_type is colorway_change.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the designer completed the revision work and submitted for review. Marks the end of active design work.']
  construction_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of changes to garment construction, seaming, or assembly methods. Applicable when revision_type is construction_change.']
  cost_impact_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost impact. Examples: USD, EUR, GBP.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revision record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  cycle_time_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in hours from revision request to approval. Key performance indicator for design process efficiency.']
  due_date date [note: 'Target completion date for the revision. Used to manage design calendar and ensure revisions do not delay collection launch.']
  estimated_cost_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated change in unit cost resulting from this revision. Positive values indicate cost increase, negative values indicate cost reduction.']
  instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the changes requested. Provides specific guidance to the designer on what modifications are needed and why.']
  is_final_revision boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is the final approved revision before the design moves to technical design or sampling. True when design is locked for production.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this revision record was last updated. Audit trail for data lineage and change tracking.']
  original_cad_file_path varchar [note: 'Full file system path or cloud storage location of the original CAD design file. Enables designers to access the baseline version for comparison.']
  print_pattern_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of changes to print designs or patterns. Applicable when revision_type is print_change.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Urgency level assigned to this revision. Critical revisions may be required to meet launch deadlines or address safety/compliance issues.']
  product_category varchar [note: 'High-level product classification for the design being revised. Examples: tops, bottoms, outerwear, footwear, accessories.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation provided when a revision is rejected. Captures feedback for the designer to address in a subsequent iteration.']
  request_date date [note: 'Date when the revision was formally requested. Marks the start of the revision lifecycle.']
  requestor_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the person requesting the revision. Helps analyze which stakeholder groups drive the most design changes.']
  revised_cad_file_path varchar [note: 'Full file system path or cloud storage location of the revised CAD design file. Captures the output of the revision work.']
  revision_number varchar [note: 'Sequential revision number for this design, starting from 1. Increments with each revision cycle to maintain version history from concept through final approval.']
  revision_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the revision request. Tracks progression from initial request through completion or cancellation.']
  revision_type varchar [note: 'Category of design change being requested. Classifies the nature of the revision to track which design elements are most frequently modified.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Season identifier for the collection this design belongs to. Examples: SS24 (Spring/Summer 2024), FW24 (Fall/Winter 2024).']
  silhouette_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of changes to the garment silhouette or shape. Applicable when revision_type is silhouette_change.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the designer began working on the revision. Enables cycle time analysis.']
  sustainability_impact varchar [note: 'Assessment of whether the revision improves, maintains, or reduces the sustainability profile of the design. Tracks alignment with environmental goals.']
  technical_feasibility_assessment varchar [note: 'Evaluation of whether the requested revision can be manufactured with current capabilities and supplier base. Identifies potential production risks.']
  trigger varchar [note: 'Business event or decision that initiated this revision request. Captures whether the change was driven by design review feedback, fit session results, commercial strategy shift, or other factors.']
  trim_change_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of changes to trims, buttons, zippers, labels, or other embellishments. Applicable when revision_type is trim_change.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a design revision event triggered by a design review decision, fit session feedback, or commercial direction change. Captures the original sketch or CAD file reference, revision number, revision type (silhouette change, colorway change, construction change, print change, trim change), revision instructions, requestor, assigned designer, revision due date, and revision completion status. Maintains the full revision history of each design from concept through final approval.'
}

Table design.fabric_swatch {
  fabric_swatch_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the fabric swatch record. Primary key for the fabric swatch entity.']
  alternate_fabric_swatch_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on fabric_swatch (alternate_fabric_swatch_id)']
  assigned_designer_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the designer responsible for evaluating and making decisions about the fabric swatch.']
  concept_id varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the fabric swatch to a specific design concept or theme within the collection planning process.']
  higg_index_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.higg_index_assessment. Business justification: Fabric mills with Higg FEM scores provide swatches; designers consider facility environmental performance. Real process: swatch cards display Higg scores; designers prioritize high-scoring mills for s']
  primary_fabric_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who created the fabric swatch record in the system.']
  supplier_esg_assessment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.supplier_esg_assessment. Business justification: Fabric swatches are sourced from ESG-assessed suppliers. Real process: swatch evaluation includes supplier sustainability scoring (Higg FEM, social audits); designers consider facility sustainability ']
  supplier_mill_vendor_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the textile mill or fabric supplier that provided the swatch. Links to the supplier master data for sourcing and procurement workflows.']
  sustainable_material_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to sustainability.sustainable_material. Business justification: Fabric swatches represent physical samples of sustainable materials in design libraries. Real process: swatch cards display sustainability certifications (GOTS, GRS, Oeko-Tex) and designers select swa']
  vendor_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supplier.vendor. Business justification: Fabric swatches are submitted by vendors/mills for designer evaluation and adoption decisions - core material sourcing workflow linking vendor submissions to design library. Replaces denormalized supp']
  adoption_decision varchar [note: 'Final decision status regarding the fabric swatch\'s inclusion in the collection. Selected indicates approval for production, shortlisted indicates further consideration, rejected indicates exclusion, and pending indicates evaluation in progress.']
  care_instructions varchar [note: 'Recommended care and maintenance instructions for the fabric, including washing, drying, ironing, and dry cleaning guidance. Required for garment labeling compliance.']
  collection_name varchar [note: 'Name of the design collection or product line for which the fabric swatch is being considered.']
  color_code varchar [note: 'Standardized color reference code, typically Pantone TPX/TCX code or supplier-specific color code, used for precise color matching and communication.']
  color_hex_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Hexadecimal color code representing the digital approximation of the fabric color for use in digital design tools and CAD systems.']
  color_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the fabric color as provided by the supplier or assigned by the design team, such as \'Navy Heather\' or \'Sunset Orange\'.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fabric swatch record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the estimated cost, such as USD, EUR, or GBP.']
  designer_evaluation_notes varchar [note: 'Detailed notes and observations recorded by the designer during swatch evaluation, including aesthetic assessment, performance considerations, and potential applications.']
  digital_file_path varchar [note: 'File system path or cloud storage URL where the digital representation of the fabric swatch is stored, including high-resolution images or 3D texture files.']
  estimated_cost_per_meter decimal(10,2) [note: 'Estimated cost of the fabric per linear meter as quoted by the supplier, used for preliminary costing and feasibility analysis during design phase.']
  fabric_finish varchar [note: 'Surface treatment or finishing process applied to the fabric, such as enzyme wash, stone wash, coating, lamination, or water repellent finish.']
  fabric_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the fabric construction method. Determines the structural characteristics and performance attributes of the material.']
  fabric_weight_gsm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Weight of the fabric measured in grams per square meter. Critical specification for determining fabric hand, drape, and suitability for specific garment types.']
  fabric_width_cm decimal(8,2) [note: 'Width of the fabric in centimeters, measured from selvage to selvage. Important specification for pattern making and material yield calculations.']
  fiber_composition varchar [note: 'Detailed breakdown of fiber content by percentage, such as \'60% Cotton, 40% Polyester\'. Required for compliance with FTC Textile Fiber Products Identification Act.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the fabric swatch record was last updated or modified.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required from order placement to fabric delivery, as specified by the supplier. Used for production timeline planning.']
  minimum_order_quantity decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum quantity of fabric that must be ordered from the supplier, typically measured in meters or yards. Critical constraint for production planning and feasibility.']
  moq_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the minimum order quantity, such as meters, yards, or kilograms.']
  performance_attributes varchar [note: 'Special performance characteristics of the fabric, such as moisture-wicking, stretch recovery, UV protection, antimicrobial, or water resistance. Comma-separated list of attributes.']
  physical_swatch_location varchar [note: 'Storage location or bin reference for the physical fabric swatch within the design studio or material library.']
  product_category_suitability varchar [note: 'Types of garments or product categories for which the fabric is suitable, such as outerwear, activewear, dresses, or accessories. Comma-separated list.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the fabric swatch was rejected, such as cost constraints, performance issues, aesthetic mismatch, or sustainability concerns.']
  season_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the fashion season for which the swatch is being evaluated, such as SS24 (Spring/Summer 2024) or FW24 (Fall/Winter 2024).']
  supplier_fabric_reference varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s own reference code or catalog number for the fabric, used for ordering and communication with the mill.']
  sustainability_certification varchar [note: 'Environmental or ethical certifications held by the fabric, such as GOTS (Global Organic Textile Standard), OEKO-TEX Standard 100, BCI (Better Cotton Initiative), or GRS (Global Recycled Standard).']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numerical score representing the environmental impact and sustainability attributes of the fabric, typically on a scale of 0-100, based on factors such as fiber source, production process, and certifications.']
  swatch_code varchar [note: 'Unique business identifier code assigned to the fabric swatch for tracking and reference purposes across design and sourcing workflows.']
  swatch_format varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the swatch is a physical sample, a digital representation, or both formats are available.']
